meta.title: Barron's 1100 Words You Need to Know

days.title: READING WISELY

words.definition: choosing at random without careful selection

examples.en: A young man who has read the life story of every eminent athlete of the twentieth century, or a coed who has steeped herself in every social-protest novel she can get her hands on, may very well be learning all there is to know in a very limited area.
examples.zh: 一个读过二十世纪每一位杰出运动员生平故事的年轻人，或者一个浸淫于所有社会抗议小说的女大学生，很可能在有限的领域里学到了所有知识。

examples.en: The eminent author received the Nobel Prize for literature.
examples.zh: 这位杰出作家获得了诺贝尔文学奖。

examples.en: We invited the eminent engineer to address our club.
examples.zh: 我们邀请了这位杰出工程师在俱乐部发表演讲。

examples.en: A young man who has read the life story of every eminent athlete of the twentieth century, or a coed who has steeped herself in every social-protest novel she can get her hands on, may very well be learning all there is to know in a very limited area.
examples.zh: 一个读过二十世纪每一位杰出运动员生平故事的年轻人，或者一个浸淫于所有社会抗议小说的女大学生，很可能在有限的领域里学到了所有知识。

examples.en: My cousin is so steeped in schoolwork that his friends call him a bookworm.
examples.zh: 我表弟学业太投入，朋友们都叫他书呆子。

examples.en: The learned of the nation were steeped in Latin.
examples.zh: 民族的学者都浸润着拉丁语。

examples.en: But books are replete with so many wonders that it is often discouraging to see bright young people limit their own experiences.
examples.zh: 但书籍充满了许多奇迹，看到聪明的年轻人限制自己的经历常常令人沮丧。

examples.en: The football game was replete with excitement and great plays.
examples.zh: 橄榄球比赛充满了激动人心和精彩的表现。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be replete.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是饱腹。

days.passage: The youngster who reads voraciously, though indiscriminately, does not necessarily gain in wisdom over the teenager who is more selective in his reading choices. A young man who has read the life story of every eminent athlete of the twentieth century, or a coed who has steeped herself in every social-protest novel she can get her hands on, may very well be learning all there is to know in a very limited area. But books are replete with so many wonders that it is often discouraging to see bright young people limit their own experiences.

exercises.text: The football game was ________________ with excitement and great plays.
The ________________ author received the Nobel Prize for literature.
My cousin is so ________________ in schoolwork that his friends call him a bookworm.
After skiing, I find that I have a ________________ appetite.
Modern warfare often results in the ________________ killing of combatants and innocent civilians alike.

idiom.meaning: to admit your error and apologize
idiom.example: After his candidate had lost the election, the boastful campaign manager had to eat humble pie.

days.passageZh: 那些贪婪地阅读但无差别阅读的年轻人，并不一定比选择性更为挑剔的青少年获得智慧。一个读过二十世纪每一位杰出运动员生平故事的年轻人，或者一个浸淫于所有社会抗议小说的女大学生，很可能在有限的领域里学到了所有知识。但书籍充满了许多奇迹，看到聪明的年轻人限制自己的经历常常令人沮丧。

days.title: SOLVING THE SERVANT PROBLEM

examples.en: The worlds of science-fiction abound with wonders.
examples.zh: 科幻世界充满了奇迹。

examples.en: The western plains used to abound with bison before those animals were slaughtered by settlers.
examples.zh: 西部平原曾有大量野牛，直到这些动物被定居者屠杀。

examples.en: In the Catskill Mountains, the woods abound with deer.
examples.zh: 在卡茨基尔山脉，树林里满是鹿。

words.definition: branch of knowledge dealing with engineering, applied science, etc.

examples.en: Yet modern technology progresses so rapidly that what may be today’s wild dream may be next year’s kitchen appliance.
examples.zh: 然而，现代科技进步如此之快，今天可能成为梦想中的厨房电器，明年才会成为厨房电器。

examples.en: Man may be freed from backbreaking labor by the products of scientific technology.
examples.zh: 人类可以通过科学技术的成果摆脱繁重劳动。

examples.en: With his expertise in technology, my uncle is able to earn a good salary.
examples.zh: 凭借他的技术专长，我叔叔能赚到不错的薪水。

words.definition: to predict or foretell a future event

examples.en: One task this domesticated automaton will not have to contend with will be scouring the oven because even today the newest ranges can be “programmed” to reduce their own baked-on grime to easily disposed of ashes.
examples.zh: 这台被驯化的自动机不必面对的一项任务是彻底清理烤箱，因为即使在今天，最新的系列也可以“编程”将自己烤制的污垢变成易于处理的灰烬。

examples.en: Due to her strict adherence to her daily schedule, Jessica was becoming more and more convinced that she was an automaton.
examples.zh: 由于严格遵守每日作息，杰西卡越来越相信自己是个自动人偶。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be automaton.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了成为自动机的含义。

examples.en: A British scientist has prognosticated that within ten years every suburban matron will have her own robot servant.
examples.zh: 一位英国科学家预测，十年内每个郊区的女主人都会拥有自己的机器人仆人。

examples.en: A clever salesman will always ask a matron if her mother is at home.
examples.zh: 聪明的推销员总会问护士长她母亲是否在家。

examples.en: the matron of a school or hospital
examples.zh: 学校或医院的护士长

days.passage: The worlds of science-fiction abound with wonders. Yet modern technology progresses so rapidly that what may be today’s wild dream may be next year’s kitchen appliance. A British scientist has prognosticated that within ten years every suburban matron will have her own robot servant. One task this domesticated automaton will not have to contend with will be scouring the oven because even today the newest ranges can be “programmed” to reduce their own baked-on grime to easily disposed of ashes.

The western plains used to ________________ with bison before those animals were slaughtered by settlers.
Man may be freed from backbreaking labor by the products of scientific ________________.

idiom.meaning: an item you purchase without having seen; a disappointment
idiom.example: The mail order bicycle that my nephew bought turned out to be a pig in a poke, and he is now trying to get his money back.

days.passageZh: 科幻世界充满了奇迹。然而，现代科技进步如此之快，今天可能成为梦想中的厨房电器，明年才会成为厨房电器。一位英国科学家预测，十年内每个郊区的女主人都会拥有自己的机器人仆人。这台被驯化的自动机不必面对的一项任务是彻底清理烤箱，因为即使在今天，最新的系列也可以“编程”将自己烤制的污垢变成易于处理的灰烬。

days.title: IT’S A MAN’S WORLD

words.definition: a statement that at first seems to be absurd or self-contradictory but which may in fact turn out to be true

examples.en: How paradoxical that the world’s greatest chefs have all been men!
examples.zh: 真是矛盾，世界上最伟大的厨师全都是男性！

examples.en: "This sentence is false" is a paradox.
examples.zh: “这句话是假的”是一个悖论。

examples.en: It is an interesting paradox that drinking a lot of water can often make you feel thirsty.
examples.zh: 一个有趣的悖论是，喝很多水常常会让你感到口渴。

words.definition: special field of something or someone; kingdom

examples.en: Cooking would clearly seem to be a field that lies exclusively within women’s realm, yet the annals of cookery are replete* with masculine names: Brillat Savarin, Ritz, Diat, Larousse.
examples.zh: 烹饪显然似乎是女性专属领域，但烹饪史上充满了男性名字：Brillat Savarin、Ritz、Diat、Larousse。

examples.en: The cruel king would not allow the prince to enter his realm , restricting him to the forest, which abounded* with wild animals.
examples.zh: 残酷的国王不允许王子进入他的领地，将他限制在森林中，那里满是野兽。

examples.en: In the realm of the blind, the one-eyed man is king.
examples.zh: 在盲人领域，独眼人是王者。

examples.en: Cooking would clearly seem to be a field that lies exclusively within women’s realm, yet the annals of cookery are replete* with masculine names: Brillat Savarin, Ritz, Diat, Larousse.
examples.zh: 烹饪显然似乎是女性专属领域，但烹饪史上充满了男性名字：Brillat Savarin、Ritz、Diat、Larousse。

examples.en: The annals of sports are replete* with the names of great black athletes.
examples.zh: 体育史上充满了伟大黑人运动员的名字。

examples.en: Annals of Science
examples.zh: 科学年鉴

examples.en: To compound the puzzle, there has rarely been a tinge of rumor or scandal casting doubts on the masculinity of these heroes of cuisine.
examples.zh: 更复杂的是，几乎没有传闻或丑闻质疑这些美食英雄的男子气概。

examples.en: to compound a medicine
examples.zh: 调配药物

examples.en: to compound a debt
examples.zh: 复利债务

words.definition: An enclosure within which workers, prisoners, or soldiers are confined

examples.en: To compound the puzzle, there has rarely been a tinge of rumor or scandal casting doubts on the masculinity of these heroes of cuisine.
examples.zh: 更复杂的是，几乎没有传闻或丑闻质疑这些美食英雄的男子气概。

examples.en: A tinge of garlic is all that’s necessary in most recipes.
examples.zh: 大多数食谱只需要一点大蒜味。

examples.en: Without a tinge of evidence, the coroner could not solve the murder.
examples.zh: 没有任何证据，验尸官无法破案。

words.definition: A small added amount of colour; (by extension) a small added amount of some other thing.

days.passage: How paradoxical that the world’s greatest chefs have all been men! Cooking would clearly seem to be a field that lies exclusively within women’s realm, yet the annals of cookery are replete* with masculine names: Brillat Savarin, Ritz, Diat, Larousse. To compound the puzzle, there has rarely been a tinge of rumor or scandal casting doubts on the masculinity of these heroes of cuisine.

The cruel king would not allow the prince to enter his ________________ , restricting him to the forest, which abounded* with wild animals.

days.title: HOW NOT TO GET YOUR WAY

examples.en: It is difficult to change someone’s opinion by badgering him.
examples.zh: 通过不断纠缠一个人很难改变他的看法。

examples.en: He kept badgering her about her bad habits.
examples.zh: 他不断纠缠她，指责她的坏习惯。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be badger.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了獾的含义。

words.definition: Any mammal of three subfamilies, which belong to the family Mustelidae: Melinae (Eurasian badgers), Mellivorinae (ratel or honey badger), and Taxideinae (American badger).
to plead urgently for aid or mercy

examples.en: The child who begs his mother to “get off his back” when she implores him for some assistance with the household drudgery, may very well plead interminably for some special privilege when he wants something for himself.
examples.zh: 当母亲恳求他帮忙做家务时，孩子会乞求“别再烦他了”，当他想要为自己争取一些东西时，可能会无休止地乞求某种特殊待遇。

examples.en: She does her homework on Fridays to save herself from the drudgery of having to do it during the weekend.
examples.zh: 她周五做作业，是为了避免周末做的枯燥乏味。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be drudgery.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了苦差事的含义。

examples.en: I listened to the boring lecture for what seemed an interminable fifty minutes.
examples.zh: 我听了那场无聊的讲座，似乎长达五十分钟。

examples.en: After a seemingly interminable wait, the surgeon came to give us the news.
examples.zh: 经过似乎漫长的等待，外科医生来告诉我们消息。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be interminable.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“无尽”的含义。

words.definition: to understand, know, become aware of

examples.en: How paradoxical* that neither is able to perceive that no one likes being nagged.
examples.zh: 真是矛盾*，两人都没意识到没人喜欢被唠叨。

examples.en: I cannot perceive why people voted for the corrupt senator.
examples.zh: 我无法理解人们为何投票给那个腐败的参议员。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be perceive.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被感知的含义。

days.passage: It is difficult to change someone’s opinion by badgering him. The child who begs his mother to “get off his back” when she implores him for some assistance with the household drudgery, may very well plead interminably for some special privilege when he wants something for himself. How paradoxical* that neither is able to perceive that no one likes being nagged.

days.story:
In the ______ of crime, there are few scoundrels who could match the exploits of Reggie Hayes, who also used the names of Reginald Haven, Ricardo Hermosa, Father Harris, and dozens of other aliases. Reggie’s police record, principally in Chicago and Baltimore, is ______ with scams that he perpetrated upon gullible people. Generally, his favorite target was a ______ who should have known better.

Dressed as a priest (“Father Harris”), he was most convincing, however. His method of operation was to “find” a wallet stuffed with hundred dollar bills outside a supermarket and then ______ an unsuspecting woman to share his good fortune, since there was no identification in the wallet. But first, to establish her credibility, his victim had to put up a sum of money as a testimonial to her good faith. Mrs. Emma Schultz, age 72, tearfully told the police that she had withdrawn $14,000 from her bank and placed it in a shopping bag supplied by the helpful priest. He told her to hold onto the bag while he went next door to a lawyer’s office to make the sharing of their good fortune legal.

After a seemingly ______ wait, Mrs. Schultz discovered to her chagrin that the heartless thief had skipped out the back way, leaving her “holding the bag”—a switched bag containing shredded newspaper—while he made his getaway with her life savings.
days.storyZh:
在犯罪______中，很少有恶棍能与雷吉·海耶斯的事迹相媲美，他还使用了雷金纳德·海文、里卡多·赫莫萨、哈里斯神父以及数十个其他化名。雷吉的警察记录主要在芝加哥和巴尔的摩，______他对轻信者实施的诈骗。通常，他最喜欢的目标是那些本该更明智的______。

不过，他穿着神父装（“哈里斯神父”），表现得非常逼真。他的作案手法是“找到”超市外一个装满百美元钞票的钱包，然后______毫无戒心的女人分享他的好运，因为钱包里没有身份证明。但首先，为了证明她的可信度，受害者必须出示一笔钱，以证明她的诚信。72岁的艾玛·舒尔茨夫人含泪告诉警方，她从银行取出了14,000美元，并将其放入由好心神父提供的购物袋中。他让她保管包，自己则去隔壁律师事务所，让他们分享好运合法化。

经过看似______的等待，舒尔茨夫人懊恼地发现那个无情的小偷从后门逃走了，留下她“拿包”——一个装着撕碎报纸的换包包——而他则带着她的毕生积蓄逃走了。

days.title: TO THE POINT

examples.en: Calvin Coolidge, our thirtieth president, was named “Silent Cal” by reporters because of his laconic speech.
examples.zh: 卡尔文·柯立芝，我们的第三十任总统，因其简短的演讲被记者称为“沉默的卡尔”。

examples.en: His speech was usually rambling, but this time I found it brief and laconic .
examples.zh: 他的演讲通常很杂乱，但这次我觉得简短而简洁。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be laconic.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是寡言。

examples.en: Coolidge had listened to an interminable* sermon, a throng of newsmen gathered around him.
examples.zh: 柯立芝听了一场冗长的*布道，周围聚集着一群新闻记者。

examples.en: A howling throng of teenage girls surrounded the rap artists.
examples.zh: 一群嚎叫的少女围住了说唱歌手们。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be throng.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“拥挤”的含义。

examples.en: An intrepid reporter accosted the Chief Executive: “Mr.
examples.zh: 一位勇敢的记者质问了这位首席执行官：“先生，

examples.en: The corporal received the Silver Star for his intrepid deeds in combat.
examples.zh: 该下士因其在战斗中的英勇行为获得了银星勋章。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be intrepid.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是无畏。

examples.en: What did the minister say?” “He was against it,” the reticent Coolidge replied.
examples.zh: 部长说了什么？”“他反对，”沉默寡言的库立芝回答。

examples.en: Even under repeated questioning, the witness remained reticent .
examples.zh: 即使多次提问，证人依然保持沉默。

examples.en: They may appear reticent about the bother and expense of altering their wardrobe, but they give tacit approval to the change by rushing to the department stores where they jostle* each other to buy copies of the more expensive dresses.
examples.zh: 他们可能对更换衣橱的麻烦和花费显得有些顾虑，但他们默许了这种改变，匆匆赶往百货商店，互相推搡，去买更贵的裙子。

days.passage: Calvin Coolidge, our thirtieth president, was named “Silent Cal” by reporters because of his laconic speech. One Sunday, after Mr. Coolidge had listened to an interminable* sermon, a throng of newsmen gathered around him. An intrepid reporter accosted the Chief Executive: “Mr. President, we know that the sermon was on the topic of sin. What did the minister say?” “He was against it,” the reticent Coolidge replied.

exercises.text: His speech was usually rambling, but this time I found it brief and ________________ .
If a surly panhandler should ________________ you, keep on walking.
Even under repeated questioning, the witness remained ________________ .
A howling ________________ of teenage girls surrounded the rap artists.
The corporal received the Silver Star for his ________________ deeds in combat.

idiom.meaning: any imminent danger (a king seated one of his subjects underneath a sword that was hanging by a hair, in order to teach him the dangers a king faces)
idiom.example: Although the president of the company seemed quite secure, he always complained that there was a sword of Damocles hanging over his head.

days.passageZh: 卡尔文·柯立芝，我们的第三十任总统，因其简短的演讲被记者称为“沉默的卡尔”。一个星期天，柯立芝先生听完冗长的布道后，一群新闻记者围在他身边。一位勇敢的记者质问首席执行官：“总统先生，我们知道布道主题是罪恶。部长说了什么？”“他反对，”沉默寡言的库立芝回答。

reviewOptions.text: defy an opponent in his home

days.story:
Gloria Rogers overslept and then had to sprint to catch the same Greyhound Bus that she boarded on the last Thursday of every month. After a three-hour uneventful ride, she finally arrived at the bus terminal where a courtesy van was ready to transport bus passengers to Visitors Day at the State Penitentiary.

Although Gloria tried to act casual, she was more than a little nervous. Her boyfriend, Art, a convicted ______ , had managed to gain admittance to the prison’s hospital on the ______ of having a gall bladder attack. Under her own slacks and bulky sweater, Gloria was wearing a set of clothes that she removed in the hospital bathroom and passed on to Art. He planned to use them after making his escape in the back of the prison ambulance that was parked outside his ward.

Art had spelled out his escape plan during Gloria’s last visit, spending an hour trying to ______ her into being his accomplice. All that she had to do was appear to have a seizure. Then she would ______ a story about her epilepsy while Art, with the smuggled clothes concealed under his prison bathrobe, would slip out of the ward during the excitement. Unfortunately for the schemers, a ______ hospital guard spotted Art climbing into the rear of the ambulance and quickly foiled the escape attempt. The result was that Art had three years added to his sentence and Gloria was imprisoned for her role in the misadventure.
days.storyZh:
格洛丽亚·罗杰斯睡过头了，然后不得不冲去赶上她每个月最后一个星期四上过的同一班灰狗巴士。经过三小时平静的车程，她终于抵达公交总站，一辆礼宾车已准备好，准备将巴士乘客运送到州立监狱的访客日。

虽然格洛丽亚试图装作若无其事，但她还是有些紧张。她的男友阿特是一名有前科的______，______胆囊发作时设法进入监狱医院。格洛丽亚穿着自己裤子和厚重毛衣，穿着一套衣服，她在医院浴室脱下后递给阿特。他计划在逃跑后使用这些工具，乘坐停在病房外的监狱救护车后面。

阿特在格洛丽亚上次来访时详细说明了他的逃跑计划，花了一个小时试图______她成为他的同伙。她所要做的只是表现得像癫痫发作一样。然后她会______关于自己癫痫的故事，而阿特则穿着藏在囚笼浴袍下的偷偷衣服，趁着兴奋悄悄溜出病房。不幸的是，一名______医院警卫发现阿特爬上救护车后部，迅速挫败了逃跑企图。结果是阿特的刑期增加了三年，格洛丽亚因参与这起意外事件而被监禁。

days.title: THE PEP TALK

examples.en: “If there’s one thing I loathe,” the coach said, “it’s a quitter.” He had good reason to reprimand us at half-time, because the scoreboard revealed that we were losing, 45–20.
examples.zh: 教练说：“如果有一件事我最讨厌，那就是放弃者。”他有充分理由在半场时训斥我们，因为记分牌显示我们以45比20落败。

examples.en: I loathe spinach but I love other green vegetables.
examples.zh: 我讨厌菠菜，但我喜欢其他绿色蔬菜。

examples.en: I absolutely loathe this place.
examples.zh: 我真的非常讨厌这个地方。

examples.en: “If there’s one thing I loathe,” the coach said, “it’s a quitter.” He had good reason to reprimand us at half-time, because the scoreboard revealed that we were losing, 45–20.

examples.en: When Ed arrived late, he knew that the grocer would reprimand him.

days.passageZh: 教练说：“如果有一件事我最讨厌，那就是放弃者。”他有充分理由在半场时训斥我们，因为记分牌显示我们以45比20落败。我们表现平平让他觉得我们已经忘记了篮球的基本要素。不过，他的尖刻言语激发了我们的热情，我们冲出去，决心从对手手中夺回比赛的控制权。

days.title: THE HANDCUFF IS QUICKER THAN THE EYE

examples.en: Slippery Eddie, the infamous pickpocket, was back at work, and every detective had to be especially vigilant.* Eddie’s technique was to jostle a victim toward a confederate who would then slip the man’s wallet out of his back pocket while Eddie was stammering an apology to the confused dupe.
examples.zh: 臭名昭著的扒手滑头埃迪又回来了，每个侦探都必须格外警惕。* 埃迪的手法是把受害者推向同伙，然后在埃迪结结巴巴地向那个困惑的傻瓜道歉时，从后口袋里偷偷掏出他的钱包。

examples.en: The bombing of Pearl Harbor was referred to as an infamous deed.
examples.zh: 珍珠港轰炸被称为臭名昭著的事迹。

examples.zh: 他是臭名昭著的伪证者。

examples.en: Slippery Eddie, the infamous pickpocket, was back at work, and every detective had to be especially vigilant.* Eddie’s technique was to jostle a victim toward a confederate who would then slip the man’s wallet out of his back pocket while Eddie was stammering an apology to the confused dupe.
examples.zh: 臭名昭著的扒手滑头埃迪又回来了，每个侦探都必须格外警惕。* 埃迪的手法是把受害者推向同伙，然后在埃迪结结巴巴地向那个困惑的傻瓜道歉时，从后口袋里偷偷掏出他的钱包。

examples.en: When he attempted to jostle the old lady, she struck him with her umbrella.
examples.zh: 当他试图推开老妇人时，她用伞打了他。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be jostle.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“挤压”的含义。

examples.en: Slippery Eddie, the infamous pickpocket, was back at work, and every detective had to be especially vigilant.* Eddie’s technique was to jostle a victim toward a confederate who would then slip the man’s wallet out of his back pocket while Eddie was stammering an apology to the confused dupe.
examples.zh: 臭名昭著的扒手滑头埃迪又回来了，每个侦探都必须格外警惕。* 埃迪的手法是把受害者推向同伙，然后在埃迪结结巴巴地向那个困惑的傻瓜道歉时，从后口袋里偷偷掏出他的钱包。

examples.en: The wealthy dupe consented to buy the often-sold Brooklyn Bridge.
examples.zh: 这位富有的傀儡同意购买了经常被出售的布鲁克林大桥。

examples.en: Can you dupe this photo for me?
examples.zh: 你能帮我复制这张照片吗？

words.definition: A person who has been deceived.

examples.en: Within a week the incipient crimewave came to an end when Slippery Eddie inadvertently chose the chief of police for his victim.
examples.zh: 一周内，这波初起的犯罪浪潮结束了，因为滑头艾迪无意中选择了警察局长作为受害者。

examples.en: By telling the truth, we stopped the incipient rumor from spreading.
examples.zh: 通过说实话，我们阻止了初萌芽的谣言传播。

examples.en: After 500 years, incipient towns appeared.
examples.zh: 500年后，出现了新兴的城镇。

days.passage: Slippery Eddie, the infamous pickpocket, was back at work, and every detective had to be especially vigilant.* Eddie’s technique was to jostle a victim toward a confederate who would then slip the man’s wallet out of his back pocket while Eddie was stammering an apology to the confused dupe. Within a week the incipient crimewave came to an end when Slippery Eddie inadvertently chose the chief of police for his victim. Although Eddie loathes* Sing Sing, it’s his permanent address now.

exercises.text: By telling the truth, we stopped the ________________ rumor from spreading.
The bombing of Pearl Harbor was referred to as an ________________ deed.
The wealthy ________________ consented to buy the often-sold Brooklyn Bridge.
When he attempted to ________________ the old lady, she struck him with her umbrella.
Through an ________________ error, the guided missile sped out of control.

idiom.meaning: to win the approval of the majority
idiom.example: The secretary’s motion that we adjourn for lunch carried the day, and we headed for the restaurant

days.passageZh: 臭名昭著的扒手滑头埃迪又回来了，每个侦探都必须格外警惕。* 埃迪的手法是把受害者推向同伙，然后在埃迪结结巴巴地向那个困惑的傻瓜道歉时，从后口袋里偷偷掏出他的钱包。一周内，这波初起的犯罪浪潮结束了，因为滑头艾迪无意中选择了警察局长作为受害者。虽然埃迪讨厌*辛辛监狱，但那是他的永久住址。

days.title: COURTROOM DRAMA

examples.en: There was an ominous silence when the jittery defendant rose in court.
examples.zh: 当紧张的被告在法庭上站起身时，现场陷入了不祥的沉默。

examples.en: After the weatherman had seen the ominous clouds, he prognosticated* rain.
examples.zh: 气象员看到阴云后，预测了*雨。

examples.en: California poll support for Jerry Brown's tax increases has ominous implications for U.S. taxpayers too Los Angeles Times Headline April 25, 2011
examples.zh: 加州民调对杰瑞·布朗增税的支持对美国纳税人同样有不祥影响 洛杉矶时报 2011年4月25日

examples.en: He explained in a tremulous voice what had led him to repudiate his confession made at the police station on the night of the crime.
examples.zh: 他颤抖着解释了是什么让他否认了案发当晚在警察局所做的供词。

examples.en: The widow’s tremulous hands revealed her nervousness.
examples.zh: 寡妇颤抖的双手透露出她的紧张。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be tremulous.
examples.zh: 这个语境清楚地展现了颤抖的含义。

examples.en: He explained in a tremulous voice what had led him to repudiate his confession made at the police station on the night of the crime.
examples.zh: 他颤抖着解释了是什么让他否认了案发当晚在警察局所做的供词。

examples.en: The general attempted to repudiate the testimony of the lieutenant, claiming that the young officer was not an authority on low level bombing.
examples.zh: 将军试图否认中尉的证词，声称这位年轻军官并非低空轰炸的权威。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be repudiate.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被拒绝的含义。

examples.en: The audience began to buzz excitedly until the judge demanded a cessation of the noise.
examples.zh: 观众开始兴奋地喧闹起来，直到法官要求停止喧闹。

examples.en: The cessation of the bombing in Iraq was urged by the United Nations.
examples.zh: 联合国敦促停止对伊拉克的轰炸。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be cessation.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“停止”的含义。

words.definition: To rise or stand erect, like bristles.

days.passage: There was an ominous silence when the jittery defendant rose in court. He explained in a tremulous voice what had led him to repudiate his confession made at the police station on the night of the crime. The audience began to buzz excitedly until the judge demanded a cessation of the noise. Although the district attorney bristled with anger, the defendant kept insisting that his rights had been violated because he had not been told that he could see a lawyer before confessing.

exercises.text: After the weatherman had seen the ________________ clouds, he prognosticated* rain.
The general attempted to ________________ the testimony of the lieutenant, claiming that the young officer was not an authority on low level bombing.
Upon seeing the snake, the cat began to ________________ with fear.
The widow’s ________________ hands revealed her nervousness.
The ________________ of the bombing in Iraq was urged by the United Nations.

idiom.meaning: disreputable part of town, inhabited by derelicts and people “on the skid”
idiom.example: The presence of so many bars has turned our neighborhood into another Skid Row.

days.passageZh: 当紧张的被告在法庭上站起身时，现场陷入了不祥的沉默。他颤抖着解释了是什么让他否认了案发当晚在警察局所做的供词。观众开始兴奋地喧闹起来，直到法官要求停止喧闹。尽管地区检察官愤怒不已，被告仍坚持认为自己的权利被侵犯，因为他没有被告知可以在认罪前见律师。

days.title: CALL ME BY MY RIGHT NAME

examples.en: My cousin refers to himself as a “sanitary engineer”—a euphemism for garbage collector.
examples.zh: 我表弟自称“卫生工程师”——这是垃圾收集员的委婉说法。

examples.en: The word “expired” is a euphemism for “died.”
examples.zh: “过期”一词是“去世”的委婉说法。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be euphemism.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了委婉语的含义。

examples.en: There are any number of people who try to find more respectable or glamorous titles for the mundane jobs they hold.
examples.zh: 有许多人试图为他们所从事的平凡工作寻找更体面或更有光鲜的头衔。

examples.en: The philosopher dealt with spiritual things, ignorning the mundane ones.
examples.zh: 哲学家处理的是精神层面，忽视了世俗的。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be mundane.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了平凡的含义。

examples.en: It may seem incongruous to call an undertaker a “condolence counselor,” or to refer to a taxi driver as a “transportation expediter,” but some prefer those titles.
examples.zh: 称殡葬师为“慰问顾问”或称出租车司机为“交通加班员”似乎有些不协调，但有些人更喜欢这些称呼。

examples.en: The play was so incongruous that it seemed to be the work of several authors.
examples.zh: 这部剧极其不协调，仿佛是多位作者的作品。

examples.en: 20 and 25 are incongruous with respect to 4.
examples.zh: 20和25相对于4是不协调的。

examples.en: It may seem incongruous to call an undertaker a “condolence counselor,” or to refer to a taxi driver as a “transportation expediter,” but some prefer those titles.
examples.zh: 称殡葬师为“慰问顾问”或称出租车司机为“交通加班员”似乎有些不协调，但有些人更喜欢这些称呼。

examples.en: When my neighbor’s dog was run over, we sent a condolence card.
examples.zh: 当我邻居的狗被撞时，我们寄了一张慰问卡。

examples.en: There was not much to do after the accident but offer what condolence I could.
examples.zh: 事故发生后没什么可做的，只能尽力表达慰问。

days.passage: My cousin refers to himself as a “sanitary engineer”—a euphemism for garbage collector. There are any number of people who try to find more respectable or glamorous titles for the mundane jobs they hold. It may seem incongruous to call an undertaker a “condolence counselor,” or to refer to a taxi driver as a “transportation expediter,” but some prefer those titles. As a matter of fact, our butcher has stipulated that from now on he wants to be known as a “meat coordinator.” He became irate* when I inadvertently* called him “Butch.”

exercises.text: We repudiated* the contract because it did not ________________ a cost of living bonus.
The word “expired” is a ________________ for “died.”
When my neighbor’s dog was run over, we sent a ________________ card.
The philosopher dealt with spiritual things, ignorning the ________________ ones.
The play was so ________________ that it seemed to be the work of several authors.

idiom.meaning: to come to no practical result (kindling smokes but it will not light a fire)
idiom.example: The mayor’s plans to get the gubernatorial nomination went up in smoke when he couldn’t end the costly strike.

days.passageZh: 我表弟自称“卫生工程师”——这是垃圾收集员的委婉说法。有许多人试图为他们所从事的平凡工作寻找更体面或更有光鲜的头衔。称殡葬师为“慰问顾问”或称出租车司机为“交通加班员”似乎有些不协调，但有些人更喜欢这些称呼。事实上，我们的屠夫已经明确表示，从现在起他要被称为“肉类协调员”。当我无意中*叫他“布奇”时，他变得非常生气。

reviewOptions.text: to have one’s hair stand up
saying something in a less direct way

days.story:
In the 1991 Persian Gulf War, where the United Nations forces, led by Americans, ousted the invading Iraqi army from Kuwait’s soil, the ______ of combat took place in short order after the Allies were able to ______ control of the skies from the ______ Saddam Hussein’s air force.

General H. Norman Schwarzkopf, the U.S. field commander, tended to ______ when asked by the media why he hadn’t pursued the enemy all the way to Baghdad, saying:

“It would have been foolhardy for us to try to occupy that capital city and pile up American casualties from sniper attacks by Iraq’s guerillas. That may be hard for you Monday morning quarterbacks to understand but I thoroughly agreed with the president who was convinced that such an action would have sent a bad message to the Arab world and would have splintered the Allied partnership.”

Schwarzkopf reiterated that it was his mission to hurl back the invaders with a minimum of bloodshed but not, he added in a ______ tone, “to splatter Saddam over the desert sands. That dictator’s days are numbered,” the general concluded, “but I expect his end is likely to come at the hands of his own people.”
days.storyZh:
1991年波斯湾战争中，联合国军队由美国人领导，驱逐入侵的伊拉克军队，战斗______迅速展开，盟军成功______萨达姆·侯赛因空军控制制______空权。

美国野战指挥官H·诺曼·施瓦茨科普夫将军在媒体问及为何没有一路追击敌人至巴格达时，倾向于倾向于______，他说：

“我们试图占领那座首都，并因伊拉克游击队的狙击手袭击而造成美军伤亡，那将是愚蠢的。这对你们这些周一早上的四分卫来说可能很难理解，但我完全同意总统的看法，他坚信这样的行动会向阿拉伯世界传递坏信号，并会分裂盟军的伙伴关系。”

施瓦茨科普重申，他的任务是以最小的流血方式击退入侵者，但他用______语气补充道，“不是要把萨达姆打得遍体鳞伤。那位独裁者的日子屈指可数，“将军总结道，”但我预计他的结局很可能是他自己的人民之手。”

days.title: MULLINS A K.O. VICTIM

examples.en: Mullins responded with alacrity.
examples.zh: 穆林斯迅速回应。

examples.en: The waiter moved with alacrity because he perceived* they were big tippers.
examples.zh: 服务员动作迅速，因为他觉得他们是大额小费的。

examples.en: We were impressed with the new maid because she cleaned the house with alacrity.
examples.zh: 我们对新女佣印象深刻，因为她打扫房子非常迅速。

examples.en: He sprang from his stool and charged across the ring, showing disdain for the champion’s strength.
examples.zh: 他从凳子上跳起，冲过擂台，对冠军的力量表现出轻蔑。

examples.en: When the curtain came down, the critic’s face registered the disdain she felt for the lackluster* play.
examples.zh: 当帷幕落下时，评论家的脸上浮现出她对这部平淡无奇*剧作的轻蔑。

examples.en: Treated with disdain by his stepfather, Artie grew closer to his natural father.
examples.zh: 继父对他不屑一顾，阿蒂与亲生父亲关系逐渐亲近。

words.definition: A feeling of contempt or scorn.

examples.en: Although this belligerent attitude impressed the referee, it failed to intimidate the champ.
examples.zh: 尽管这种咄咄逼人的态度给裁判留下了深刻印象，但并未让冠军感到畏惧。

examples.en: His belligerent manner caused him to lose one friend after another.
examples.zh: 他咄咄逼人的态度让他失去了一个又一个朋友。

examples.en: The normally belligerent police dog was unusually quiet this morning.
examples.zh: 这只平时好斗的警犬今早异常安静。

examples.en: Although this belligerent attitude impressed the referee, it failed to intimidate the champ.
examples.zh: 尽管这种咄咄逼人的态度给裁判留下了深刻印象，但并未让冠军感到畏惧。

examples.en: Y2K concerns of the problems with computers failed to intimidate our company.
examples.zh: 关于计算机问题的千禧年问题未能吓倒我们的公司。

examples.en: He's trying to intimidate you. If you ignore him, hopefully he'll stop.
examples.zh: 他是在试图恐吓你。如果你忽视他，希望他会停止。

examples.en: That intrepid* battler laid the hapless* Mullins low with an adroit* feint and an uppercut.
examples.zh: 这位勇敢的战士用巧妙的假动作和上勾拳击倒了倒霉的穆林斯。

examples.en: The Germans were duped* by the Allies’ feint toward the south, leaving the way open for the Normandy invasion.
examples.zh: 德国人被盟军向南的佯攻所欺骗，为诺曼底登陆打开了通路。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be feint.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是假动作。

days.passage: When the bell sounded, K.O. Mullins responded with alacrity. He sprang from his stool and charged across the ring, showing disdain for the champion’s strength. Although this belligerent attitude impressed the referee, it failed to intimidate the champ. That intrepid* battler laid the hapless* Mullins low with an adroit* feint and an uppercut.

exercises.text: Y2K concerns of the problems with computers failed to ________________ our company.
The Germans were duped* by the Allies’ ________________ toward the south, leaving the way open for the Normandy invasion.
The waiter moved with ________________ because he perceived* they were big tippers.
His ________________ manner caused him to lose one friend after another.
When the curtain came down, the critic’s face registered the ________________ she felt for the lackluster* play.

idiom.meaning: to challenge someone (when the gauntlet, or medieval glove, was thrown down, the challenged one was required to pick it up)
idiom.example: The principal of our rival school threw down the gauntlet, and we had no choice but to accept the challenge.

days.passageZh: 铃声响起时，K.O. Mullins迅速响应。他从凳子上跳起，冲过擂台，对冠军的力量表现出轻蔑。尽管这种咄咄逼人的态度给裁判留下了深刻印象，但并未让冠军感到畏惧。这位勇敢的战士用巧妙的假动作和上勾拳击倒了倒霉的穆林斯。

days.title: MULLINS THROWS DOWN THE GAUNTLET*

examples.en: Since everyone can outpunch my cousin, he cannot afford to be pugnacious .
examples.zh: 既然每个人都能打得比我表弟强，他就不能好斗。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be pugnacious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展现了好斗的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "pugnacious" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“好斗”这个词。

examples.en: He took a full-page newspaper advertisement to promulgate his challenge.
examples.zh: 他刊登了整版报纸广告来宣传他的挑战。

examples.en: We implored* the faculty advisor to promulgate the requirements for the presidency of the club.
examples.zh: 我们恳请*教师顾问公布俱乐部主席职位的要求。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be promulgate.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了被传播者的含义。

examples.en: When the champ’s manager saw the brash announcement, he accosted* Mullins, who was surrounded by a throng* of newsmen.
examples.zh: 当冠军的经理看到这突兀的宣布时，他拦住了*马林斯，后者被一群*新闻记者包围。

examples.en: My mother liked the salesman’s brash personality, but he irritated most people.
examples.zh: 我妈妈喜欢推销员那种直率的性格，但他让大多数人都很烦。

examples.en: a brash young businessman; a brash tabloid; a brash sense of humour
examples.zh: 一位鲁莽的年轻商人;一本张扬的小报;鲁莽的幽默感

days.passage: The pugnacious K.O. Mullins demanded a rematch. He took a full-page newspaper advertisement to promulgate his challenge. When the champ’s manager saw the brash announcement, he accosted* Mullins, who was surrounded by a throng* of newsmen. The manager openly scoffed at Mullins and belittled his fighting ability. Mullins then lost his temper and fearlessly punched the manager, knocking him off of his crutches.

exercises.text: We implored* the faculty advisor to ________________ the requirements for the presidency of the club.
My mother liked the salesman’s ________________ personality, but he irritated most people.
I don’t understand modern art, but I neither loathe* nor ________________ at it.
Since everyone can outpunch my cousin, he cannot afford to be ________________ .
Although Ralph can’t play, he doesn’t hesitate to ________________ the efforts of our football team.

idiom.meaning: drunk
idiom.example: Although the party had just begun, after his first drink he was feeling no pain.

days.passageZh: 好斗的K.O.穆林斯要求复赛。他刊登了整版报纸广告来宣传他的挑战。当冠军的经理看到这突兀的宣布时，他拦住了*马林斯，后者被一群*新闻记者包围。主教练公开嘲笑穆林斯，贬低他的战斗能力。随后穆林斯失控，毫无畏惧地挥拳击打了经理一拳，将他从拐杖上击落。

days.title: MULLINS FORCED TO EAT HUMBLE PIE*

examples.en: As tangible evidence of the attack, he pointed to a deep laceration over his eyebrow that had required ten stitches.
examples.zh: 作为攻击的具体证据，他指着眉毛上一道深深的割伤，缝了十针。

examples.en: Mr. Dixon belittled* our request for tangible proof of his loyalty.
examples.zh: 迪克森先生轻视了我们要求他忠诚的实质证明。

examples.en: After finding the tangible evidence in his drawer, Roger took it to the police.
examples.zh: 在抽屉里找到实物证据后，罗杰把它交给了警察。

examples.en: As tangible evidence of the attack, he pointed to a deep laceration over his eyebrow that had required ten stitches.
examples.zh: 作为攻击的具体证据，他指着眉毛上一道深深的割伤，缝了十针。

examples.en: The medic reached into his kit to find a bandage for the ugly laceration .
examples.zh: 医护人员从装备中找出伤口的绷带。

examples.en: The doctor sewed up the laceration in his arm.
examples.zh: 医生缝合了他手臂上的割伤。

examples.en: When the case was brought before the court, the judge castigated Mullins for the sordid incident.
examples.zh: 案件提交法院时，法官对穆林斯的肮脏事件进行了严厉批评。

examples.en: Stories of their sordid youth poured forth from the unhappy felons.*
examples.zh: 他们肮脏的童年故事从那些不幸的罪犯口中倾泻而出。*

examples.en: The newspaper specialized in printing the sordid details of crime in the city.
examples.zh: 该报专门报道城市犯罪的肮脏细节。

words.phonetic: ok´ tə jə nār´ i ən
words.definition: person in his or her eighties

examples.en: In addition to a costly financial settlement, Mullins was required to make a public apology to the octogenarian.
examples.zh: 除了昂贵的和解金外，穆林斯还被要求向这位八旬老人公开道歉。

examples.en: When the teenager announced her engagement to the octogenarian , the public suspected it to be a publicity stunt.
examples.zh: 当这位少女宣布与这位八十多岁的老人订婚时，公众怀疑这是一场炒作。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be octogenarian.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了成为八旬老人的含义。

days.passage: The irate* 80-year-old manager pressed charges against K.O. Mullins, suing him for assault. As tangible evidence of the attack, he pointed to a deep laceration over his eyebrow that had required ten stitches. When the case was brought before the court, the judge castigated Mullins for the sordid incident. In addition to a costly financial settlement, Mullins was required to make a public apology to the octogenarian.

Mr. Dixon belittled* our request for ________________ proof of his loyalty.
The kindly foreman was too reticent* to openly ________________ the clumsy new worker.
When the teenager announced her engagement to the ________________ , the public suspected it to be a publicity stunt.
Stories of their ________________ youth poured forth from the unhappy felons.*

idiom.example: Despite all the talk about democracy in my family, my father usually gives the rest of us Hobson’s choice.

days.passageZh: 这位愤怒的80岁经理对K.O. Mullins提起诉讼，指控他袭击。作为攻击的具体证据，他指着眉毛上一道深深的割伤，缝了十针。案件提交法院时，法官对穆林斯的肮脏事件进行了严厉批评。除了昂贵的和解金外，穆林斯还被要求向这位八旬老人公开道歉。

days.title: THE DECLINE OF MULLINS

examples.en: Mullins sought solace in whiskey.
examples.zh: 穆林斯在威士忌中寻求慰藉。

examples.en: In trying to offer solace to the deceased’s wife, the reporter inadvertently* made the situation worse.
examples.zh: 记者试图安慰死者的妻子，却无意中*使情况变得更糟。

examples.en: You cannot put a monetary value on emotional solace.
examples.zh: 你不能给情感上的慰藉赋予金钱价值。

examples.en: Once a highly respected aspirant for the lightweight crown, he now found himself associating with the dregs of Skid Row.* He would work himself into an alcoholic frenzy in which he would trumpet scurrilous attacks on the champ, the old manager, and the judge.
examples.zh: 他曾是备受尊敬的轻量级冠军争夺者，如今却与贫民区的渣滓们结为一群。* 他会陷入酗酒狂热，大声对冠军、老经理和裁判进行恶意攻击。

examples.en: The publisher scoffed* at the reports that he was an aspirant for the job of Secretary of State.
examples.zh: 出版人对他被认为竞选国务卿的报道嗤之以鼻*。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be aspirant.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是有志者。

examples.en: Once a highly respected aspirant for the lightweight crown, he now found himself associating with the dregs of Skid Row.* He would work himself into an alcoholic frenzy in which he would trumpet scurrilous attacks on the champ, the old manager, and the judge.
examples.zh: 他曾是备受尊敬的轻量级冠军争夺者，如今却与贫民区的渣滓们结为一群。* 他会陷入酗酒狂热，大声对冠军、老经理和裁判进行恶意攻击。

examples.en: At the bottom of the beautiful wine bottle, only the dregs remained.
examples.zh: 在那瓶美丽的酒瓶底部，只剩下残渣。

examples.en: Once he was a millionaire, but today he can be found among the dregs of society.
examples.zh: 他曾是百万富翁，但如今他已身处社会底层。

examples.en: Once a highly respected aspirant for the lightweight crown, he now found himself associating with the dregs of Skid Row.* He would work himself into an alcoholic frenzy in which he would trumpet scurrilous attacks on the champ, the old manager, and the judge.
examples.zh: 他曾是备受尊敬的轻量级冠军争夺者，如今却与贫民区的渣滓们结为一群。* 他会陷入酗酒狂热，大声对冠军、老经理和裁判进行恶意攻击。

examples.en: In a frenzy , the teenager overturned every drawer while searching for the car keys.
examples.zh: 少年慌乱中翻遍了所有抽屉，寻找车钥匙。

examples.en: She went into a cleaning frenzy to prepare for the unexpected guests.
examples.zh: 她开始疯狂地打扫，为突如其来的客人做准备。

examples.en: Once a highly respected aspirant for the lightweight crown, he now found himself associating with the dregs of Skid Row.* He would work himself into an alcoholic frenzy in which he would trumpet scurrilous attacks on the champ, the old manager, and the judge.
examples.zh: 他曾是备受尊敬的轻量级冠军争夺者，如今却与贫民区的渣滓们结为一群。* 他会陷入酗酒狂热，大声对冠军、老经理和裁判进行恶意攻击。

examples.en: Vigilant* censors protect the public from listening to scurrilous language on television.
examples.zh: 警觉的*审查员保护公众不听到电视上的恶毒言论。

examples.en: We have had our address used by scurrilous crooks in the past to gain assets by fraud.
examples.zh: 过去我们的地址曾被恶意骗子利用，通过欺诈获取资产。

days.passage: Mullins sought solace in whiskey. Once a highly respected aspirant for the lightweight crown, he now found himself associating with the dregs of Skid Row.* He would work himself into an alcoholic frenzy in which he would trumpet scurrilous attacks on the champ, the old manager, and the judge. One avid* fight fan attributed Mullins’ absence from the ring to sickness, saying that he was “recovering from a bad case of—SCOTCH.”

exercises.text: Vigilant* censors protect the public from listening to ________________ language on television.
The publisher scoffed* at the reports that he was an ________________ for the job of Secretary of State.
In a ________________ , the teenager overturned every drawer while searching for the car keys.
At the bottom of the beautiful wine bottle, only the ________________ remained.
In trying to offer ________________ to the deceased’s wife, the reporter inadvertently* made the situation worse.

idiom.meaning: to be in charge, to be master (a roost is a perch where domestic birds can sleep)
idiom.example: Although he is a lowly private in the army, at home he rules the roost.

days.passageZh: 穆林斯在威士忌中寻求慰藉。他曾是备受尊敬的轻量级冠军争夺者，如今却与贫民区的渣滓们结为一群。* 他会陷入酗酒狂热，大声对冠军、老经理和裁判进行恶意攻击。一位狂热的拳迷将穆林斯缺席拳台归因于疾病，称他“正在从严重的苏格兰病中恢复”。

days.story:
In recent years, we have seen the phenomenon of incumbent politicians retiring in record numbers. When interviewed, many of them admitted that they had lost their taste for the job because of the abuse to which an ______ for office is subjected.

“My last campaign was a ______ affair in which my opponents did everything to ______ my record and air ______ charges about my private life,” said one congressman. “I don’t have to stand still for such treatment,” he added, “which was terribly embarrassing to me and my entire family.”

Citizen groups, appalled by the candidates’ mudslinging, have sought to do something about the situation. Committees have been formed in a number of states to study ways to elevate the tone of the process, reduce the emotionalism, and eliminate the ______ of name calling that is generated as election day draws near.

“Unless we clean up this mess,” said the chairman of an Illinois caucus, “we will lose the best and the brightest from the political arena. After all, who but a masochist wants to be a punching bag, the subject of daily vilification in the media, and a target for every malcontent in town?”
days.storyZh:
近年来，我们看到现任政治家以创纪录数量退休的现象。在采访中，许多人承认由于竞选官员______遭受的虐待，他们对这份工作失去了兴趣。

一位国会议员说：“我上一次竞选是一场______事件，我的对手竭尽全力______我的记录，并对我的私生活提出______指控。”“我不必为这种待遇站着，”他补充道，“这对我和我全家来说都非常尴尬。”

公民团体对候选人的抹黑感到震惊，试图采取措施解决这一局势。多个州已成立委员会，研究如何提升选举过程的基调，减少情绪化，并消除随着选举日临近而产生的辱骂______。

伊利诺伊州一个党团主席说：“除非我们清理这场烂摊子，否则我们将失去政治领域中最优秀和最聪明的人。毕竟，除了受虐狂，谁愿意成为出气筒，成为媒体每日诋毁的对象，成为城中所有不满分子的目标呢？”

days.title: CHEATING

examples.en: During my first weeks at the new school I observed that cheating was rampant.
examples.zh: 在新学校的最初几周，我观察到作弊现象普遍存在。

examples.en: When the plague was rampant on the island, Dr. Arrowsmith’s wife died.
examples.zh: 当瘟疫在岛上肆虐时，阿罗史密斯医生的妻子去世了。

examples.en: The Vienna riding school displays splendid rampant movement.
examples.zh: 维也纳骑术学校展现了华丽且蓬勃的运动。

examples.en: I had always considered it rather inane to cheat on a test because of my code of ethics, and because so much was at stake.
examples.zh: 我一直觉得作弊很无聊，因为我的道德准则，而且关系重大。

examples.en: Orville Wright was criticized for his inane desire to fly.
examples.zh: 奥维尔·赖特因愚蠢的飞行欲望而受到批评。

examples.en: This supremely gifted kid told me that in the early elementary grades, the songs sung in music class were so inane that he wanted to skip grades already! Eventually he did, so better late than never.
examples.zh: 这个极具天赋的孩子告诉我，小学低年级时，音乐课唱的歌太无聊了，他甚至想跳级了！最终他真的做了，迟到总比不到好。

examples.en: I had always considered it rather inane to cheat on a test because of my code of ethics, and because so much was at stake.
examples.zh: 我一直觉得作弊很无聊，因为我的道德准则，而且关系重大。

examples.en: A special management committee was asked to investigate business ethics .
examples.zh: 特别管理委员会被要求调查商业伦理问题。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be ethics.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是伦理学者。

examples.en: Apparently the other students didn’t concur.
examples.zh: 显然其他学生并不认同。

examples.en: If I can get my parents to concur , I’ll join the Peace Corps.
examples.zh: 如果我能让父母同意，我会加入和平队。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be concur.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“同意”的含义。

examples.en: Far from being a clandestine activity, the cheating was open and obvious.
examples.zh: 这绝非秘密活动，而是公开且明显的。

examples.en: The spies thought their meeting was a clandestine one, but a throng* of F.B.I. agents gathered outside the building.
examples.zh: 间谍们以为他们的会面是秘密的，但大批联邦调查局特工聚集在大楼外。

examples.en: clandestine military operations
examples.zh: 秘密军事行动

days.passage: During my first weeks at the new school I observed that cheating was rampant. I had always considered it rather inane to cheat on a test because of my code of ethics, and because so much was at stake. Apparently the other students didn’t concur. In fact, even the presence of a proctor did not intimidate* them. Far from being a clandestine activity, the cheating was open and obvious.

exercises.text: When the plague was ________________ on the island, Dr. Arrowsmith’s wife died.
The spies thought their meeting was a ________________ one, but a throng* of F.B.I. agents gathered outside the building.
A special management committee was asked to investigate business ________________ .
Orville Wright was criticized for his ________________ desire to fly.
If I can get my parents to ________________ , I’ll join the Peace Corps.

idiom.meaning: the goods, tools, and other requisites of a profession
idiom.example: A quick wit and a warm smile were the salesman’s stock in trade.

days.passageZh: 在新学校的最初几周，我观察到作弊现象普遍存在。我一直觉得作弊很无聊，因为我的道德准则，而且关系重大。显然其他学生并不认同。事实上，即使监考员的存在也没有让他们感到害怕。这绝非秘密活动，而是公开且明显的。

days.title: CRACKING DOWN

examples.en: Dorsey, our new principal, determined to do something about the flagrant cheating at our high school.
examples.zh: 我们的新校长多尔西决心对我们高中公然作弊采取行动。

examples.en: Parking in front of a hydrant is a flagrant violation of the city’s law.
examples.zh: 在消防栓前停车是公然违反市政府法律。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be flagrant.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是公然的。

examples.en: He issued bulletins and began to admonish those teachers who did not proctor alertly.
examples.zh: 他发布公告，开始训诫那些不及时监考的教师。

examples.en: I suspect that my father will admonish me for coming home late.
examples.zh: 我怀疑我父亲会因为我回家晚而责备我。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be admonish.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被训诫的含义。

examples.en: Under duress, the faculty reported the names of the culprits.
examples.zh: 在压力下，教职员工报告了肇事者的姓名。

examples.en: The confession was signed under duress , the attorney claimed.
examples.zh: 律师声称，供词是在胁迫下签署的。

examples.en: Someone was duressing her.
examples.zh: 有人在逼迫她。

examples.en: Dorsey’s inexorable campaign against the wrong-doers seemed to be paying off.
examples.zh: 多尔西对不法分子的坚定打击似乎开始见效。

examples.en: Television sleuths are inexorable in their pursuit of lawbreakers.
examples.zh: 电视侦探们在追捕违法者时势不可挡。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be inexorable.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了不可阻挡的含义。

days.passage: Mr. Dorsey, our new principal, determined to do something about the flagrant cheating at our high school. He issued bulletins and began to admonish those teachers who did not proctor alertly. Under duress, the faculty reported the names of the culprits. Several crib sheets were turned in as tangible* evidence of the cheating. Mr. Dorsey’s inexorable campaign against the wrong-doers seemed to be paying off.

The confession was signed under ________________ , the attorney claimed.
I suspect that my father will ________________ me for coming home late.
Parking in front of a hydrant is a ________________ violation of the city’s law.

idiom.meaning: to take the conceit out of a braggart (ship’s colors used to be raised or lowered by pegs— the higher the colors, the greater the honor)
idiom.example: The alumni thought they had a great basketball team, but our varsity took them down a peg.

days.passageZh: 我们的新校长多尔西先生决心对我们高中公然作弊采取行动。他发布公告，开始训诫那些不及时监考的教师。在压力下，教职员工报告了肇事者的姓名。几张婴儿床单被提交，作为作弊的实物证据。多尔西先生对不法分子的坚定打击似乎开始见效。

days.title: STAR PLAYER IS CAUGHT

examples.en: The cheating scandal came to a head when Art Krause, our football captain, made the egregious mistake of getting caught cheating on a midterm exam.
examples.zh: 作弊丑闻在我们的足球队长阿特·克劳斯犯下严重错误——期中考试作弊被抓到时达到了高潮。

examples.en: The bank teller’s egregious error was difficult to correct.
examples.zh: 银行柜员的严重错误难以纠正。

examples.en: The student has made egregious errors on the examination.
examples.zh: 该学生在考试中犯了严重错误。

examples.en: If Art were suspended for his part in that sordid* affair, our chances for winning the city championship would go up in smoke.* The distraught coach asked the principal to overlook Art’s duplicity, but Mr.
examples.zh: 如果阿特因为那件卑劣*事件被停学，我们赢得市冠军的机会将化为乌有。* 这位心烦意乱的教练请求校长忽略阿特的欺骗，但先生

examples.en: The soldiers were distraught to learn that their furloughs had been canceled.
examples.zh: 士兵们得知休假被取消后非常沮丧。

examples.en: His distraught widow cried for days, feeling very alone.
examples.zh: 他悲痛的遗孀哭了好几天，感到非常孤独。

examples.en: If Art were suspended for his part in that sordid* affair, our chances for winning the city championship would go up in smoke.* The distraught coach asked the principal to overlook Art’s duplicity, but Mr.
examples.zh: 如果阿特因为那件卑劣*事件被停学，我们赢得市冠军的机会将化为乌有。* 这位心烦意乱的教练请求校长忽略阿特的欺骗，但先生

examples.en: duplicity is the stock in trade of all adroit* counterspies.
examples.zh: 欺骗是所有灵巧*反间谍的共同技能。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be duplicity.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了欺骗的含义。

examples.en: Dorsey replied in an acrimonious fashion that the players had been given “a plethora”* of athletic instruction but a paucity of moral guidance.”
examples.zh: 多尔西以尖锐的语气回应说，球员们接受了“大量”的体育训练*，但道德指导却极为匮乏。”

examples.en: We tried to ignore her acrimonious comments, but that took considerable restraint.
examples.zh: 我们试图忽视她尖刻的言论，但这需要相当大的克制。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be acrimonious.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“敌意”的含义。

examples.en: Dorsey replied in an acrimonious fashion that the players had been given “a plethora”* of athletic instruction but a paucity of moral guidance.”
examples.zh: 多尔西以尖锐的语气回应说，球员们接受了“大量”的体育训练*，但道德指导却极为匮乏。”

examples.en: Although it was a creative writing class, the teacher complained about the paucity of talent there.
examples.zh: 虽然这是一门创意写作课，但老师抱怨那里的人才稀少。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be paucity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了贫穷的含义。

days.passage: The cheating scandal came to a head when Art Krause, our football captain, made the egregious mistake of getting caught cheating on a midterm exam. If Art were suspended for his part in that sordid* affair, our chances for winning the city championship would go up in smoke.* The distraught coach asked the principal to overlook Art’s duplicity, but Mr. Dorsey replied in an acrimonious fashion that the players had been given “a plethora”* of athletic instruction but a paucity of moral guidance.”

exercises.text: The bank teller’s ________________ error was difficult to correct.
We tried to ignore her ________________ comments, but that took considerable restraint.
________________ is the stock in trade of all adroit* counterspies.
Although it was a creative writing class, the teacher complained about the ________________ of talent there.

idiom.meaning: to evade responsibility (the “buck” may have been a piece of buckshot passed from one poker player to another to keep track of whose turn it was to deal)
idiom.example: He always gives me a straight answer and never tries to pass the buck.

days.passageZh: 作弊丑闻在我们的足球队长阿特·克劳斯犯下严重错误——期中考试作弊被抓到时达到了高潮。如果阿特因那件肮脏的事件被停学，我们赢得市冠军的机会将化为乌有。* 沮丧的教练请求校长宽容阿特的两面派，但多尔西先生尖锐地回应说，球员们接受了“大量”体育训练*，但道德指导却极为匮乏。”

days.title: OUR PYRRHIC VICTORY*

examples.en: Dorsey summoned a representative group of teachers and student leaders to his office in order to elicit their reactions to the suspension of the football captain.
examples.zh: 多尔西召集了一组教师和学生领袖到他的办公室，以征求他们对橄榄球队长被停职的反应。

examples.en: It isn’t easy to elicit answers from a sleepy class on Monday morning.
examples.zh: 周一早晨，从一班昏昏欲睡的班级中获得答案并不容易。

examples.en: Did you elicit a response?
examples.zh: 你有得到回应吗？

examples.en: He told them that cheating was a pernicious disease that could not be tolerated at our school.
examples.zh: 他告诉他们，作弊是一种恶性疾病，在我们学校是不能容忍的。

examples.en: The dictator’s pernicious rules failed to intimidate* the leaders of the underground.
examples.zh: 独裁者的恶毒规则未能威慑*地下组织的领导者。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be pernicious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是有害的。

words.definition: to put up with, to bear
to make a deduction, to infer

examples.en: He loathed* having to discipline Art Krause so severely, but unless strict measures were taken, the student body would construe the incident as an open invitation to cheat with impunity.
examples.zh: 他讨厌*必须如此严厉地惩罚阿特·克劳斯，但除非采取严格措施，否则学生们会把这件事解读为公开的作弊自由。

examples.en: She hoped that we would not construe her decision to run for office as a thirst for power.
examples.zh: 她希望我们不会将她竞选公职的决定解读为对权力的渴望。

examples.en: The world must construe according to its wits; this court must construe according to the law.
examples.zh: 世界必须根据自己的智慧去理解;本法院必须依法解释。

examples.en: He loathed* having to discipline Art Krause so severely, but unless strict measures were taken, the student body would construe the incident as an open invitation to cheat with impunity.
examples.zh: 他讨厌*必须如此严厉地惩罚阿特·克劳斯，但除非采取严格措施，否则学生们会把这件事解读为公开的作弊自由。

examples.en: The border guards allowed the doctor to cross the frontier with impunity .
examples.zh: 边防人员允许这位医生逍遥法外越境。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be impunity.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是有罪不罚。

days.passage: Mr. Dorsey summoned a representative group of teachers and student leaders to his office in order to elicit their reactions to the suspension of the football captain. He told them that cheating was a pernicious disease that could not be tolerated at our school. He loathed* having to discipline Art Krause so severely, but unless strict measures were taken, the student body would construe the incident as an open invitation to cheat with impunity. “We may lose a football game,” the principal said, “but we can salvage our self-respect.”

exercises.text: The border guards allowed the doctor to cross the frontier with ________________ .
It isn’t easy to ________________ answers from a sleepy class on Monday morning.
Dentists appreciate patients who can ________________ pain.
She hoped that we would not ________________ her decision to run for office as a thirst for power.
The dictator’s ________________ rules failed to intimidate* the leaders of the underground.

options.text: to make a deduction, to infer
to put up with, to bear
to make a deduction, to infer
to put up with, to bear
to make a deduction, to infer
to put up with, to bear

exercises.correctDefinition: to put up with, to bear

options.text: to make a deduction, to infer
to put up with, to bear

exercises.correctDefinition: to make a deduction, to infer

options.text: to make a deduction, to infer
to put up with, to bear

idiom.meaning: to make a big fuss over someone (the lions at the Tower of London were considered its main attraction)
idiom.example: When the famous poet Dylan Thomas visited the United States, he was lionized wherever he lectured.

days.passageZh: 多尔西先生召集了一组教师和学生领袖到他的办公室，以征求他们对橄榄球队长被停职的反应。他告诉他们，作弊是一种恶性疾病，在我们学校是不能容忍的。他讨厌*必须如此严厉地惩罚阿特·克劳斯，但除非采取严格措施，否则学生们会把这件事解读为公开的作弊自由。校长说：“我们可能会输掉一场橄榄球比赛，但我们可以挽回自尊。”

reviewOptions.text: cannot be moved by persuasion, inflexible
to permit, to put up with

days.story:
Throughout literature we find recurring tales of forthright people who are outspoken in condemning illegal practices only to be brought low themselves when they, or members of their families, commit such acts. Since literature reflects life, we can expect to find similar instances in which a person’s ______ are compromised, and he falls prey to the ______ evil that he had publicly denounced.

Take the story of Barry Vernon (not his real name), an aggressive Ohio district attorney. Vernon could be counted upon to make ______ remarks about anyone who was driving while intoxicated. On numerous speaking engagements, he railed against drunkenness and swore that any such ______ who was found behind the wheel of a car would be prosecuted to the full extent of the law.

As fate would have it, Vernon’s own son smashed into several cars, injuring four people seriously, and then failed a sobriety test.

Following that ______ violation of the law, Vernon resigned from office, saying that as a private citizen he would continue his crusade against those who drive under the influence of alcohol. Meanwhile, he wished to spend more time with his son to try to understand the young man’s behavior.
days.storyZh:
在文学中，我们反复看到一些直言不讳、公开谴责非法行为的人，却在自己或其家族成员实施此类行为时被打击。既然文学反映了生活，我们也可以期待看到类似的例子，一个人的______受损，最终沦为他公开谴责的______恶的牺牲品。

以巴里·弗农（化名）为例，他是俄亥俄州一名激进的地方检察官。弗农对任何酒驾的人都会说______话。在多次演讲中，他抨击酗酒行为，并誓言任何被发现驾驶汽车的酒后______将依法严惩。

命运弄人，弗农的儿子撞上了几辆车，造成四人重伤，随后酒精测试未通过。

在那次______违法后，弗农辞去了公职，表示作为一名普通公民，他将继续打击酒驾者的斗争。与此同时，他希望能多花时间陪伴儿子，试图理解年轻人的行为。

days.title: THE NEWSPAPER UMBRELLA

examples.en: Our neighbor is an affluent inventor whose latest brainstorm, a feasible umbrella substitute, has been featured in many magazines.
examples.zh: 我们的邻居是一位富有的发明家，他最新的头脑风暴——一种可行的雨伞替代品——已被许多杂志报道。

examples.en: Under duress* from the tax officer, the beggar admitted that he was truly affluent .
examples.zh: 在税务官员的胁迫下，乞丐承认自己确实富有。

examples.en: They were affluent, but aspired to true wealth.
examples.zh: 他们富裕，但渴望真正的财富。

examples.en: Our neighbor is an affluent inventor whose latest brainstorm, a feasible umbrella substitute, has been featured in many magazines.
examples.zh: 我们的邻居是一位富有的发明家，他最新的头脑风暴——一种可行的雨伞替代品——已被许多杂志报道。

examples.en: It’s feasible to build an electric auto, but wouldn’t you need a terribly long extension cord?
examples.zh: 电动自动车是可行的，但你不是需要非常长的延长线吗？

examples.en: His plan to rid Trafalgar Square of pigeons by bringing in peregrine falcons to eat them was dismissed as not feasible.
examples.zh: 他计划通过引入游隼来捕食特拉法加广场的鸽子，但被认为不可行而被否决。

examples.en: As simply as the eye can discern, it is a hard plastic strip, about the size of a ruler, which fits comfortably into a woman’s handbag or a man’s suit jacket.
examples.zh: 肉眼一看，它是一条硬塑料条，大小约如尺子，舒适地放入女性手提包或男性西装夹克中。

examples.en: When we could discern the city lights, we knew we were safe at last.
examples.zh: 当我们能辨认出城市灯光时，我们知道终于安全了。

examples.en: He was too young to discern right from wrong.
examples.zh: 他太年轻，无法分辨对错。

examples.en: Now, equipped with a rigid head covering he can sally forth to face the elements.
examples.zh: 现在，他戴上了坚硬的头罩，可以勇敢地迎接恶劣的挑战。

examples.en: Some prisoners planned a disturbance while others would sally toward the gate.
examples.zh: 有些囚犯策划骚乱，另一些则向城门冲去。

examples.en: Flocks of these birds stir up flying insects, which can then be picked off in quick sallies.
examples.zh: 成群的这些鸟会搅动飞虫，然后可以快速猎杀它们。

examples.en: To the consternation of the umbrella manufacturers, it has been enjoying a brisk sale, especially among commuters.
examples.zh: 令雨伞制造商感到不安的是，这款伞最近销售非常火热，尤其是在通勤者中。

examples.en: To the consternation of the sergeant, there was a paucity* of volunteers for the dangerous mission.
examples.zh: 令中士沮丧的是，执行这项危险任务的志愿者数量极少。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be consternation.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“困惑”的含义。

days.passage: Our neighbor is an affluent inventor whose latest brainstorm, a feasible umbrella substitute, has been featured in many magazines. As simply as the eye can discern, it is a hard plastic strip, about the size of a ruler, which fits comfortably into a woman’s handbag or a man’s suit jacket. If a person is caught in a sudden rainstorm, he swings the plastic open in the shape of a cross. Attached to each arm is a clip-like device. Next, he takes the newspaper he is carrying and slides it under each of the four clips. Now, equipped with a rigid head covering he can sally forth to face the elements. To the consternation of the umbrella manufacturers, it has been enjoying a brisk sale, especially among commuters. If it continues to do well, it could have a pernicious* effect upon the umbrella industry.

exercises.text: Some prisoners planned a disturbance while others would ________________ toward the gate.
Under duress* from the tax officer, the beggar admitted that he was truly ________________ .
To the ________________ of the sergeant, there was a paucity* of volunteers for the dangerous mission.
It’s ________________ to build an electric auto, but wouldn’t you need a terribly long extension cord?
When we could ________________ the city lights, we knew we were safe at last.

idiom.meaning: a skeptic, one who is not easily convinced
idiom.example: You might swallow his promises, but I’m from Missouri.

days.passageZh: 我们的邻居是一位富有的发明家，他最新的头脑风暴——一种可行的雨伞替代品——已被许多杂志报道。肉眼一看，它是一条硬塑料条，大小约如尺子，舒适地放入女性手提包或男性西装夹克中。如果有人遭遇突如其来的暴雨，他会把塑料袋甩开成十字形。每只手臂上都装有一个夹子状装置。接着，他拿起手中的报纸，滑到四个剪辑夹下面。现在，他戴上了坚硬的头罩，可以勇敢地迎接恶劣的挑战。令雨伞制造商感到不安的是，这款伞最近销售非常火热，尤其是在通勤者中。如果它继续表现良好，可能会对伞行业产生有害*影响。

days.title: PATENT PENDING

examples.en: My buddy Verne, a precocious automotive wizard, and I were inspired to do some inventing on our own.
examples.zh: 我的朋友凡尔纳，一位早熟的汽车天才，和我受到启发，决定自己去发明一些东西。

examples.en: The children in Shakespeare’s plays are so precocious that they all sound like grandparents.
examples.zh: 莎士比亚戏剧中的孩子们都早熟到听起来像祖父母。

examples.en: The precocious plant was already blooming flowers by day 4.
examples.zh: 这株早熟的植物在第四天就已经开花了。

examples.en: After a perfunctory glance at our plans, to our chagrin Ed snorted that our idea was inane,* but we decided that he was just jealous of our brilliance.
examples.zh: 在敷衍地看了一眼我们的计划后，令我们懊恼的是，埃德嗤之以鼻，说我们的主意很无聊*，但我们决定他只是嫉妒我们的聪明才智。

examples.en: Edith gave only perfunctory attention to the new millennium, skipping our New Year’s Eve party.
examples.zh: 伊迪丝对新千年只敷衍地关注，跳过了我们的新年前夜派对。

examples.en: He did a perfunctory job cleaning his dad's car, finishing quickly but leaving a few spots still dirty.
examples.zh: 他敷衍地清理了爸爸的车，很快完成，但留下了几处脏污。

examples.en: After a perfunctory glance at our plans, to our chagrin Ed snorted that our idea was inane,* but we decided that he was just jealous of our brilliance.
examples.zh: 在敷衍地看了一眼我们的计划后，令我们懊恼的是，埃德嗤之以鼻，说我们的主意很无聊*，但我们决定他只是嫉妒我们的聪明才智。

examples.en: To the chagrin of many taxpayers, some citizens seem to cheat the government with impunity.*
examples.zh: 令许多纳税人懊恼的是，一些公民似乎可以肆无忌惮地欺骗政府。*

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be chagrin.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了懊恼的含义。

examples.en: Tomorrow we are going to start on a computer that will enable us to measure the intelligence of perverse automobile dealers who like to deride the efforts of junior geniuses.
examples.zh: 明天我们将开始使用一台计算机，它能让我们测量那些喜欢嘲笑小天才努力的怪异汽车经销商的智商。

examples.en: When I correct my kid brother’s math errors, he is perverse enough to insist that he is right.
examples.zh: 当我纠正我弟弟的数学错误时，他反常到坚持自己是对的。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be perverse.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了的含义。

examples.en: Tomorrow we are going to start on a computer that will enable us to measure the intelligence of perverse automobile dealers who like to deride the efforts of junior geniuses.
examples.zh: 明天我们将开始使用一台计算机，它能让我们测量那些喜欢嘲笑小天才努力的怪异汽车经销商的智商。

examples.en: The Wright brothers didn’t become distraught* when a skeptic would deride their work.
examples.zh: 当怀疑论者嘲笑他们的作品时，莱特兄弟并没有因此感到沮丧。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be deride.
examples.zh: 这个语境清楚地展示了“嘲讽”的含义。

days.passage: My buddy Verne, a precocious automotive wizard, and I were inspired to do some inventing on our own. We thought it might be feasible* to park a car parallel to a space on the street. Then, by pressing a button, we could raise the four tires off the ground slightly, while dropping two special wheels perpendicular to the curb. It would then be child’s play to roll into the narrowest of parking spaces. We took the idea to Ed Greene who runs the Ford agency in order to elicit* his reaction. After a perfunctory glance at our plans, to our chagrin Ed snorted that our idea was inane,* but we decided that he was just jealous of our brilliance. Tomorrow we are going to start on a computer that will enable us to measure the intelligence of perverse automobile dealers who like to deride the efforts of junior geniuses.

exercises.text: The children in Shakespeare’s plays are so ________________ that they all sound like grandparents.
Edith gave only ________________ attention to the new millennium, skipping our New Year’s Eve party.
The Wright brothers didn’t become distraught* when a skeptic would ________________ their work.
When I correct my kid brother’s math errors, he is ________________ enough to insist that he is right.
To the ________________ of many taxpayers, some citizens seem to cheat the government with impunity.*

idiom.meaning: day of happiness, time for rejoicing (holidays are red-letter days on our calendars)
idiom.example: My red-letter day came when I was chosen as senior class president.

days.passageZh: 我的朋友凡尔纳，一位早熟的汽车天才，和我受到启发，决定自己去发明一些东西。我们觉得可以*把车停在与街道上一个停车位平行的位置。然后，按下按钮，我们可以稍微抬起四个轮胎，同时垂直于路缘放置两个特殊轮子。然后，开进最狭窄的停车位就像小菜鸟一样。我们把这个想法带给了负责福特代理的埃德·格林，以激发他的反应。在敷衍地看了一眼我们的计划后，令我们懊恼的是，埃德嗤之以鼻，说我们的主意很无聊*，但我们决定他只是嫉妒我们的聪明才智。明天我们将开始使用一台计算机，它能让我们测量那些喜欢嘲笑小天才努力的怪异汽车经销商的智商。

days.title: HOLD THAT NOBEL PRIZE!

examples.en: I hate to disparage such a laudable achievement, but to me it seems like a most objectionable discovery.
examples.zh: 我不想贬低这样值得称赞的成就，但在我看来，这似乎是一个极其令人反感的发现。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be disparage.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了贬低的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "disparage" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“贬低”一词。

examples.en: I hate to disparage such a laudable achievement, but to me it seems like a most objectionable discovery.
examples.zh: 我不想贬低这样值得称赞的成就，但在我看来，这似乎是一个极其令人反感的发现。

examples.en: The paradox* is that Javert’s inexorable* pursuit of Jean Valjean was both laudable and despicable.
examples.zh: 矛盾在于，贾维尔对冉阿让的不可阻挡*追求既值得称赞，也令人厌恶。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be laudable.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了值得称赞的含义。

examples.en: Rather than a scientific triumph, I’d be inclined to label it as an egregious* blunder, a scientific disaster, a laboratory fiasco.
examples.zh: 与其说是科学上的胜利，我更倾向于称之为一个严重的*错误、科学灾难、实验室灾难。

examples.en: In an acrimonious* letter, her father described the project as a complete fiasco .
examples.zh: 在一封激烈的信中，她的父亲形容这个项目是彻底的失败。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be fiasco.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是灾难。

examples.en: To aid in digestion, you must masticate each piece of meat one dozen times.
examples.zh: 为了帮助消化，你必须咀嚼每块肉十几次。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be masticate.
examples.zh: 这个语境清楚地展示了咀嚼的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "masticate" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“咀嚼”这个词。

examples.en: I’m afraid that this is one pill I’ll have to eschew from chewing.
examples.zh: 我担心这药丸我得避免嚼。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be eschew.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了被排斥的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "eschew" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“回避”一词。

days.passage: Speaking of inventions and discoveries, I just learned that an eminent* scientist in Ohio has developed a pill that contains all the nutritive value of three complete meals. In addition to providing us with the vitamins and minerals we need daily, this pill also gives a feeling of fullness. According to its sponsors, the pill will nourish and satisfy. I hate to disparage such a laudable achievement, but to me it seems like a most objectionable discovery. Rather than a scientific triumph, I’d be inclined to label it as an egregious* blunder, a scientific disaster, a laboratory fiasco. Is there anyone in his right mind who thinks that a pill can replace the pleasures of devouring hot corn bread, masticating on a thick steak, biting into crisp french fries, or attacking a chocolate sundae? I’m afraid that this is one pill I’ll have to eschew from chewing.

exercises.text: The paradox* is that Javert’s inexorable* pursuit of Jean Valjean was both ________________ and despicable.
The affluent* storeowner ________________ the efforts of his small competitor, saying that he could always tolerate* that kind of rivalry.
To aid in digestion, you must ________________ each piece of meat one dozen times.
In an acrimonious* letter, her father described the project as a complete ________________ .
Once he sought the limelight, but now he ________________ all interviews.

idiom.meaning: to let well enough alone, to avoid stirring up old hostilities
idiom.example: The lawyer wanted to open up the old case, but his partner advised him to let sleeping dogs lie.

days.passageZh: 说到发明和发现，我刚刚得知俄亥俄州一位杰出科学家开发出一种药丸，营养价值相当于三餐完整餐。除了每天提供所需的维生素和矿物质外，这颗药丸还能带来饱腹感。据其赞助商介绍，这种避孕药将滋养并带来满足感。我不想贬低这样值得称赞的成就，但在我看来，这似乎是一个极其令人反感的发现。与其说是科学上的胜利，我更倾向于称之为一个严重的*错误、科学灾难、实验室灾难。有谁会理智地认为，一颗药丸能取代吞噬热玉米面包、咀嚼厚牛排、咬下一口脆薯条或吃巧克力圣代的乐趣吗？我担心这药丸我得避免嚼。

days.title: PERFECT PRODUCTS

examples.en: I guess we’ll never be able to quell those persistent rumors about the invention of auto tires that will never wear out, stockings that cannot tear, and pens that won’t run dry.
examples.zh: 我想我们永远无法平息那些关于汽车轮胎永不磨损、长袜不破、笔永不干涸的那些持续传闻。

examples.en: In order to quell the riot, the police sallied* forth with tear gas.
examples.zh: 为了平息骚乱，警方挥舞催泪瓦斯。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be quell.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被平静的含义。

examples.en: A voluble economist informed me that such products will never be marketed.
examples.zh: 一位多言的经济学家告诉我，这类产品永远不会被推向市场。

examples.en: I could not doze in the chair because of the voluble barber.
examples.zh: 因为那个话多的理发师，我根本无法在椅子上打盹。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be voluble.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“多话”的含义。

words.definition: A person in whom one can confide or share one's secrets: a friend.

examples.en: No,” my confidant whispered, “it’s part of their scheme of planned obsolescence to sell you merchandise with a limited life span in order to keep you coming back for more.” I am dubious about the existence of those perfect products, but then I’m from Missouri.*
examples.zh: 不，“我的知己低声说，”这是他们计划性报废计划的一部分，卖给你寿命有限的商品，让你不断回来。”我对那些完美产品的存在持怀疑态度，但我毕竟来自密苏里州。*

examples.en: The obsolescence built into many products could be regarded as a flagrant* insult toward the duped* consumer.
examples.zh: 许多产品内置的过时性可以被视为对被欺骗消费者的公然侮辱。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be obsolescence.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是过时。

examples.en: No,” my confidant whispered, “it’s part of their scheme of planned obsolescence to sell you merchandise with a limited life span in order to keep you coming back for more.” I am dubious about the existence of those perfect products, but then I’m from Missouri.*
examples.zh: 不，“我的知己低声说，”这是他们计划性报废计划的一部分，卖给你寿命有限的商品，让你不断回来。”我对那些完美产品的存在持怀疑态度，但我毕竟来自密苏里州。*

examples.en: When the duplicity* was revealed, the jury became dubious about Ed’s innocence.
examples.zh: 当两面手法被揭露后，陪审团开始怀疑埃德的清白。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be dubious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是可疑的。

days.passage: I guess we’ll never be able to quell those persistent rumors about the invention of auto tires that will never wear out, stockings that cannot tear, and pens that won’t run dry. A voluble economist informed me that such products will never be marketed. “Can you imagine,” he asked, “a manufacturer cutting his own throat? Why would he sell you an item that you will never have to replace? No,” my confidant whispered, “it’s part of their scheme of planned obsolescence to sell you merchandise with a limited life span in order to keep you coming back for more.” I am dubious about the existence of those perfect products, but then I’m from Missouri.*

reviewOptions.text: person you tell your secrets to

days.story:
It was a quiet morning at Harry S Truman High School. “Too quiet,” Principal Edna Suarez remarked to her secretary. “It’s just when things are this serene that I start to get an uneasy feeling.”

Mrs. Suarez’s sensitivity to life among 3,000 teenagers quickly proved to be accurate. The first evidence of trouble came with a phone call from the teacher in charge of the cafeteria who needed help to ______ a disturbance. When Mrs. Suarez arrived on the scene, much to her ______ , students were pounding on their tables, throwing food on the lunchroom floor, and making a complete ______ of school regulations. It took the principal only a moment to ______ who the two ringleaders were and to summon them to her office.

Vincent, 16, and Elena, 15, admitted to having stirred up the protest. They gave as their reasons the poor quality of food served and the dirty environment. “It’s like a pigsty down there,” Elena declared, “and the food is fit only for animals!”

What they had done, Mrs. Suarez told them, was inexcusable, and she ticked off a list of reasons that made their conduct dangerous and subject to school discipline. “What you were trying to do,” Mrs. Suarez explained, “might be considered ______ by some but you could have come to me, alone or with a committee, to register your complaints. I would have investigated and, if there was merit to your charges, would have taken the necessary action. Now I’ll have to ask you to bring your parents to see me on Monday and to stay home until then.”

Vincent and Elena seemed to be chastened by Mrs. Suarez’s lecture. However, on leaving her office, Elena told an assistant principal that in a similar incident on a television show she learned that direct, dramatic action usually gets quicker results than lengthy debate. He advised her to bring that question up in her social studies class when she returned from suspension.
days.storyZh:
哈里·S·杜鲁门高中是一个安静的早晨。“太安静了，”校长埃德娜·苏亚雷斯对秘书说道。“只是当一切如此宁静时，我会开始感到不安。”

苏亚雷斯夫人对3000名青少年中生活的敏感度很快被证明是准确的。第一个麻烦的迹象来自食堂负责老师的电话，她需要帮忙______骚乱。苏亚雷斯夫人到场时，令她非常______惊讶的是，学生们正在敲打桌子，把食物扔到餐厅地板上，完全______学校规定。校长只花了片刻就______出了两个主谋是谁，并召他们到办公室。

16岁的文森特和15岁的埃琳娜承认是他们煽动了抗议。他们以食物质量差和环境肮脏为理由。“下面像猪圈，”埃琳娜宣称，“食物只有动物才吃！”

苏亚雷斯夫人告诉他们，他们的行为不可原谅，她列举了一系列使他们行为危险且可能受到学校纪律处分的理由。“你试图做的事情，”苏亚雷斯夫人解释道，“有些人可能觉得______，但你完全可以单独或带着委员会来找我，登记你的投诉。我会调查，如果你的指控有根据，我会采取必要的行动。现在我得请你周一带父母来看我，然后在那之前待在家里。”

文森特和埃琳娜似乎被苏亚雷斯夫人的训诫吓到了。然而，离开办公室时，埃琳娜告诉一位助理校长，在类似的电视节目中，她了解到直接、戏剧性的行动通常比长时间辩论更快见效。他建议她停学回来后在社会学课上提出这个问题。

days.title: MUCH ADO ABOUT A HAIRCUT

days.passage: Perhaps you read about our school in the newspapers? We were one of the first to have a showdown on the topic of long hair for boys. Two honor students, Ron Harris and Len Chester, were sent to the principal by their French teacher, an implacable foe of nonconformists, who went into a paroxysm of anger when she spied the boys in the hall. At first it seemed like a simple case. The school would reprimand* the boys for their reprehensible appearance and order them to cut their hair or be suspended. But the boys’ parents decided that the school had overstepped its jurisdiction; they took their case to the newspapers. What had started as a local skirmish now began to take on the appearance of a full-scale war.

days.passageZh: 也许你在报纸上读过我们学校的消息？我们是最早就男孩长发问题展开对决的学校之一。两名优等生罗恩·哈里斯和伦·切斯特被他们的法语老师派去找校长，老师是非国教徒的坚定敌人，当她看到男孩们在走廊时，勃然大怒。起初看似简单的案件。学校会因为男孩们的丑陋外貌而训斥他们，并命令他们剪头发或被停学。但男孩的父母认为学校越权了;他们把自己的案件带到了报纸上。原本只是局部冲突的局面，如今逐渐演变成全面战争的局面。

days.title: THE TEMPEST SPILLS OUT OF THE TEAPOT

examples.en: “These are not delinquents or hoods,” one reporter wrote, “but clean-cut American boys who are being harassed by a monolithic school system.” A caustic* editorial referred to the school’s decision as arbitrary and inane.* A false story even circulated about the boys being rock-’n-roll performers whose indigent families needed their salaries.
examples.zh: “这些不是问题少年或恶棍，”一位记者写道，“而是被单一学校系统骚扰的干净利落的美国男孩。”一篇尖刻的社论称学校的决定武断且荒谬。甚至流传了一则虚假报道，称这些男孩是摇滚乐表演者，贫困家庭需要他们的工资。

days.passage: Once the newspapers got the story, the case of the longhairs became a cause célèbre.* Ron and Len were interviewed, seen on TV, and regarded by their fellow students as heroes. “These are not delinquents or hoods,” one reporter wrote, “but clean-cut American boys who are being harassed by a monolithic school system.” A caustic* editorial referred to the school’s decision as arbitrary and inane.* A false story even circulated about the boys being rock-’n-roll performers whose indigent families needed their salaries. Finally, the Civil Liberties Union jumped into the fray with a court order stipulating* that the principal be required to show cause why the boys should not be allowed to return to class.

days.passageZh: 报纸报道后，长发事件成为轰动一时的新闻。罗恩和伦接受采访，出现在电视上，被同学们视为英雄。“这些不是问题少年或恶棍，”一位记者写道，“而是被单一学校系统骚扰的干净利落的美国男孩。”一篇尖刻的社论称学校的决定武断且荒谬。甚至流传了一则虚假报道，称这些男孩是摇滚乐表演者，贫困家庭需要他们的工资。最终，公民自由联盟介入，发布法院命令*，要求校长必须说明为什么不允许这些男孩重返教室。

days.passage: The school authorities were stymied. Public opinion had been marshaled against them. No longer was it a simple case of disciplining two wayward lads. Suddenly it had taken on the appearance of a nightmare in which the principal was either hanged in effigy or pictured in cartoons making a villainous swipe at the two innocent Samsons. days.title: HAPPY ENDING?

examples.en: Following an executive session, the school board ordered the principal to terminate the suspension and to send the boys back to class forthwith.
examples.zh: 在一次执行会议后，校董会命令校长解除停学，并立即让男孩们回到教室。

examples.en: It seemed incongruous* to terminate his employment just when he was so successful.

days.passage: Following an executive session, the school board ordered the principal to terminate the suspension and to send the boys back to class forthwith. Unless it could be shown that their presence disrupted the learning process, there was no reason to bar the boys. It was a bitter pill to swallow* for the principal whose irritation was exacerbated by the ruling. But some of the sting was taken out of the victory when the boys appeared in school the next day with their hair clipped to a respectable length. Everyone breathed a sigh of relief. Just as things were about to revert to normalcy, however, the same French teacher then demanded that a girl be ousted from school for wearing a mini skirt.

exercises.text: It seemed incongruous* to ________________ his employment just when he was so successful.

The ushers moved with alacrity* to ________________ the disorderly patrons.
