idiom.meaning: having a selfish motive in the background
idiom.example: I am always dubious* about the motives of a man who tells me that he has no ax to grind.

days.passageZh: 在一次执行会议后，校董会命令校长解除停学，并立即让男孩们回到教室。除非能证明他们的存在干扰了学习过程，否则没有理由禁止这些男孩入学。这对校长来说是一颗苦涩的果酱*，他的恼怒因裁决而加剧。但当男孩们第二天出现在学校时，头发剪得体面，胜利的压力也有所减弱。大家都松了口气。然而，就在一切即将恢复正常时，同一位法语老师又要求因为一个穿迷你裙的女孩被学校开除。

days.story:
One full week after the funeral, the immediate family of millionaire Charles Hudson was gathered in a law office to hear the reading of the deceased’s will. Mr. Hudson’s wife, thirty years his junior, was prepared for a bitter ______ with his former wife and her son. The lawyer, Don Rollins, anticipated a ______ session because he was the only one who was ______ of the contents of the revised will that Hudson had ordered drawn up six months prior to his death.

The current Mrs. Hudson, attired in her smart widow’s weeds, expected that she would receive the lion’s share of the estate. The former Mrs. Hudson felt that she was entitled to most of the estate since she was practically ______ at the present time, despite her substantial alimony payments.

Lawyer Rollins cleared his throat and began to read:

“To my present spouse I leave my town house where she can continue to store the jewels, shoes, dresses, and furs she accumulated in two years of shopping and marriage.

“To my son, who has put off finding a career until my estate would enrich him, I leave the sum of ten dollars for cab fare to the unemployment office.

“To my former wife whose ______ behavior I tolerated for three decades, I leave my beach house where she can continue to work on her tan, something that she prized above our happiness.

“To the Society For the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals I leave the remainder of my entire estate, knowing they will put it to better use than anyone in this room.”

The lawyer was wrong. No outcries. Silence, supreme silence, reigned among the shocked audience.
days.storyZh:
葬礼结束整整一周后，百万富翁查尔斯·哈德森的直系亲属聚集在一家律师事务所，聆听逝者遗嘱的宣读。哈德森先生的妻子比他小三十岁，已经准备好与前妻及其儿子展开一场苦涩的______。律师唐·罗林斯预计会有______庭审，因为他是唯一______哈德森在去世前六个月下令起草的修订遗嘱内容的人。

现任哈德森夫人穿着整洁的寡妇服，期望自己能继承遗产的大部分。前夫人认为自己有权继承大部分遗产，因为她目前几乎______，尽管她支付了大量赡养费。

律师罗林斯清了清嗓子，开始朗读：

“我把我的联排别墅留给了现在的丈夫，让她继续存放她两年购物和婚姻积累的珠宝、鞋子、裙子和皮草。

“给我儿子，他一直拖着找事业，直到我的遗产让他变得富有，我留给失业办公室十美元出租车费。

“致我前妻，我容忍她三十年来______行为，我离开我的海边别墅，让她继续晒黑，她把它看得比我们的幸福更重要。

“我将剩余的全部遗产留给防止虐待动物协会，我知道他们会比在座任何人都更好地利用它。”

律师错了。不要喊叫。震惊的观众陷入了极致的沉默。

days.title: ENTER DR. THOMAS A. DOOLEY

examples.en: Dooley had already distinguished himself by caring for a half-million sick and emaciated Vietnamese refugees.
examples.zh: 杜利此前已因照顾五十万病弱消瘦的越南难民而声名鹊起。

examples.en: The emaciated residents of the Warsaw Ghetto managed to win several skirmishes* from the Nazis.
examples.zh: 华沙犹太区瘦弱的居民成功赢得了几场与纳粹的小规模冲突*。

examples.en: The emaciated prisoners in the death camps were weak and sickly.
examples.zh: 死亡营中瘦弱的囚犯虚弱多病。

examples.en: Dooley was a lieutenant, assigned to a tranquil naval hospital in Yokosuka, Japan.
examples.zh: 杜利是一名中尉，被分配到日本横须贺一所宁静的海军医院。

examples.en: A firecracker terminated* the tranquil climate of the neighborhood.
examples.zh: 一声鞭炮声终结了社区宁静的气候。

examples.en: The tranquil tone of the spring morning was suddenly broken by the loud explosion.
examples.zh: 春日清晨的宁静氛围突然被一声巨响打破。

examples.en: Forthwith* he volunteered for duty on a navy ship that had been chosen to transport the refugees to sanctuary in Saigon.
examples.zh: 随即，他自愿加入一艘海军舰艇，负责将难民运送到西贡避难所。

examples.en: When Richard III violated the sanctuary of the church to seize the princes, he exceeded his jurisdiction.*
examples.zh: 当理查三世侵犯教会圣地抓捕王子时，他超越了自己的管辖范围。*

examples.en: My car is a sanctuary, where none can disturb me except for people who cut me off.
examples.zh: 我的车是个避风港，除了那些割让我开车的人，没人能打扰我。

examples.en: The curtain was beginning to ascend on Dooley’s real career.
examples.zh: 杜利的真实职业生涯开始拉下帷幕。

examples.en: Inexorably* the determined climber began to ascend the Himalayan peak.
examples.zh: 这位坚定的登山者不可抗拒地开始攀登喜马拉雅山峰。

examples.en: He ascended to heaven upon a cloud.
examples.zh: 他乘云升天。

days.passage: In 1956, Look Magazine named Thomas Dooley as one of the year’s ten most outstanding men. Just under thirty years of age at the time, Dr. Dooley had already distinguished himself by caring for a half-million sick and emaciated Vietnamese refugees. When fighting broke out in the divided country of Viet Nam, the northern Communist Viet Minh forces surged southward, scattering thousands of refugees before them. At the time, Dr. Dooley was a lieutenant, assigned to a tranquil naval hospital in Yokosuka, Japan. Forthwith* he volunteered for duty on a navy ship that had been chosen to transport the refugees to sanctuary in Saigon. The curtain was beginning to ascend on Dooley’s real career.

exercises.text: The ________________ residents of the Warsaw Ghetto managed to win several skirmishes* from the Nazis.
A firecracker terminated* the ________________ climate of the neighborhood.
When Richard III violated the ________________ of the church to seize the princes, he exceeded his jurisdiction.*
Chicago put its heaviest players up front, but they were helpless as the Giants’ line ________________ toward them.
Inexorably* the determined climber began to ________________ the Himalayan peak.

idiom.meaning: to disparage* something that you cannot have (from Aesop’s fable about the fox who called the grapes sour because he could not reach them)
idiom.example: Marcia said that she didn’t want to be on the Principal’s Honor Roll anyway, but we knew that it was just sour grapes on her part.

days.passageZh: 1956年，《Look》杂志将托马斯·杜利评为当年十大最杰出人物之一。当时年仅不到三十岁的杜利博士，已经因照顾五十万病弱消瘦的越南难民而声名鹊起。当越南分裂国家爆发战斗时，北方共产党越盟部队向南推进，将数千难民驱散在他们面前。当时，杜利医生是一名中尉，被分配到日本横须贺一所宁静的海军医院。随即，他自愿加入一艘海军舰艇，负责将难民运送到西贡避难所。杜利的真实职业生涯开始拉下帷幕。

days.title: DOOLEY’S MISSION

examples.en: He became painfully cognizant* of the malnutrition, disease, ignorance, and fear that afflicted the natives.
examples.zh: 他深刻意识到当地人所面临的营养不良、疾病、无知和恐惧。

examples.en: The emaciated* prisoners were obviously suffering from advanced malnutrition .
examples.zh: 这些消瘦的囚犯显然患有严重营养不良。

examples.en: We were worried lest the hostages be suffering from malnutrition.
examples.zh: 我们担心人质会营养不良。

examples.en: He told the story of this exciting experience in Deliver Us from Evil, a best seller that alerted America to the plight of the Vietnamese as well as to the sinister menace of communism.
examples.zh: 他在畅销书《拯救我们脱离邪恶》中讲述了这段激动人心的经历，这本书提醒美国人们关注越南人的困境以及共产主义的阴险威胁。

examples.en: The stool pigeon, the detective’s confidant,* told him about the sinister plot.
examples.zh: 线人，侦探的知己，*告诉了他这个阴险的阴谋。

examples.en: sinister influences
examples.zh: 阴险影响

days.passage: Aboard the refugee ship, Dooley’s destiny took shape. He became painfully cognizant* of the malnutrition, disease, ignorance, and fear that afflicted the natives. In addition, he discerned* how active the Communists had been in spreading their anti-American propaganda. Tom Dooley pitched in to build shelters in Haiphong, and to comfort the poor Vietnamese there before that besieged city fell to the powerful Viet Minh forces. He was seemingly unconcerned by the many privations he had to endure. For his services, Dooley received the U.S. Navy’s Legion of Merit. He told the story of this exciting experience in Deliver Us from Evil, a best seller that alerted America to the plight of the Vietnamese as well as to the sinister menace of communism.

exercises.text: The stool pigeon, the detective’s confidant,* told him about the ________________ plot.
By running up a white flag, the ________________ troops indicated their desire to withdraw from the fray.*
Citizens of several Kentucky mountain communities are ________________ by the worst poverty in the nation.
The emaciated* prisoners were obviously suffering from advanced ________________ .
Albert Schweitzer endured considerable ________________ as a jungle doctor.

idiom.meaning: to vote against a candidate running for reelection, to change one’s mind
idiom.example: The mayor asked for our support, pointing out how foolish it would be to swap horses in midstream.

days.passageZh: 在难民船上，杜利的命运逐渐成形。他深刻意识到当地人所面临的营养不良、疾病、无知和恐惧。此外，他还察觉到共产党在传播反美宣传方面的积极性。汤姆·杜利参与建造了海防的避难所，并在被围困的城市被强大的越盟攻陷前安抚了那里的越南人。他似乎对自己所经历的种种艰难毫不在意。因其服务，杜利获得了美国海军功绩勋章。他在畅销书《拯救我们脱离邪恶》中讲述了这段激动人心的经历，这本书提醒美国人们关注越南人的困境以及共产主义的阴险威胁。

days.title: STYMIED* BY PERSONAL SICKNESS

words.definition: being everywhere at the same time

examples.en: Next year, despite a growing illness, the ubiquitous Dooley turned up in the remote village of Muong Sing, attempting to thwart his traditional enemies—disease, dirt, ignorance, starvation—and hoping to quell* the spread of communism.
examples.zh: 次年，尽管病情日益严重，杜利无处不在地出现在偏远的孟辛村，试图抵御他的传统敌人——疾病、污垢、无知、饥饿——并希望遏制共产主义的蔓延。

examples.en: Sprinting all over the court, the ubiquitous referee called one foul after another.
examples.zh: 裁判在场上奔跑，一次又一次地判罚犯规。

examples.en: The ubiquitous mayor was photographed in four different parts of the city yesterday.
examples.zh: 这位无处不在的市长昨天在城市的四个不同地方被拍到了照片。

examples.en: Next year, despite a growing illness, the ubiquitous Dooley turned up in the remote village of Muong Sing, attempting to thwart his traditional enemies—disease, dirt, ignorance, starvation—and hoping to quell* the spread of communism.
examples.zh: 次年，尽管病情日益严重，杜利无处不在地出现在偏远的孟辛村，试图抵御他的传统敌人——疾病、污垢、无知、饥饿——并希望遏制共产主义的蔓延。

examples.en: Ben’s reprehensible* table manners led his fraternity brothers to seat him in a remote corner of the dining room.
examples.zh: 本那令人厌恶的餐桌礼仪让他的兄弟会成员把他安排在餐厅的一个偏僻角落。

examples.en: A remote operator may control the vehicle with a wireless handset.
examples.zh: 远程操作员可能使用无线手持设备控制车辆。

examples.en: Next year, despite a growing illness, the ubiquitous Dooley turned up in the remote village of Muong Sing, attempting to thwart his traditional enemies—disease, dirt, ignorance, starvation—and hoping to quell* the spread of communism.
examples.zh: 次年，尽管病情日益严重，杜利无处不在地出现在偏远的孟辛村，试图抵御他的传统敌人——疾病、污垢、无知、饥饿——并希望遏制共产主义的蔓延。

examples.en: In an attempt to thwart the voracious* ants, he surrounded his house with a moat of burning oil.
examples.zh: 为了阻止贪婪的蚂蚁，他用燃烧的油护城河包围了房子。

examples.en: The fisherman sat on the aft thwart to row.
examples.zh: 渔夫坐在船尾划桨。

examples.en: But his trained medical eye soon told him that the pain in his chest and back was a harbinger of a malignant cancer.
examples.zh: 但他训练有素的医学眼很快告诉他，胸口和背部的疼痛是恶性癌症的预兆。

examples.en: The excellent soup was a harbinger of the delicious meal to follow.
examples.zh: 这美味的汤预示着接下来美味的大餐。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be harbinger.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“先驱者”的含义。

examples.en: But his trained medical eye soon told him that the pain in his chest and back was a harbinger of a malignant cancer.
examples.zh: 但他训练有素的医学眼很快告诉他，胸口和背部的疼痛是恶性癌症的预兆。

examples.en: The surgeon finally located the malignant tumor that had afflicted* his patient for many months.
examples.zh: 外科医生终于找到了困扰患者数月的恶性肿瘤。

examples.en: malignant temper;  malignant revenge;  malignant infection
examples.zh: 恶性脾气; 恶性复仇; 恶性感染

days.passage: After an extensive lecture tour in 1956, Dr. Dooley returned to Laos to set up a mobile medical unit. Because the Geneva Agreement barred the entrance of military personnel to the country, he resigned from the Navy and went to work as a civilian. That story is told in The Edge of Tomorrow. Next year, despite a growing illness, the ubiquitous Dooley turned up in the remote village of Muong Sing, attempting to thwart his traditional enemies—disease, dirt, ignorance, starvation—and hoping to quell* the spread of communism. But his trained medical eye soon told him that the pain in his chest and back was a harbinger of a malignant cancer.

exercises.text: Sprinting all over the court, the ________________ referee called one foul after another.
Ben’s reprehensible* table manners led his fraternity brothers to seat him in a ________________ corner of the dining room.
The excellent soup was a ________________ of the delicious meal to follow.
In an attempt to ________________ the voracious* ants, he surrounded his house with a moat of burning oil.
The surgeon finally located the ________________ tumor that had afflicted* his patient for many months.

idiom.meaning: to be kept waiting
idiom.example: The shrewd mayor made the angry delegates cool their heels in his outer office.

days.passageZh: 1956年，杜利博士进行了广泛的巡回讲座，返回老挝组建了一支流动医疗单位。由于日内瓦协议禁止军人入境，他辞去海军职务，转而从事平民工作。这个故事在《明日边缘》中讲述。次年，尽管病情日益严重，杜利无处不在地出现在偏远的孟辛村，试图抵御他的传统敌人——疾病、污垢、无知、饥饿——并希望遏制共产主义的蔓延。但他训练有素的医学眼很快告诉他，胸口和背部的疼痛是恶性癌症的预兆。

days.title: “PROMISES TO KEEP”

examples.en: From August, 1959 until his death in January, 1961, Dooley suffered almost continuous, excruciating pain.
examples.zh: 从1959年8月直到1961年1月去世，杜利几乎持续遭受剧烈疼痛。

examples.en: With excruciating slowness, the minute hand inched its way around the clock.
examples.zh: 分针缓慢地绕着钟表转动。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be excruciating.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展现了痛苦的含义。

examples.en: Dooley worked without respite on behalf of MEDICO, the organization he had founded to bring medical aid and hope to the world’s sick and needy.
examples.zh: 杜利不间断地为他创立的MEDICO组织工作，该组织旨在为世界病患和贫困者带来医疗援助和希望。

examples.en: The voluble* insurance salesman gave my father no respite .
examples.zh: 那个多言的保险推销员没有给我父亲任何喘息的机会。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be respite.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了喘息的含义。

examples.en: The lines of Robert Frost kept reverberating in his mind during those fretful days: “The woods are lovely, dark and deep/ But I have promises to keep/ And miles to go before I sleep.” When he finally succumbed, millions throughout the world were stunned and grief-stricken by the tragedy.
examples.zh: 在那些焦虑的日子里，罗伯特·弗罗斯特的诗句不断在他脑海中回响：“树林美丽、幽暗而深邃/但我有承诺要遵守/睡前还有千里要走。”当他最终去世时，全球数百万人为这场悲剧震惊和悲痛。

examples.en: The rescue team heard the miner’s voice reverberating through the caves.
examples.zh: 救援队听到矿工的声音在洞穴中回荡。

examples.en: I heard his voice reverberating through the corridors.
examples.zh: 我听到他的声音在走廊里回荡。

examples.en: The lines of Robert Frost kept reverberating in his mind during those fretful days: “The woods are lovely, dark and deep/ But I have promises to keep/ And miles to go before I sleep.” When he finally succumbed, millions throughout the world were stunned and grief-stricken by the tragedy.
examples.zh: 在那些焦虑的日子里，罗伯特·弗罗斯特的诗句不断在他脑海中回响：“树林美丽、幽暗而深邃/但我有承诺要遵守/睡前还有千里要走。”当他最终去世时，全球数百万人为这场悲剧震惊和悲痛。

examples.en: Around income tax time fretful faces are ubiquitous.*
examples.zh: 在所得税期间，焦虑的表情无处不在。*

examples.en: The children were fretful when awakened from their nap.
examples.zh: 孩子们被叫醒时很焦虑。

days.passage: From August, 1959 until his death in January, 1961, Dooley suffered almost continuous, excruciating pain. His normal weight of 180 was cut in half, and even the pain-killing drugs could no longer bring relief. Knowing that he did not have long to live, Dr. Dooley worked without respite on behalf of MEDICO, the organization he had founded to bring medical aid and hope to the world’s sick and needy. The lines of Robert Frost kept reverberating in his mind during those fretful days: “The woods are lovely, dark and deep/ But I have promises to keep/ And miles to go before I sleep.” When he finally succumbed, millions throughout the world were stunned and grief-stricken by the tragedy.

exercises.text: With ________________ slowness, the minute hand inched its way around the clock.
The rescue team heard the miner’s voice ________________ through the caves.
Around income tax time ________________ faces are ubiquitous.*
The voluble* insurance salesman gave my father no ________________ .
Besieged* by debts, the corporation finally had to ________________ to bankruptcy.

idiom.meaning: something that diverts attention from the main issue (a red herring drawn across a fox’s path destroys the scent)
idiom.example: We felt that the introduction of his war record was a red herring to keep us from inquiring into his graft.

days.passageZh: 从1959年8月直到1961年1月去世，杜利几乎持续遭受剧烈疼痛。他原本180磅的体重减半，甚至止痛药也无法带来缓解。知道自己生命不多，杜利医生不间断地为他创立的组织MEDICO工作，该组织旨在为世界病患和贫困者带来医疗援助和希望。在那些焦虑的日子里，罗伯特·弗罗斯特的诗句不断在他脑海中回响：“树林美丽、幽暗而深邃/但我有承诺要遵守/睡前还有千里要走。”当他最终去世时，全球数百万人为这场悲剧震惊和悲痛。

questions.text: Which noun tells you that something is on the way?
Which verb means to extract, to get something out of?

days.story:
The Egyptian earthquake in October 1992 killed 600 residents of Cairo and hospitalized thousands of others, many of whom were expected to ______ as a result of their injuries. Especially hard hit were the people who inhabited the city’s slums, who had to seek ______ in those government buildings, schools, and factories that remained standing.

Muslim fundamentalists were active in providing relief to the survivors in the form of food, water, blankets, and tents to house the more than 300 families made homeless by the disaster. In the midst of a rubble-strewn street, a large tent was set up, bearing the banner, “Islam is the Solution.” Believers took the opportunity to spread the message that the earthquake was a ______ of worse things to come, and that a wayward population must follow God’s laws if they expected to ______ to heaven.

Throughout history, following volcanic eruptions, hurricanes, tidal waves, and other calamities that periodically ______ mankind, religious leaders have used such occurrences to bring the people back to their faith.

“Unless we return to Allah,” said a priest, “we can expect more divine punishment.”

Since many Egyptians had expressed unhappiness about their government prior to the earthquake, there was a good chance for Muslim fundamentalists to seize the opportunity to win new converts by showing that the answer to recovery was not through man’s efforts but through God’s.
days.storyZh:
1992年10月的埃及地震造成开罗600名居民死亡，数千人住院，其中许多人预计因伤势将______。受打击尤为严重的是居住在贫民窟的人们，他们不得不在那些仍然屹立的政府建筑、学校和工厂中寻求______。

穆斯林原教旨主义者积极为幸存者提供食物、水、毯子和帐篷，安置因灾难而无家可归的300多个家庭。在满是瓦砾的街道中央，搭起了一顶大帐篷，上面挂着“伊斯兰是解决方案”的横幅。信徒们借此机会传播信息：地震是未来更严重事件的前______，迷失的人们若想______天堂，必须遵守上帝的律法。

纵观历史，在火山爆发、飓风、海啸及其他周期性______人类的灾难之后，宗教领袖们利用这些事件将人们带回信仰。

一位祭司说：“除非我们回归真主，否则我们只能预料到更多的神圣惩罚。”

由于许多埃及人在地震前对政府表达了不满，穆斯林原教旨主义者有很大机会抓住机会，通过展示恢复的答案不是靠人类努力，而是靠真主。

days.passage: P. T. Barnum, the great circus impresario, was once accosted* by a woman who showed him a scurrilous* manuscript about himself, and said that unless he paid her, she would have the book printed. Barnum rejected the extortion attempt. “Say what you please,” he replied, “but make sure that you mention me in some way. Then come to me and I will estimate the value of your services as a publicity agent.” Barnum obviously felt that adverse criticism was an asset for a public figure. A man who seeks the limelight should not care what is written about him but should be concerned only when they stop writing about him. Barnum’s philosophy suggests that we might do well to review the plethora* of publicity given to rabble-rousers and bigots.

days.passageZh: 著名的马戏团经理P.T.巴纳姆曾被一位女子拦住*，她给他看了一份关于自己的诽谤手稿，并说除非他付钱，否则她会让这本书印刷出来。巴纳姆拒绝了勒索企图。“随你怎么说，”他回答，“但一定要以某种方式提到我。那就来找我，我会评估你作为宣传代理人的价值。”巴纳姆显然认为负面批评对公众人物来说是一种优势。一个渴望曝光的人不应该在意别人怎么写他，只有当别人不再写他时才应该关心。巴纳姆的理念建议，我们或许应该审视那些煽动者和偏执狂所获得的大量宣传。

days.title: BIGOTS* GET PUBLICITY

examples.en: Today, the blatant bigot, the leader of a lunatic fringe, and the hate-monger, each with his tiny entourage, find it relatively easy to attract publicity.
examples.zh: 如今，那些赤裸裸的偏执狂、疯狂边缘分子的领袖和仇恨煽动者，各自带着一小撮随从，发现自己相对容易地吸引关注。

examples.en: With blatant discourtesy the reporters continued to harass* the bereaved family.
examples.zh: 记者们公然无礼地继续骚扰*这名丧亲的家属。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be blatant.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“赤裸裸”的含义。

examples.en: Today, the blatant bigot, the leader of a lunatic fringe, and the hate-monger, each with his tiny entourage, find it relatively easy to attract publicity.
examples.zh: 如今，那些赤裸裸的偏执狂、疯狂边缘分子的领袖和仇恨煽动者，各自带着一小撮随从，发现自己相对容易地吸引关注。

examples.en: The visiting dictator’s ubiquitous* entourage of bodyguards disturbed our tranquil* city.
examples.zh: 这位来访独裁者无处不在的保镖随从打扰了我们宁静的城市。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be entourage.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了作为随从的含义。

examples.en: Newspapers give space to the virulent activities of those agitators on the grounds that they are newsworthy.
examples.zh: 报纸以这些煽动者的恶毒活动为由，给予了报道空间。

examples.en: Europe’s population was afflicted* by a virulent plague known as the Black Death.
examples.zh: 欧洲人口遭受一种被称为黑死病的剧烈瘟疫。

examples.en: The politicians were virulent in their hatred of the president.
examples.zh: 政客们对总统的仇恨极为激烈。

examples.en: “Yes,” said the host of one such program, “we invite bigots, but it is only for the purpose of making them look ridiculous by displaying their inane* policies to the public.” Some civic-minded organizations have answered, however, that the hosts are not always equipped to demolish those guests, and even if they were, the audience would still be exposed to the venom they spew forth.
examples.zh: “是的，”其中一档节目的主持人说，“我们邀请偏执狂，但目的只是为了让他们在公众面前显得荒谬，让他们看起来很可笑。”然而，一些有公民意识的组织回应称，主持人并不总具备摧毁这些嘉宾的能力，即使有，观众仍会暴露在他们喷出的毒液中。

examples.en: Clym Yeobright’s mother succumbed* to the venom of a snake bite.
examples.zh: 克莱姆·约布赖特的母亲死于蛇咬毒液。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be venom.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是毒液。

examples.en: “Yes,” said the host of one such program, “we invite bigots, but it is only for the purpose of making them look ridiculous by displaying their inane* policies to the public.” Some civic-minded organizations have answered, however, that the hosts are not always equipped to demolish those guests, and even if they were, the audience would still be exposed to the venom they spew forth.
examples.zh: “是的，”其中一档节目的主持人说，“我们邀请偏执狂，但目的只是为了让他们在公众面前显得荒谬，让他们看起来很可笑。”然而，一些有公民意识的组织回应称，主持人并不总具备摧毁这些嘉宾的能力，即使有，观众仍会暴露在他们喷出的毒液中。

examples.en: From each candidate’s headquarters acrimonious* charges would spew forth daily.
examples.zh: 每个候选人总部每天都会有尖锐的指控。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be spew.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“喷吐”的含义。

days.passage: Today, the blatant bigot, the leader of a lunatic fringe, and the hate-monger, each with his tiny entourage, find it relatively easy to attract publicity. Newspapers give space to the virulent activities of those agitators on the grounds that they are newsworthy. TV producers and radio executives, seeking for sensationalism, often extend a welcome to such controversial characters. “Yes,” said the host of one such program, “we invite bigots, but it is only for the purpose of making them look ridiculous by displaying their inane* policies to the public.” Some civic-minded organizations have answered, however, that the hosts are not always equipped to demolish those guests, and even if they were, the audience would still be exposed to the venom they spew forth.

idiom.phrase: to keep a stiff upper lip
idiom.meaning: to be courageous in the face of trouble
idiom.example: It was admirable to see how the British managed to keep a stiff upper lip in spite of the German bombing.

days.passageZh: 如今，那些赤裸裸的偏执狂、疯狂边缘分子的领袖和仇恨煽动者，各自带着一小撮随从，发现自己相对容易地吸引关注。报纸以这些煽动者的恶毒活动为由，给予了报道空间。电视制片人和电台高管为了制造轰动效应，常常欢迎这些有争议的角色。“是的，”其中一档节目的主持人说，“我们邀请偏执狂，但目的只是为了让他们在公众面前显得荒谬，让他们看起来很可笑。”然而，一些有公民意识的组织回应称，主持人并不总具备摧毁这些嘉宾的能力，即使有，观众仍会暴露在他们喷出的毒液中。

days.title: COPING WITH BIGOTS*

examples.en: Since we cherish freedom of speech, we are loath to deny the request, even if he preaches hatred.
examples.zh: 由于我们珍视言论自由，即使他宣扬仇恨，我们也不愿拒绝他的请求。

examples.en: Since we felt that the ruling was arbitrary,* we were loath to obey it.
examples.zh: 由于我们认为该裁决是武断的，*我们不愿遵守。

examples.en: I was loath to return to the office without the Henderson file.
examples.zh: 我不愿在没有亨德森档案的情况下回办公室。

examples.en: As a result, hate-mongers are given the opportunity to rent halls, conduct meetings, publish abusive literature, and solicit contributions.
examples.zh: 因此，仇恨煽动者有机会租用大厅、召开会议、发表辱骂性文献并征集捐款。

examples.en: Daily the volunteers went out to solicit funds for the indigent* families.
examples.zh: 志愿者们每天都外出为贫困家庭筹款。

examples.en: to solicit alms, or a favour
examples.zh: 去募捐或帮忙

examples.en: My neighbor was astute enough to discern* the adverse* features of the mortgage.
examples.zh: 我的邻居足够敏锐，能够辨别*抵押贷款的不利*特征。

words.definition: Someone whose job is to speak for someone's case in a court of law; a counsel.

examples.en: Without the warmth of the spotlight, he feels that the bigot will freeze and become ineffectual.
examples.zh: 没有聚光灯的温暖，他觉得这个偏执狂会僵住，变得无能为力。

examples.en: The play was so blatantly* bad that the impresario* fired its ineffectual director.
examples.zh: 这部戏糟糕得如此明显，以至于策划人解雇了无能的导演。

examples.en: Our group leader proved highly ineffectual, caving to every whim put forth by the other members.
examples.zh: 我们的组长表现得非常无能，总是屈服于其他成员的各种意愿。

days.passage: Suppose a bigot wished to organize a meeting in your neighborhood. Since we cherish freedom of speech, we are loath to deny the request, even if he preaches hatred. As a result, hate-mongers are given the opportunity to rent halls, conduct meetings, publish abusive literature, and solicit contributions. What can be done about them? One astute observer, Prof. S. Andhil Fineberg, advocates the “quarantine method.” His plan is to give such groups no publicity and to ignore them completely. Without the warmth of the spotlight, he feels that the bigot will freeze and become ineffectual. Debating with such warped minds is not feasible* and only tends to exacerbate* the situation.

exercises.text: Since we felt that the ruling was arbitrary,* we were ________________ to obey it.

idiom.meaning: to hesitate because of fear or uncertainty
idiom.example: My cousin was all set to join the paratroops, but at the last moment he got cold feet.

days.passageZh: 假设一个偏执狂想在你所在的社区组织一次会议。由于我们珍视言论自由，即使他宣扬仇恨，我们也不愿拒绝他的请求。因此，仇恨煽动者有机会租用大厅、召开会议、发表辱骂性文献并征集捐款。他们能做些什么？一位敏锐的观察者S. Andhil Fineberg教授主张“隔离方法”。他的计划是不给这些团体任何宣传，完全忽视他们。没有聚光灯的温暖，他觉得这个偏执狂会僵住，变得无能为力。与如此扭曲的思想辩论不可行*，只会让情况更加恶化。

days.title: MORE THAN SILENCE

examples.en: Fineberg urges community-relations organizations to scrutinize the nefarious activities of hate-mongers and to be prepared to furnish information about them to amicable inquirers.
examples.zh: 芬伯格敦促社区关系组织密切关注仇恨煽动者的恶劣活动，并准备向友好的询问者提供相关信息。

examples.en: No sooner did the lawyer scrutinize the extortion* note than she called the police.
examples.zh: 律师刚仔细查看勒索信件，她立刻报警了。

examples.en: to scrutinize the conduct or motives of individuals
examples.zh: 审查个人的行为或动机

examples.en: Fineberg urges community-relations organizations to scrutinize the nefarious activities of hate-mongers and to be prepared to furnish information about them to amicable inquirers.
examples.zh: 芬伯格敦促社区关系组织密切关注仇恨煽动者的恶劣活动，并准备向友好的询问者提供相关信息。

examples.en: We were distraught* upon hearing the venom* spewed* forth by the nefarious bigot.*
examples.zh: 当我们听到那个邪恶的偏执狂所喷吐的毒液时，我们感到非常痛苦。

examples.en: Aliens have a nefarious connotation in many science fiction books.
examples.zh: 在许多科幻小说中，外星人带有阴暗的含义。

examples.en: Fineberg urges community-relations organizations to scrutinize the nefarious activities of hate-mongers and to be prepared to furnish information about them to amicable inquirers.
examples.zh: 芬伯格敦促社区关系组织密切关注仇恨煽动者的恶劣活动，并准备向友好的询问者提供相关信息。

examples.en: Our amicable relations with Latin America are an asset* to hemispheric trade.
examples.zh: 我们与拉丁美洲的友好关系是半球贸易的资产*。

examples.en: He was an amicable fellow with an easy smile.
examples.zh: 他是个和蔼可亲、笑容轻松的人。

examples.en: Bigger than the vexatious immediate problem is the need to find out the cause for such bigotry and to counteract this sinister* malady that afflicts* a segment of our society.
examples.zh: 比当前令人烦恼的眼前问题更大的是查明这种偏见的根源，并对抗困扰我们社会某部分群体的这种阴险*病态。

examples.en: The vexatious buzzing of the mosquitoes as they surged* about our heads nearly drove us insane.
examples.zh: 蚊子在我们头顶盘旋时嗡嗡作响，几乎让我们发疯。

examples.en: a vexatious lawsuit
examples.zh: 一场令人烦恼的诉讼

examples.en: Bigger than the vexatious immediate problem is the need to find out the cause for such bigotry and to counteract this sinister* malady that afflicts* a segment of our society.
examples.zh: 比当前令人烦恼的眼前问题更大的是查明这种偏见的根源，并对抗困扰我们社会某部分群体的这种阴险*病态。

examples.en: Once the virulent* malady had run its course, my temperature dropped.
examples.zh: 当这种恶毒*的疾病消退后，我的体温下降了。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be malady.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“疾病”的含义。

days.passage: The quarantine method for handling bigots implies more than giving them the silent treatment. Prof. Fineberg urges community-relations organizations to scrutinize the nefarious activities of hate-mongers and to be prepared to furnish information about them to amicable inquirers. When a rabble-rouser is coming, those organizations should privately expose him to opinion-molders. In addition, constructive efforts should be taken to induce people to involve themselves in projects for improving intergroup relations. Bigger than the vexatious immediate problem is the need to find out the cause for such bigotry and to counteract this sinister* malady that afflicts* a segment of our society.

exercises.text: The ________________ buzzing of the mosquitoes as they surged* about our heads nearly drove us insane.
Our ________________ relations with Latin America are an asset* to hemispheric trade.
Once the virulent* ________________ had run its course, my temperature dropped.
We were distraught* upon hearing the venom* spewed* forth by the ________________ bigot.*
No sooner did the lawyer ________________ the extortion* note than she called the police.

idiom.phrase: to look a gift horse in the mouth
idiom.meaning: to be critical of a present (from the practice of judging a horse’s age by his teeth)
idiom.example: Although I didn’t have much use for Uncle Roy’s present, I took it with a big smile since I have been taught never to look a gift horse in the mouth.

days.passageZh: 隔离方式处理偏执者不仅仅是对他们冷战。芬伯格教授敦促社区关系组织严肃审查仇恨煽动者的恶劣活动，并准备向友好的询问者提供相关信息。当煽动者来时，这些组织应私下让他接触影响舆论的人。此外，应采取建设性努力，鼓励人们参与改善群体间关系的项目。比当前令人烦恼的眼前问题更大的是查明这种偏见的根源，并对抗困扰我们社会某部分群体的这种阴险*病态。

days.story:
When the American public started to hear about the AIDS virus in the 1980s, there was a measure of concern but no real alarm. After all, some said, it was a problem solely for a small group of intravenous drug users who shared dirty needles, and for the homosexual community.

But as the numbers of afflicted people grew during the 1980s and 1990s, we began to ______ the tragic news stories more closely. The deaths of young people like Ryan White and Kimberly Bergalis, not members of the at-risk groups referred to above, convinced us that what was at first regarded merely as a ______ illness was actually a ______ threat to the general community.

In the mid-1980s, ______ medical researchers were optimistic that a vaccine for AIDS would be found in short order. Those predictions proved to be inaccurate. In October 1992, former Surgeon-General C. Everett Koop said that he doubted we would ever find a cure for the disease. With over 200,000 Americans already having succumbed to the ______ killer, and another 300,000 who were HIV-positive and could contract a full-blown form of AIDS, Koop’s statement sent chills throughout the country.

A prominent AIDS expert, however, took issue with Koop. “The fight will be difficult,” said Dr. Harley Smith, “but we will find an answer very shortly.” Now, in the twenty-first century, the answer is apparently at hand.
days.storyZh:
当美国公众在1980年代开始听说艾滋病病毒时，虽然有一定的担忧，但并没有真正的警觉。毕竟，有人说，这只是少数静脉注射毒品使用者和同性恋群体的问题。

但随着20世纪80年代和90年代受影响人数的增加，我们开始更加密切地______这些悲惨的新闻。像瑞安·怀特和金伯利·伯加利斯这样的年轻人的死亡，他们并非上述高风险群体成员，这让我们相信，最初被视为______疾病，实际上对整个社区构成了______威胁。

在1980年代中期，______医学研究人员乐观地认为艾滋病疫苗很快就会被发现。这些预测被证明是不准确的。1992年10月，前外科总监C·埃弗雷特·库普表示，他怀疑我们永远找不到治愈这种疾病的方法。在已有超过20万美国人死于______杀手，另有30万艾滋病毒阳性且可能感染全面艾滋病的情况下，库普的声明震惊了全国。

然而，一位著名的艾滋病专家对库普提出了异议。“这场战斗会很艰难，”哈雷·史密斯博士说，“但我们很快就会找到答案。”如今，进入二十一世纪，答案显然就在眼前。

days.title: JERRY HART’S SIXTH SENSE

examples.en: Just as some people can predict the onset of inclement weather because of an aching in their bones, so could Jerry detect incipient* disaster.
examples.zh: 正如有些人能因骨头酸痛而预测恶劣天气的到来，杰瑞也能察觉到即将来临的灾难。

examples.en: She blamed her absence from the game on the inclement weather, but we knew that was sour grapes.*
examples.zh: 她把缺席归咎于恶劣天气，但我们知道那只是酸葡萄。*

examples.en: inclement weather
examples.zh: 恶劣天气

examples.en: He sat at his desk, trying to peruse a company report but his efforts were ineffectual.* The gnawing at his insides, the tinge* of uneasiness, the premonition of calamity that besieged* him would not desist.
examples.zh: 他坐在办公桌前，试图翻阅公司报告，但努力徒劳无功。* 内心的啃噬感、一丝不安、即将降临的灾难预感挥之不去。

examples.en: After being admonished* by his father, he began to peruse the want ads daily.
examples.zh: 在父亲的训斥*后，他开始每天浏览招聘广告。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be peruse.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被阅读的含义。

examples.en: He sat at his desk, trying to peruse a company report but his efforts were ineffectual.* The gnawing at his insides, the tinge* of uneasiness, the premonition of calamity that besieged* him would not desist.
examples.zh: 他坐在办公桌前，试图翻阅公司报告，但努力徒劳无功。* 内心的啃噬感、一丝不安、即将降临的灾难预感挥之不去。

examples.en: When the black cat crossed her path, Ellen had a premonition of disaster.
examples.zh: 当黑猫从她面前走过时，艾伦预感灾难将至。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be premonition.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了预知的含义。

examples.en: He sat at his desk, trying to peruse a company report but his efforts were ineffectual.* The gnawing at his insides, the tinge* of uneasiness, the premonition of calamity that besieged* him would not desist.
examples.zh: 他坐在办公桌前，试图翻阅公司报告，但努力徒劳无功。* 内心的啃噬感、一丝不安、即将降临的灾难预感挥之不去。

examples.en: The pickets promulgated* a warning that they would not desist in their efforts to enhance* their standard of living.
examples.zh: 纠察队发出警告*，表示他们不会停止努力提升*生活水平。

examples.en: Please desist from telephoning me on this number.
examples.zh: 请不要用这个号码打电话给我。

days.passage: An uneasy feeling had made Jerry Hart miserable all day long. It was difficult to explain, but the similar sensations in the past had been accurate—trouble was on the way. Just as some people can predict the onset of inclement weather because of an aching in their bones, so could Jerry detect incipient* disaster. He sat at his desk, trying to peruse a company report but his efforts were ineffectual.* The gnawing at his insides, the tinge* of uneasiness, the premonition of calamity that besieged* him would not desist. When the phone rang, he recoiled with fear—it was his wife and she was hysterical. Their son had been bitten by a mad dog!

exercises.text: After being admonished* by his father, he began to ________________ the want ads daily.
When the black cat crossed her path, Ellen had a ________________ of disaster.
The pickets promulgated* a warning that they would not ________________ in their efforts to enhance* their standard of living.
As the snake prepared to strike, the girls ________________ in horror.
She blamed her absence from the game on the ________________ weather, but we knew that was sour grapes.*

idiom.meaning: to bear the consequences (from the story of the Pied Piper of Hamelin)
idiom.example: The cruel leader was doing well at the present time, but he knew that one day he might have to pay the piper.

days.passageZh: 一种不安的感觉让杰瑞·哈特整天都感到痛苦。很难解释，但过去类似的感觉确实如此——麻烦即将来临。正如有些人能因骨头酸痛而预测恶劣天气的到来，杰瑞也能察觉到即将来临的灾难。他坐在办公桌前，试图翻阅公司报告，但努力徒劳无功。* 内心的啃噬感、一丝不安、即将降临的灾难预感挥之不去。电话响起时，他吓得退缩——是他的妻子，她歇斯底里。他们的儿子被疯狗咬了！

days.title: CRISIS!

examples.en: As soon as Jerry Hart could get the pertinent facts from his wife, he dashed out of the office on his way home.
examples.zh: 杰瑞·哈特一从妻子那里得知相关事实，便匆匆离开办公室回家。

examples.en: During the trial the astute* lawyer was able to elicit* the pertinent information from the key witness.
examples.zh: 在审判过程中，这位精明的律师能够从关键证人那里套出*相关信息。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be pertinent.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是相关性的。

examples.en: Visions of an ugly mastiff with foaming jaws obsessed him.
examples.zh: 他脑海中浮现出一只下颚起泡沫的丑陋獒犬的画面。

examples.en: My nervous neighbor bought a pugnacious* mastiff to frighten burglars.
examples.zh: 我紧张的邻居买了一只好斗的*獒犬来吓唬窃贼。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be mastiff.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了獒犬的含义。

examples.en: Little Bobby was on his bed, surrounded by a doctor, a policeman, Jerry’s doleful wife, his two daughters, and a half-dozen wan neighbors.
examples.zh: 小鲍比躺在床上，周围有医生、警察、杰瑞忧郁的妻子、他的两个女儿和六个脸色憔悴的邻居。

examples.en: doleful expressions abounded* throughout headquarters on the night of the election.
examples.zh: 选举当晚，总部各处充满了哀伤的表情*。

examples.en: The doleful peal of the bell indicated another funeral was being held.
examples.zh: 钟声哀伤地敲响，预示着又一场葬礼正在举行。

examples.en: Little Bobby was on his bed, surrounded by a doctor, a policeman, Jerry’s doleful wife, his two daughters, and a half-dozen wan neighbors.
examples.zh: 小鲍比躺在床上，周围有医生、警察、杰瑞忧郁的妻子、他的两个女儿和六个脸色憔悴的邻居。

examples.en: After the tension, his normally ruddy face was wan and tired.
examples.zh: 紧张过后，他平时红润的脸色苍白疲惫。

examples.en: A wan expression
examples.zh: 一副苍白的表情

days.passage: As soon as Jerry Hart could get the pertinent facts from his wife, he dashed out of the office on his way home. He jostled* people in the hallway, implored* the elevator operator to hurry, and with flagrant* disregard for an elderly gentleman jumped into the cab he had hailed. The twenty-minute taxi ride seemed interminable* and all the while horrible thoughts occurred to Jerry. Visions of an ugly mastiff with foaming jaws obsessed him. A crowd of people had gathered in front of his house so that Jerry had to force his way through them. Little Bobby was on his bed, surrounded by a doctor, a policeman, Jerry’s doleful wife, his two daughters, and a half-dozen wan neighbors.

exercises.text: The stockbroker was ________________ with the idea of becoming a painter.
My nervous neighbor bought a pugnacious* ________________ to frighten burglars.
________________ expressions abounded* throughout headquarters on the night of the election.
During the trial the astute* lawyer was able to elicit* the ________________ information from the key witness.
After the tension, his normally ruddy face was ________________ and tired.

idiom.meaning: being scolded
idiom.example: Because of her repeated lateness, Betty’s boss called her on the carpet.

days.passageZh: 杰瑞·哈特一从妻子那里得知相关事实，便匆匆离开办公室回家。他在走廊里推搡着人群，恳求电梯操作员快点，毫不顾忌一位老先生，跳上了他招手的出租车。二十分钟的出租车旅程似乎漫长无尽*，与此同时，杰瑞脑海中浮现出可怕的想法。他脑海中浮现出一只下颚起泡沫的丑陋獒犬的画面。一群人聚集在他家门前，杰瑞不得不强行挤过人群。小鲍比躺在床上，周围有医生、警察、杰瑞忧郁的妻子、他的两个女儿和六个脸色憔悴的邻居。

days.title: A TIME FOR DECISION

examples.en: Saying that he would not tolerate* her histrionics , the director fired the temperamental actress.
The doctor explained the situation calmly, avoiding histrionics.
examples.zh: 医生平静地解释了情况，避免了夸张。

examples.en: I’m tired of Mary’s histrionics.
examples.zh: 我受够了玛丽的戏剧化。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be histrionics.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是戏剧化。

words.definition: Exaggerated, overemotional behaviour, especially when calculated to elicit a response; melodramatics.

examples.en: All his life he found happiness elusive , but wealth easy to come by.
Secondly, the elusive dog had frustrated all attempts to find him so far.
examples.zh: 其次，这只神秘的狗迄今为止挫败了所有寻找他的尝试。

examples.en: The elusive criminal was arrested
examples.zh: 那个难以捉摸的罪犯被逮捕了

examples.en: A precise definition of diarrhea is elusive (Robbin's pathology, 8th ed)
examples.zh: 腹泻的精确定义难以确定（Robbin's pathology，第8版）

words.definition: To disappoint or defeat; to vex by depriving of something expected or desired.

examples.en: The warden frustrated the prisoners’ attempt to escape by adding more guards.
The sordid* rioting was symptomatic of the problems facing the large cities.
Hart said that a neighbor who had seen the dog claimed that it had been foaming at the mouth, barking, and growling constantly—all symptomatic of rabies.
examples.zh: 哈特说，一位邻居见过这只狗，称它一直口吐白沫、吠叫和咆哮——这些都是狂犬病的症状。

examples.en: The city's problems are symptomatic of the crisis that is spreading throughout the country.
examples.zh: 这座城市的问题反映了正在全国范围内蔓延的危机。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be symptomatic.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“有症状”的含义。

words.definition: To insert something between other things.

examples.en: Most viewers hate it when a commercial is interjected into a suspense drama.

days.passage: The doctor explained the situation calmly, avoiding histrionics. First of all, they didn’t know whether the dog had rabies. Secondly, the elusive dog had frustrated all attempts to find him so far. Finally, the decision would have to be made whether Bobby was to undergo the painful vaccination administered daily for two weeks. Mrs. Hart said that a neighbor who had seen the dog claimed that it had been foaming at the mouth, barking, and growling constantly—all symptomatic of rabies. But the policeman interjected that there hadn’t been a case of a mad dog in the county in over twenty years; he repudiated* the neighbor’s report, advocating* that they do nothing for at least another day. Mr. and Mrs. Hart sat down to think about their next step.

All his life he found happiness ________________ , but wealth easy to come by.

idiom.example: When someone joined in bidding for the antique, the dealer was forced to show his hand.

days.passageZh: 医生平静地解释了情况，避免了夸张。首先，他们不知道这只狗是否感染了狂犬病。其次，这只神秘的狗迄今为止挫败了所有寻找他的尝试。最后，必须决定是否让鲍比接受为期两周每天注射的痛苦疫苗。哈特夫人说，一位邻居见过这只狗，称它一直在口吐白沫、吠叫和咆哮——这些都是狂犬病的症状。但警察插话说，县里已经二十多年没有疯狗事件了;他否认了*邻居的报告*，主张*他们至少还要再待一天什么都不做。哈特夫妇坐下思考下一步该怎么办。

days.title: THE PERTINENT* FACTS ABOUT RABIES

examples.en: “Give me some of the rudimentary* information about the disease, Doc,” said Jerry, glancing toward the inert figure of his son.
examples.zh: “医生，给我一些关于这种疾病的基础*信息，”杰瑞说着，目光投向那个无动于衷的儿子。

examples.en: Seeing the inert player, the manager dashed out onto the field.
examples.zh: 看到这位无力的球员，主教练冲上场。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be inert.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是惰性。

examples.en: Once the salient characteristics appear (ten days to six months) then death is imminent.” “What are the symptoms?” asked Mrs.
examples.zh: 一旦显著特征出现（十天到六个月），死亡就迫在眉睫。”“症状是什么？”夫人问道。

examples.en: One of the salient features of her poetry is a dependence upon euphemisms.*
examples.zh: 她诗歌的一个显著特点是对委婉语的依赖。*

examples.en: The article is not exhaustive, but it covers the salient points pretty well.
examples.zh: 文章虽不详尽，但涵盖了主要点。

examples.en: Once the salient characteristics appear (ten days to six months) then death is imminent.” “What are the symptoms?” asked Mrs.
examples.zh: 一旦显著特征出现（十天到六个月），死亡就迫在眉睫。”“症状是什么？”夫人问道。

examples.en: The senator loathed* it when people said that an atomic war was imminent .
examples.zh: 当人们说原子战争迫在眉睫时，参议员非常痛恨。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be imminent.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“迫在眉睫”的含义。

examples.en: Also, muscle spasms and convulsions.” The squeamish neighbors who were engrossed in the doctor’s remarks gasped.
examples.zh: 还有肌肉痉挛和抽搐。”那些胆小的邻居们正专注地听着医生的话，倒吸一口气。

examples.en: When his squeamish partner complained about a lack of ethics,* the businessman laughed at his innocence.
examples.zh: 当他那胆小的伴侣抱怨缺乏道德时，商人嘲笑他的无辜。

examples.en: He might have made a good doctor, had he not been so squeamish about the sight of blood.
examples.zh: 如果不是他对血腥感到害怕，他本可以成为一名好医生。

examples.en: Also, muscle spasms and convulsions.” The squeamish neighbors who were engrossed in the doctor’s remarks gasped.
examples.zh: 还有肌肉痉挛和抽搐。”那些胆小的邻居们正专注地听着医生的话，倒吸一口气。

examples.en: engrossed in his crossword puzzle, he failed to notice the paucity* of customers in the restaurant.
examples.zh: 他全神贯注地做着填字游戏，没注意到餐厅里顾客的稀少*。

examples.en: She seems to be completely engrossed in that book.
examples.zh: 她似乎完全沉浸在那本书里。

days.passage: “Give me some of the rudimentary* information about the disease, Doc,” said Jerry, glancing toward the inert figure of his son. “Well, as you know, the malady* used to be called ‘hydrophobia’ (fear of water) because one of the symptoms is an inability to swallow liquids. Actually, it is caused by a live virus from the saliva of an infected animal. If saliva gets into a bite wound, the victim may get rabies. The virus travels along the nerves to the spine and brain. Once the salient characteristics appear (ten days to six months) then death is imminent.” “What are the symptoms?” asked Mrs. Hart. “Pain and numbness, difficulty in swallowing, headaches and nervousness. Also, muscle spasms and convulsions.” The squeamish neighbors who were engrossed in the doctor’s remarks gasped. “I think we should go ahead with the injections,” the distraught* Mrs. Hart said. “I’ve heard enough.”

exercises.text: The senator loathed* it when people said that an atomic war was ________________ .
When his ________________ partner complained about a lack of ethics,* the businessman laughed at his innocence.
________________ in his crossword puzzle, he failed to notice the paucity* of customers in the restaurant.
One of the ________________ features of her poetry is a dependence upon euphemisms.*
Seeing the ________________ player, the manager dashed out onto the field.

idiom.meaning: to fight imaginary enemies (from Don Quixote)
idiom.example: The vice president told the committee, “We’re really on your side, and if you fight us you’ll be tilting at windmills.”

days.passageZh: “医生，给我一些关于这种疾病的基础*信息，”杰瑞说着，目光投向那个无动于衷的儿子。“嗯，正如你所知，这种病症*以前叫'恐水症'（怕水），因为其中一个症状是无法吞咽液体。实际上，它是由感染动物唾液中的活病毒引起的。如果唾液进入咬伤，受害者可能会感染狂犬病。病毒沿神经传播至脊柱和大脑。一旦显著特征出现（十天到六个月），死亡就迫在眉睫。”“有哪些症状？”哈特夫人问道。“疼痛和麻木，吞咽困难，头痛和紧张。还有肌肉痉挛和抽搐。”那些胆小的邻居们正专注地听着医生的话，倒吸一口气。“我觉得我们应该继续注射，”痛苦的哈特夫人说。“我听够了。”

days.story:
People who are ______ about the sight of blood or ______ in horror from most forms of violence would do well to avoid some of the movies now being shown at their local cinemas. Producers have learned that films that scare the patrons out of their seats, ironically, put millions of fans into those seats, keeping them ______ in the goose pimple-inducing spectacles that flash across the screen.

Of course, each movie carries with it a rating that indicates its suitability for certain age groups, either because of its subject matter, language, presentation, or level of violence. Pictures with a “G” rating are approved for all audiences, while, at the other end of the scale, those that are given an “X” rating are for adults only with no children allowed under any circumstance. Getting an “R” rating indicates that the movie is restricted (no one under 18 admitted without an adult) but some Hollywood moguls consider the “R” to be the magnet that insures box office success. And we can be sure that as long as shock films ring up a merry tune on the cash registers, producers will not ______ from making them.

A director who specializes in making gory films involving monsters, vampires, and brutal serial killers boasted in a college lecture that his work was in good taste. One student who disasgreed was provoked to ______ that in his opinion the diet of “shock-schlock” movies was in worse taste than those pictures that contained vulgar language and nudity. “At least they’re honest,” he declared.
days.storyZh:
那些对血腥或因暴力形式而感到恐惧的人____________，最好避开现在在本地影院放映的一些电影。制片人发现，那些吓得观众离座的电影，讽刺的是，反而让数百万粉丝坐进座位，让他们______在屏幕上闪烁的令人起鸡皮疙瘩的眼镜中。

当然，每部电影都会有一个分级，表明其适合特定年龄段，可能是因为题材、语言、表现方式或暴力程度。获得“G”级的影片对所有观众均有批准，而在另一端，获得“X”级的影片仅限成人观看，任何情况下都不允许儿童观看。获得“R”级意味着该片有限制（未满18岁者未经成年人陪同不得入场），但一些好莱坞大亨认为“R”级是票房成功的磁石。我们可以肯定，只要震撼电影能在收银机上奏响欢快的曲调，制片人就不会______放弃制作它们。

一位专门制作涉及怪物、吸血鬼和残忍连环杀手血腥电影的导演，在大学讲座中自夸自己的作品品味得体。一位不满的学生被激怒，______他认为“震惊垃圾”电影的饮食比那些包含粗俗语言和裸露的电影更不合适。“至少他们很诚实，”他宣称。

days.title: THE SEARCH FOR THE DOG (CONTINUED)

examples.en: Meanwhile, the Harts had notified the local radio stations to broadcast a poignant appeal for the dog’s owner to come forward.
examples.zh: 与此同时，哈特夫妇通知当地电台，向狗主人发出感人呼吁，呼吁其站出来。

examples.en: The sermon was poignant enough to bring tears to the brash* delinquent’s eyes.
examples.zh: 这场布道感人至深，让这位鲁莽*的不良少年眼眶湿润。

examples.en: A poignant reply will garner more credence than hours of blown smoke.
examples.zh: 一个感人的回复比数小时的吹嘘更能赢得信誉。

examples.en: The station was inundated with phone calls but all leads were fruitless.
examples.zh: 电台接到电话，但所有线索都毫无结果。

examples.en: After a fruitless attempt to wrest* control of the government, the traitors were incarcerated.
examples.zh: 在一次徒劳的夺取政府控制权尝试后，叛徒们被监禁。

examples.en: The unskilled man’s attempt at fixing his car engine was fruitless.
examples.zh: 那个没经验的男人试图修车发动机，但毫无结果。

examples.en: The boy’s story was garbled, but he did remember that the animal was gray and had a collar.
examples.zh: 男孩的故事有些含糊，但他记得那只动物是灰色的，戴着项圈。

examples.en: Although the message was garbled , its salient* points were clear enough.
examples.zh: 虽然信息有些杂乱，但其主要*观点足够清晰。

examples.en: to garble a quotation
examples.zh: 引用内容的含糊

examples.en: There was little tangible* evidence to go on, but the police remained sanguine.
examples.zh: 几乎没有具体的证据可依据，但警方依然保持乐观。

examples.en: Because the malignancy* had gone unchecked, the surgeons were not sanguine about the patient’s chances.
examples.zh: 由于恶性肿瘤*未被控制，外科医生对患者的生存机会并不乐观。

examples.en: a sanguine bodily temperament
examples.zh: 血性多血的身体

days.passage: Meanwhile, the Harts had notified the local radio stations to broadcast a poignant appeal for the dog’s owner to come forward. The station was inundated with phone calls but all leads were fruitless. From what Bobby had told them, a huge dog had leaped out from a red station wagon in the supermarket’s parking lot. After biting Bobby it vanished. The six-year-old was too concerned with the bites he had received to see where the dog disappeared to. The boy’s story was garbled, but he did remember that the animal was gray and had a collar. There was little tangible* evidence to go on, but the police remained sanguine.

exercises.text: The sermon was ________________ enough to bring tears to the brash* delinquent’s eyes.
Although the message was ________________ , its salient* points were clear enough.
After a ________________ attempt to wrest* control of the government, the traitors were incarcerated.
Even though his boat was almost ________________ , the skipper was loath* to radio for help.
Because the malignancy* had gone unchecked, the surgeons were not ________________ about the patient’s chances.

idiom.meaning: grow rich by taking advantage of circumstances
idiom.example: While working as the tax collector, he adroitly* feathered his own nest.

days.passageZh: 与此同时，哈特夫妇通知当地电台，向狗主人发出感人呼吁，呼吁其站出来。电台接到电话，但所有线索都毫无结果。据鲍比说，一只巨大的狗从超市停车场的红色旅行车上跳了出来。咬了鲍比后，它消失了。六岁的男孩太在意被咬伤了，没注意狗狗去了哪里。男孩的故事有些含糊，但他记得那只动物是灰色的，戴着项圈。几乎没有具体的证据可依据，但警方依然保持乐观。

days.title: NO RELIEF

words.definition: calm, hard to rouse to action

examples.en: The normally phlegmatic Jerry Hart was deeply upset.
examples.zh: 一向冷静的杰瑞·哈特深感难过。

examples.en: Harriet’s egregious* error disturbed even her phlegmatic employer.
examples.zh: 哈丽雅特的严重错误甚至让她那冷静的雇主感到不安。

examples.en: a phlegmatic constitution
examples.zh: 一部冷静的宪法

examples.en: At the suggestion of some friends, he organized a comprehensive search party, zealously fanning out in circles around the supermarket.
examples.zh: 在朋友建议下，他组织了一支全面的搜寻队，热情地在超市周围分开搜索。

examples.en: In order to get the job, you had to go through the drudgery* of filling out a ten-page comprehensive questionnaire.
examples.zh: 为了获得这份工作，你必须经历填写一份十页的综合问卷这一繁琐过程。

examples.en: When there are diametrically opposing views on a big issue that concerns millions of people, doing comprehensive research just makes sense.
examples.zh: 当对数百万人关心的重大问题存在截然相反的观点时，进行全面研究是理所当然的。

days.passage: The normally phlegmatic Jerry Hart was deeply upset. Twenty-four hours had passed without result, and even if the rabies could not be corroborated, Jerry was determined to see that his son received the vaccine. At the suggestion of some friends, he organized a comprehensive search party, zealously fanning out in circles around the supermarket. They knocked on every door, inspected every dog, and came back empty-handed. Although the Harts were sick with worry (they had to be coerced into going to sleep), little Bobby seemed to be in great spirits. The excruciating* vigil continued.

exercises.text: Harriet’s egregious* error disturbed even her ________________ employer.
The fund raiser was so ________________ that he solicited* money from a Salvation Army Santa Claus.
In order to get the job, you had to go through the drudgery* of filling out a ten-page ________________ questionnaire.
The elusive* fugitive was ________________ by his attorney into surrendering.
Even the swindler’s nefarious* accomplice refused to ________________ his alibi.

options.text: calm, hard to rouse to action

exercises.correctDefinition: calm, hard to rouse to action

options.text: calm, hard to rouse to action
calm, hard to rouse to action
calm, hard to rouse to action
calm, hard to rouse to action

idiom.meaning: unreliable, they fail one in time of distress
idiom.example: The general was chagrined* to learn that so many of his supposed supporters were actually fair-weather friends.

days.passageZh: 一向冷静的杰瑞·哈特深感难过。二十四小时过去了，依然没有结果，即使无法证实狂犬病，杰瑞也决心让儿子接种疫苗。在朋友建议下，他组织了一支全面的搜寻队，热情地在超市周围分开搜索。他们敲遍了每扇门，检查了每只狗，结果却空手而归。虽然哈特一家担心得不行（他们不得不被强迫睡觉），但小鲍比看起来心情很好。这场令人痛苦的守夜仍在继续。

days.title: THE POLICE FIND THE DOG

examples.en: After a few telephone calls, the apologetic owner was located and directed to bring her muzzled German shepherd to the Hart domicile.
examples.zh: 经过几通电话，找到了那位道歉的主人，并被指示将她那只戴着口套的德国牧羊犬带到哈特家。

examples.en: A man’s domicile is his castle.
examples.zh: 一个人的住所就是他的城堡。

examples.en: The call to jury duty was sent to my legal domicile; too bad I was on vacation at the time.
examples.zh: 陪审团通知被发到了我的法定住所;可惜那时我正在度假。

examples.en: Because the watchman was lax , thievery was rampant* at the warehouse.
examples.zh: 因为守夜人松懈，仓库里盗窃猖獗*。

examples.en: The rules are fairly lax, but you have to know which ones you can bend.
examples.zh: 规则相对宽松，但你必须知道哪些可以灵活。

examples.en: The rope fell lax.
examples.zh: 绳子松了下来。

examples.en: McGraw, admitted that the dog had a sporadic mean streak, but she scoffed* at the idea of rabies.
examples.zh: 麦格劳承认这只狗偶尔会有恶意，但她对狂犬病这个想法嗤之以鼻*。

examples.en: The lackluster* battle was punctuated by sporadic mortar fire.
examples.zh: 这场平淡的战斗被零星的迫击炮火击中。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be sporadic.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“零星”的含义。

days.passage: Forty hours had elapsed before the police work and the publicity paid off. By meticulously checking the registrations of every red station wagon in the neighborhood and then cross-checking dog licenses, the police narrowed the search to four owners. After a few telephone calls, the apologetic owner was located and directed to bring her muzzled German shepherd to the Hart domicile. Bobby identified the dog, and the animal was taken to a veterinary’s clinic to have the necessary tests performed. The lax owner, Mrs. McGraw, admitted that the dog had a sporadic mean streak, but she scoffed* at the idea of rabies. Jerry Hart noticed for the first time in two days that his uneasy feeling had departed.

exercises.text: Inadvertently,* Emma had allowed two months to ________________ before paying her rent.
The lackluster* battle was punctuated by ________________ mortar fire.
A man’s ________________ is his castle.
Because the watchman was ________________ , thievery was rampant* at the warehouse.
The ________________ musician had nothing but disdain* for his disorganized friends.

idiom.meaning: to lead a wild, carefree life
idiom.example: During his teen years, the millionaire avidly* sowed his wild oats.

days.passageZh: 四十小时后，警方工作和宣传才见效。警方通过仔细核查社区内每辆红色旅行车的登记，并交叉核对狗狗执照，将搜索范围缩小到四名车主。经过几通电话，找到了那位道歉的主人，并被指示将她那只戴着口套的德国牧羊犬带到哈特家。鲍比认出了这只狗，动物被带到兽医诊所进行必要的检查。松懈的主人麦格劳夫人承认这只狗偶尔会有凶狠的性格，但她对狂犬病的想法嗤之以鼻*。杰瑞·哈特两天来第一次注意到，他那种不安的感觉已经消失了。

days.title: ALL’S WELL THAT ENDS WELL

examples.en: The Harts were greatly relieved to learn that the rash conjecture about the dog was not true.
examples.zh: 哈特夫妇得知关于狗的鲁莽猜测并非真实，感到非常宽慰。

examples.en: It was exceedingly rash of the lightweight to insult the belligerent* longshoreman.
examples.zh: 轻量级者侮辱这位好斗的码头工人实在太鲁莽了。

examples.en: rash words spoken in the heat of debate
examples.zh: 在激烈辩论中说出的鲁莽言论

words.definition: Acting too quickly without considering the risks and consequences; not careful; hasty.

examples.en: The Harts were greatly relieved to learn that the rash conjecture about the dog was not true.
examples.zh: 哈特夫妇得知关于狗的鲁莽猜测并非真实，感到非常宽慰。

examples.en: It was sheer conjecture on the detective’s part but it led to the arrest of the vexatious* counterfeiters.
examples.zh: 这只是侦探的纯属猜测，但最终导致了那些恶意*伪钞犯的逮捕。

examples.en: I explained it, but it is pure conjecture whether he understood, or not.
examples.zh: 我解释过，但他是否理解纯属猜测。

examples.en: The neighbors searched for other lurid happenings, and Jerry Hart went back to his office.
examples.zh: 邻居们寻找其他骇人听闻的事件，杰瑞·哈特回到了他的办公室。

examples.en: The newspaper switched from mundane* coverage to lurid reporting.
examples.zh: 报纸从平凡*的报道转向耸人听闻的报道。

examples.en: The accident was described with lurid detail.
examples.zh: 事故被详细描述。

examples.en: “Oh, his bark was worse than his bite,” quipped Jerry.
examples.zh: “哦，他的吠声比咬人还难听，”杰瑞打趣道。

examples.en: Hamlet remembered that Yorick was always ready with a lusty quip .
examples.zh: 哈姆雷特记得约里克总是准备好用充满欲望的俏皮话语。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be quip.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了什么是“俏皮”。

days.passage: The Harts were greatly relieved to learn that the rash conjecture about the dog was not true. Because the German shepherd was not rabid, the necessity for the painful treatment was obviated. The police gave the dog’s owner a summons for allowing the animal to go unmuzzled. Little Bobby was treated to an ice cream sundae and a Walt Disney double feature. The neighbors searched for other lurid happenings, and Jerry Hart went back to his office. “What kind of dog was that?” his secretary asked. “Oh, his bark was worse than his bite,” quipped Jerry.

exercises.text: It was sheer ________________ on the detective’s part but it led to the arrest of the vexatious* counterfeiters.
The newspaper switched from mundane* coverage to ________________ reporting.
It was exceedingly ________________ of the lightweight to insult the belligerent* longshoreman.
The necessity for preparing sandwiches was ________________ when the picnic was postponed.
Hamlet remembered that Yorick was always ready with a lusty ________________ .

idiom.meaning: unexpected financial gain
idiom.example: When the bankrupt company struck oil, the surprised investor received a windfall of $20,000.

days.passageZh: 哈特夫妇得知关于狗的鲁莽猜测并非真实，感到非常宽慰。由于德国牧羊犬没有狂犬病，因此无需进行这种痛苦的治疗。警方因允许狗未戴口套而向狗主人发出传票。小鲍比享用了冰淇淋圣代和沃尔特·迪士尼双片放映。邻居们寻找其他骇人听闻的事件，杰瑞·哈特回到了他的办公室。“那是什么狗？”他的秘书问道。“哦，他的吠声比咬人还难听，”杰瑞打趣道。

reviewOptions.text: provide for oneself at the expense of others

days.story:
“I pledge allegiance to the flag of the United States of America and to the republic for Richard Sands.”

“Deliver us from evil. Lead us not into Penn Station.”

Teachers who train students to memorize and then do rote recitations sometimes find that the youngsters have a ______ interpretation of the actual words. Eliza Berman, an educator who is ______ about her own use of language, invited colleagues to send her examples of confusion in students’ writings. Little did she realize that they would quickly ______ her letterbox with their pet mistakes. As a result, Ms. Berman was able to compile a fairly ______ list of howlers that include the following:

“The inhabitants of ancient Egypt were called Mummies. They lived in the Sarah Dessert and traveled by Camelot.”

“Homer wrote The Oddity in which Penelope was the first hardship Ulysses endured on his journey.”

“Socrates died from an overdose of wedlock.”

“King Alfred conquered the Dames.”

“Indian squabs carried porpoises on their backs.”

“Under the Constitution, the people enjoy the right to keep bare arms.”

“In the Olympic Games, the Greeks ran, jumped, hurled the bisquits and threw the java.”

“Lincoln was America’s greatest Precedent.”

Ms. Berman is not too ______ about eliminating such errors from pupils’ compositions and test papers. Her advice: enjoy!
days.storyZh:
“我向美利坚合众国国旗和共和国宣誓效忠理查德·桑兹。”

“救我们脱离邪恶。不要带我们进宾夕法尼亚车站。”

那些训练学生记忆然后背诵的老师，有时会发现孩子们对实际单词有______的理解。伊莱扎·伯曼是一位对自己语言使用______的教育者，她邀请同事们分享学生写作中出现的困惑例子。她没想到他们会很快______她的信箱，装满他们的宠物错误。因此，伯曼女士能够整理出一份相当______的咆哮信清单，其中包括以下内容：

“古埃及的居民被称为木乃伊。他们住在莎拉沙漠，乘坐卡美洛旅行。”

“荷马写了《奇异之谜》，其中佩涅洛普是尤利西斯旅途中经历的第一个艰难。”

“苏格拉底死于婚姻过量。”

“阿尔弗雷德国王征服了贵妇们。”

“印第安乳鸽背着鼠海豚。”

“根据宪法，人民享有持有裸枪的权利。”

“在奥运会上，希腊人跑、跳、投掷bisquits和掷java。”

“林肯是美国最伟大的先例。”

伯曼女士对消除学生作文和试卷中的这些错误并不______。她的建议是：享受吧！

days.title: OFF BROADWAY

examples.en: Foy realized that Monte was obsessed* with the idea, he became quite serious, launching into a diatribe on the importance of responsibility in the younger generation.
examples.zh: 福伊意识到蒙特对这个想法着迷*，他变得相当严肃，开始长篇大论，强调年轻一代责任的重要性。

examples.en: When a large expenditure is imminent,* my father goes into a long diatribe on the need for economy.
examples.zh: 当一笔巨额开支迫在眉睫时，我父亲会长篇大论地强调节约的必要性。

examples.en: The senator went into a lengthy diatribe about government waste in the military budget.
examples.zh: 参议员对政府军费浪费进行了长篇抨击。

words.phonetic: in´ ə bish´ ən or in´ hi bish´ ən

examples.en: Now a fortuitous opportunity to get into show business required Monte to make an immediate decision.
examples.zh: 现在，一个进入演艺圈的幸运机会迫使蒙特做出了立即决定。

examples.en: The meeting was a fortuitous one, but the jealous husband construed* it as pre-arranged and clandestine.*
examples.zh: 这次相遇是偶然的，但嫉妒的丈夫却认为这是事先安排好的秘密行动。*

examples.en: I got my job as a result of a fortuitous meeting with the director of personnel.
examples.zh: 我之所以得到这份工作，是因为一次偶然与人事主管的相遇。

examples.en: Foy stormed out of the office, muttering incoherently about hippies, beatniks, and others of that ilk.
examples.zh: 福伊怒气冲冲地走出办公室，嘴里含糊地嘟囔着嬉皮士、垮掉的一代和其他类似的人。

examples.en: It is often fruitless* to argue with racists, bigots*, and others of that ilk .
examples.zh: 与种族主义者、偏执者*以及类似的人争论往往是徒劳的。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be ilk.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“同类”的含义。

days.passage: When Monte Ziltch told his boss, Mr. Foy, that he was quitting as an accountant to become an actor, the man was convulsed with laughter. After Mr. Foy realized that Monte was obsessed* with the idea, he became quite serious, launching into a diatribe on the importance of responsibility in the younger generation. Monte confessed that he had been developing ulcers as an accountant, and when his psychiatrist suggested that the sickness was a result of inhibitions, Monte agreed. Now a fortuitous opportunity to get into show business required Monte to make an immediate decision. Mr. Foy stormed out of the office, muttering incoherently about hippies, beatniks, and others of that ilk.

exercises.text: When a large expenditure is imminent,* my father goes into a long ________________ on the need for economy.
It is often fruitless* to argue with racists, bigots*, and others of that ________________ .
Since the patient’s speech was garbled* and ________________ , we could only conjecture* as to his message.
The meeting was a ________________ one, but the jealous husband construed* it as pre-arranged and clandestine.*
After two drinks the usually phlegmatic* dentist lost all his ________________ .

idiom.phrase: to wear one’s heart on one’s sleeve
idiom.meaning: to make one’s feelings evident
idiom.example: People who wear their hearts on their sleeves frequently suffer emotional upsets.

days.passageZh: 当蒙特·齐尔奇告诉他的老板福伊先生他要辞去会计工作去当演员时，福伊笑得前仰后合。福伊先生意识到蒙特对这个想法着迷*后，变得相当严肃，开始长篇大论，强调年轻一代责任的重要性。蒙特坦言自己作为会计师时开始出现溃疡，当精神科医生认为这种病是抑制导致的时，蒙特同意了。现在，一个进入演艺圈的幸运机会迫使蒙特做出了立即决定。福伊先生怒气冲冲地离开了办公室，嘴里含糊地嘟囔着嬉皮士、垮掉一代和其他类似的人。

days.title: AN ALL-ROUND MAN

examples.en: The need for a decision came about when Monte was invited to join a prestigious summer stock company, starting in mid-June.
examples.zh: 决定的必要性始于蒙特受邀加入一家著名的夏季剧团，该剧团将于六月中旬开始。

examples.en: A salient* feature of the prestigious company’s success was its fair treatment of employees.
examples.zh: 这家享有盛誉的公司成功的一个显著特点是对员工的公平对待。

examples.en: My brother was promoted to a prestigious job in his company.
examples.zh: 我哥哥在公司里升职了。

examples.en: Since the company would stage five major plays during the summer, as well as a half-dozen shows for children, there was a chance that Monte might actually get a part before too many weeks had elapsed.* In addition, he would be attending the drama classes that were an integral part of the summer theater.
examples.zh: 由于剧团将在夏季上演五部主要戏剧，以及六场儿童剧目，蒙特有可能在太多周内就获得角色。* 此外，他还将参加夏季剧场不可或缺的戏剧课程。

examples.en: She seemed to be an unimportant member of the president’s entourage* but actually she played an integral role in White House affairs.
examples.zh: 她看似是总统随行人员中不重要的一员*，但实际上她在白宫事务中扮演了不可或缺的角色。

examples.en: The bookkeeper is such an integral part of our organization that we pay her a very high salary.
examples.zh: 簿记员是我们组织不可或缺的一部分，我们给她很高的薪水。

examples.en: The remuneration would be nominal but at last Monte Ziltch would be fulfilling a life-long ambition.
examples.zh: 报酬虽然很低，但最终蒙特·齐尔奇实现了毕生的梦想。

examples.en: Derek Jeter’s remuneration from the New York Yankees made him a millionaire many times over.
examples.zh: 德里克·基特从纽约洋基队获得的报酬让他多次成为百万富翁。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be remuneration.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了报酬的含义。

examples.en: The remuneration would be nominal but at last Monte Ziltch would be fulfilling a life-long ambition.
examples.zh: 报酬虽然很低，但最终蒙特·齐尔奇实现了毕生的梦想。

examples.en: For allowing his ferocious mastiff* to appear on a commercial, the trainer was paid a nominal sum.
examples.zh: 因为让他凶猛的獒犬*出现在广告中，训犬师获得了象征性的报酬。

examples.en: This sentence contains two nominals.
examples.zh: 该句包含两个名词。

days.passage: The need for a decision came about when Monte was invited to join a prestigious summer stock company, starting in mid-June. As a mature “apprentice,” he would be required to take tickets, paint scenery, prepare placards, assist with lighting, costumes, and props, and carry an occasional spear in a walk-on role. Since the company would stage five major plays during the summer, as well as a half-dozen shows for children, there was a chance that Monte might actually get a part before too many weeks had elapsed.* In addition, he would be attending the drama classes that were an integral part of the summer theater. The remuneration would be nominal but at last Monte Ziltch would be fulfilling a life-long ambition.

exercises.text: The police posted a ________________ asking all citizens to desist* from looting.
A salient* feature of the ________________ company’s success was its fair treatment of employees.
Derek Jeter’s ________________ from the New York Yankees made him a millionaire many times over.
For allowing his ferocious mastiff* to appear on a commercial, the trainer was paid a ________________ sum.
She seemed to be an unimportant member of the president’s entourage* but actually she played an ________________ role in White House affairs.

idiom.phrase: to wash dirty linen in public
idiom.meaning: to openly discuss private affairs
idiom.example: “Let’s talk about it privately,” his uncle said, “rather than wash our dirty linen in public.”

days.passageZh: 决定的必要性始于蒙特受邀加入一家著名的夏季剧团，该剧团将于六月中旬开始。作为一名成熟的“学徒”，他需要领票、绘制布景、准备标语牌、协助灯光、服装和道具，偶尔还需携带长矛作为客串角色。由于剧团将在夏季上演五部主要戏剧，以及六场儿童剧目，蒙特有可能在太多周内就获得角色。* 此外，他还将参加夏季剧场不可或缺的戏剧课程。报酬虽然很低，但最终蒙特·齐尔奇实现了毕生的梦想。

days.title: FROM LEDGERS TO SCRIPTS

examples.en: He had expunged all traces of debits and credits from his mind, burying himself in the more flamboyant world of the theater.
examples.zh: 他已经把所有借方和信用从脑海中抹去，把自己埋进了剧院那更为华丽的世界里。

examples.en: Our theatrical pages were inundated* with press releases from the flamboyant producer.
examples.zh: 我们的院线版面被这位张扬制片人的新闻稿淹没*。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be flamboyant.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是张扬。

examples.en: Accounting became anathema to him as the schism between his present utopia and his former drudgery* widened.
examples.zh: 随着他当前乌托邦与过去苦差事的分裂加深，会计对他来说变得不可接受。

examples.en: The traitor’s name was anathema in his father’s domicile.*
examples.zh: 叛徒的名字在他父亲的住所里是禁忌。*

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be anathema.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是“诅咒者”。

examples.en: Accounting became anathema to him as the schism between his present utopia and his former drudgery* widened.
examples.zh: 随着他当前乌托邦与过去苦差事的分裂加深，会计对他来说变得不可接受。

examples.en: A pernicious* schism developed between the two sisters.
examples.zh: 两姐妹之间出现了严重的*分裂。

examples.en: The schism between Sunnis and Shias happened quite early in Islamic history.
examples.zh: 逊尼派与什叶派之间的分裂发生在伊斯兰历史早期。

examples.en: Accounting became anathema to him as the schism between his present utopia and his former drudgery* widened.
examples.zh: 随着他当前乌托邦与过去苦差事的分裂加深，会计对他来说变得不可接受。

examples.en: In Lost Horizon a character recoiled* at the idea of living in a utopia .
examples.zh: 在《失落的地平线》中，有个角色对生活在乌托邦的想法感到反感*。

examples.en: Most reformers are seeking to create a utopia.
examples.zh: 大多数改革者都在寻求创造乌托邦。

days.passage: During the first weeks of the summer, Monte Ziltch didn’t even have time to consider whether he had made an egregious* mistake. He was too engrossed* with his work, performing a thousand and one odd jobs around the theater. First there was the opening production of A Chorus Line, then two weeks of The Fantasticks, followed by a poignant* Diary of Anne Frank, which did excellent business. All through those weeks, Monte painted, carried, nailed, collected, ran, studied, and perspired. He had expunged all traces of debits and credits from his mind, burying himself in the more flamboyant world of the theater. Accounting became anathema to him as the schism between his present utopia and his former drudgery* widened.

exercises.text: In Lost Horizon a character recoiled* at the idea of living in a ________________ .
A pernicious* ________________ developed between the two sisters.
The traitor’s name was ________________ in his father’s domicile.*
Our theatrical pages were inundated* with press releases from the ________________ producer.
After having made the rash* statements, the senator wished that he could ________________ them from the record.

idiom.meaning: to avoid disgrace
idiom.example: Instead of firing the corrupt executive, they allowed him to retire in order that he might save face.

days.passageZh: 夏天的最初几周，蒙特·齐尔奇甚至没时间考虑自己是否犯了严重的*错误。他太专注于工作了，在剧院里做了一千零一件事。首先是《合唱线》的开幕演出，接着是两周的《奇幻家族》，接着是感人至深的《安妮·弗兰克日记》，票房非常好。在那几周里，蒙特画画、搬运、钉钉、收集、奔跑、学习和汗水流淌。他已经把所有借方和信用从脑海中抹去，把自己埋进了剧院那更为华丽的世界里。随着他当前乌托邦与过去苦差事的分裂加深，会计对他来说变得不可接受。

days.title: IRONY FOR MERRYWEATHER

examples.en: He had played the timorous Lion in a truncated version of “The Wizard of Oz,” which the apprentices had staged.
examples.zh: 他曾在学徒们上演的《绿野仙踪》中一个被删减版的狮子。

examples.en: Under duress* the normally timorous husband was coerced* into demanding a raise.
examples.zh: 在压力下，这位一向胆小的丈夫被迫*要求加薪。

examples.en: Anyone so timorous should not have been selected to guard the castle.
examples.zh: 如此胆小的人不该被选来守卫城堡。

examples.en: He had played the timorous Lion in a truncated version of “The Wizard of Oz,” which the apprentices had staged.
examples.zh: 他曾在学徒们上演的《绿野仙踪》中一个被删减版的狮子。

examples.en: It is frustrating* to have one’s lengthy remarks printed in truncated form.
examples.zh: 长篇发言被截断印刷，令人沮丧*。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be truncated.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被截断的含义。

examples.en: It was to be a jaunty original comedy, given a summer tryout prior to a Broadway opening.
examples.zh: 这是一部轻快的原创喜剧，在百老汇开幕前安排了夏季试演。

examples.en: With his cap set at a jaunty angle, the amicable* sailor strutted down the street.
examples.zh: 帽子歪斜地戴着，这位和蔼的水手昂首阔步地走在街上。

examples.en: Although his speech was jaunty we were able to sense its underlying seriousness.
examples.zh: 虽然他的演讲语气轻快，但我们能感受到他内在的严肃。

examples.en: Monte, who by now had adopted the stage name of Monte Merryweather, read for the producers, hoping to get the part of the hero’s fractious landlord.
examples.zh: 此时已采用艺名蒙特·梅里韦瑟的蒙特为制片人试镜，希望能获得主角那个多变房东的角色。

examples.en: Roger’s fractious behavior compounded* the bad relationship he had already had with his partner.
examples.zh: 罗杰的矛盾行为加剧了他与搭档本已存在的糟糕关系。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be fractious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了分裂的含义。

examples.en: Unfortunately, the competition was too rough—but the director assigned Monte to a less ostentatious part.
examples.zh: 遗憾的是，竞争过于激烈——但导演还是给蒙特安排了一个不那么张扬的角色。

examples.en: In an ostentatious display of histrionics* the star refused to perform.
examples.zh: 在一场夸张的表演中，这位明星拒绝了表演。

examples.en: Lorraine was criticized sharply for the ostentatious way in which she furnished her apartment.
examples.zh: 洛琳因布置公寓的奢华方式而受到严厉批评。

days.passage: At last, Monte’s chance to perform came. He had played the timorous Lion in a truncated version of “The Wizard of Oz,” which the apprentices had staged. But now there was an open audition to cast the final show of the season. It was to be a jaunty original comedy, given a summer tryout prior to a Broadway opening. Monte, who by now had adopted the stage name of Monte Merryweather, read for the producers, hoping to get the part of the hero’s fractious landlord. Unfortunately, the competition was too rough—but the director assigned Monte to a less ostentatious part. And so for the first two weeks in September the stage-struck accountant had a two-minute, two-line part. What was his role? The hero’s accountant!

exercises.text: It is frustrating* to have one’s lengthy remarks printed in ________________ form.
With his cap set at a ________________ angle, the amicable* sailor strutted down the street.
In an ________________ display of histrionics* the star refused to perform.
Under duress* the normally ________________ husband was coerced* into demanding a raise.
Roger’s ________________ behavior compounded* the bad relationship he had already had with his partner.

idiom.meaning: warm autumn weather
idiom.example: Parts of the country were deep in snow, but the East was enjoying an Indian summer.

days.passageZh: 终于，蒙特有机会表演。他曾在学徒们上演的《绿野仙踪》中一个被删减版的狮子。但现在有公开试镜，选出本季最后一集的选角。这是一部轻快的原创喜剧，在百老汇开幕前安排了夏季试演。此时已采用艺名蒙特·梅里韦瑟的蒙特为制片人试镜，希望能获得主角那个多变房东的角色。遗憾的是，竞争过于激烈——但导演还是给蒙特安排了一个不那么张扬的角色。因此，九月的前两周，这位舞台迷的会计师有一个两分钟、两句台词的角色。他的角色是什么？英雄的会计！
