days.story:
The television talk shows of our era, featuring such ______ public figures as Regis Philbin, Oprah Winfrey, and Ellen De Generes, attract millions of daytime viewers and constitute a powerful influence on the American scene. When the media can hold the attention of so sizable a chunk of couch potatoes, it pays to scrutinize it closely.

A student at Stanford University, doing her doctoral thesis on the unusual popularity of the afternoon talk shows, noted the fierce competition among those programs for guests who are off the beaten track. According to her:

“Almost every irregular, ______ life-style you can think of has already been featured on one of the shows and probably on all of them, when you add Montel Williams, Jerry Springer, and others of that ______ who serve as network hosts. They have shown teenagers who marry people in their sixties, daughters and mothers who date the same man, men who have gone through a marriage ceremony with other men, women with prominent tattoos, and other people who are totally free of ______ .”

“ ______ for our guests is so small,” said a producer, “that these shows are inexpensive to put on. And say what you want about good taste, millions watch us every day, and as long as the ratings are that healthy, sponsors will pay good money to be identified with us.”
days.storyZh:
我们这个时代的电视脱口秀节目，邀请了雷吉斯·菲尔宾、奥普拉·温弗瑞和艾伦·德·杰尼勒斯等______公众人物，吸引了数百万白天观众，对美国舞台产生了强大的影响。当媒体能吸引这么多沙发无聊者的注意力时，仔细审视它就值得。

斯坦福大学一位学生正在撰写关于下午脱口秀异常受欢迎的博士论文，她注意到这些节目之间对那些偏离主流的嘉宾竞争异常激烈。据她说：

“你能想到的几乎所有不规律、______生活方式都曾在某个节目中出现过，甚至可能所有节目都有出现，再加上蒙特尔·威廉姆斯、杰瑞·斯普林格以及其他担任网络主持人的______。他们展示了六十多岁的青少年结婚，女儿和母亲与同一个男人约会，男性与其他男性举行婚礼，女性身上有显著纹身，以及完全不受______影响的人。”

一位制片人说：“我们______客人的规模非常小，所以这些演出的费用很低廉。无论你怎么说品味，每天都有数百万人观看我们，只要收视率如此高涨，赞助商愿意花大价钱与我们建立联系。”

days.title: A VISIT TO THE PRESIDENT

words.phonetic: in´ kon trə vėr´ tə bəl

examples.en: The scientists were alarmed by incontrovertible evidence of surreptitious German experiments, and they asked for speedy approval.
examples.zh: 科学家们对德国秘密实验的确凿证据感到震惊，并请求尽快批准。

examples.en: The expert’s incontrovertible testimony corroborated* the police report.
examples.zh: 专家无可辩驳的证词证词证实了警方报告。

examples.en: Her statement that Hitler was evil is incontrovertible.
examples.zh: 她说希特勒是邪恶的，这一点无可辩驳。

examples.en: The scientists were alarmed by incontrovertible evidence of surreptitious German experiments, and they asked for speedy approval.
examples.zh: 科学家们对德国秘密实验的确凿证据感到震惊，并请求尽快批准。

examples.en: With a surreptitious movement, the meticulous* bookkeeper emptied the ash tray.
examples.zh: 细致入微的簿记员悄悄地把烟灰缸清空。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be surreptitious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是秘密行动。

examples.en: Fermi had found a haven from the Fascists (his wife was Jewish) and he knew that if the Germans were the first to develop an atomic bomb it would mean that Hitler could subjugate the entire world.
examples.zh: 费米找到了逃避法西斯的避难所（他的妻子是犹太人），他知道如果德国人是第一个研发原子弹的人，那就意味着希特勒可以征服全世界。

examples.en: In inclement* weather our barn is a haven for many animals.
examples.zh: 在恶劣天气下，我们的谷仓是许多动物的避风港。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be haven.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是避难所。

examples.en: Fermi had found a haven from the Fascists (his wife was Jewish) and he knew that if the Germans were the first to develop an atomic bomb it would mean that Hitler could subjugate the entire world.
examples.zh: 费米找到了逃避法西斯的避难所（他的妻子是犹太人），他知道如果德国人是第一个研发原子弹的人，那就意味着希特勒可以征服全世界。

examples.en: The dictator used duplicity* in order to subjugate his rivals.
examples.zh: 独裁者利用欺骗手段*来压制他的对手。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be subjugate.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了被征服的含义。

days.passage: In the winter of 1941, Enrico Fermi and a number of other distinguished scientists importuned President Franklin Roosevelt for authorization to begin an all-out effort in atomic energy research. The scientists were alarmed by incontrovertible evidence of surreptitious German experiments, and they asked for speedy approval. Italian-born Enrico Fermi was the ideal man to lead the atomic research. Already in 1938 he had won the Nobel Prize for work with radioactive elements and neutron bombardment. Fermi had found a haven from the Fascists (his wife was Jewish) and he knew that if the Germans were the first to develop an atomic bomb it would mean that Hitler could subjugate the entire world. The international race for atomic supremacy was on.

exercises.text: Although Eddie was not sanguine* about his chances, he continued to ________________ his boss for a winter vacation.
In inclement* weather our barn is a ________________ for many animals.
The dictator used duplicity* in order to ________________ his rivals.
With a ________________ movement, the meticulous* bookkeeper emptied the ash tray.
The expert’s ________________ testimony corroborated* the police report.

idiom.phrase: to take the bull by the horns
idiom.meaning: to face a problem directly
idiom.example: After several days of delay, the minister decided to take the bull by the horns, and so he sent for the vandals.

days.passageZh: 1941年冬天，恩里科·费米和其他几位杰出科学家恳请富兰克林·罗斯福总统批准全面开展原子能研究。科学家们对德国秘密实验的确凿证据感到震惊，并请求尽快批准。意大利出生的恩里科·费米是领导原子研究的理想人选。早在1938年，他就因放射性元素和中子轰击工作获得了诺贝尔奖。费米找到了逃避法西斯的避难所（他的妻子是犹太人），他知道如果德国人是第一个研发原子弹的人，那就意味着希特勒可以征服全世界。国际原子霸权竞赛开始了。

days.title: THE ULTIMATE WEAPON TAKES SHAPE

examples.en: Gaining better housing for all was the ultimate goal of the zealous* reformer.
examples.zh: 为所有人争取更好的住房是这位狂热改革者的最终目标。

examples.en: the ultimate disappointment
examples.zh: 终极的失望

examples.en: an ultimate constituent of matter
examples.zh: 物质的终极组成部分

examples.en: Enrico Fermi designed a device that could eventuate in a chain reaction.
examples.zh: 恩里科·费米设计了一种可能引发连锁反应的装置。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be eventuate.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“被终结”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "eventuate" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“最终”一词。

examples.en: The uranium emitted neutrons, and the graphite slowed them down.
examples.zh: 铀发射中子，石墨减缓了中子速度。

examples.en: The mayor issued a diatribe* against companies whose smokestacks emit poisonous fumes.
examples.zh: 市长发表了一篇抨击烟囱排放有毒烟雾的公司。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be emit.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“发出”的含义。

examples.en: Slowly, Fermi’s first atomic pile began to grow in a subterranean room at Columbia University.
examples.zh: 费米的第一个原子堆慢慢地在哥伦比亚大学的地下室中逐渐生长。

examples.en: After two years of confinement in a subterranean dungeon, the prisoner was thin and wan.*
examples.zh: 在地下地牢被囚禁两年后，囚犯瘦弱憔悴。*

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be subterranean.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“地下生活”的含义。

examples.en: The big question remained—was it viable?
examples.zh: 最大的问题是——它是否可行？

examples.en: When the schism* in the company was healed, a viable arrangement was worked out.
examples.zh: 当公司内部的分裂*得到修复后，达成了可行的安排。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be viable.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是可行的。

days.passage: Enrico Fermi designed a device that could eventuate in a chain reaction. It consisted of layers of graphite, alternated with chunks of uranium. The uranium emitted neutrons, and the graphite slowed them down. Holes were left for long cadmium safety rods. By withdrawing those control rods Fermi could speed up the production of neutrons, thus increasing the number of uranium atoms that would be split (fission). When the rods were withdrawn to a critical point, then the neutrons would be produced so fast that the graphite and cadmium could not absorb them. In that manner a chain reaction would result. Slowly, Fermi’s first atomic pile began to grow in a subterranean room at Columbia University. The big question remained—was it viable?

exercises.text: A thorough investigation ________________ in a comprehensive* report.
After two years of confinement in a ________________ dungeon, the prisoner was thin and wan.*
The mayor issued a diatribe* against companies whose smokestacks ________________ poisonous fumes.
Gaining better housing for all was the ________________ goal of the zealous* reformer.
When the schism* in the company was healed, a ________________ arrangement was worked out.

idiom.meaning: the major portion
idiom.example: Because the salesman was essential to the business, he demanded the lion’s share of the profits.

days.passageZh: 恩里科·费米设计了一种可能引发连锁反应的装置。它由石墨层和铀块交替组成。铀发射中子，石墨减缓了中子速度。为长镉安全杆留有孔洞。通过移除这些控制棒，费米可以加快中子的产生速度，从而增加被分裂（裂变）的铀原子数量。当棒被拉到临界点时，中子产生的速度极快，石墨和镉无法吸收。这样一来，就会引发连锁反应。费米的第一个原子堆慢慢地在哥伦比亚大学的地下室中逐渐生长。最大的问题是——它是否可行？

days.title: THE SQUASH COURT EXPERIMENT

examples.en: His pace accelerated because they were proceeding on the premise that the Germans were close to atomic success.
examples.zh: 他的进度加快是因为他们基于德国接近原子弹成功的前提进行。

examples.en: Acting on the premise that there were no burglars around, the police became quite lax.*
examples.zh: 警方以没有窃贼为前提，采取了相当松懈的态度。*

examples.en: trespass on another’s premises
examples.zh: 擅自进入他人场所

examples.en: Almost fifty scientists and several incredulous observers mounted a balcony to watch.
examples.zh: 近五十名科学家和几位难以置信的观察者登上阳台观看。

examples.en: After I had perused* the Yankee lineup, I was incredulous about their chances of winning.
examples.zh: 在我浏览完洋基队的阵容后，我对他们获胜的机会感到难以置信。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be incredulous.

days.passage: As the pile grew, so did the entire project. Fermi moved his materials to an abandoned squash court under a football stadium at the University of Chicago. His pace accelerated because they were proceeding on the premise that the Germans were close to atomic success. Six weeks after the pile had been started, its critical size was reached. Three brave young men jeopardized their lives by ascending* the pile, ready to cover it with liquid cadmium if anything went wrong. Almost fifty scientists and several incredulous observers mounted a balcony to watch. One physicist remained on the floor; it was his job to extract the final cadmium control rod. Unbearable tension permeated the atmosphere. Fermi completed his calculations, waited for a propitious moment, and then gave the signal.

days.passageZh: 随着堆积，整个项目也随之增长。费米将他的材料搬到了芝加哥大学一座橄榄球场下方的废弃壁球场。他的进度加快是因为他们基于德国接近原子弹成功的前提进行。堆栈启动六周后，达到临界规模。三个勇敢的年轻人冒着生命危险爬上堆，准备在出事时用液态镉覆盖。近五十名科学家和几位难以置信的观察者登上阳台观看。一名物理学家留在大楼上;他的任务是提取最后一根镉控制棒。难以忍受的紧张气氛弥漫在空气中。费米完成计算，等待时机成熟，然后发出信号。

days.passage: The chain reaction took place precisely as Enrico Fermi had surmised. After twenty-eight minutes he curtailed the experiment, giving the signal to replace the control rod. The normally reserved scientists, unable to repress their excitement, let out a tremendous cheer and gathered around Fermi to shake his hand. Although it was time to celebrate, some of the men remarked soberly that “the world would never be the same again.” On December 2, 1942, the news of Fermi’s achievement was relayed in a cryptic telephone message:

days.story:
The ______ message came to Officer Matt Jagusak: “Drug search tomorrow—bring pig.”

Jagusak, with the Union County New Jersey Sheriff’s Department Search and Rescue Unit, had to ______ his superiors to put Ferris E. Lucas, a super sniffer, to work. Lucas is a Vietnamese pot-bellied pig with a fantastic olfactory sense that is one million times greater than a human’s and could be our ______ weapon in breaking up the drug trade.

A canine trainer offered the pig to Union City, suggesting that its intelligence and unique skill will make Lucas a ______ fighter against illegal narcotics. Jagusak has already taught his 55-pound porker-detective how to find cocaine, hashish, and marijuana. While some law enforcement officials were ______ at first, they quickly became believers when they saw the Sherlock Holmes of the sty locate underground drug scents that had eluded trained dogs.

“I don’t care if it’s a dog, a pig, or an elephant,” Jagusak’s boss said. “If it benefits the department and our community, we’ll try it.”
days.storyZh:
______消息传给警官马特·贾古萨克：“明天进行毒品搜查——带上猪。”

贾古萨克在新泽西州联合县警长局搜救队工作，不得不______上级让超级嗅探员费里斯·E·卢卡斯工作。卢卡斯是一只越南大肚猪，嗅觉极佳，是人类的百万倍，可能是我们打击毒品交易的首______武器。

一位训犬师将这头猪推荐给联合城，暗示其聪明和独特技能将使卢卡斯成为打击非法毒品的战士______。贾古萨克已经教他那个55磅重的猪警探如何找到可卡因、大麻和大麻。虽然有些执法人员起初很______有信心，但当他们看到“福尔摩斯”在监狱里找到了那些训练有素的狗都无法察觉的地下毒品气味后，很快就信了。

“我不管是狗、猪还是大象，”贾古萨克的老板说。“如果对警局和社区有利，我们就试试。”

days.passage: At breakfast time on Sunday morning, December 7, 1941, Dorie Miller was serving coffee aboard the battleship West Virginia. Dorie was black, and the highest job to which he could then aspire in the U.S. Navy was that of messman. While Dorie was technically a member of a great fighting fleet, he was not expected to fight. Most Army and Navy officers inveighed against blacks as fighting men. Although blacks were nettled by such overt prejudice, Dorie Miller apparently accepted being relegated to the role of a messhall servant. Now, as he poured the coffee, Dorie was wondering why the airplanes above were making so much noise on a peaceful Sunday morning.

days.passageZh: 1941年12月7日星期日早餐时分，多丽·米勒正在战列舰西弗吉尼亚号上供应咖啡。多丽是黑人，他当时在美国海军中能追求的最高职位是杂货员。虽然多莉技术上是一支强大战斗舰队的成员，但他并不被期望参战。大多数陆军和海军军官都抨击黑人是战斗人员。尽管黑人因如此明显的偏见而感到不满，多丽·米勒显然接受了被降格为女仆的身份。现在，他倒咖啡时，多丽在想为什么飞机在一个宁静的星期天早晨会发出这么大的声音。

days.passage: The coffee cups suddenly went spinning as an explosion knocked Dorie Miller flat on his back. Jumping up from his supine position, the powerfully built messman from Waco, Texas, headed for the deck. Everywhere that Dorie looked he saw smoke and mammoth warships lying on their sides. Overhead, dozens of Japanese dive bombers controlled the skies without a U.S. plane to repulse their attack. The havoc was enormous. Without hesitating, Dorie joined a team that was feeding ammunition to a machine gunner who was making an ineffectual* attempt to protect their battleship from being razed by the torpedo planes.

days.passage: Men all around Miller were succumbing* to the lethal spray of Japanese bullets. He dragged his captain to safety and turned back to see that the machine-gunner had been killed. Dorie took the big gun and trained it on the incoming bombers. Within the space of ten minutes he was credited with destroying four bombers while dodging the bullets of their fighter escorts. The enemy scurried away, having struck the incisive blow that precipitated U.S. entrance into World War II. Amidst the dead bodies and the ruined fleet were the heroes such as Dorie Miller. The Navy had told him that he did not have to fight but he hadn’t listened. The Navy had attempted to stereotype him, but Dorie changed all that.

days.passage: Some months later Dorie Miller was serving on an aircraft carrier when Admiral Chester Nimitz, the Commander of the Pacific Fleet, came aboard to preside over a special awards ceremony. In stentorian tones the Admiral presented Miller with the prestigious* Navy Cross, commending him for a singular act of valor and “disregard for his own personal safety.” Miller’s heroism helped to shatter the bias against African-Americans in the armed forces. Although he could have accepted a sinecure at a U.S. naval base, Dorie chose to remain in the combat zone where he was killed in action in December, 1943.

days.passageZh: 几个月后，多丽·米勒号在航空母舰上服役时，太平洋舰队司令切斯特·尼米兹上将登舰主持特别颁奖典礼。上将以洪亮的嗓音授予米勒海军十字勋章，表彰他表现出独特的英勇行为和“无视个人安危”。米勒的英勇事迹帮助打破了军队中对非裔美国人的偏见。尽管他本可以接受美国海军基地的闲职，多里选择留在战区，并于1943年12月阵亡。

reviewOptions.text: to beat someone to the punch

days.storyTitle: Sugar and Spice and Everything Nice
days.story:
Teen Talk Barbie, the best-selling $50 model, has gone a step too far in the opinion of the American Association of University Women. Representatives of that group were ______ to hear that one of the four phrases that the doll is programmed to utter is, “Math class is tough.”

For years the university professors, as well as members of feminist organizations, have ______ against the ______ that portrays girls as weak math and science students. “Because that brainwashing message is conveyed to girls at an early age, they come to accept what we consider to be a blatant ______ ,” said Dr. Ellen Kaner, a Dallas chemist. “We are just beginning to make progress in our campaign to recruit women for challenging, well-paying careers in math and science,” she added, “and were shocked to learn that Barbie is spreading such harmful nonsense.”

Executives of the company that manufactures Teen Talk Barbie had to ______ to set matters right. They admitted that the phrase in question, one of 270 selected by computer chips, was a mistake. In a press release, their president said, “We didn’t fully consider the potentially negative implications of this phrase. Not only will we remove it immediately but will swap with anyone who bought the offending doll.”

We wonder how Ken feels about the matter.
days.storyZh:
Teen Talk Barbie，这款畅销的50美元车型，在美国大学妇女协会看来已经走得太远了。该小组的代表们______听到，娃娃被编程说的四句话之一就是：“数学课很难。”

多年来，大学教授以及女权组织成员一直______反对将女孩描绘成数学和科学弱学生的______。达拉斯化学家艾伦·卡纳博士说：“因为这种洗脑信息从小就传达给女孩，她们会接受我们认为的赤裸裸的______。”她补充道：“我们才刚刚开始在招募女性从事具有挑战性且高薪的数学和科学职业方面取得进展，”她补充道，“得知芭比竟然传播如此有害的胡言乱语，感到震惊。”

制造青少年谈话芭比的公司高管不得不______来纠正局面。他们承认，被计算机芯片选出的270个短语之一，是个错误。他们的主席在一份新闻稿中表示：“我们没有充分考虑这句话可能带来的负面影响。我们不仅会立即移除它，还会与购买该问题娃娃的人交换。”

我们想知道肯对此事的感受。

days.title: DANNY ESCOBEDO GOES TO JAIL

examples.en: Danny had confessed to complicity in the killing of his brother-in-law after the police had refused to allow him to see his lawyer.
examples.zh: 丹尼承认参与了杀害姐夫的行为，因为警方拒绝让他见律师。

examples.en: Proceeding on the premise* that the broker was guilty of complicity in the swindle, the detective followed him surreptitiously.*
examples.zh: 侦探以中介参与诈骗为前提，秘密跟踪他。*

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be complicity.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了共谋的含义。

examples.en: Actually, Danny was tricked into blaming a friend for the liquidation of his sister’s husband, thereby establishing himself as an accomplice.
examples.zh: 实际上，丹尼被骗嫁祸给朋友，导致他姐姐的丈夫被清算，从而确立了自己作为共犯的身份。

examples.en: After the liquidation of the gang leader, a mammoth* conflict arose among his ambitious lieutenants who aspired* to be boss.
examples.zh: 帮派头目被清除后，他那些野心勃勃的副手们爆发了巨大的冲突，他们渴望成为老大。

examples.en: The store is having a liquidation sale: everything must go as they go out of business.
examples.zh: 店里正在进行清仓大甩卖：所有东西都必须在他们倒闭时清仓。

examples.en: Actually, Danny was tricked into blaming a friend for the liquidation of his sister’s husband, thereby establishing himself as an accomplice.
examples.zh: 实际上，丹尼被骗嫁祸给朋友，导致他姐姐的丈夫被清算，从而确立了自己作为共犯的身份。

examples.en: Although he was judged as a minor accomplice , the driver had actually played an integral* part in planning the crime.
examples.zh: 虽然司机被判定为次要共犯，但实际上他在策划犯罪中扮演了不可或缺的角色。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be accomplice.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“共犯”的含义。

examples.en: Despite the fact that Danny later recanted his confession, he was found culpable and jailed.
examples.zh: 尽管丹尼后来撤回了供词，但他仍被判有罪并入狱。

examples.en: Once the incontrovertible* evidence was offered, the servant was held culpable in the theft of the jewels.
examples.zh: 一旦提出无可辩驳的*证据，仆人就被判定为珠宝盗窃案的罪魁祸首。

examples.en: I am culpable for stealing your money.
examples.zh: 我为偷了你的钱负责。

days.passage: In 1960, a young Chicagoan, Danny Escobedo, was given a 20-year jail sentence for first-degree murder. Danny had confessed to complicity in the killing of his brother-in-law after the police had refused to allow him to see his lawyer. Actually, Danny was tricked into blaming a friend for the liquidation of his sister’s husband, thereby establishing himself as an accomplice. Despite the fact that Danny later recanted his confession, he was found culpable and jailed. Danny had been stereotyped* as a hoodlum and nobody raised an eyebrow over the hapless* felon’s* troubles.

exercises.text: Proceeding on the premise* that the broker was guilty of ________________ in the swindle, the detective followed him surreptitiously.*
After the ________________ of the gang leader, a mammoth* conflict arose among his ambitious lieutenants who aspired* to be boss.
Once the incontrovertible* evidence was offered, the servant was held ________________ in the theft of the jewels.
When the clergyman refused to ________________ , his superiors were so nettled* that they relegated* him to an isolated parish in Alaska.
Although he was judged as a minor ________________ , the driver had actually played an integral* part in planning the crime.

idiom.meaning: absentmindedness or daydreaming
idiom.example: When the young genius should have been doing his homework, he was frequently engaged in woolgathering.

days.passageZh: 1960年，一位芝加哥年轻人丹尼·埃斯科贝多因一级谋杀被判处20年监禁。丹尼承认参与了杀害姐夫的行为，因为警方拒绝让他见律师。实际上，丹尼被骗嫁祸给朋友，导致他姐姐的丈夫被清算，从而确立了自己作为共犯的身份。尽管丹尼后来撤回了供词，但他仍被判有罪并入狱。丹尼一直被刻板印象为流氓，没人对这个倒霉的重犯的麻烦皱眉。

days.title: ESCOBEDO’S LAWYER APPEALS

examples.en: Since the alleged accomplice,* Escobedo, had been denied access to an attorney, Kroll asked the courts to invalidate the conviction.
examples.zh: 由于涉嫌同谋埃斯科贝多被拒绝见律师，克罗尔请求法院撤销定罪。

examples.en: It is symptomatic* of some newspapers that an alleged criminal is regarded in print as guilty.
examples.zh: 有些报纸的症状是，被指控的罪犯在印刷品上被视为有罪。

examples.en: The agency alleged my credit history had problems.
examples.zh: 机构声称我的信用记录有问题。

examples.en: Since the alleged accomplice,* Escobedo, had been denied access to an attorney, Kroll asked the courts to invalidate the conviction.
examples.zh: 由于涉嫌同谋埃斯科贝多被拒绝见律师，克罗尔请求法院撤销定罪。

examples.en: Once the druggist had been duped* into opening the store, the addict gained access to the pep pills.
examples.zh: 一旦药剂师被骗开店，瘾君子便获得了提神药的使用权。

examples.en: an access of territory
examples.zh: 领土通道

words.definition: to deprive of legal force, to nullify

examples.en: Since the alleged accomplice,* Escobedo, had been denied access to an attorney, Kroll asked the courts to invalidate the conviction.
examples.zh: 由于涉嫌同谋埃斯科贝多被拒绝见律师，克罗尔请求法院撤销定罪。

examples.en: The general was sure to invalidate the court-martial’s decision once he learned of the flagrant* bias* of the presiding officer.
examples.zh: 将军一旦得知主持官明显的偏见，肯定会推翻军事法庭的决定。

examples.en: The circuit court judge's ruling was invalidated by a superior judge.
examples.zh: 巡回法院法官的裁决被一名上级法官无效。

examples.en: He proposed that lawyers be entitled to sit in when the police question a suspect but the Illinois courts rejected that on the grounds that it would effectively preclude all questioning by legal authorities.
examples.zh: 他提议律师有权旁听警方讯问嫌疑人，但伊利诺伊州法院以该提问实际上排除法律机关的质询为由予以拒绝。

examples.en: The manager was distraught* when he realized that the slugger’s sickness would preclude a World Series victory.
examples.zh: 当经理意识到这位强打手的病情将导致无法赢得世界大赛冠军时，他感到非常沮丧*。

examples.en: It has been raining for days, but that doesn’t preclude the possibility that the skies will clear by this afternoon!
examples.zh: 已经下了好几天雨，但这并不排除今天下午天空会放晴的可能性！

days.passage: Barry Kroll, a Chicago lawyer, took an interest in Danny Escobedo’s case. Kroll felt that his client’s rights under the Constitution had been abrogated. Since the alleged accomplice,* Escobedo, had been denied access to an attorney, Kroll asked the courts to invalidate the conviction. He proposed that lawyers be entitled to sit in when the police question a suspect but the Illinois courts rejected that on the grounds that it would effectively preclude all questioning by legal authorities. If such a law were upheld, the police felt that it would play havoc* with all criminal investigations.

exercises.text: The manager was distraught* when he realized that the slugger’s sickness would ________________ a World Series victory.
It is symptomatic* of some newspapers that an ________________ criminal is regarded in print as guilty.
The wealthy uncle decided to ________________ his inane* nephew’s sinecure.*
The general was sure to ________________ the court-martial’s decision once he learned of the flagrant* bias* of the presiding officer.
Once the druggist had been duped* into opening the store, the addict gained ________________ to the pep pills.

idiom.meaning: to conceal defects, to give a falsely virtuous appearance to something
idiom.example: Although a committee was appointed to investigate the corruption, many citizens felt that their report would be a whitewash of the culprits.*

days.passageZh: 芝加哥律师巴里·克罗尔对丹尼·埃斯科贝多的案件产生了兴趣。克罗尔认为其当事人在宪法下的权利已被废除。由于涉嫌同谋埃斯科贝多被拒绝见律师，克罗尔请求法院撤销定罪。他提议律师有权旁听警方讯问嫌疑人，但伊利诺伊州法院以该提问实际上排除法律机关的质询为由予以拒绝。如果该法律被维持，警方认为它将严重破坏所有刑事调查。

days.title: AN HISTORIC SUPREME COURT RULING

examples.en: The case was argued before the Supreme Court, and in 1964, in a landmark decision, the Court reversed Danny’s conviction.
examples.zh: 此案在最高法院辩论，1964年法院作出具有里程碑意义的判决，推翻了丹尼的定罪。

examples.en: The Battle of Midway was a landmark victory in the U.S. campaign for ultimate* victory over the Japanese in World War II.
examples.zh: 中途岛海战是美国在二战中对日军取得最终胜利的标志性胜利。

examples.en: a landmark paper in neurosurgery
examples.zh: 神经外科领域的里程碑论文

words.definition: An object that marks the boundary of a piece of land (usually a stone, or a tree).

examples.en: “A system of law enforcement that comes to depend on the confession,” one Justice declared, “will, in the long run, be less reliable than a system that depends on extrinsic evidence independently secured through skillful investigation.” A Justice who declaimed against the decision said, however, “I think the rule is ill-conceived and that it seriously fetters perfectly legitimate methods of criminal enforcement.”
examples.zh: 一位大法官宣称：“依赖供词的执法体系，从长远来看，将不如依赖通过巧妙调查独立获得的外部证据的系统可靠。”一位大法官反对该决定时表示：“我认为该规则设计不当，严重限制了完全合法的刑事执法方式。”

examples.en: Collectors avidly* sought the rare coin for its extrinsic value.
examples.zh: 收藏家们热衷于*寻找这枚稀有硬币，因其外在价值。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be extrinsic.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是外在的。

words.definition: A chain or similar object used to bind a person or animal – often by its legs (usually in plural).

days.passage: Lawyer Kroll persevered in his defense of Danny Escobedo. The case was argued before the Supreme Court, and in 1964, in a landmark decision, the Court reversed Danny’s conviction. Legal aid, said the judges, must be instantly available to a suspect. “A system of law enforcement that comes to depend on the confession,” one Justice declared, “will, in the long run, be less reliable than a system that depends on extrinsic evidence independently secured through skillful investigation.” A Justice who declaimed against the decision said, however, “I think the rule is ill-conceived and that it seriously fetters perfectly legitimate methods of criminal enforcement.”

exercises.text: Collectors avidly* sought the rare coin for its ________________ value.
If we ________________ , we can overcome many of our inhibitions.*
The Battle of Midway was a ________________ victory in the U.S. campaign for ultimate* victory over the Japanese in World War II.
I knew that my father would ________________ against Mother’s choice of ostentatious* fabrics.
The senator inveighed* against the policy because he felt it would ________________ our Air Force.

historic, turning point of a period

idiom.meaning: to make a start by overcoming initial difficulties
idiom.example: The auto salesman had a poor week, but he finally broke the ice by selling a fully equipped Cadillac.

days.passageZh: 律师克罗尔坚持为丹尼·埃斯科贝多辩护。此案在最高法院辩论，1964年法院作出具有里程碑意义的判决，推翻了丹尼的定罪。法官们表示，法律援助必须对嫌疑人立即开放。一位大法官宣称：“依赖供词的执法体系，从长远来看，将不如依赖通过巧妙调查独立获得的外部证据的系统可靠。”一位大法官反对该决定时表示：“我认为该规则设计不当，严重限制了完全合法的刑事执法方式。”

days.title: THE EFFECTS OF THE ESCOBEDO DECISION

examples.en: After getting out, Danny was not a paragon of virtue, according to the police.
examples.zh: 警方称丹尼出狱后并非道德楷模。

examples.en: The town planners looked upon their utopia* as a paragon for other communities.
examples.zh: 城市规划者将他们的乌托邦*视为其他社区的典范。

examples.en: In the novel, Constanza is a paragon of virtue who would never compromise her reputation.
examples.zh: 在小说中，康斯坦萨是美德典范，绝不会妥协自己的名声。

examples.en: He led a nomadic existence, drifting from job to job, and was arrested frequently.
examples.zh: 他过着游牧生活，辗转各行各业，经常被逮捕。

examples.en: In the desert, nomadic tribes wander back and forth, enduring much privation.*
examples.zh: 在沙漠中，游牧部落来回流浪，忍受着极大的艰苦。*

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be nomadic.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了游牧的含义。

examples.en: With asperity, and a few choice epithets, Danny referred to police harassment.* Although the Escobedo case was a controversial one, most agree that it inspired better police training, better law enforcement procedures, and improved scientific crime detection.
examples.zh: 丹尼带着严厉和几句刻薄的词汇提到了警察骚扰。* 尽管埃斯科贝多案颇具争议，但大多数人同意它激发了更好的警察培训、更完善的执法程序和科学犯罪侦测的改进。

examples.en: We were amazed at the display of asperity from our normally phlegmatic* neighbor.
examples.zh: 我们对这位平时冷静的邻居表现出的冷漠感到惊讶。

examples.en: the asperity of Maine's winter
examples.zh: 缅因州冬天的严酷

examples.en: With asperity, and a few choice epithets, Danny referred to police harassment.* Although the Escobedo case was a controversial one, most agree that it inspired better police training, better law enforcement procedures, and improved scientific crime detection.
examples.zh: 丹尼带着严厉和几句刻薄的词汇提到了警察骚扰。* 尽管埃斯科贝多案颇具争议，但大多数人同意它激发了更好的警察培训、更完善的执法程序和科学犯罪侦测的改进。

examples.en: Some school principals attempt to repress* the publication of controversial editorials.
examples.zh: 一些校长试图压制*发表有争议的社论。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be controversial.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是有争议的。

days.passage: After Danny Escobedo’s release from prison, hundreds of inmates began suits for their freedom on the grounds that their rights had been violated, too. Each case was heard on its merits, and in numerous instances people who had been convicted of serious offenses were freed because of the new standards established in the Escobedo case. After getting out, Danny was not a paragon of virtue, according to the police. He led a nomadic existence, drifting from job to job, and was arrested frequently. With asperity, and a few choice epithets, Danny referred to police harassment.* Although the Escobedo case was a controversial one, most agree that it inspired better police training, better law enforcement procedures, and improved scientific crime detection.

exercises.text: In the desert, ________________ tribes wander back and forth, enduring much privation.*
The town planners looked upon their utopia* as a ________________ for other communities.
Some school principals attempt to repress* the publication of ________________ editorials.
We were amazed at the display of ________________ from our normally phlegmatic* neighbor.
A bitter quarrel was precipitated* when both politicians hurled vile ________________ at each other.

idiom.meaning: a secret means of spreading information
idiom.example: The grapevine has it that Ernie will be elected president of the school’s student council.

days.passageZh: 丹尼·埃斯科贝多出狱后，数百名囚犯以权利也被侵犯为由，开始提起诉讼寻求自由。每起案件均根据案件本身内容审理，许多因埃斯科贝多案确立的新标准，被判严重罪行的人得以获释。警方称丹尼出狱后并非道德楷模。他过着游牧生活，辗转各行各业，经常被逮捕。丹尼带着严厉和几句刻薄的词汇提到了警察骚扰。* 尽管埃斯科贝多案颇具争议，但大多数人同意它激发了更好的警察培训、更完善的执法程序和科学犯罪侦测的改进。

reviewOptions.text: to deprive of legal force, cancel

days.story:
The Nostalgia Factory, a Boston art gallery, staged an exhibit of advertisements that had outraged various segments of the community. For example, one of the fast food chains ran a TV commercial that showed unattractive school cafeteria workers in hairnets, making that experience less tasty than a visit to Roy Rogers. Another ad that drew criticism from psychiatrists and groups such as the Alliance for the Mentally Ill suggested to readers that, if they had paid $100 for a dress shirt, they were fit candidates for a straitjacket. Similar sensitivity had restricted ad writers from using terms such as “nuts” or “crazy.”

Why such protests and where do they come from? Who is asking companies to ______ contracts with those agencies that are ______ in creating racist types of commercial messages? Parents who took exception to the Burger King spot that announced, “Sometimes You Gotta Break the Rules,” said no to it because it gave the wrong message to their children. And when a potato chip maker’s ad featured a “bandito,” angry Mexican-Americans used some choice ______ in denouncing such a stereotype.

The conclusion to be reached is that segments of the population have become increasingly vocal about “insensitive” ads, demanding that corporations ______ and never again commission advertisements that are clearly ______ , provocative, and harmful to good human relationships.
days.storyZh:
波士顿的一家艺术画廊怀旧工厂举办了一场广告展览，这些广告激怒了社区的多个群体。例如，一家快餐连锁店播出了一则电视广告，展示了戴着发网的不吸引人的学校食堂工作人员，这让这种体验不如去罗伊·罗杰斯那里吃。另一则广告遭到精神科医生和精神病患者联盟等团体的批评，该广告向读者暗示，如果他们花100美元买一件衬衫，他们就适合穿紧身衣。类似的敏感性限制了广告作者使用“疯子”或“疯子”等词汇。

为什么会有这样的抗议？它们从何而来？谁会要求公司与那些在制造种族主义商业信息______的机构______合同？那些对汉堡王广告“有时候你得打破规则”感到不满的家长们拒绝了，因为那给孩子们传递了错误的信息。当一则薯片厂商的广告出现了“土匪”时，愤怒的墨西哥裔美国人用______谴责这种刻板印象。

结论是，部分民众对“不敏感”广告的声音日益高涨，要求企业再也无法______、且明显具有挑衅性、对良好人际关系有害的______广告。

days.title: MEET THE BEES

examples.en: One of the most interesting inhabitants of our world is the bee, an insect that is indigenous to all parts of the globe except the polar regions.
examples.zh: 我们世界上最有趣的生物之一是蜜蜂，这是一种原生于全球各地的昆虫，唯独极地不在。

examples.en: The forest was replete* with the kind of wildlife that is indigenous to Africa.
examples.zh: 森林里充满了非洲本土的野生动物。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be indigenous.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了成为土著的含义。

examples.en: The honeybee is a gregarious insect whose habitat is a colony that he shares with as many as 80,000 bees.
examples.zh: 蜜蜂是一种群居的昆虫，其栖息地是一个群落，与多达8万只蜜蜂共享。

examples.en: At the party, the gregarious hostess scurried* from group to group, making friends and influencing people.
examples.zh: 在派对上，这位外向的女主人匆匆忙忙*在各个群体之间奔波，结交朋友并影响着大家。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be gregarious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是善交际。

examples.en: The honeybee is a gregarious insect whose habitat is a colony that he shares with as many as 80,000 bees.
examples.zh: 蜜蜂是一种群居的昆虫，其栖息地是一个群落，与多达8万只蜜蜂共享。

examples.en: The alcoholic was found supine* in his favorite habitat—Ryan’s Bar.
examples.zh: 这位酒鬼被发现仰卧*，地点是他最喜欢的栖息地——瑞安酒吧。

examples.en: This park offers important amphibian habitat and breeding area.
examples.zh: 该公园为重要的两栖动物栖息地和繁殖区提供重要资源。

examples.en: A cursory study of the activities of these insects reveals an orderliness and a social structure that is truly amazing.
examples.zh: 对这些昆虫活动的粗略研究揭示了其有序和令人惊叹的社会结构。

examples.en: Sherlock Holmes took a cursory glance at the cryptic* message and decoded it instantly.
examples.zh: 福尔摩斯粗略扫了一眼那条神秘的信息，立刻解码了。

examples.en: Most junk mail requires only a cursory glance.
examples.zh: 大多数垃圾邮件只需粗略浏览即可。

examples.en: For example, bees in a particular hive have a distinct odor; therefore, when an interloper seeks access* they can identify him quickly and repulse* his invasion.
examples.zh: 例如，某个蜂箱里的蜜蜂有独特的气味;因此，当入侵者寻求进入时，他们可以迅速识别并击退他的入侵。

examples.en: Electric eyes, watchdogs, and other nuances* were there to keep out an interloper .
examples.zh: 电眼、看门狗和其他细微差别*都存在，用来阻挡外来者。

examples.en: They disliked the interloper, and forced him to leave.
examples.zh: 他们不喜欢这个闯入者，强迫他离开。

days.passage: One of the most interesting inhabitants of our world is the bee, an insect that is indigenous to all parts of the globe except the polar regions. The honeybee is a gregarious insect whose habitat is a colony that he shares with as many as 80,000 bees. Although the individual bees live for only a few days, their colony can be operative for several years. A cursory study of the activities of these insects reveals an orderliness and a social structure that is truly amazing. For example, bees in a particular hive have a distinct odor; therefore, when an interloper seeks access* they can identify him quickly and repulse* his invasion.

exercises.text: Sherlock Holmes took a ________________ glance at the cryptic* message and decoded it instantly.
The forest was replete* with the kind of wildlife that is ________________ to Africa.
Electric eyes, watchdogs, and other nuances* were there to keep out an _______________ .
The alcoholic was found supine* in his favorite __________—Ryan’s Bar.
At the party, the ________________ hostess scurried* from group to group, making friends and influencing people.

idiom.meaning: taking the straightest, shortest route (that’s the way a bee flies back to the hive after he has gathered food)
idiom.example: When the couple left, the babysitter made a bee line for the refrigerator.

days.passageZh: 我们世界上最有趣的生物之一是蜜蜂，这是一种原生于全球各地的昆虫，唯独极地不在。蜜蜂是一种群居的昆虫，其栖息地是一个群落，与多达8万只蜜蜂共享。虽然单个蜜蜂寿命仅几天，但其群体可持续运作数年。对这些昆虫活动的粗略研究揭示了其有序和令人惊叹的社会结构。例如，某个蜂箱里的蜜蜂有独特的气味;因此，当入侵者寻求进入时，他们可以迅速识别并击退他的入侵。

days.title: QUEENS, WORKERS, DRONES

examples.en: Each colony of honeybees consists of three classes: a) the queen who is a prolific layer of eggs; b) the worker who is the bulwark of the colony; and c) the sedentary drone whose only function is to mate with a young queen.
examples.zh: 每个蜂群由三个类别组成：a）蜂后，蜂后是大量产卵的产卵者;b）作为殖民地堡垒的工蜂;c）定居的雄蜂，其唯一功能是与年轻蜂王交配。

examples.en: The usually prolific novelist was frustrated* by her failure to come up with a good plot.
examples.zh: 这位通常多产的小说家因未能构思出好情节而感到沮丧*。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be prolific.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了多产的含义。

examples.en: Each colony of honeybees consists of three classes: a) the queen who is a prolific layer of eggs; b) the worker who is the bulwark of the colony; and c) the sedentary drone whose only function is to mate with a young queen.
examples.zh: 每个蜂群由三个类别组成：a）蜂后，蜂后是大量产卵的产卵者;b）作为殖民地堡垒的工蜂;c）定居的雄蜂，其唯一功能是与年轻蜂王交配。

examples.en: Some feel that the United States should be a bulwark to the inchoate* democracies around the world.
examples.zh: 有人认为美国应成为全球尚未成型的民主国家的堡垒。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be bulwark.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了成为堡垒的含义。

examples.en: Each colony of honeybees consists of three classes: a) the queen who is a prolific layer of eggs; b) the worker who is the bulwark of the colony; and c) the sedentary drone whose only function is to mate with a young queen.
examples.zh: 每个蜂群由三个类别组成：a）蜂后，蜂后是大量产卵的产卵者;b）作为殖民地堡垒的工蜂;c）定居的雄蜂，其唯一功能是与年轻蜂王交配。

examples.en: The typist shook off the fetters* of her sedentary life and joined a mountain climbing expedition.
examples.zh: 这位打字员摆脱了久坐生活的束缚，加入了一次登山探险。

examples.en: The oyster is a sedentary mollusk; the barnacles are sedentary crustaceans.
examples.zh: 牡蛎是一种定居性软体动物;藤壶是定居性甲壳类动物。

examples.en: The frugal worker builds and maintains the nest, collects and stores the honey, and is the antithesis of the lazy drone, or male honeybee, who does not work and has no sting.
examples.zh: 节俭的工蜂负责筑巢和维护，收集和储存蜂蜜，是懒惰的雄蜂的对立面，雄蜂不工作且没有蜇人。

examples.en: frugal shoppers occasionally badger* supermarket managers for bargains.
examples.zh: 节俭的购物者偶尔会向超市经理索求优惠。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be frugal.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了节俭的含义。

examples.en: The frugal worker builds and maintains the nest, collects and stores the honey, and is the antithesis of the lazy drone, or male honeybee, who does not work and has no sting.
examples.zh: 节俭的工蜂负责筑巢和维护，收集和储存蜂蜜，是懒惰的雄蜂的对立面，雄蜂不工作且没有蜇人。

examples.en: Len, the gregarious* twin, was the antithesis of Lon, the reticent one.
examples.zh: 伦，那个外向的双胞胎，和朗的性格截然不同，朗是那个沉默寡言的人。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be antithesis.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是对立。

days.passage: Each colony of honeybees consists of three classes: a) the queen who is a prolific layer of eggs; b) the worker who is the bulwark of the colony; and c) the sedentary drone whose only function is to mate with a young queen. The queen lays the eggs that hatch into thousands of female workers; some queens live as long as five years and lay up to one million eggs. The frugal worker builds and maintains the nest, collects and stores the honey, and is the antithesis of the lazy drone, or male honeybee, who does not work and has no sting. When the drone is no longer needed, the workers, in effect, liquidate* him by letting him starve to death. It’s a cruel, cruel world!

exercises.text: The usually ________________ novelist was frustrated* by her failure to come up with a good plot.
Len, the gregarious* twin, was the ________________ of Lon, the reticent one.
The typist shook off the fetters* of her ________________ life and joined a mountain climbing expedition.
________________ shoppers occasionally badger* supermarket managers for bargains.
Some feel that the United States should be a ________________ to the inchoate* democracies around the world.

idiom.phrase: the world, the flesh, and the devil
idiom.meaning: temptations that cause man to sin
idiom.example: By entering the monastery he sought to avoid the world, the flesh, and the devil.

days.passageZh: 每个蜂群由三个类别组成：a）蜂后，蜂后是大量产卵的产卵者;b）作为殖民地堡垒的工蜂;c）定居的雄蜂，其唯一功能是与年轻蜂王交配。蜂后产下孵化成数千只雌工蜂的卵;有些蜂王寿命可达五年，产卵量可达一百万枚。节俭的工蜂负责筑巢和维护，收集和储存蜂蜜，是懒惰的雄蜂的对立面，雄蜂不工作且没有蜇人。当无人机不再需要时，工人们实际上通过让他饿死来消灭了他。这个世界真是残酷无情！

days.title: SPOTLIGHT ON THE WORKER

examples.en: Let us examine the activities of the altruistic workers in greater detail.
examples.zh: 让我们更详细地审视这些利他工人的活动。

examples.en: The good-hearted doctor went into the jungle purely for altruistic reasons.
examples.zh: 这位善良的医生纯粹出于无私的原因进入丛林。

examples.en: His helping the old woman with her shopping was deemed highly altruistic by everyone, especially since her home was a mile away.
examples.zh: 他帮老妇人购物被所有人认为非常无私，尤其是她的家离得很远。

examples.en: While some workers embellish the hive, others fly out in search of nectar and pollen.
examples.zh: 一些工蜂装饰蜂巢，另一些则飞出寻找花蜜和花粉。

examples.en: Everytime the irate* motorist told about the accident he had a tendency to embellish the story.
examples.zh: 每次愤怒的司机谈论事故时，他总会夸大故事。

examples.en: The old book cover was embellished with golden letters
examples.zh: 旧书封面装饰着金色字母

examples.en: Meanwhile, other workers have been cleaning cells, caring for the young, and guarding the precious cache of nectar.
examples.zh: 与此同时，其他工人们在清理牢房，照顾幼崽，守护珍贵的花蜜储备。

examples.en: Through a fortuitous* remark, the cache of the art thieves was discovered.
examples.zh: 通过一次偶然的*言论，艺术窃贼的藏匿点被发现。

examples.en: Members of the 29-man Discovery team laid down food caches to allow the polar team to travel light, hopping from food cache to food cache on their return journey.
examples.zh: 29人“发现号”团队成员布置了食物储备点，使极地团队能够轻装上阵，在返回途中从一个食物储藏点跳到另一个食物储藏点。

words.definition: small group having something in common

examples.en: Another special coterie is entrusted with heating or cooling the hive.
examples.zh: 另一个特殊小队负责给蜂巢供暖或制冷。

examples.en: A coterie of bridge players made our clubroom their permanent habitat.*
examples.zh: 一群桥牌选手把我们的俱乐部活动室变成了他们的永久栖息地。*

examples.en: The new junior employee joined our merry after-hours coterie.
examples.zh: 这位新来的初级员工加入了我们欢快的下班小队。

examples.en: Dedicated to the welfare of the queen and the entire insect community, all of these workers display a complete absence of cupidity.
examples.zh: 这些工蚁致力于女王和整个昆虫群体的福祉，完全没有任何贪婪。

examples.en: We warned him that his reprehensible* cupidity would eventuate* in a loss of all his friends.
examples.zh: 我们警告他，他那可恶的*贪婪*最终会导致失去所有朋友。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be cupidity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了贪婪的含义。

days.passage: Let us examine the activities of the altruistic workers in greater detail. After the workers have constructed a hive of waterproof honeycomb (made from beeswax), the queen begins to lay eggs in the first cells. While some workers embellish the hive, others fly out in search of nectar and pollen. With their long tongues they gather nectar and use their hind legs to carry the pollen from the flowers. They fly directly back to the hive and then dance around the honeycomb, their movements indicating the direction of the flowers. Meanwhile, other workers have been cleaning cells, caring for the young, and guarding the precious cache of nectar. Another special coterie is entrusted with heating or cooling the hive. Dedicated to the welfare of the queen and the entire insect community, all of these workers display a complete absence of cupidity.

A ________________ of bridge players made our clubroom their permanent habitat.*

idiom.meaning: to attempt to do something without having the necessary materials (In the Bible we read that the Egyptians commanded the Israelites to do so)
idiom.example: My uncle’s business schemes always fail because he tries to make bricks without straw.

days.passageZh: 让我们更详细地审视这些利他工人的活动。工蜂用蜂蜡制成防水蜂巢后，蜂后开始在第一个蜂箱中产卵。一些工蜂装饰蜂巢，另一些则飞出寻找花蜜和花粉。它们用长舌采集花蜜，并用后腿携带花粉。它们直接飞回蜂巢，然后围绕蜂巢跳舞，动作指示花朵的方向。与此同时，其他工人们在清理牢房，照顾幼崽，守护珍贵的花蜜储备。另一个特殊小队负责给蜂巢供暖或制冷。这些工蚁致力于女王和整个昆虫群体的福祉，完全没有任何贪婪。

days.title: THE SAGA OF THE QUEEN BEE

examples.en: Although the virtuosity of the workers is remarkable, the queen bee is really the main story.
examples.zh: 虽然工蜂的高明令人惊叹，但女王蜂才是真正的故事。

examples.en: The eminent* artist, famous for his virtuosity , was admired by classicists and beatniks alike.
examples.zh: 这位以精湛技艺著称的著名*艺术家，深受古典学家和垮掉派的钦佩。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be virtuosity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是炫技。

examples.en: After she kills any rivals who have the temerity to challenge her, an amorous note is injected.
examples.zh: 在她杀死所有敢于挑战她的对手后，会注入一封情愫。

examples.en: For having the temerity to declaim* against the majority leader, the freshman senator was given the worst committee assignments.
examples.zh: 因为他敢于对多数党领袖大声宣扬*，这位新任参议员被分配到了最糟糕的委员会任务。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be temerity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是大胆。

examples.en: After she kills any rivals who have the temerity to challenge her, an amorous note is injected.
examples.zh: 在她杀死所有敢于挑战她的对手后，会注入一封情愫。

examples.en: A landmark* in the history of amorous drama is Romeo and Juliet.
examples.zh: 爱情戏剧史上的一个里程碑*是《罗密欧与朱丽叶》。

examples.en: We were both feeling amorous so the inevitable happened.
examples.zh: 我们俩都感到爱慕，所以不可避免的事情发生了。

examples.en: When her progeny saturate the hive, scouts are dispatched to find a new location, and the bees swarm after their leader to begin the amazing cycle again.
examples.zh: 当她的后代充满蜂巢时，侦察员被派去寻找新地点，蜜蜂蜂群追逐领袖，重新开始这奇妙的循环。

examples.en: The Bantu chief and all his progeny were noted for their valor.*
examples.zh: 班图酋长及其所有后代都以英勇著称。*

examples.en: I treasure this five-generation photograph of my great-great grandmother and her progeny.
examples.zh: 我珍惜这张我曾曾祖母及其后代的五代合影。

examples.en: When her progeny saturate the hive, scouts are dispatched to find a new location, and the bees swarm after their leader to begin the amazing cycle again.
examples.zh: 当她的后代充满蜂巢时，侦察员被派去寻找新地点，蜜蜂蜂群追逐领袖，重新开始这奇妙的循环。

examples.en: After walking home in the driving rain, his clothes were saturated.
examples.zh: 在倾盆大雨中走回家，他的衣服已经湿透了。

examples.en: Modern television is saturated with violence.
examples.zh: 现代电视充斥着暴力。

days.passage: Although the virtuosity of the workers is remarkable, the queen bee is really the main story. Workers choose a few larvae to be queens, feeding them royal jelly, a substance rich in proteins and vitamins. While the queen is changing from a larva to a pupa, a team of workers builds a special cell for her. Soon the young queen hatches, eats the prepared honey, and grows strong. After she kills any rivals who have the temerity to challenge her, an amorous note is injected. She flies from the hive and mates with one or more drones on her first flight. Then the process of egg laying begins. When her progeny saturate the hive, scouts are dispatched to find a new location, and the bees swarm after their leader to begin the amazing cycle again.

exercises.text: A landmark* in the history of ________________ drama is Romeo and Juliet.
The eminent* artist, famous for his ________________ , was admired by classicists and beatniks alike.
The Bantu chief and all his ________________ were noted for their valor.*
For having the ________________ to declaim* against the majority leader, the freshman senator was given the worst committee assignments.
Television in the new century was ________________ with the rebirth of the old quiz shows.

idiom.meaning: to gain control
idiom.example: I had him at my mercy, but now he has the upper hand.

days.passageZh: 虽然工蜂的高明令人惊叹，但女王蜂才是真正的故事。工蚁会选择几只幼虫作为蚁后，喂食蜂王浆，这是一种富含蛋白质和维生素的物质。当蜂王从幼虫变成蛹时，一队工蜂为她建造了一个特殊的巢穴。很快，年轻的蜂后孵化，吃下准备好的蜂蜜，变得强壮。在她杀死所有敢于挑战她的对手后，会注入一封情愫。她从蜂巢飞出，第一次飞行时与一台或多台无人机交配。然后开始产卵过程。当她的后代充满蜂巢时，侦察员被派去寻找新地点，蜜蜂蜂群追逐领袖，重新开始这奇妙的循环。

reviewWords.word: the world, the flesh, and the devil

days.story:
“Our neighborhood was so tough,” the comedian joked, “that two guys held up a bank and were mugged as they ran to their getaway car.”

Later that evening, as Roy and Timmy were discussing the comic’s routine, Roy was reminded of a true (he said) story that went like this:

Mr. D., the gang kingpin in our community, loved money. Like Silas Marner, the ______ weaver of George Eliot’s novel, he enjoyed counting his treasure each Friday night. Mr. D’s ______ was concealed in a wall safe behind a painting in his office. The $50 and $100 bills made his hands dirty as he counted them but Mr. D didn’t mind. The filth of the lucre did not disturb him at all.

One Friday evening, Roy continued, a brash ______ had the ______ to try to steal the ill-gotten gains. Having bought the combination from a relative who had installed Mr. D’s safe, he stuffed his loot into a laundry bag and was halfway out the door when he spied a $10 bill on the floor. His ______ made him go back for that small change, and in that moment, Mr. D. arrived on the scene.

The quick-thinking thief blurted out, “I’ll have the shirts back on Friday.” Hoisting the laundry bag over his shoulder, he was out the door before the confused mobster could figure out what had happened.

Timmy, who had listened patiently, said, “I don’t believe a word of that story because it would take a guy with a great deal of starch to pull it off!”
days.storyZh:
“我们社区太艰难了，”喜剧演员开玩笑道，“两个人抢劫银行，跑向逃跑车时被抢劫。”

当天晚上，当罗伊和蒂米讨论喜剧演员的套路时，罗伊想起了一个真实的故事（他说是这样）：

D先生，我们社区的帮派老大，爱钱。就像乔治·艾略特小说中的______织工西拉斯·马纳一样，他喜欢每个周五晚上数着自己的宝藏。D先生的______藏在他办公室一幅画后面的墙壁保险箱里。50美元和100美元的钞票让他数着手，但D先生并不介意。那份肮脏的钱丝毫没有打扰他。

一个星期五晚上，罗伊继续说，一个鲁莽的______竟有______试图偷走非法所得。他从一位安装了D先生保险箱的亲戚那里买了密码，把赃物塞进洗衣袋，正要走到门口一半时，他发现地上有一张10美元的钞票。他的______让他回去找那点小钱，就在这时，D先生出现了。

反应迅速的小偷脱口而出：“我周五会把衬衫还回来。”他把洗衣袋扛在肩上，冲出门外，还没等那个困惑的黑帮分子反应过来。

耐心听着的蒂米说：“我一点也不相信这个故事，因为要做到这事，得是个有很大胆量的人！”

days.title: A PLAN TO FOOL THE NAZIS

examples.en: One of the truly remarkable stories of World War II concerns a ruse* that was perpetrated with such consummate skill that it saved the lives of many Allied troops and helped to shorten the war.
examples.zh: 二战中一个真正非凡的故事涉及一个诡计*，该骗局以极高的技巧实施，挽救了许多盟军士兵的生命，并帮助缩短了战争。

examples.en: Johnny Cochrane, the famous and controversial* lawyer, gave consummate attention to the preparation of every case.
examples.zh: 著名且颇具争议的律师约翰尼·科克伦对每一个案件的准备都极为关注。

examples.en: After the reception, he escorted her to the honeymoon suite to consummate their marriage.
examples.zh: 婚宴结束后，他护送她到蜜月套房，完成了他们的婚姻。

examples.en: The simple, bold, and ingenious subterfuge which British officers concocted is the subject of Ewen Montagu’s classic, The Man Who Never Was.
examples.zh: 英国军官们策划的简单、大胆且巧妙的诡计，正是尤恩·蒙塔古经典作品《从未存在的人》的主题。

examples.en: The experienced teacher realized that Ricky’s stomachache was merely a subterfuge to keep him from taking the French test.
examples.zh: 这位经验丰富的老师意识到，瑞奇的胃痛只是为了阻止他参加法语考试而设的伪装。

examples.en: Overt subterfuge in a region nearly caused a minor accident.
examples.zh: 该地区的公开掩饰差点导致一起小事故。

examples.en: In short, the idea was to plant fallacious documents concerning the Allied invasion of Europe upon a dead officer, have his body recovered by agents who would transmit the false information to Germany, and then observe the effects of the plan.
examples.zh: 简而言之，这个计划是将有关盟军入侵欧洲的虚假文件植入一名阵亡军官身上，让特工找回他的尸体，这些特工会将虚假信息传递给德国，然后观察该计划的效果。

examples.en: Because the inspector had given only cursory* attention to the reports, I surmised* that his conclusion would be fallacious .
examples.zh: 因为督察对报告只做了表面*关注，我推测*他的结论是错误的。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be fallacious.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了什么是谬误。

days.passage: One of the truly remarkable stories of World War II concerns a ruse* that was perpetrated with such consummate skill that it saved the lives of many Allied troops and helped to shorten the war. The simple, bold, and ingenious subterfuge which British officers concocted is the subject of Ewen Montagu’s classic, The Man Who Never Was. In short, the idea was to plant fallacious documents concerning the Allied invasion of Europe upon a dead officer, have his body recovered by agents who would transmit the false information to Germany, and then observe the effects of the plan.

exercises.text: Because the inspector had given only cursory* attention to the reports, I surmised* that his conclusion would be ________________ .
Johnny Cochrane, the famous and controversial* lawyer, gave ________________ attention to the preparation of every case.
It was necessary for the interloper* to ________________ a convincing story in order to gain access* to the exhibit.
In order to ________________ the swindle, the jaunty* confidence man adopted an amorous* approach toward the wealthy widow.
The experienced teacher realized that Ricky’s stomachache was merely a ________________ to keep him from taking the French test.

idiom.meaning: to check one’s anger, to restrain oneself
idiom.example: The performer drew in his horns when he saw that his critic was an eight-year-old boy.

days.passageZh: 二战中一个真正非凡的故事涉及一个诡计*，该骗局以极高的技巧实施，挽救了许多盟军士兵的生命，并帮助缩短了战争。英国军官们策划的简单、大胆且巧妙的诡计，正是尤恩·蒙塔古经典作品《从未存在的人》的主题。简而言之，这个计划是将有关盟军入侵欧洲的虚假文件植入一名阵亡军官身上，让特工找回他的尸体，这些特工会将虚假信息传递给德国，然后观察该计划的效果。

days.title: “MAJOR MARTIN” GOES TO WAR

examples.en: Burdened by her manifold responsibilities, the young executive was precluded* from enjoying her new wealth.
After Commander Montagu and his colleagues had been given official approval for their dangerous escapade, they encountered manifold problems.
examples.zh: 在蒙塔古指挥官和他的同事们获得官方批准这场危险的冒险行动后，他们遇到了诸多问题。

examples.en: The manifold meanings of the simple English word set are infamous among dictionary makers.
examples.zh: 简单英语单词集的多重含义在词典编纂者中臭名昭著。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be manifold.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了多元的含义。

words.definition: A copy made by the manifold writing process.

examples.en: Florence Nightingale was a paragon* of mercy in her assiduous care for the wounded soldiers.
First, they conducted an assiduous search for a body that looked as though it had recently been killed in an airplane disaster.
examples.zh: 首先，他们进行了细致的搜寻，寻找一具看起来像是最近在飞机事故中丧生的尸体。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be assiduous.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了勤奋的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "assiduous" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“勤奋”这个词。

examples.en: Although basically frugal,* his taste in clothing is impeccable .
Then, a detailed history of the man had to be invented that would be so impeccable that the enemy would accept its authenticity.
examples.zh: 然后，必须编造一部关于这个人的详细历史，要做到无可挑剔，让敌人相信其真实性。

examples.en: He grew up in Norway, but he writes impeccable English.
examples.zh: 他在挪威长大，但英语写得无可挑剔。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be impeccable.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了无可挑剔的含义。

examples.en: Fear permeated* the crippled airplane as the passengers realized that their situation was fraught with danger.
Each step was fraught with difficulty, but the schemers were unbelievably resourceful.
examples.zh: 每一步都充满艰难，但策划者们极其机智。

examples.en: fraught money.
examples.zh: 金钱充满风险。

examples.en: a fraught relationship; a fraught process
examples.zh: 一段紧张的关系;这是一个充满挑战的过程

examples.en: The store owner was resourceful enough to run a sale the day after his building had been razed* by the flames.
Each step was fraught with difficulty, but the schemers were unbelievably resourceful.
examples.zh: 每一步都充满艰难，但策划者们极其机智。

examples.en: With a resourceful use of space and a fresh coat of paint, the room became a pleasant library with a comfortable seating area.
examples.zh: 通过巧妙利用空间和重新粉刷，房间变成了一个舒适的图书馆，配有舒适的休息区。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be resourceful.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是资源丰富。

days.passage: After Commander Montagu and his colleagues had been given official approval for their dangerous escapade, they encountered manifold problems. First, they conducted an assiduous search for a body that looked as though it had recently been killed in an airplane disaster. Then, a detailed history of the man had to be invented that would be so impeccable that the enemy would accept its authenticity. This meant documents, love letters, personal effects, keys, photographs, etc. Each step was fraught with difficulty, but the schemers were unbelievably resourceful. As a result, in the late spring of 1942, “Major Martin” was prepared to do his part for his country.

exercises.text: Burdened by her ________________ responsibilities, the young executive was precluded* from enjoying her new wealth.
Fear permeated* the crippled airplane as the passengers realized that their situation was ________________ with danger.
Although basically frugal,* his taste in clothing is ________________ .
The store owner was ________________ enough to run a sale the day after his building had been razed* by the flames.
Florence Nightingale was a paragon* of mercy in her ________________ care for the wounded soldiers.

idiom.phrase: to put the cart before the horse
idiom.meaning: to reverse the proper order, do things backwards
idiom.example: My assistant was so eager to get the job done that he often put the cart before the horse.

days.passageZh: 在蒙塔古指挥官和他的同事们获得官方批准这场危险的冒险行动后，他们遇到了诸多问题。首先，他们进行了细致的搜寻，寻找一具看起来像是最近在飞机事故中丧生的尸体。然后，必须编造一部关于这个人的详细历史，要做到无可挑剔，让敌人相信其真实性。这意味着文件、情书、个人物品、钥匙、照片等等。每一步都充满艰难，但策划者们极其机智。因此，1942年晚春，“马丁少校”准备为国家尽一份力。

days.title: THE PLOT THICKENS

examples.en: Then, “Major Martin,” the man who never was, was slid into the murky Atlantic waters off the coast of Huelva, Spain.
examples.zh: 随后，“马丁少校”，那个从未成为的人，被滑入西班牙韦尔瓦海岸外浑浊的大西洋水域。

examples.en: The murky quality of the artist’s latest painting is the antithesis* of her former style.
examples.zh: 艺术家最新画作的浑浊质感与她以往风格截然相反*。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be murky.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了模糊的含义。

examples.en: Attached to this courier’s coat was a briefcase that contained the components of the hoax.
examples.zh: 信使外套上附着一个装有骗局组件的公文包。

examples.en: The practical joker had the temerity* to perpetrate* a hoax upon the Dean of Boys.
examples.zh: 这个恶作剧者竟然厚颜无耻地*对男生主任实施了一场骗局。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be hoax.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是骗局。

examples.en: The secret information was transmitted to the German High Command, through a labyrinth of underground networks, to be evaluated.
examples.zh: 这些机密信息通过复杂的地下网络传送到德国最高指挥部进行评估。

examples.en: After wandering through the labyrinth , the young hero came face to face with the dragon who was indigenous* to the caves.
examples.zh: 在迷宫中徘徊后，这位年轻的英雄与洞穴中原生的龙正面相遇。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be labyrinth.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是迷宫。

days.passage: A submarine took the body out to sea. Then, “Major Martin,” the man who never was, was slid into the murky Atlantic waters off the coast of Huelva, Spain. Attached to this courier’s coat was a briefcase that contained the components of the hoax. Shortly thereafter, the Spanish Embassy notified the British that the body had been recovered. But Commander Montagu learned that the important documents had already been scrutinized* and later resealed so that the British would not be suspicious. The secret information was transmitted to the German High Command, through a labyrinth of underground networks, to be evaluated. Now the true test of the months of assiduous* planning would come—the question remained, would the Germans swallow the bait?

exercises.text: The practical joker had the temerity* to perpetrate* a ________________ upon the Dean of Boys.
A good motion picture producer should be skilled in all the manifold* ________________ of film-making.
After wandering through the ________________ , the young hero came face to face with the dragon who was indigenous* to the caves.
When I asked the English teacher to ________________ my plan for the term paper, her incisive* comments were very helpful.
The ________________ quality of the artist’s latest painting is the antithesis* of her former style.

idiom.meaning: to turn a situation to one’s own advantage
idiom.example: The wrestler thought that he could pin me to the mat, but I quickly turned the tables on him.

days.passageZh: 一艘潜艇将尸体拖向大海。随后，“马丁少校”，那个从未成为的人，被滑入西班牙韦尔瓦海岸外浑浊的大西洋水域。信使外套上附着一个装有骗局组件的公文包。不久之后，西班牙大使馆通知英国人尸体已被发现。但蒙塔古指挥官得知，这些重要文件已经被仔细审查*，后来又重新封存，以免英国人起疑心。这些机密信息通过复杂的地下网络传送到德国最高指挥部进行评估。现在，数月精心策划的真正考验将到来——问题依然是，德国人会不会上钩？

days.title: A PUZZLE FOR HIS MAJESTY

examples.en: When Osama Bin Laden was killed, our public had reason to exult .

The conspirators had reason to exult, for all evidence attested to the fact that the German High Command was gullible about “Major Martin.” Their defense troops were moved away from the true invasion sites and deployed to areas that were inconsequential.
examples.zh: 阴谋者们有理由欢呼，因为所有证据都表明德国最高指挥部对“马丁少校”的轻信。他们的防御部队被调离真正的入侵地点，部署到无关紧要的地区。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be exult.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是欢欣鼓舞。

examples.en: She used the word "exult" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“欢欣”一词。

examples.en: Explaining that the bookkeeper was merely a gullible dupe,* the judge freed him from complicity* in the crime.

The conspirators had reason to exult, for all evidence attested to the fact that the German High Command was gullible about “Major Martin.” Their defense troops were moved away from the true invasion sites and deployed to areas that were inconsequential.
examples.zh: 阴谋者们有理由欢呼，因为所有证据都表明德国最高指挥部对“马丁少校”的轻信。他们的防御部队被调离真正的入侵地点，部署到无关紧要的地区。

examples.en: Andrew is so gullible, the way he still believes in Santa Claus, the Easter Bunny, the Tooth Fairy, and the Sandman at the age of fourteen.
examples.zh: 安德鲁太轻信了，十四岁时他依然相信圣诞老人、复活节兔子、牙仙和睡魔。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be gullible.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了轻信的含义。

examples.en: I could not solve the enigma of why an altruistic* person should exhibit such cupidity.*
“At the Admiralty,” Montagu replied, presenting the king with a genuine enigma.
examples.zh: “在海军部，”蒙塔古回答，向国王呈现了一个真正的谜团。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be enigma.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展现了什么是谜。

examples.en: She used the word "enigma" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“谜”一词。

days.passage: The conspirators had reason to exult, for all evidence attested to the fact that the German High Command was gullible about “Major Martin.” Their defense troops were moved away from the true invasion sites and deployed to areas that were inconsequential. Subsequently, when the actual attack took place, Allied casualties were minimized. After the war, Commander Montagu received a medal from the king of England. At the presentation ceremony, the king politely inquired where the young officer had earned his citation. “At the Admiralty,” Montagu replied, presenting the king with a genuine enigma.

exercises.text: Explaining that the bookkeeper was merely a ________________ dupe,* the judge freed him from complicity* in the crime.
As the audience watched the master ________________ his chess pieces, they applauded his virtuosity.*

When Osama Bin Laden was killed, our public had reason to ________________ .

days.passageZh: 阴谋者们有理由欢呼，因为所有证据都表明德国最高指挥部对“马丁少校”的轻信。他们的防御部队被调离真正的入侵地点，部署到无关紧要的地区。随后，当实际攻击发生时，盟军伤亡被最大限度地减少。战后，蒙塔古指挥官获得了英国国王颁发的奖章。在颁奖仪式上，国王礼貌地询问这位年轻军官的表彰来自何处。“在海军部，”蒙塔古回答，向国王呈现了一个真正的谜团。

days.storyTitle: Brother, Can You Spare a Dime?
days.story:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, in a ______ review of Social Security disability payments, focused on Jack Benson, a ______ Seattle panhandler. Mr. Benson had claimed that whatever money he collects on the street can be compared to the funds raised by legitimate charities, and, therefore, he is entitled to a federal deduction. Government officials regard his analogy as ______ and disagree. It is their contention that, since Benson’s income is unearned, it should be subtracted from his disability payments.

Mr. Benson may not be highly regarded as a street beggar but that didn’t stop him from going into the Federal District Court in Oregon to plead that his appeals for cash are an art form, thereby making him eligible for most of the $472 a month that he had been receiving. Not so, declared the government, quoting from a 1990 ruling that found that “money received through begging is better classified as ‘gifts’ rather than as ‘wages’ or ‘net earnings from self-employment.’”

Mr. Benson’s lawyer, plunging into the legal ______ , has not given up. She countered that, if Jack merely sat on a street corner with his hand out, the government had a good case. However, in her words, “Jack Benson is a ______ professional who has elevated begging to a respectable level because of his skill in actively seeking contributions.”

It may take all of Benson’s talent as a salesman to get the government to put some money in his collection basket.
days.storyZh:
美国卫生与公共服务部在______对社会保障残疾支付的审查中，重点关注了西雅图______乞讨者杰克·本森。本森先生声称，他在街头收集的钱可以与合法慈善机构筹集的资金相提并论，因此他有权获得联邦扣除。政府官员认为他的比喻______，持不同意见。他们主张，由于本森的收入是非劳动所得，应从他的残疾补助中扣除。

本森先生或许不被高高评价为街头乞丐，但这并未阻止他前往俄勒冈联邦地区法院辩护，称他的现金上诉是一种艺术形式，从而使他有资格领取大部分每月472美元的收入。政府声明事实并非如此，援引1990年一项裁决，该裁决认定“通过乞讨获得的钱更应归类为'礼物'，而非'工资'或'自雇净收入'。”

本森先生的律师深入法律______，并未放弃。她反驳说，如果杰克只是坐在街角伸出手，政府就有充分的理由。不过，用她的话说，“杰克·本森是一位______专业人士，他因积极寻求捐款的能力，将乞讨提升到了值得尊敬的层次。”

本森可能需要他作为销售员的所有才能，才能让政府给他的募捐篮子投钱。

days.title: TEACHING CHIMPANZEES TO TALK

examples.en: Following a number of abortive attempts to teach French, German, or English to chimps, the researchers persevered* until they hit upon the American Sign Language system that is often used by deaf persons.
examples.zh: 在多次尝试教黑猩猩法语、德语或英语未果后，研究人员坚持不懈*，最终发现了美国手语系统，这套系统常被聋人使用。

examples.en: The divers made an abortive attempt to rescue the dog from the murky* waters.
examples.zh: 潜水员曾试图从浑浊的水中救出这只狗，但未果。

examples.en: an abortive child
examples.zh: 一个流产的孩子

examples.en: They have had to modify the language somewhat in order to accommodate the animals’ spontaneous gestures.
examples.zh: 他们不得不对语言做些调整，以适应动物们的自发动作。

examples.en: Because Phil refused to modify his philosophy, the directors were forced to invalidate* his appointment.
examples.zh: 由于菲尔拒绝修改他的理念，董事们被迫取消*他的任命。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be modify.
examples.zh: 上下文清楚地展示了被修改的含义。

examples.en: They have had to modify the language somewhat in order to accommodate the animals’ spontaneous gestures.
examples.zh: 他们不得不对语言做些调整，以适应动物们的自发动作。

examples.en: A quarrel was precipitated* when the dietician refused to accommodate the patient’s special needs.
examples.zh: 当营养师拒绝满足患者的特殊需求时，双方爆发了争吵*。

examples.en: to accommodate ourselves to circumstances
examples.zh: 适应环境

examples.en: They have had to modify the language somewhat in order to accommodate the animals’ spontaneous gestures.
examples.zh: 他们不得不对语言做些调整，以适应动物们的自发动作。

examples.en: My English teacher admonished* me: “I realize that the speech was to be spontaneous , but it was not supposed to be incoherent* or fraught* with fallacious* statements.”
examples.zh: 我的英语老师训斥我：“我知道演讲应该是即兴的，但它不应该是语无伦次的*或充满谬误*的陈述。”

examples.en: He made a spontaneous offer of help.
examples.zh: 他突然提出帮助。

examples.en: With a mixture of innate movements and learned ones, some laboratory chimps now have an extensive vocabulary.
examples.zh: 由于天生动作与后天动作的混合，一些实验室黑猩猩现在拥有丰富的词汇量。

examples.en: His innate cunning allowed him to see through the spy’s subterfuge.*
examples.zh: 他天生的狡猾让他能看穿间谍的诡计。*

examples.en: innate ideas
examples.zh: 先天观念

days.passage: Two resourceful* psychologists at the University of Nevada have made splendid progress in vocabulary development in chimpanzees. Following a number of abortive attempts to teach French, German, or English to chimps, the researchers persevered* until they hit upon the American Sign Language system that is often used by deaf persons. They have had to modify the language somewhat in order to accommodate the animals’ spontaneous gestures. With a mixture of innate movements and learned ones, some laboratory chimps now have an extensive vocabulary.

exercises.text: His ________________ cunning allowed him to see through the spy’s subterfuge.*
The divers made an ________________ attempt to rescue the dog from the murky* waters.
Because Phil refused to ________________ his philosophy, the directors were forced to invalidate* his appointment.
My English teacher admonished* me: “I realize that the speech was to be ________________ , but it was not supposed to be incoherent* or fraught* with fallacious* statements.”

idiom.meaning: just in time
idiom.example: Hank hesitated about his term paper for two months and finally submitted it just under the wire.

days.passageZh: 内华达大学的两位足智多谋的心理学家在黑猩猩词汇发展方面取得了显著进展。在多次尝试教黑猩猩法语、德语或英语未果后，研究人员坚持不懈*，最终发现了美国手语系统，这套系统常被聋人使用。他们不得不对语言做些调整，以适应动物们的自发动作。由于天生动作与后天动作的混合，一些实验室黑猩猩现在拥有丰富的词汇量。

days.title: CHIMPANZEES ARE SURPRISINGLY SMART

examples.en: The lieutenant confessed to a veneer of ignorance in order to properly evaluate* his corporal’s resourcefulness.*
Washoe, the chimpanzee, has more than a veneer of intelligence; she can signal her desire to eat, go in or out, be covered, or brush her teeth.
examples.zh: 黑猩猩沃肖不仅表面上有智慧;她可以表达想吃东西、进出、被盖好或刷牙的意愿。

examples.en: to veneer a piece of furniture with mahogany
examples.zh: 用红木为家具贴面

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be veneer.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是表面功夫。

examples.en: Once the hoax had been concocted*, a myriad of problems arose.
In addition, she can make signs for “I’m sorry,” “I hurt,” “Hurry,” “Give me,” and a myriad of other terms that are familiar to young children.
examples.zh: 此外，她还能做出“对不起”、“我很痛”、“快点”、“给我”以及许多其他小朋友熟悉的词语。

examples.en: Earth hosts a myriad of animals.
examples.zh: 地球上栖息着各种动物。

examples.en: Earth hosts myriad animals.
examples.zh: 地球上栖息着无数动物。

examples.en: By embellishing* her work with urbane humor, the sophisticated playwright succeeded on Broadway.
This urbane animal can indicate that she craves more dessert by putting her fingers together (“more”) and then placing her index and second fingers on top of her tongue (“sweet”).
examples.zh: 这种都市型动物可以通过将手指合拢（“更多”），然后用食指和无名指放在舌头上（“甜”）来表示她渴望更多的甜点。

examples.en: 1949: George Orwell, Nineteen Eighty-Four, p12
examples.zh: 1949年：乔治·奥威尔，《一九八四》，第12页

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be urbane.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是都市化。

examples.en: The defendant was alleged* to have been an army deserter, but the judge said that was irrelevant to the case.
It is irrelevant that Washoe cannot actually talk.
examples.zh: 沃肖实际上不能说话这并不重要。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be irrelevant.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“无关紧要”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "irrelevant" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“无关紧要”这个词。

days.passage: Washoe, the chimpanzee, has more than a veneer of intelligence; she can signal her desire to eat, go in or out, be covered, or brush her teeth. In addition, she can make signs for “I’m sorry,” “I hurt,” “Hurry,” “Give me,” and a myriad of other terms that are familiar to young children. This urbane animal can indicate that she craves more dessert by putting her fingers together (“more”) and then placing her index and second fingers on top of her tongue (“sweet”). It is irrelevant that Washoe cannot actually talk. What is important, however, is the consummate* ease with which she has mastered her daily assignments.

exercises.text: Why did Silas Marner ________________ wealth and practice cupidity*?
Once the hoax had been concocted*, a ________________ of problems arose.
The defendant was alleged* to have been an army deserter, but the judge said that was ________________ to the case.
By embellishing* her work with ________________ humor, the sophisticated playwright succeeded on Broadway.
The lieutenant confessed to a ________________ of ignorance in order to properly evaluate* his corporal’s resourcefulness.*

idiom.meaning: not confined or in jail
idiom.example: Since the dangerous criminal was at large, all the townspeople began to buy dogs for protection.

days.passageZh: 黑猩猩沃肖不仅表面上有智慧;她可以表达想吃东西、进出、被盖好或刷牙的意愿。此外，她还能做出“对不起”、“我很痛”、“快点”、“给我”以及许多其他小朋友熟悉的词语。这种都市型动物可以通过将手指合拢（“更多”），然后用食指和无名指放在舌头上（“甜”）来表示她渴望更多的甜点。沃肖实际上不能说话这并不重要。然而，重要的是她轻松掌握日常作业的能力。

examples.en: The animal did beautifully, convincing the couple of the inherent ability of the chimpanzee.
examples.zh: 这只动物表现得非常出色，让这对夫妇相信了黑猩猩与生俱来的能力。

examples.en: Certain mice have an inherent alertness that enables them to conquer the researchers’ labyrinths.*

examples.en: Cinema buffs who have seen Tarzan’s clever monkey romp through the jungle also recognize the latent intelligence of those animals.
examples.zh: 看过泰山那聪明猴子在丛林中奔跑的影迷也会认出这些动物潜藏的智慧。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be latent.

days.passage: The chimpanzees are deemed by scientists to be the closest to man of all the living apes; consequently, they are fairly easy to train. Several years ago, two married researchers embarked on an interesting project: they reared and trained a chimp in almost the same manner as they would have raised a child. The animal did beautifully, convincing the couple of the inherent ability of the chimpanzee. Cinema buffs who have seen Tarzan’s clever monkey romp through the jungle also recognize the latent intelligence of those animals.

days.passageZh: 科学家认为黑猩猩是所有现存猿类中最接近人类的;因此，它们相对容易训练。几年前，两位已婚研究人员开始了一个有趣的项目：他们以几乎养育孩子的方式抚养和训练黑猩猩。这只动物表现得非常出色，让这对夫妇相信了黑猩猩与生俱来的能力。看过泰山那聪明猴子在丛林中奔跑的影迷也会认出这些动物潜藏的智慧。

days.title: MORE FACTS ABOUT CHIMPS

examples.en: The other drivers were nettled* about the ease with which our car ascended* the tortuous road.
Chimps in the laboratory have demonstrated their ability to find their way out of the most tortuous maze.
examples.zh: 实验室里的黑猩猩已经证明了它们能在最曲折迷宫中找到出路的能力。

examples.en: Signs were posted in the lobby to prevent itinerant beggars and others of that ilk* from entering.
In his natural habitat* the chimpanzee is something of an itinerant.
examples.zh: 在它的自然栖息地*，黑猩猩有点像是游荡动物。

Chimps are loyal in their conjugal relationships, taking only one mate at a time.
