days.passageZh: 恋物癖*的衍生是与禁忌密切相关的实践。而神祇或超自然力量仅仅附身于一个物体并赋予其魔法，他们会惩罚那些违反禁忌行为或使用禁忌的轻率土著。如果禁忌被打破，违规者必须受到惩罚。然而，在许多情况下，神父举行特殊仪式后，社区的污点可能会被清除。通常，违反禁忌的人会因对自己所做可怕事情的恐惧而受到惩罚或丧命。

days.title: AN ABSURDITY

examples.en: Although it is probably universal human behavior to be contemptuous of the bizarre* superstitions practiced by inhabitants of unfamiliar cultures, it seems to be somewhat imprudent* to laugh at others before one takes a good, hard look at the absurd taboos* and fetishes* one accepts as part of one’s everyday life.
examples.zh: 虽然对陌生文化居民所实践的怪异迷信抱有轻蔑可能是普遍的人类行为，但在认真审视那些荒谬的禁忌和癖好之前，嘲笑他人似乎有些不明智*。

examples.en: Would relations between countries be simpler if a universal language were spoken rather than hundreds of separate ones?
examples.zh: 如果说一种通用语言，而不是数百种独立语言，国家之间的关系会不会更简单？

examples.en: She achieved universal fame.
examples.zh: 她获得了全球名声。

words.definition: expressing a feeling that something is worthless

examples.en: Although it is probably universal human behavior to be contemptuous of the bizarre* superstitions practiced by inhabitants of unfamiliar cultures, it seems to be somewhat imprudent* to laugh at others before one takes a good, hard look at the absurd taboos* and fetishes* one accepts as part of one’s everyday life.
examples.zh: 虽然对陌生文化居民所实践的怪异迷信抱有轻蔑可能是普遍的人类行为，但在认真审视那些荒谬的禁忌和癖好之前，嘲笑他人似乎有些不明智*。

examples.en: Bob felt contemptuous of his best friend after he saw him cheating during an exam.
examples.zh: 鲍勃在看到他最好的朋友考试时作弊后，对他感到轻蔑。

examples.en: I don't know that guy, but he just gave me a contemptuous look.
examples.zh: 我不认识那个人，但他刚才给了我一个轻蔑的眼神。

examples.en: Although it is probably universal human behavior to be contemptuous of the bizarre* superstitions practiced by inhabitants of unfamiliar cultures, it seems to be somewhat imprudent* to laugh at others before one takes a good, hard look at the absurd taboos* and fetishes* one accepts as part of one’s everyday life.
examples.zh: 虽然对陌生文化居民所实践的怪异迷信抱有轻蔑可能是普遍的人类行为，但在认真审视那些荒谬的禁忌和癖好之前，嘲笑他人似乎有些不明智*。

examples.en: Isn’t it somewhat absurd when the “dyed-in-the-wool” bigot, who illogically fears the taint* of close association with blacks (behavior that resembles fear of a taboo), spends most of the summer lying in the sun trying to acquire the color he claims to abhor?
examples.zh: 当那个“根深蒂固”的偏执狂，不合逻辑地害怕与黑人亲密交往带来的污点（这种行为类似于对禁忌的恐惧），整个夏天大部分时间都躺在阳光下，试图获得他声称厌恶的颜色时，这难道不荒谬吗？

examples.en: The teacher felt like laughing after he heard Sally’s absurd excuse for not having done her homework.
examples.zh: 老师听到莎莉那荒谬的借口没做作业后，差点笑出声。

words.definition: a person who is intolerant of other people or ideas

examples.en: Barnum’s philosophy suggests that we might do well to review the plethora* of publicity given to rabble-rousers and bigots.
examples.zh: 巴纳姆的理念建议，我们或许应该审视那些煽动者和偏执狂所获得的大量宣传。

examples.en: Attacked by the irate* crowd, the bigot asked the police for sanctuary.*
examples.zh: 遭到愤怒的人群攻击，这名偏执狂请求警方庇护。*

examples.en: Isn’t it somewhat absurd when the “dyed-in-the-wool” bigot, who illogically fears the taint* of close association with blacks (behavior that resembles fear of a taboo), spends most of the summer lying in the sun trying to acquire the color he claims to abhor?
examples.zh: 当那个“根深蒂固”的偏执狂，不合逻辑地害怕与黑人亲密交往带来的污点（这种行为类似于对禁忌的恐惧），整个夏天大部分时间都躺在阳光下，试图获得他声称厌恶的颜色时，这难道不荒谬吗？

examples.en: Isn’t it somewhat absurd when the “dyed-in-the-wool” bigot, who illogically fears the taint* of close association with blacks (behavior that resembles fear of a taboo), spends most of the summer lying in the sun trying to acquire the color he claims to abhor?
examples.zh: 当那个“根深蒂固”的偏执狂，不合逻辑地害怕与黑人亲密交往带来的污点（这种行为类似于对禁忌的恐惧），整个夏天大部分时间都躺在阳光下，试图获得他声称厌恶的颜色时，这难道不荒谬吗？

examples.en: I abhor some one who is constantly changing channels with a remote while I’m trying to read in the same room.
examples.zh: 我讨厌有人在我试图在同一个房间里看书时，总是用遥控器换台。

examples.en: I absolutely abhor being stuck in traffic jams
examples.zh: 我非常讨厌被堵在交通堵塞里

days.passage: Although it is probably universal human behavior to be contemptuous of the bizarre* superstitions practiced by inhabitants of unfamiliar cultures, it seems to be somewhat imprudent* to laugh at others before one takes a good, hard look at the absurd taboos* and fetishes* one accepts as part of one’s everyday life. Isn’t it somewhat absurd when the “dyed-in-the-wool” bigot, who illogically fears the taint* of close association with blacks (behavior that resembles fear of a taboo), spends most of the summer lying in the sun trying to acquire the color he claims to abhor? Since doctors tell us that excessive sun-tanning may be a cause of skin cancer, our strange yearning for sun-darkened skin has all the qualities of a fetish.*

idiom.meaning: set in one’s ways
idiom.example: He was a dyed-in-the-wool Republican who would not consider voting for a Democrat.

days.passageZh: 虽然对陌生文化居民所实践的怪异迷信抱有轻蔑可能是普遍的人类行为，但在认真审视那些荒谬的禁忌和癖好之前，嘲笑他人似乎有些不明智*。当那个“根深蒂固”的偏执狂，不合逻辑地害怕与黑人亲密交往带来的污点（这种行为类似于对禁忌的恐惧），整个夏天大部分时间都躺在阳光下，试图获得他声称厌恶的颜色时，这难道不荒谬吗？医生告诉我们过度晒黑可能是皮肤癌的原因，我们对阳光变暗皮肤的奇怪渴望具备了所有恋物癖的特征。*

days.title: GESUNDHEIT!

examples.en: During the Middle Ages most people believed that the devil could enter our bodies when we sneezed, because at that propitious* moment we left our bodies vulnerable.
examples.zh: 在中世纪，大多数人相信魔鬼可以在我们打喷嚏时进入我们的身体，因为在那个吉利的时刻，我们的身体变得脆弱。

examples.en: It appears that many computers are vulnerable to “viruses” that can cause great damage.
examples.zh: 看起来许多计算机容易受到“病毒”的影响，这些病毒可能造成巨大破坏。

examples.en: Racing car drivers are vulnerable to dangers that other sportsmen seldom face.
examples.zh: 赛车手容易受到其他运动员很少遇到的危险影响。

examples.en: However, this catastrophic* event could be avoided if another person immediately made an entreaty to God.
examples.zh: 然而，如果有人立即向上帝祈求，这一灾难性*事件是可以避免的。

examples.en: The murderer made a(n) entreaty to the governor for a pardon.
examples.zh: 凶手向州长请求赦免。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be entreaty.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展现了什么是恳求。

words.definition: custom that has been handed down

examples.en: Although the tradition continues today, few people are aware of its history.
examples.zh: 尽管这一传统至今仍在延续，但鲜有人知其历史。

examples.en: Eskimos have a(n) tradition of rubbing noses to show affection.
examples.zh: 爱斯基摩人有一种通过摩擦鼻子表达亲昵的传统。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be tradition.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是传统的意义。

examples.en: But it continues inviolable over the years because it usually represents our deepest fears.
examples.zh: 但多年来它依然不可侵犯，因为它通常代表我们最深的恐惧。

examples.en: Despite the inexorable* torture, 007 kept the inviolable secret of the labyrinth* leading to the underground headquarters.
examples.zh: 尽管遭受无情*折磨，007仍守护着通往地下总部的迷宫*不可侵犯的秘密。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be inviolable.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了不可侵犯的含义。

days.passage: During the Middle Ages most people believed that the devil could enter our bodies when we sneezed, because at that propitious* moment we left our bodies vulnerable. However, this catastrophic* event could be avoided if another person immediately made an entreaty to God. This was how the practice began of saying “God bless you” after someone sneezes. Although the tradition continues today, few people are aware of its history. A superstition originates in ignorance—when people are unsure of the causes of events. But it continues inviolable over the years because it usually represents our deepest fears.

exercises.text: Some bad habits ________________ in adolescence and continue throughout a person’s life.
The murderer made a(n) ________________ to the governor for a pardon.
Despite the inexorable* torture, 007 kept the ________________ secret of the labyrinth* leading to the underground headquarters.
It appears that many computers are ________________ to “viruses” that can cause great damage.
Eskimos have a(n) ________________ of rubbing noses to show affection.

custom that has been handed down

custom that has been handed down

idiom.meaning: a highly valuable asset, stock, or property In poker, the blue chips are those with the highest value.
idiom.example: My father’s broker recommended that for safety we invest in blue chip stocks only.

days.passageZh: 在中世纪，大多数人相信魔鬼可以在我们打喷嚏时进入我们的身体，因为在那个吉利的时刻，我们的身体变得脆弱。然而，如果有人立即向上帝祈求，这一灾难性*事件是可以避免的。这就是人们打喷嚏后说“上帝保佑你”的习俗的起因。尽管这一传统至今仍在延续，但鲜有人知其历史。迷信起源于无知——当人们不确定事件的原因时。但多年来它依然不可侵犯，因为它通常代表我们最深的恐惧。

reviewWords.word: to get off one’s high horse

reviewOptions.text: one who is not tolerant of others’ ideas
looking down on someone or something
to act like an ordinary person

days.story:
We are all caught up in the events that change history and the shape of the countries in Asia, Africa, and the Middle East. Each time a country changes its name or its borders, there are some people who have their work cut out for them. They are the map makers—the cartographers. These skilled artists know it is ______ to believe that this year’s borders will remain fixed. Has there ever been an ______ border?

Looking through an atlas of just a few years back, we realize it is simply an ______ of an ever-changing world. If there is one thing for map makers to do, it is to realize how ______ it is for them to keep abreast of world events.

The study of world history is replete with exciting events that have shaken the economic and political past. Geography is the physical rendering of these events. As history moves and changes our lives, it is up to the cartographer to take the ______ lines of a map and shape the picture of this world in motion. A quick search of Google Earth brings our ever-changing planet to our screen.
days.storyZh:
我们都卷入了改变历史和亚洲、非洲和中东国家形态的事件。每当一个国家改变国名或边界时，总有一些人面临艰巨的挑战。他们是制图者——制图师。这些技艺娴熟的艺术家知道，______相信今年的边界会保持固定。曾经有过______边界吗？

回顾几年前的地图集，我们意识到这只是一个不断变化世界的一个______。如果地图制作者必须做一件事，那就是意识到他们跟上世界事件的多么______。

世界历史的研究充满了动摇经济和政治历史的激动人心的事件。地理是这些事件的物理表现。随着历史的流逝和生活的变化，制图师必须将地图上的______线条塑造出这个运动世界的画面。在Google Earth上快速搜索，我们不断变化的地球出现在我们的屏幕上。

days.title: THE EXPLOSION OF KRAKATOA

examples.en: There are few sights that are more impressive and awesome than the eruption of an active volcano.
examples.zh: 没有什么比活火山喷发更令人震撼和壮观的景象了。

examples.en: Many of those who witnessed the first atomic explosion reported that it was an awesome sight.
examples.zh: 许多目击首次原子爆炸的人都表示，那是一场令人震撼的景象。

examples.en: pure awesome; made of awesome
examples.zh: 纯粹的酷炫;由酷炫制造

examples.en: There are few sights that are more impressive and awesome than the eruption of an active volcano.
examples.zh: 没有什么比活火山喷发更令人震撼和壮观的景象了。

examples.en: Perhaps the greatest volcanic eruption of modern times took place in 1883 when the island of Krakatoa in Indonesia blew up as the result of a volcanic explosion.
examples.zh: 现代史上最大规模的火山喷发或许发生在1883年，当时印度尼西亚的喀拉喀托岛因火山爆发而爆发。

examples.en: Fred had been known for his gentle ways, so his friends were stunned by the eruption of angry words that issued from him.
examples.zh: 弗雷德以温和著称，所以他的朋友们被他爆发出的愤怒话语震惊了。

examples.en: There are few natural events that so singularly* dwarf man’s puny attempts to control his environment.
examples.zh: 很少有自然现象能如此独特地**让矮人微不足道地试图控制他的环境。

examples.en: We were surprised by the puny resistance put up by the voracious* tiger to its capture.
examples.zh: 我们对这只贪婪的老虎对捕获它的抵抗感到惊讶。

examples.en: You puny earthlings are no match for Ming the Merciless!
examples.zh: 你们这些渺小的地球人根本不是明的对手！

examples.en: New islands were formed by the lava that poured out, and debris was scattered across the Indian Ocean for hundreds of miles.
examples.zh: 熔岩涌出形成了新的岛屿，碎片散布在印度洋数百英里内。

examples.en: After her house had burned to the ground, Mrs. Wiley searched through the debris for her valuable jewelry.
examples.zh: 房子被烧毁后，怀利夫人在废墟中寻找她珍贵的珠宝。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be debris.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了成为碎片的含义。

examples.en: Volcanic material, dispersed seventeen miles into the atmosphere, created startlingly beautiful sunsets for years afterwards.
examples.zh: 火山物质散布在大气中十七英里外，创造出多年间令人惊叹的美丽日落。

examples.en: The fluffy seeds of the milkweed are dispersed by the wind.
examples.zh: 乳草蓬松的种子被风吹散。

examples.en: The Jews are dispersed among all nations.
examples.zh: 犹太人分散在各民族中。

days.passage: There are few sights that are more impressive and awesome than the eruption of an active volcano. There are few natural events that so singularly* dwarf man’s puny attempts to control his environment. Perhaps the greatest volcanic eruption of modern times took place in 1883 when the island of Krakatoa in Indonesia blew up as the result of a volcanic explosion. An enormous tidal wave resulted that proved catastrophic* to the nearby coasts of Java and Sumatra. New islands were formed by the lava that poured out, and debris was scattered across the Indian Ocean for hundreds of miles. Volcanic material, dispersed seventeen miles into the atmosphere, created startlingly beautiful sunsets for years afterwards.

exercises.text: Fred had been known for his gentle ways, so his friends were stunned by the ________________ of angry words that issued from him.
We were surprised by the ________________ resistance put up by the voracious* tiger to its capture.
After her house had burned to the ground, Mrs. Wiley searched through the ________________ for her valuable jewelry.
Many of those who witnessed the first atomic explosion reported that it was an ________________ sight.
The fluffy seeds of the milkweed are ________________ by the wind.

idiom.phrase: as broad as it is long
idiom.meaning: it makes very little difference
idiom.example: Since both jobs pay $7.25 an hour and are equally boring, it is about as broad as it is long whether I take one or the other.

days.passageZh: 没有什么比活火山喷发更令人震撼和壮观的景象了。很少有自然现象能如此独特地**让矮人微不足道地试图控制他的环境。现代史上最大规模的火山喷发或许发生在1883年，当时印度尼西亚的喀拉喀托岛因火山爆发而爆发。随后出现了巨大的海啸，对爪哇和苏门答腊附近的海岸造成灾难性*。熔岩涌出形成了新的岛屿，碎片散布在印度洋数百英里内。火山物质散布在大气中十七英里外，创造出多年间令人惊叹的美丽日落。

days.title: A UNIVERSAL* DANGER

examples.en: Man’s ability to obliterate life on this planet has increased at a rapid rate.
examples.zh: 人类消灭地球生命的能力迅速增强。

examples.en: No matter how one tries to delete material from a computer, it is almost impossible to obliterate it.
examples.zh: 无论如何试图从电脑中删除材料，几乎不可能将其彻底抹去。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be obliterate.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了被抹杀的含义。

examples.en: We are now faced with the deplorable prospect of new weapons that can cause destruction of life and property on a scale far beyond our imagination.
examples.zh: 我们现在面临着令人痛心的新武器前景，这些武器可能对生命和财产造成远超想象的破坏。

examples.en: The hometown fans thought the umpire’s decision was deplorable .
examples.zh: 主场球迷认为裁判的判罚令人遗憾。

examples.en: Poor children are often accused of having deplorable manners, when they are, in fact, simply responding to society in ways that mirror how society treats them.
examples.zh: 贫穷的孩子常被指责举止不当，实际上他们只是以反映社会对待的方式回应社会。

examples.en: No matter who takes the first step to initiate a conflict, the possibility exists that the conflagration will spread and envelop the world.
examples.zh: 无论谁率先挑起冲突，都存在这场大火蔓延并席卷全球的可能性。

examples.en: She could not imagine how she was going to get him to initiate a conversation about marriage.
examples.zh: 她无法想象自己该如何让他主动谈论婚姻。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be initiate.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“入会”的含义。

examples.en: No matter who takes the first step to initiate a conflict, the possibility exists that the conflagration will spread and envelop the world.
examples.zh: 无论谁率先挑起冲突，都存在这场大火蔓延并席卷全球的可能性。

examples.en: Who could imagine a more bizarre* story than the one having to do with a cow causing the conflagration in Chicago?
examples.zh: 还有谁能想象比一头牛引发芝加哥大火的更离奇的故事呢？

examples.en: It took sixty firefighters to put out the conflagration.
examples.zh: 共有六十名消防员扑灭了这场大火。

examples.en: Much thought has been given to ways and means of preventing this catastrophe.* Some consider it mandatory* that the nuclear powers seek agreement on methods of limiting and controlling these weapons, for in the absence of such an agreement, we may rue the day atomic energy was made practical.
examples.zh: 关于防止这场灾难的方法和手段，已经进行了大量思考。* 有人认为核大国必须就限制和控制这些武器的方法达成共识，因为如果没有这样的协议，我们可能会后悔原子能的实现。

examples.en: You will rue that display of histrionics* when I asked you to help.
examples.zh: 你会后悔我让你帮忙时那种戏剧化*的表现。

examples.en: I rued the day I crossed paths with her.
examples.zh: 我后悔那天和她相遇。

days.passage: Man’s ability to obliterate life on this planet has increased at a rapid rate. We are now faced with the deplorable prospect of new weapons that can cause destruction of life and property on a scale far beyond our imagination. No matter who takes the first step to initiate a conflict, the possibility exists that the conflagration will spread and envelop the world. Much thought has been given to ways and means of preventing this catastrophe.* Some consider it mandatory* that the nuclear powers seek agreement on methods of limiting and controlling these weapons, for in the absence of such an agreement, we may rue the day atomic energy was made practical.

exercises.text: Who could imagine a more bizarre* story than the one having to do with a cow causing the ________________ in Chicago?
No matter how one tries to delete material from a computer, it is almost impossible to ________________ it.
You will _________________ that display of histrionics* when I asked you to help.
She could not imagine how she was going to get him to ________________ a conversation about marriage.
The hometown fans thought the umpire’s decision was ________________ .

idiom.meaning: swing for and against something
idiom.example: I told Charlie to give up his summer job and come cross-country biking with us. He’s blowing hot and cold on the deal at this point.

days.passageZh: 人类消灭地球生命的能力迅速增强。我们现在面临着令人痛心的新武器前景，这些武器可能对生命和财产造成远超想象的破坏。无论谁率先挑起冲突，都存在这场大火蔓延并席卷全球的可能性。关于防止这场灾难的方法和手段，已经进行了大量思考。* 有人认为核大国必须就限制和控制这些武器的方法达成共识，因为如果没有这样的协议，我们可能会后悔原子能的实现。

days.title: TAKEN FOR GRANTED

examples.en: Unfortunately, this congenial condition is fast disappearing.
examples.zh: 不幸的是，这种良好的状况正在迅速消失。

examples.en: The tree in front of my house has the dubious* honor of being the spot voted the most congenial by the dogs of the neighborhood.
examples.zh: 我家门前的那棵树有个不太光彩的荣誉，是邻居狗们投票选为最喜欢的地方。

examples.en: The congenial bartender makes the Hog’s Head an inviting place to hang out during the weekends.
examples.zh: 和蔼的调酒师让Hog's Head成为周末的聚会场所。

examples.en: There is no way to hoard water; there are many ways to conserve it.
examples.zh: 没有办法囤积水源;有很多方法可以保护它。

examples.en: Isn’t it a pity we can’t hoard the ideal days of autumn?
examples.zh: 难道不可惜我们不能珍惜理想的秋天吗？

examples.en: a hoard of provisions; a hoard of money
examples.zh: 一批补给;一大笔钱

examples.en: Only then did the residents begin to heed the sage advice to limit the wasteful uses of water.
examples.zh: 直到那时，居民才开始听取明智的建议，减少浪费用水。

examples.en: A man may be a sage everywhere, but at home he’s called a “square” by his youngsters.
examples.zh: 一个人或许在各处都是智者，但在家里，他的孩子们却称他为“死板”。

examples.en: Reminder to sage and report.
examples.zh: 提醒贤者并报告。

examples.en: Under the aegis of the Water Commissioner, citizens were encouraged to develop habits that would save water.
examples.zh: 在水务专员的指导下，市民被鼓励养成节约用水的习惯。

examples.en: It was fortuitous* that at the last moment the mayor offered the aegis of his office in finding a solution to the problem.
examples.zh: 幸运的是，市长在最后时刻提供了其办公室的庇护，协助寻找解决方案。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be aegis.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了成为aegis的含义。

examples.en: The continued imprudent* waste by each of us of this most basic resource will work to the detriment of all.
examples.zh: 我们每个人持续不慎地浪费这一最基本资源，将对所有人造成伤害。

examples.en: A settlement that causes detriment to neither side is imperative.*
examples.zh: 必须达成一个对双方都不造成损害的和解。*

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be detriment.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“有害”的含义。

days.passage: The presence of an ever-flowing supply of fresh, clean water is taken for granted. Unfortunately, this congenial condition is fast disappearing. As our population increases, as industry consumes more water each year, the level of our underground water supply sinks measurably. There is no way to hoard water; there are many ways to conserve it. During a particularly dry spell, New York City found its reservoirs going dry. Only then did the residents begin to heed the sage advice to limit the wasteful uses of water. Under the aegis of the Water Commissioner, citizens were encouraged to develop habits that would save water. The continued imprudent* waste by each of us of this most basic resource will work to the detriment of all.

exercises.text: Isn’t it a pity we can’t ________________ the ideal days of autumn?
A man may be a ________________ everywhere, but at home he’s called a “square” by his youngsters.
The tree in front of my house has the dubious* honor of being the spot voted the most ________________ by the dogs of the neighborhood.
It was fortuitous* that at the last moment the mayor offered the ________________ of his office in finding a solution to the problem.
A settlement that causes ________________ to neither side is imperative.*

idiom.meaning: in a bored or depressed state
idiom.example: Mary has been in the doldrums since her best friend moved away.

days.passageZh: 不断供应的新鲜清洁水源被视为理所当然。不幸的是，这种良好的状况正在迅速消失。随着人口增加，工业每年用水量增加，地下水供应的水位会明显下降。没有办法囤积水源;有很多方法可以保护它。在一段特别干旱的时期，纽约市发现水库开始干涸。直到那时，居民才开始听取明智的建议，减少浪费用水。在水务专员的指导下，市民被鼓励养成节约用水的习惯。我们每个人持续不慎地浪费这一最基本资源，将对所有人造成伤害。

days.title: AN AGELESS STORY

examples.en: Scientists have placed the longevity of the planet earth unbelievably into the future.
The natural question is, to what do these people owe their longevity?
examples.zh: 自然的问题是，这些人长寿的原因是什么？

examples.en: Grandfather had incredible longevity: he lived to be 105 years old!
examples.zh: 祖父寿命惊人：活到了105岁！

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be longevity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了长寿的含义。

words.definition: The quality of being long-lasting, especially of life.

examples.en: If you imbibe , don’t drive!
Frequently, the answer concerns the fact that the ancient one liked to imbibe regularly of some hard liquor.
examples.zh: 答案通常与古人喜欢定期饮用烈酒有关。

examples.en: to imbibe knowledge
examples.zh: 吸收知识

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be imbibe.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“喝酒”的含义。

words.definition: To drink (used frequently of alcoholic beverages).

examples.en: The boys struck virile poses to attract the girls on the beach.
The photograph will show an apparently virile man or robust woman.
examples.zh: 照片中会显示一个看似阳刚的男性或强壮的女性。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be virile.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是充满活力的。

examples.en: She used the word "virile" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“阳刚”一词。

words.definition: Being manly; having characteristics associated with being male, such as strength; exhibiting masculine traits to an exaggerated degree such as strength, forcefulness or vigor.

examples.en: Far from being senile , the old woman was considered the sage* of the neighborhood.
There are no signs that they have become senile.
examples.zh: 没有迹象表明他们已经老年痴呆。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be senile.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了痴呆的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "senile" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“痴呆”一词。

examples.en: It was deplorable* for us to see her doddering around the house with the aid of a cane.
Smoking a pipe, or sewing on some garment, these rare specimens of hardy humanity are far from the doddering folk we expect to see.
examples.zh: 抽烟斗或缝制衣物，这些罕见的坚韧人类，远非我们期待中那些蹒跚的人类。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be doddering.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了蹒跚的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "doddering" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地用了“蹒跚”这个词。

words.definition: To shake or tremble as one moves, especially as of old age or childhood; to totter.

days.passage: Every so often we can read about a man or woman who has reached an age far beyond the limits we ordinarily expect. Reports of a man in Chile or a woman in Turkey who has celebrated the 105th or 110th birthday occur regularly. The natural question is, to what do these people owe their longevity? Frequently, the answer concerns the fact that the ancient one liked to imbibe regularly of some hard liquor. The photograph will show an apparently virile man or robust woman. Somehow, people who reach this advanced age seem to remain eternally sturdy. There are no signs that they have become senile. Smoking a pipe, or sewing on some garment, these rare specimens of hardy humanity are far from the doddering folk we expect to see.

exercises.text: Far from being ________________ , the old woman was considered the sage* of the neighborhood.
Scientists have placed the ________________ of the planet earth unbelievably into the future.
It was deplorable* for us to see her ________________ around the house with the aid of a cane.
If you ________________ , don’t drive!
The boys struck ________________ poses to attract the girls on the beach.

idiom.meaning: study or work late into the night
idiom.example: If I’m going to pass the test tomorrow, I will have to burn the midnight oil tonight.

days.passageZh: 我们时不时会看到一些男人或女人的成长远远超出我们通常预期的年龄。智利有男性或土耳其有女性庆祝了第105或110岁生日的报道时有发生。自然的问题是，这些人长寿的原因是什么？答案通常与古人喜欢定期饮用烈酒有关。照片中会显示一个看似阳刚的男性或强壮的女性。不知为何，到了这个年纪的人似乎永远坚强。没有迹象表明他们已经老年痴呆。抽烟斗或缝制衣物，这些罕见的坚韧人类，远非我们期待中那些蹒跚的人类。

reviewWords.word: as broad as it is long

reviewOptions.text: infirm, weak as a result of old age
in a bored or depressed state

days.story:
Have we all become tired of the much used word “environment”? How often we hear or read about the ______ state of the world’s rivers, forests, air, and earth. When we lose sight of the fact that countless numbers of creatures have become extinct because their environment could no longer sustain them, then we ignore the possibility that these same changes could ______ many species that we take for granted.

Our life-style, and that of the billions of others on this earth, puts waste into the air and water. We may ______ this careless behavior. While there may still be enough clean water and air for us, the loss of animals and plants can only be a ______ to a good life for the generations that follow.

No one suggests that the solutions to our environmental problems are easy. The nations and people of the world are in competition for the limited riches of this planet. It will take the sagest and most dedicated leaders, under whose ______ educated and concerned citizens will live and work, to protect the environment.
days.storyZh:
我们是否都厌倦了“环境”这个常用词？我们经常听到或读到关于世界河流、森林、空气和大地的现状______。当我们忽视无数生物因环境无法再维持而灭绝的事实时，我们便忽视了这些变化可能______许多我们习以为常的物种。

我们的生活方式，以及地球上数十亿人的生活方式，都向空气和水中排放废弃物。我们可能会______这种粗心的行为。虽然我们可能仍有足够的清洁水和空气，但动植物的消失只能成为后代美好生活的一______。

没有人认为解决我们环境问题的方案是容易的。世界上的国家和人民都在争夺这个星球有限的财富。保护环境需要最聪明、最敬业的领导者，在他们的______领导下，受过教育和关心的公民生活和工作。

days.title: INFORMING THE PUBLIC

examples.en: The public, often lethargic, is susceptible* to a wide variety of influences.
examples.zh: 公众往往懒散，容易受到各种影响。

examples.en: We are all susceptible* to a lethargic feeling after a heavy meal.
examples.zh: 我们都容易在吃完一顿丰盛的饭后感到疲倦。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be lethargic.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了懒散的含义。

examples.en: The most prevalent of these is the mass media.
examples.zh: 其中最普遍的是大众媒体。

examples.en: Long hair among boys is so prevalent today, there is no longer a prohibition against it in most schools.
examples.zh: 如今男孩中留长发非常普遍，大多数学校已不再禁止长发。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be prevalent.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“普遍存在”的含义。

examples.en: These communications media—the press, radio, and television—have a paramount position in initiating,* influencing, and shaping public opinion.
examples.zh: 这些传播媒介——新闻媒体、广播和电视——在发起、影响和塑造公众舆论方面扮演着至关重要的角色。

examples.en: A good politician seeks the paramount issue in his community.
examples.zh: 一个优秀的政治家会关注社区中最重要的问题。

examples.en: Getting those credit cards paid off is paramount.
examples.zh: 把这些信用卡还清至关重要。

examples.en: Bearing this responsibility, the mass media are often accused of being remiss in their duty to inform the public.
examples.zh: 承担这一责任的主流媒体常被指责未能向公众提供信息。

examples.en: We would be remiss if we overlooked the importance of the Internet to the interchange of ideas and information.
examples.zh: 如果忽视互联网在思想和信息交流中的重要性，那将是我们的失职。

examples.en: I would certainly be remiss if I did not give credit where credit was due.
examples.zh: 如果我不给予应有的认可，那我肯定会失职。

examples.en: There has been a great deal of hostile comment leveled against these opinion molders.
examples.zh: 针对这些塑造舆论的人，已经收到了大量敌意评论。

examples.en: The audience became extremely hostile when the bigot* began to attack minority groups.
examples.zh: 当这位偏执狂*开始攻击少数群体时，观众变得极为敌对。

examples.en: a hostile country
examples.zh: 一个敌对国家

days.passage: Public opinion has an important place in a democracy. The public, often lethargic, is susceptible* to a wide variety of influences. The most prevalent of these is the mass media. These communications media—the press, radio, and television—have a paramount position in initiating,* influencing, and shaping public opinion. Bearing this responsibility, the mass media are often accused of being remiss in their duty to inform the public. There has been a great deal of hostile comment leveled against these opinion molders.

exercises.text: The audience became extremely ________________ when the bigot* began to attack minority groups.
Long hair among boys is so ________________ today, there is no longer a prohibition against it in most schools.
We are all susceptible* to a ________________ feeling after a heavy meal.
A good politician seeks the ________________ issue in his community.
We would be ________________ if we overlooked the importance of the Internet to the interchange of ideas and information.

idiom.meaning: to make fine distinctions
idiom.example: The mother and child spent a great deal of time arguing about the hair-splitting question of whether “going to bed” meant lights out or not.

days.passageZh: 公众舆论在民主社会中占有重要地位。公众往往懒散，容易受到各种影响。其中最普遍的是大众媒体。这些传播媒介——新闻媒体、广播和电视——在发起、影响和塑造公众舆论方面扮演着至关重要的角色。承担这一责任的主流媒体常被指责未能向公众提供信息。针对这些塑造舆论的人，已经收到了大量敌意评论。

days.title: THE LACK OF FOREIGN NEWS

examples.en: Many a husband has been given a rebuke for having imbibed* too fully at an office party.
The critics rebuke the press for the fact that most newspapers devote somewhat less than 10 percent of their news space to foreign items.
examples.zh: 批评者批评媒体，大多数报纸只将不到10%的新闻版面用于报道外国新闻。

examples.en: O Lord, do not rebuke me in Your anger or discipline me in Your wrath. (Psalm 6, BSB)
examples.zh: 主啊，不要用你的愤怒责备我，也不要用你的愤怒惩罚我。（诗篇6篇，BSB）

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be rebuke.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了被责备的含义。

examples.en: The female aversion to mice is considered absurd* by boys.
Why is there this aversion to foreign news?
examples.zh: 为什么大家对外国新闻如此排斥？

examples.en: Due to her aversion to the outdoors she complained throughout the entire camping trip.
examples.zh: 因为她不喜欢户外活动，整个露营过程中她一直在抱怨。

examples.en: Pushy salespeople are a major aversion of mine.
examples.zh: 咄咄逼人的销售人员是我非常反感的。

words.definition: Opposition or repugnance of mind; fixed dislike.

examples.en: Youngsters often do not evince any curiosity about the lives of their parents or grandparents.
Newsmen claim that readers evince no interest in foreign affairs.
examples.zh: 新闻记者声称读者对外交事务毫无兴趣。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be evince.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“显露”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "evince" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“evince”一词。

examples.en: Good manners are always in vogue .
In order to increase reader interest in foreign news, the vogue among editors is to sensationalize it to the point of distortion.
examples.zh: 为了增加读者对外国新闻的兴趣，编辑们的流行趋势是将新闻渲染到扭曲的程度。

examples.en: Miniskirts were the vogue in the '60s.
examples.zh: 迷你裙在60年代很流行。

examples.en: Hula hoops are no longer in vogue.
examples.zh: 呼啦圈已经不再流行了。

examples.en: After a superficial examination of the injured motorist, the doctor said that hospitalization was imperative.*
Many other papers do only the most superficial kind of reporting in this area.
examples.zh: 许多其他报纸在这方面只做最表面的报道。

examples.en: He always concentrates on the superficials and fails to see the real issue.
examples.zh: 他总是只关注表面问题，忽视了真正的问题。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be superficial.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了肤浅的含义。

days.passage: The critics rebuke the press for the fact that most newspapers devote somewhat less than 10 percent of their news space to foreign items. In many hundreds of papers this falls below two percent. Why is there this aversion to foreign news? Newsmen claim that readers evince no interest in foreign affairs. In order to increase reader interest in foreign news, the vogue among editors is to sensationalize it to the point of distortion. Many other papers do only the most superficial kind of reporting in this area.

exercises.text: The female ________________ to mice is considered absurd* by boys.
After a ________________ examination of the injured motorist, the doctor said that hospitalization was imperative.*
Many a husband has been given a ________________ for having imbibed* too fully at an office party.
Youngsters often do not ________________ any curiosity about the lives of their parents or grandparents.
Good manners are always in ________________ .

idiom.phrase: to strike while the iron is hot
idiom.meaning: to take an action at the right moment
idiom.example: As soon as John heard that his father had won in the lottery, he struck while the iron was hot and asked for an increase in his allowance.

days.passageZh: 批评者批评媒体，大多数报纸只将不到10%的新闻版面用于报道外国新闻。在数百家报纸中，这一比例低于2%。为什么大家对外国新闻如此排斥？新闻记者声称读者对外交事务毫无兴趣。为了增加读者对外国新闻的兴趣，编辑们的流行趋势是将新闻渲染到扭曲的程度。许多其他报纸在这方面只做最表面的报道。

days.title: PLAYING IT SAFE

examples.en: The editor must include or jettison items as he sees fit.
examples.zh: 编辑必须根据自己的意愿添加或删减内容。

examples.en: The pilot decided it would be imprudent* to jettison his fuel over the populated area.
examples.zh: 飞行员认为在有人居住的区域抛弃燃油是不明智的*。

examples.en: The ballooners had to jettison all of their sand bags to make it over the final hill.
examples.zh: 气球飞行员不得不抛弃所有沙袋才能顺利越过最后一座山丘。

examples.en: It is inevitable that his ideas of what the reader want to know, or should know, are decisive.
examples.zh: 他对读者想知道或应该知道的观点是决定性的。

examples.en: It is inevitable that children question what their elders accept as tradition.*
examples.zh: 孩子们质疑长辈所接受的传统是不可避免的。*

examples.en: We were going so fast that the collision was inevitable.
examples.zh: 我们开得太快，碰撞不可避免。

examples.en: Because the newspaper owners do not want to endanger a lucrative business, there is the constant tussle between personal opinion and the desire not to offend too many readers or advertisers.
examples.zh: 由于报纸所有者不愿危及盈利的企业，个人观点与不冒犯过多读者或广告商之间的矛盾不断。

examples.en: Each year the contracts offered to star sports figures become more lucrative .
examples.zh: 每年，明星体育明星的合同都越来越有利可图。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be lucrative.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是有钱的。

examples.en: Because the newspaper owners do not want to endanger a lucrative business, there is the constant tussle between personal opinion and the desire not to offend too many readers or advertisers.
examples.zh: 由于报纸所有者不愿危及盈利的企业，个人观点与不冒犯过多读者或广告商之间的矛盾不断。

examples.en: Our conscience must always tussle against our yearning for what we know is taboo.*
examples.zh: 我们的良知必须时刻与对禁忌事物的渴望挣扎。*

examples.en: The two sets of fans were tussling before the game.
examples.zh: 两组球迷在比赛前扭打起来。

examples.en: It is intrinsic to the operation of all mass media that they avoid being extremist in their news coverage or editorials.
examples.zh: 所有大众媒体的运作本质就是避免在新闻报道或社论中极端化。

examples.en: Man sets the price of gold; it has no intrinsic value.
examples.zh: 人决定黄金的价格;它没有内在价值。

examples.en: While there may be almost no intrinsic difference among the many brands, advertising and packaging serves to importune* her to buy one rather than another.
examples.zh: 虽然众多品牌之间几乎没有本质区别，但广告和包装却让她不断催促她买其中一个而不是另一个。

days.passage: The average newspaper office receives many times the amount of foreign news than it has space to print. The editor must include or jettison items as he sees fit. It is inevitable that his ideas of what the reader want to know, or should know, are decisive. Because the newspaper owners do not want to endanger a lucrative business, there is the constant tussle between personal opinion and the desire not to offend too many readers or advertisers. It is intrinsic to the operation of all mass media that they avoid being extremist in their news coverage or editorials.

exercises.text: Our conscience must always ________________ against our yearning for what we know is taboo.*
Man sets the price of gold; it has no ________________ value.
The pilot decided it would be imprudent* to ________________ his fuel over the populated area.
It is ________________ that children question what their elders accept as tradition.*
Each year the contracts offered to star sports figures become more ________________ .

idiom.meaning: on a very rare occasion
idiom.example: His wife complained that they go out to dinner and a show once in a blue moon.

days.passageZh: 平均报社接收的外国新闻数量远远超过其印刷空间。编辑必须根据自己的意愿添加或删减内容。他对读者想知道或应该知道的观点是决定性的。由于报纸所有者不愿危及盈利的企业，个人观点与不冒犯过多读者或广告商之间的矛盾不断。所有大众媒体的运作本质就是避免在新闻报道或社论中极端化。

days.title: A FAVORITE NEWS SOURCE

examples.en: After the catastrophe,* there was an acute need for emergency housing.
The electronic media—television and radio—have more acute problems than does the press when it comes to news reporting.
examples.zh: 电子媒体——电视和广播——在新闻报道方面比新闻媒体面临更严重的问题。

examples.en: The word ‘cafe’ often has an acute over the ‘e’.
examples.zh: “café”一词通常在“e”上方有急音。

examples.en: He acutes his rising inflection too much.
examples.zh: 他把自己渐高的语调过于强调。

examples.en: She tried to get the gist of her message into a 25-word telegram.
The object is to transmit the gist of a story without supplying its background.
examples.zh: 目的是传达故事的要点，而不提供其背景。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be gist.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“gist”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "gist" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“gist”这个词。

examples.en: The young lover was susceptible* to transient feelings of jealousy when he saw his sweetheart dancing with his best friend.
Another difficulty of electronic news broadcasting is its transient nature; the viewers or listeners may miss an important story if their attention wanders.
examples.zh: 电子新闻广播的另一个难点是其瞬时性;如果观众或听众的注意力分散，可能会错过一个重要故事。

examples.en: a transient pleasure
examples.zh: 一种短暂的快乐

examples.en: a transient disease
examples.zh: 一种短暂性疾病

examples.en: The mayor made a terse statement in which he rebuked* his election opponent for making a contemptuous* accusation.

On the other hand, because radio and television present news in a more terse and exciting way, they are accepted as the most cogent presentation of news and are preferred and believed above newspapers by most people.
examples.zh: 另一方面，由于广播和电视以更简洁、更激动人心的方式呈现新闻，它们被接受为最具说服力的新闻呈现方式，并被大多数人更倾向和相信，胜过报纸。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be terse.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了简洁的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "terse" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“简洁”这个词。

examples.en: The best debater makes the most cogent presentation.

On the other hand, because radio and television present news in a more terse and exciting way, they are accepted as the most cogent presentation of news and are preferred and believed above newspapers by most people.
examples.zh: 另一方面，由于广播和电视以更简洁、更激动人心的方式呈现新闻，它们被接受为最具说服力的新闻呈现方式，并被大多数人更倾向和相信，胜过报纸。

examples.en: The prosecution presented a cogent argument, convincing the jury of the defendant's guilt.
examples.zh: 控方提出了有力的论点，说服陪审团相信被告有罪。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be cogent.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了什么是有条理的。

days.passage: The electronic media—television and radio—have more acute problems than does the press when it comes to news reporting. A normal broadcast can cover only a small part of a news day. The object is to transmit the gist of a story without supplying its background. Another difficulty of electronic news broadcasting is its transient nature; the viewers or listeners may miss an important story if their attention wanders. On the other hand, because radio and television present news in a more terse and exciting way, they are accepted as the most cogent presentation of news and are preferred and believed above newspapers by most people.

exercises.text: After the catastrophe,* there was an ________________ need for emergency housing.
The young lover was susceptible* to ________________ feelings of jealousy when he saw his sweetheart dancing with his best friend.
She tried to get the ________________ of her message into a 25-word telegram.
The mayor made a ________________ statement in which he rebuked* his election opponent for making a contemptuous* accusation.
The best debater makes the most ________________ presentation.

idiom.meaning: postpone a decision while giving it some thought
idiom.example: He didn’t want to show his hand* immediately, so he agreed to sleep on it for a few more days.

days.passageZh: 电子媒体——电视和广播——在新闻报道方面比新闻媒体面临更严重的问题。普通广播只能覆盖一天新闻的一小部分。目的是传达故事的要点，而不提供其背景。电子新闻广播的另一个难点是其瞬时性;如果观众或听众的注意力分散，可能会错过一个重要故事。另一方面，由于广播和电视以更简洁、更激动人心的方式呈现新闻，它们被接受为最具说服力的新闻呈现方式，并被大多数人更倾向和相信，胜过报纸。

reviewWords.word: to strike while the iron is hot

reviewOptions.text: take action at the right moment

days.story:
History tells us that, in a showdown in 1881, a notorious outlaw, Billy the Kid, was killed. At least that is the ______ belief. The real Billy the Kid, William Bonney, is believed to have escaped and lived for many years in Texas. In fact, a man named Brushy Bill Roberts claimed to be the grown-up Billy the Kid.

When Roberts died in 1950, there was the ______ question about his true identity. As a result, a computer was brought in to test whether there was anything other than a ______ resemblance between the two men. A photo of the Kid and a photo of Roberts were compared on the computer.

In a ______ report from the computer technician, the identity of Roberts was proved to be different from that of the real Billy the Kid. Thus, computer analysis allows us to ______ the idea that Billy the Kid survived the famous gun duel.
days.storyZh:
历史告诉我们，1881年的一场决战中，臭名昭著的亡命之徒比利小子被杀。至少这是______的信念。真正的比利小子威廉·邦尼据说逃脱并在德克萨斯生活了多年。事实上，有个叫布鲁希·比尔·罗伯茨的人自称是成年的比利小子。

1950年罗伯茨去世时，关于他真实身份的疑问也浮现出______。因此，一台电脑被引入测试两人是否除了______相似之外还有其他相似之处。电脑上对比了小子的照片和罗伯茨的照片。

在计算机技术员的一份______报告中，罗伯茨的身份被证实与真正的比利小子不同。因此，计算机分析让我们能够推______比利小子在著名枪战中幸存的事实。

days.title: A MUSICAL WORLD

examples.en: Music reached its pinnacle in the nineteenth century.
examples.zh: 音乐在十九世纪达到了顶峰。

examples.en: The pinnacle of fame and success is often a transient* stage.
examples.zh: 名声和成功的巅峰往往是一个短暂的阶段。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be pinnacle.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是巅峰。

examples.en: There was a bewildering array of national schools and musical styles as the once obscure musician came into his own.
examples.zh: 随着这位曾经默默无闻的音乐家逐渐崭露头角，出现了令人眼花缭乱的各类国家流派和音乐风格。

examples.en: The president faced an imposing array of reporters.
examples.zh: 总统面对着众多令人敬畏的记者。

examples.en: drawn up in battle array
examples.zh: 列阵

examples.en: There was a bewildering array of national schools and musical styles as the once obscure musician came into his own.
examples.zh: 随着这位曾经默默无闻的音乐家逐渐崭露头角，出现了令人眼花缭乱的各类国家流派和音乐风格。

examples.en: Space telescopes are making our obscure planets ever clearer.
examples.zh: 太空望远镜让我们那些鲜为人知的行星变得更加清晰。

examples.en: an obscure passage or inscription;    The speaker made obscure references to little-known literary works.
examples.zh: 晦涩的段落或铭文;   演讲者提到了一些鲜为人知的文学作品。

examples.en: The ardent music lover turned to Vienna as the music center at the beginning of the nineteenth century.
examples.zh: 十九世纪初，热爱音乐的人们将维也纳视为音乐中心。

examples.en: The ardent baseball fan went to every home game.
examples.zh: 这位狂热的棒球迷每场主场比赛都去了。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be ardent.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了热情的含义。

days.passage: Music reached its pinnacle in the nineteenth century. Every leading nation produced its share of great composers. There was a bewildering array of national schools and musical styles as the once obscure musician came into his own. Music became a widespread and democratic art. The ardent music lover turned to Vienna as the music center at the beginning of the nineteenth century. However, Paris was not far behind, especially in the field of operatic music. As the century progressed, the Germans became paramount* in orchestral and symphonic music. The growth of German music can be said to have culminated with Ludwig van Beethoven.

exercises.text: The president faced an imposing ________________ of reporters.
The party will ________________ with the award for the most original costume.
The ________________ of fame and success is often a transient* stage.
The ________________ baseball fan went to every home game.
Space telescopes are making our ________________ planets ever clearer.

idiom.meaning: to make a beginning, to overcome stiffness between strangers
idiom.example: All after-dinner speakers break the ice by telling a story or joke at the start of their speeches.

days.passageZh: 音乐在十九世纪达到了顶峰。每个主要国家都培养了许多伟大的作曲家。随着这位曾经默默无闻的音乐家逐渐崭露头角，出现了令人眼花缭乱的各类国家流派和音乐风格。音乐成为一种广泛且民主的艺术。十九世纪初，热爱音乐的人们将维也纳视为音乐中心。然而，巴黎也紧随其后，尤其是在歌剧音乐领域。随着世纪的推进，德国人在管弦乐和交响乐领域成为主导者*。德国音乐的发展可以说在路德维希·范·贝多芬的出现中达到顶峰。

days.title: A GIANT COMPOSER

examples.en: Beethoven was able to free music from the traditions* that had tended to constrict it.
examples.zh: 贝多芬成功解放了音乐，摆脱了那些往往束缚它的传统。

examples.en: It is imprudent* for a youngster to constrict her circle of friends so that there is no opportunity to meet new people.
examples.zh: 年轻人限制朋友圈以阻止结识新朋友是不明智的。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be constrict.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“收缩”的含义。

examples.en: He was a child prodigy who held an important musical post at the age of 14.
examples.zh: 他是个神童，14岁时就担任重要的音乐职位。

examples.en: There is universal* wonder when some prodigy appears on the stage to perform at the age of 4 or 5.
examples.zh: 当某个天才在4、5岁时登台表演时，大家都会感到惊奇。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be prodigy.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是神童。

examples.en: Even though bereft of hearing at the age of 49, he did not falter in his work.
examples.zh: 尽管49岁时已失聪，他在工作上丝毫未曾停歇。

examples.en: The catastrophe* left him bereft of all his possessions.
examples.zh: 这场灾难让他失去了所有财产。

examples.en: Death bereaved him of his wife.
examples.zh: 他的妻子去世了。

examples.en: Even though bereft of hearing at the age of 49, he did not falter in his work.
examples.zh: 尽管49岁时已失聪，他在工作上丝毫未曾停歇。

examples.en: Though he knew well the danger involved, the knight did not falter as he entered the dragon’s cave.
examples.zh: 尽管他深知危险，骑士进入龙穴时丝毫不动摇。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be falter.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“失败”的含义。

examples.en: Some of his later compositions reflect his sadness with his physical condition, but they also evince* an exultation about man and life.
examples.zh: 他晚期的一些作品反映了他对身体状况的悲伤，但也表现出对人与生命的欢欣。

examples.en: She was filled with exultation when she learned her SAT score was near the maximum.
examples.zh: 当她得知SAT分数接近满分时，欣喜若狂。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be exultation.
examples.zh: 这个语境清楚地展示了欢欣鼓舞的含义。

days.passage: Beethoven was able to free music from the traditions* that had tended to constrict it. He was a child prodigy who held an important musical post at the age of 14. He was a successful concert pianist, but when his health began to fail he turned to composing. Even though bereft of hearing at the age of 49, he did not falter in his work. Some of his later compositions reflect his sadness with his physical condition, but they also evince* an exultation about man and life.

exercises.text: The catastrophe* left him ________________ of all his possessions.
She was filled with ________________ when she learned her SAT score was near the maximum.
It is imprudent* for a youngster to ________________ her circle of friends so that there is no opportunity to meet new people.
There is universal* wonder when some ________________ appears on the stage to perform at the age of 4 or 5.
Though he knew well the danger involved, the knight did not ________________ as he entered the dragon’s cave.

idiom.meaning: to be well prepared
idiom.example: When the enemy finally attacked the positions, the defenders were loaded for bear.

days.passageZh: 贝多芬成功解放了音乐，摆脱了那些往往束缚它的传统。他是个神童，14岁时就担任重要的音乐职位。他是一位成功的音乐会钢琴家，但当健康开始恶化时，他转而从事作曲。尽管49岁时已失聪，他在工作上丝毫未曾停歇。他晚期的一些作品反映了他对身体状况的悲伤，但也表现出对人与生命的欢欣。

days.title: A WORTHY SUCCESSOR

examples.en: Also a prodigy,* he was the object of vitriolic attacks by other composers because of the individuality of his work.
examples.zh: 他同样是个神童，*因其作品的个性而遭到其他作曲家的激烈攻击。

examples.en: The vitriolic language used by critics of the new play tended to obliterate* its good qualities.
examples.zh: 新剧评论家使用的尖刻言辞往往抹杀了其优点。

examples.en: a vitriolic taste
examples.zh: 一种毒辣的味道

examples.en: They heaped invective upon him for the intensely emotional quality and Germanic style of his writings.
examples.zh: 他们对他作品中情感丰富且带有日耳曼风格的批评。

examples.en: It is difficult to keep invective out of our discussion about the enemy.
examples.zh: 在我们讨论敌人时，很难避免带有谩骂。

examples.en: Politics can raise invective to a low art.
examples.zh: 政治有时会把谩骂提升到低俗的艺术。

examples.en: However, it was impossible to besmirch his talents for long, and he was soon one of the most popular composers in Europe.
examples.zh: 然而，他的才华难以长时间被玷污，他很快成为欧洲最受欢迎的作曲家之一。

examples.en: The candidate tried to besmirch his opponent’s record.
examples.zh: 这位候选人试图抹黑对手的政绩。

examples.en: The newspaper was on a campaign to besmirch the actor.
examples.zh: 该报正发起抹黑这位演员的运动。

examples.en: He produced voluminous varieties of compositions.
examples.zh: 他创作了大量不同的作品。

examples.en: One has to be amazed at the voluminous amount of information that can be stored on a computer chip.
examples.zh: 人们不得不惊讶于计算机芯片上能存储的海量信息量。

examples.en: a voluminous writer
examples.zh: 一位多产的作家

examples.en: Today, in retrospect, his originality is appreciated, and he is placed among the top romantic composers.
examples.zh: 如今回顾，他的原创性备受推崇，他被列为顶级浪漫主义作曲家之一。

examples.en: In the future we will, in retrospect , regard today’s bizarre* behavior as quite ordinary.
examples.zh: 将来回顾，我们会把今天这些怪异*的行为视为相当普通。

examples.en: 1800: Alexander Hamilton, Letter from Alexander Hamilton, Concerning the Public Conduct and Character of John Adams, Esq. President of the United States - To give a correct idea of the circumstances.., it may be useful to retrospect to an early period.
examples.zh: 1800年：亚历山大·汉密尔顿，《亚历山大·汉密尔顿关于美国总统约翰·亚当斯先生的公共行为与品格的信》——为了准确了解当时的情况......回顾早期时期可能很有帮助。

days.passage: A successor to Beethoven was Johannes Brahms. Also a prodigy,* he was the object of vitriolic attacks by other composers because of the individuality of his work. They heaped invective upon him for the intensely emotional quality and Germanic style of his writings. However, it was impossible to besmirch his talents for long, and he was soon one of the most popular composers in Europe. He produced voluminous varieties of compositions. Today, in retrospect, his originality is appreciated, and he is placed among the top romantic composers.

exercises.text: It is difficult to keep ________________ out of our discussion about the enemy.
One has to be amazed at the ________________ amount of information that can be stored on a computer chip.
The candidate tried to ________________ his opponent’s record.
In the future we will, in ________________ , regard today’s bizarre* behavior as quite ordinary.
The ________________ language used by critics of the new play tended to obliterate* its good qualities.

idiom.meaning: to cause great enthusiasm
idiom.example: Popular entertainers can be counted on to bring down the house at every public performance.

days.passageZh: 贝多芬的继任者是约翰内斯·勃拉姆斯。他同样是个神童，*因其作品的个性而遭到其他作曲家的激烈攻击。他们对他作品中情感丰富且带有日耳曼风格的批评。然而，他的才华难以长时间被玷污，他很快成为欧洲最受欢迎的作曲家之一。他创作了大量不同的作品。如今回顾，他的原创性备受推崇，他被列为顶级浪漫主义作曲家之一。

days.title: GRUFF BUT LIKEABLE

examples.en: However, we should not be surprised that an award winner is an egotist about his or her performance.
In his private life Brahms was considered by his friends as an egotist.
examples.zh: 在私人生活中，勃拉姆斯被朋友们视为自负者。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be egotist.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是自负。

examples.en: She used the word "egotist" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“自负者”这个词。

examples.en: We all expect humility from the actors and actresses who win the Academy Awards.
He was not noted for his humility.
examples.zh: 他并不以谦逊著称。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be humility.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了谦卑的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "humility" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“谦逊”一词。

examples.en: The pungent odor of burning leaves marks the autumn season.
Along with this quality, Brahms was known for his pungent sense of humor.
examples.zh: 除了这种品质，勃拉姆斯还以其辛辣的幽默感著称。

examples.en: I accidentally dropped the bottle of ammonia and after few seconds, a very pungent stench could be detected.
examples.zh: 我不小心把氨水瓶掉了，几秒钟后闻到了一股非常刺鼻的臭味。

examples.en: The critic gave a pungent review.
examples.zh: 评论家给出了尖锐的评论。

examples.en: Doctors agree that it is imperative* that inveterate smokers give up that imprudent* habit.
Brahms was an inveterate stay-at-home.
examples.zh: 勃拉姆斯是个坚定的居家主义者。

examples.en: an inveterate disease
examples.zh: 一种顽固的疾病

examples.en: an inveterate idler
examples.zh: 一个根深蒂固的懒散者

examples.en: The umpire was adamant about his decision to call the runner out.
Cambridge University conferred an honorary degree upon him, but he was adamant about staying at home and did not go to receive the honor.
examples.zh: 剑桥大学授予他荣誉学位，但他坚决留在家中，未亲自前往领取荣誉。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be adamant.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了坚决的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "adamant" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“坚决”一词。

days.passage: In his private life Brahms was considered by his friends as an egotist. He had an extremely lofty opinion of himself and his talents. He was not noted for his humility. Along with this quality, Brahms was known for his pungent sense of humor. While his closest friends could accept his biting jokes, others found him difficult to warm up to. Brahms was an inveterate stay-at-home. Cambridge University conferred an honorary degree upon him, but he was adamant about staying at home and did not go to receive the honor. Despite the ardent* and romantic nature of his music, Brahms never found the right girl and remained single throughout his life.

exercises.text: Doctors agree that it is imperative* that ________________ smokers give up that imprudent* habit.
The ________________ odor of burning leaves marks the autumn season.
The umpire was ________________ about his decision to call the runner out.
We all expect ________________ from the actors and actresses who win the Academy Awards.
However, we should not be surprised that an award winner is an ________________ about his or her performance.

idiom.meaning: to do a fair share of the work
idiom.example: Everyone in a pioneer family had to pull his or her own weight.

days.passageZh: 在私人生活中，勃拉姆斯被朋友们视为自负者。他对自己和自己的才能有极高的评价。他并不以谦逊著称。除了这种品质，勃拉姆斯还以其辛辣的幽默感著称。虽然他最亲密的朋友们能接受他的尖刻玩笑，但其他人却觉得难以与他打动。勃拉姆斯是个坚定的居家主义者。剑桥大学授予他荣誉学位，但他坚决留在家中，未亲自前往领取荣誉。尽管他的音乐充满热情和浪漫色彩，勃拉姆斯却始终未能找到合适的女孩，终生单身。

reviewOptions.text: to do a fair share of the work

days.story:
In ______ the year 1990 was a year of record high temperatures across the United States. The cause of this problem is complex. There are many proposed explanations, from an increase of population to the greenhouse effect. If, in fact, temperatures are continuing to rise as a result of human activity, there should be an ______ search for the causes and the cures.

Scientists are looking into even the most ______ aspects of modern society to determine what might be the long-range effects of our activities. They hope that investigations will ______ in a program to change the harmful ways we contribute to a dangerous trend.

A small increase in the earth’s temperature will lead to major difficulties for everyone. We should not ______ in our efforts to avoid such disasters.
days.storyZh:
______年，1990年是美国创下最高气温纪录的一年。这一问题的原因很复杂。有许多解释，从人口增加到温室效应。如果温度确实因人类活动持续上升，就应该______寻找原因和治疗方法。

科学家们甚至在研究现代社会最______的方面，以确定我们活动可能带来的长期影响。他们希望调查能______一个改变我们对危险趋势有害方式的项目。

地球温度的一点点上升都会给所有人带来重大困难。我们不应______避免此类灾难的努力。

days.title: A DANGEROUS SPORT

examples.en: During the Middle Ages most people believed that the devil could enter our bodies when we sneezed, because at that propitious* moment we left our bodies vulnerable.
examples.zh: 在中世纪，大多数人相信魔鬼可以在我们打喷嚏时进入我们的身体，因为在那个吉利的时刻，我们的身体变得脆弱。

examples.en: It appears that many computers are vulnerable to “viruses” that can cause great damage.
examples.zh: 看起来许多计算机容易受到“病毒”的影响，这些病毒可能造成巨大破坏。

examples.en: Racing car drivers are vulnerable to dangers that other sportsmen seldom face.
examples.zh: 赛车手容易受到其他运动员很少遇到的危险影响。

examples.en: There is the bedlam caused by the roaring motors that move the car from a standing start to 100 miles an hour in eight seconds.
examples.zh: 还有轰鸣的发动机声，将汽车从静止启动加速到每小时100英里，仅需八秒钟。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be bedlam.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是混乱。

examples.en: She used the word "bedlam" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“混乱”一词。

examples.en: One is shaken by the cacophony of the brakes, larger than the wheels and producing during the course of a 350-mile race enough heat to warm an eight-room house through a hard winter.
examples.zh: 刹车的喧嚣震撼了你，这些刹车比车轮还大，在350英里的比赛中产生的热量足以温暖一栋八居室的房子度过严冬。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be cacophony.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了喧嚣的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "cacophony" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“嘈杂”一词。

examples.en: The driver needs to be on the alert to exploit any mistake by an opponent, and he must be constantly aware of the propinquity of sudden death.
examples.zh: 驾驶员需要时刻警惕，利用对手的任何失误，并且时刻意识到突然死亡的接近性。

examples.en: The first trek to the summit of Mount Everest was a stunning exploit.
examples.zh: 第一次登顶珠穆朗玛峰的徒步是一次惊艳的壮举。

examples.en: Materialistic monsters who exploit "kind" folks will not have good outcomes, no matter how much comforts were ill-gained.
examples.zh: 那些利用“善良”人的物质主义怪物，无论获得多少舒适，都不会有好结果。

examples.en: The driver needs to be on the alert to exploit any mistake by an opponent, and he must be constantly aware of the propinquity of sudden death.
examples.zh: 驾驶员需要时刻警惕，利用对手的任何失误，并且时刻意识到突然死亡的接近性。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be propinquity.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了亲近的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "propinquity" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在论文中正确地使用了“近似”一词。

days.passage: Racing car drivers are vulnerable to dangers that other sportsmen seldom face. Drivers agree that controlling a car at top speeds on a winding course is a singularly* awesome* experience. There is the bedlam caused by the roaring motors that move the car from a standing start to 100 miles an hour in eight seconds. One is shaken by the cacophony of the brakes, larger than the wheels and producing during the course of a 350-mile race enough heat to warm an eight-room house through a hard winter. The driver needs to be on the alert to exploit any mistake by an opponent, and he must be constantly aware of the propinquity of sudden death. All of this makes car racing one of the most demanding games of all.

exercises.text: Astronauts are alert to the ________________ of sudden accidents.
The egotist* is ________________ to slights and insults.
Electronic music is considered nothing more or less than ________________ by many.
Advertisers spend large sums to ________________ the lucrative* teenage market.
The winning team’s dressing room was a scene of ________________ .

idiom.meaning: a costly and useless possession
idiom.example: When he discovered the 30-volume encyclopedia, dated 1895, in his attic, he knew he had a white elephant on his hands.

days.passageZh: 赛车手容易受到其他运动员很少遇到的危险影响。车手们一致认为，在蜿蜒赛道上以最高速度控制汽车是一种极其*棒*的体验。还有轰鸣的发动机声，将汽车从静止启动加速到每小时100英里，仅需八秒钟。刹车的喧嚣震撼了你，这些刹车比车轮还大，在350英里的比赛中产生的热量足以温暖一栋八居室的房子度过严冬。驾驶员需要时刻警惕，利用对手的任何失误，并且时刻意识到突然死亡的接近性。所有这些都让赛车成为最具挑战性的游戏之一。

days.title: THE MYSTERY OF CREATIVITY

examples.en: In order to create, it is said that a man must be disgruntled.
examples.zh: 据说要创作，人必须心怀不满。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be disgruntled.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了不满的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "disgruntled" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“不满”这个词。

examples.en: There is no infallible way to identify a potentially creative person.
examples.zh: 没有万无一失的方法能识别一个潜在的创造力。

examples.en: He knows about many things, but even he is not infallible.
examples.zh: 他知道很多事情，但即使是他也不是无懈可击。

examples.en: Try this infallible cure for hiccups.
examples.zh: 试试这个无误的打嗝良方。

examples.en: The speed-up in the sciences has forced schools and industry to seek a panacea for the shortages that they face.
examples.zh: 科学领域的加速迫使学校和产业界寻求解决短缺问题的灵丹妙药。

examples.en: A monorail will be a panacea for our traffic woes.
examples.zh: 单轨列车将成为解决我们交通问题的灵丹妙药。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be panacea.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是灵丹妙药。

examples.en: The paramount* objectives of the studies are to eradicate anything that will impede the discovery of creative talent and to exploit* this talent to the limit.
examples.zh: 这些研究的首要*目标是消除任何阻碍发现创造性人才的因素，并将这些才华发挥到极限。

examples.en: Smallpox was globally eradicated in 1980.
examples.zh: 天花于1980年被全球根除。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be eradicate.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“根除”的含义。

examples.en: The paramount* objectives of the studies are to eradicate anything that will impede the discovery of creative talent and to exploit* this talent to the limit.
examples.zh: 这些研究的首要*目标是消除任何阻碍发现创造性人才的因素，并将这些才华发挥到极限。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be impede.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被阻碍的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "impede" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在作文中正确使用了“阻碍”一词。

days.passage: In order to create, it is said that a man must be disgruntled. The creative individual is usually one who is dissatisfied with things as they are; he wants to bring something new into the world—to make it a different place. There is no infallible way to identify a potentially creative person. The speed-up in the sciences has forced schools and industry to seek a panacea for the shortages that they face. The need to discover and develop the creative person has been the source of much study. The paramount* objectives of the studies are to eradicate anything that will impede the discovery of creative talent and to exploit* this talent to the limit.

exercises.text: It is the prevalent* mood for youngsters to be ________________ with the world situation.
Many people hoped that the United Nations would be the ________________ for the problems of our time.
The criminal tried to ________________ all of the witnesses to the bizarre* murder.
An ________________ sign of spring is the blooming of the crocus.
Nothing could ________________ the bigot* from his vitriolic* verbal attack.

idiom.meaning: entirely, completely
idiom.example: The company moved its operations to another state lock, stock, and barrel.

days.passageZh: 据说要创作，人必须心怀不满。创造者通常是对现状不满的人;他想为世界带来新东西——让世界变得不同。没有万无一失的方法能识别一个潜在的创造力。科学领域的加速迫使学校和产业界寻求解决短缺问题的灵丹妙药。发掘和培养创造力的人才一直是大量研究的源泉。这些研究的首要*目标是消除任何阻碍发现创造性人才的因素，并将这些才华发挥到极限。

days.title: THE DUTCH

examples.en: The first impression one gets of Holland is that it is a calm, sedate, and simple land.
examples.zh: 人们对荷兰的第一印象是这里是一个宁静、安详且简单的土地。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be sedate.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展现了什么是镇静。

examples.en: She used the word "sedate" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“secalm”这个词。

examples.en: One gradually discovers this equanimity of daily existence is not in accord with the intrinsic* nature of the Dutch.
examples.zh: 人们逐渐发现，这种日常生活的平静与荷兰人本质的本质并不一致。

examples.en: With equanimity he hunted for a hare and soon found one.
examples.zh: 他镇定地寻找一只野兔，很快找到了一只。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be equanimity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是平静。

examples.en: These people are moved by strong feelings that are not compatible with the serenity of the world around them.
examples.zh: 这些人被强烈的情感所驱使，而这些情感与他们周围世界的宁静不兼容。

examples.en: a computer company that sells IBM compatibles
examples.zh: 一家销售IBM兼容设备的计算机公司

examples.en: This printer isn't compatible with my computer.
examples.zh: 这台打印机不兼容我的电脑。

examples.en: These people are moved by strong feelings that are not compatible with the serenity of the world around them.
examples.zh: 这些人被强烈的情感所驱使，而这些情感与他们周围世界的宁静不兼容。

examples.en: There is a conflict between the rigid, traditional* social rules and the desire for liberty and independence, both of which the Dutch revere.
examples.zh: 僵化的传统社会规则与对自由和独立的渴望之间存在冲突，而荷兰人对这两者都极为敬仰。

days.passage: The first impression one gets of Holland is that it is a calm, sedate, and simple land. The slow rhythm of life is even seen in the barges on the canals and the bicycles on the roads. One gradually discovers this equanimity of daily existence is not in accord with the intrinsic* nature of the Dutch. These people are moved by strong feelings that are not compatible with the serenity of the world around them. There is a conflict between the rigid, traditional* social rules and the desire for liberty and independence, both of which the Dutch revere.

exercises.text: There is something absurd* about a well-dressed, ________________ man throwing snowballs.
The ________________ of the countryside was shattered by the explosion.
The speaker lost his ________________ and began to use invective* when the audience started to laugh.
The boy and girl discovered they had many ________________ interests.
There are not many people in this world whom one can ________________ .

idiom.meaning: something to be proud of
idiom.example: If she could get the movie star’s autograph, she knew it would be a feather in her cap.

days.passageZh: 人们对荷兰的第一印象是这里是一个宁静、安详且简单的土地。生活的缓慢节奏甚至体现在运河上的驳船和公路上的自行车中。人们逐渐发现，这种日常生活的平静与荷兰人本质的本质并不一致。这些人被强烈的情感所驱使，而这些情感与他们周围世界的宁静不兼容。僵化的传统社会规则与对自由和独立的渴望之间存在冲突，而荷兰人对这两者都极为敬仰。

days.title: TULIP FEVER

examples.en: There soon developed an irrational demand for new species.
examples.zh: 很快，人们对新物种产生了非理性的需求。

examples.en: The number π is irrational.
examples.zh: π这个数是无理数的。

examples.en: In their avarice, speculators bought and sold the same tulip ten times in one day.

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be avarice.

examples.en: She used the word "avarice" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“贪婪”一词。

examples.en: There was an insatiable desire for each new color or shape.

examples.zh: 不久，不可避免的崩盘随之而来，灯泡需求迅速跌至谷底。

examples.en: The nadir of the sun is the axis of the shadow projected by the Earth.
examples.zh: 太阳的天底是地球投射出的阴影轴。

examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是低谷。

words.definition: dying, at the point of death

examples.en: With the moribund tulip market came financial disaster to thousands of people.
examples.zh: 郁金香市场的低迷也带来了成千上万的经济灾难。

examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“死亡”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "moribund" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“垂死”一词。

days.passage: The tulip reached Holland in 1593 and was, at first, looked upon as a curiosity. There soon developed an irrational demand for new species. Specimens were sold at awesomely* high prices. In their avarice, speculators bought and sold the same tulip ten times in one day. The entire Dutch population suffered from the craze. There was an insatiable desire for each new color or shape. At one point a man purchased a house for three bulbs! Before long the inevitable* crash came and the demand for bulbs quickly reached its nadir. A $1,500 bulb could be bought for $1.50. With the moribund tulip market came financial disaster to thousands of people.

exercises.text: Who is not vulnerable* to some measure of ________________ ?
The American consumer appears to have an ________________ need for new products.
He looked upon the last-place finish of his team with equanimity;* from this ________________ the only place to go was up.
We ought to expect some ________________ behavior from a senile* person.
With the expansion of the supermarket, the small, local grocery store is in a ________________ state.

dying, at the point of death

idiom.meaning: in a dangerous or exposed position
idiom.example: He went out on a limb and predicted he would win the election by a wide margin.

days.passageZh: 郁金香于1593年传入荷兰，起初被视为一种奇珍异彩。很快，人们对新物种产生了非理性的需求。标本价格极高。投机者贪婪地一天内买卖同一朵郁金香十次。整个荷兰民众都受到了这场热潮的折磨。他们对每一种新的颜色或形状都有着无法满足的渴望。有一次，有个男人买了一套房子，装了三个灯泡！不久，不可避免的崩盘随之而来，灯泡需求迅速跌至谷底。一个价值1500美元的灯泡只需1.50美元。郁金香市场的低迷也带来了成千上万的经济灾难。

reviewOptions.text: at the point of death, dying
in a dangerous or exposed position

days.instruction: Select seven of the twelve words below that can be used as more than one part of speech (for example: noun and verb, noun and adjective). Then compose sentences using each word both ways.

days.story:
For many years it has been the goal of computer specialists to perfect a machine that would understand human speech. The problem is that the speaker has to be alone and in a quiet room. Noise will ______ the computer’s ability. In the ______ of a special room, the computer works well.

Now, math wizards are trying to develop a computer that will read lips despite any surrounding ______ . While some of us think it ______ to believe that a computer can read lips, the experiments go on. And there has been some success.

Progress in all aspects of computer science has been so remarkable that we hesitate to rule out any possibility. There is one ______ rule about the world of computers: the seemingly impossible gets done more quickly than we ever imagined.
days.storyZh:
多年来，计算机专家的目标一直是完善一台能够理解人类语音的机器。问题在于说话者必须独自一人，且在安静的房间里。噪声会______计算机的能力。在一个特殊房间的房间______，电脑运行良好。

现在，数学高手们正试图开发一台能够在周围环境下读唇的计算机______。虽然我们中的一些人认为相信计算机能读唇语______，但实验仍在继续。而且也取得了一些成效。

计算机科学各方面的进步如此显著，以至于我们不愿排除任何可能性。关于计算机世界有一个______规则：看似不可能的事情比我们想象的更快完成。

days.title: A SPORT FOR EVERYONE

examples.en: This sport allows the speedy and lithe athlete to join with the slower and obese one in a team effort.
examples.zh: 这项运动让速度快、身形灵活的运动员与较慢且肥胖的运动员组成团队合作。

examples.zh: 轻盈天气

examples.en: lithe body
examples.zh: 苗条的身体

examples.zh: 这项运动让速度快、身形灵活的运动员与较慢且肥胖的运动员组成团队合作。

examples.en: He is in a state of bliss when his team executes a movement to perfection.

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be bliss.

examples.en: However, there is no one more pathetic than the same fan when the opposition functions to equal perfection.
examples.zh: 然而，当对手同样发挥完美时，没有人比同一个球迷更可悲了。

examples.en: The child’s pathetic pleas for forgiveness stirred the young man’s heart.
examples.zh: 孩子可怜的求饶激起了年轻人的心。

examples.en: You can't even run two miles? That’s pathetic.
examples.zh: 你连两英里都跑不了？真可悲。

days.passage: Of the many highly popular sports in the United States, football must be rated around the top. This sport allows the speedy and lithe athlete to join with the slower and obese one in a team effort. The skills and strengths of many men are welded together so that one team may work as a unit to gain mastery over its opponent. The knowledgeable adherent of a team can follow action covering many parts of the playing field at the same time. He is in a state of bliss when his team executes a movement to perfection. However, there is no one more pathetic than the same fan when the opposition functions to equal perfection.

exercises.text: The disgruntled* ________________ switched his loyalty to the opposition party.
It was a pleasure to watch the ________________ body of the ballet dancer as she performed the most difficult steps.
There is something ________________ about a great athlete who continues to compete long after he has been bereft* of his talents.
His insatiable* hunger for sweets soon made him ________________ .
Oh, what ________________ could be seen in the eyes of the ardent* couple as they announced their engagement!

idiom.phrase: on the spur of the moment
idiom.meaning: on impulse, without thinking
idiom.example: On the spur of the moment he turned thumbs down* on the new job.

days.passageZh: 在美国众多极受欢迎的体育项目中，橄榄球必须排名前列。这项运动让速度快、身形灵活的运动员与较慢且肥胖的运动员组成团队合作。许多人的技能和优势被融合在一起，使一支队伍能够作为一个整体协作，争取对手的统治力。有经验的球队支持者可以同时关注球场多个部分的动作。当他的队伍完美执行动作时，他正处于一种幸福的状态。然而，当对手同样发挥完美时，没有人比同一个球迷更可悲了。

days.title: RAH! RAH! RAH!

examples.en: Cheer leaders exhort the fans, who may be in a state of apathy because their team is losing, to spur on the team.
examples.zh: 啦啦队员们鼓励那些可能因球队落败而冷漠的球迷们，激励球队。

examples.en: She used the word "exhort" correctly in her essay.

examples.en: Cheer leaders exhort the fans, who may be in a state of apathy because their team is losing, to spur on the team.

examples.en: In particularly close games between rivals of long standing, feelings begin to run high, and from time to time a fracas may break out in the stands.
examples.zh: 在长期宿敌之间的特别激烈的比赛中，情绪会变得高涨，偶尔看台上爆发冲突。

examples.en: While the teams compete below, the fan who is a bit inebriated may seek out a personal adversary.
examples.zh: 当队伍在下方比赛时，稍有醉意的球迷可能会寻找个人对手。

examples.en: She used the word "inebriated" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“醉酒”一词。

examples.en: While the teams compete below, the fan who is a bit inebriated may seek out a personal adversary.
examples.zh: 当队伍在下方比赛时，稍有醉意的球迷可能会寻找个人对手。

examples.en: He prepared to fight his adversary.
examples.zh: 他准备迎战对手。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be adversary.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是对手。

days.passage: The spectators at a football game play more than a superficial* role. A spirited cheer from the stands often gives the player on the field a reason to try even harder. Cheer leaders exhort the fans, who may be in a state of apathy because their team is losing, to spur on the team. In particularly close games between rivals of long standing, feelings begin to run high, and from time to time a fracas may break out in the stands. While the teams compete below, the fan who is a bit inebriated may seek out a personal adversary. On the whole the enthusiasm of the spectators is usually constricted* to cheering and shouting for their favorite teams.

exercises.text: The feeling of ________________ was so prevalent* during the election campaign that the candidates hardly bothered to make speeches.
Doctors ________________ obese* individuals to go on diets.
He was usually sedate,* but when ________________ he became hostile.*
The ________________ started when he besmirched* my good name.
My ________________ became disgruntled* because my arguments were so cogent.*

idiom.meaning: some small thing that spoils or lessens the enjoyment
idiom.example: He was offered a lucrative* position with the firm, but the fly in the ointment was that he would have to work on Saturday and Sunday.

days.passageZh: 橄榄球比赛中的观众不仅仅是表面作用。看台上的热烈欢呼常常给场上的球员激励他们更加努力。啦啦队员们鼓励那些可能因球队落败而冷漠的球迷们，激励球队。在长期宿敌之间的特别激烈的比赛中，情绪会变得高涨，偶尔看台上爆发冲突。当队伍在下方比赛时，稍有醉意的球迷可能会寻找个人对手。总体来说，观众的热情通常局限于为他们喜爱的球队欢呼和呐喊。

days.title: THE 23-INCH FOOTBALL FIELD

examples.en: This has the great advantage of permitting an indolent fan to sit in the comfort of his living room and watch two teams play in the most inclement* weather.
examples.zh: 这有个巨大优势，就是让懒散的球迷可以坐在客厅里，在最恶劣的天气下观看两支球队的比赛。

examples.en: The indolent girl resisted doing her homework.
examples.zh: 这个懒散的女孩抗拒做作业。

examples.en: indolent comfort

examples.en: However, some of the spirit, the gusto, is missing when one watches a game on a small screen away from the actual scene of the contest.
examples.zh: 然而，当人们在远离比赛现场的小屏幕上观看比赛时，缺少了一些精神和激情。

examples.en: He sang with more gusto than talent.
examples.zh: 他唱歌时更有激情而非才华。

examples.en: Also, the viewer is constantly exposed to a garrulous group of announcers who continue to chatter in an endless way throughout the afternoon.
examples.zh: 此外，观众还会不断被一群喋喋不休的播音员接连进行，整个下午都不停地喋喋不休。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be garrulous.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了多话的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "garrulous" correctly in her essay.

examples.en: Should the game be a dull one, the announcers discuss the most banal bits of information.

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be banal.

examples.en: Even in the poorest game there is constant chatter involving one platitude after another about the laudable* performances of each and every player.
examples.zh: 即使在最差的游戏中，也不断有关于每位球员表现值得称赞*的陈词滥调。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be platitude.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了陈词滥调的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "platitude" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“陈词滥调”一词。

days.passage: The football fan who cannot attend a contest in person may watch any number of games on television. This has the great advantage of permitting an indolent fan to sit in the comfort of his living room and watch two teams play in the most inclement* weather. However, some of the spirit, the gusto, is missing when one watches a game on a small screen away from the actual scene of the contest. Also, the viewer is constantly exposed to a garrulous group of announcers who continue to chatter in an endless way throughout the afternoon. Should the game be a dull one, the announcers discuss the most banal bits of information. Even in the poorest game there is constant chatter involving one platitude after another about the laudable* performances of each and every player.

exercises.text: He began to eat the food served at the sumptuous* feast with ________________ .
Men believe that women’s conversation is filled with ________________ comments concerning clothing or food.
During the most sultry* days of summer, one often hears the ________________ , “Is it hot enough for you?”
The ________________ person goes to great lengths to eschew* work.
She was usually so ________________ , we considered anything under a five minute speech as a cryptic* remark.

idiom.meaning: to go away without permission
idiom.example: The star player was fined $100 when he took French leave from the training camp.

days.passageZh: 无法亲自观看比赛的足球迷可以在电视上观看任意数量的比赛。这有个巨大优势，就是让懒散的球迷可以坐在客厅里，在最恶劣的天气下观看两支球队的比赛。然而，当人们在远离比赛现场的小屏幕上观看比赛时，缺少了一些精神和激情。此外，观众还会不断被一群喋喋不休的播音员接连进行，整个下午都不停地喋喋不休。如果比赛无聊，解说员们会讨论最平淡无奇的信息。即使在最差的游戏中，也不断有关于每位球员表现值得称赞*的陈词滥调。

days.title: WHAT’S ON?

examples.en: Nothing can put an avid* viewer into a pique more quickly than missing an important contest.

days.passage: One day each week is set aside for college football, and another for the professional brand. Most fans enjoy both varieties. Nothing can put an avid* viewer into a pique more quickly than missing an important contest. It is the dilettante who eschews* the amateur variety and watches only the professional games. The atypical fan will watch only his home team play; however, enthusiasts will continue to view the most nondescript contests involving teams that have no connection with their own town or school. Some intrepid* fans have been known to watch high school games when that was all that was offered. Public interest in football grows each year, while interest in other sports may be on the wane.
