days.story:
Of all the democracies in the world, the United States has the most lackluster record when it comes to citizen participation in elections. Every four years the experts try to analyze the reasons for voter ______ . Often the eligible voter turnout at election time falls below 50%. This, after months of political campaigning, including televised debates, is a ______ situation.

No matter how hard the candidates woo the voters, the end results are often disappointing. Are the voters so ______ that they would rather stay home watching television than cast a ballot? Does the voter feel that the candidates are stating one ______ after another and is therefore turned off?

The right to vote is so precious that revolutions have taken place where it has been denied. The civil rights struggles of the past were sparked by those who had been denied this right. The greatest ______ of democracy in this country is said to be the failure of citizen participation in the election process.
days.storyZh:
在所有民主国家中，美国在公民参与选举方面表现最为平淡。每四年，专家们都会尝试分析选民______的原因。选举时合格选民的投票率常常低于50%。经过数月的政治竞选，包括电视辩论，这确实是一个______的情况。

无论候选人如何努力争取选民支持，最终结果往往令人失望。选民真的______到宁愿待在家里看电视也不愿投票吗？选民是否觉得候选人一言______，因此感到反感？

投票权如此珍贵，以至于革命发生在被剥夺投票权的地方。过去的民权斗争正是那些被剥夺了这项权利的人所引发的。据说，这个国家民主的最大问题______是公民在选举过程中的参与失败。

days.passage: The man who best described the now extinct life aboard a steamer on the Mississippi River is Mark Twain. Having actually worked aboard the river boats, his writing captures the tranquil* or turbulent* events of those days. In his book about life on the Mississippi, Twain recalls the idyllic times when man was not in such a great rush to get from one place to another. One chapter deals with the races conducted between the swiftest of the boats. When a race was set, the excitement would galvanize activity along the river. Politics and the weather were forgotten, and people talked with gusto* only of the coming race. The two steamers “stripped” and got ready; every encumbrance that might slow the passage was removed. Captains went to extremes to lighten their boats. Twain writes of one captain who scraped the paint from the gaudy figure that hung between the chimneys of his steamer.

days.passage: The riverboat pilot was a man considered omnipotent by all. Mark Twain once held that high position. He writes that he felt at the zenith of his life at that time. Starting out as a fledgling pilot’s apprentice, he could not abjure dreams of the time he would become, “the only unfettered and entirely independent human being that lived in the earth.” Kings, parliaments, and newspaper editors, Twain comments, are hampered and restricted. The river pilot issued peremptory commands as absolute monarch. The captain was powerless to interfere. Even though the pilot was much younger than the captain, and the steamer seemed to be in imminent* danger, the older man was helpless. The captain had to behave impeccably,* for any criticism of the pilot would establish a pernicious* precedent that would have undermined the pilot’s limitless authority.

days.passage: Many incidents that took place aboard his ship are re-told by Twain. One has to do with a wealthy cattle man who was approached by three gamblers. The cattle farmer had let it be known that he had a great deal of money, and the gamblers were trying to wheedle him into a card game. He protested that he knew nothing about cards. His rustic appearance confirmed that fact. On the last night before landing the three gamblers got him drunk. When the first hand was dealt, a jubilant expression came over his face. The betting became furious. All of the proper decorum was put aside, and ten thousand dollars soon lay on the table. With the last wager one of the gamblers showed a hand of four kings. His partner was to have dealt the sucker a hand of four queens. At this point the victim, the charlatan, removed the veneer* of respectability, and showed a hand of four aces! One of the three professional gamblers was a clandestine* confederate of the “rich cattle farmer.” They had been planning this duplicity* for many weeks.

exercises.text: The child tried to ________________ from her mother the place where the cookies had been cached.*
They could discern* that the faith healer was a ________________ .
The ________________ life is supposed to be a tranquil* one.
Repress* your uncouth manners and act with ________________ at the party.
We were ________________ when our indolent* cousin got a job.

idiom.meaning: weak spot
idiom.example: He wanted to lead an ascetic* life, but his obsession with liquor was his Achilles heel.

days.passageZh: 吐温重述了许多在他船上发生的事件。其中一个故事涉及一位富有的牧牛人，他被三名赌徒接近。养牛农户公开表示自己有大量财富，赌徒们试图哄他参加一场牌局。他抗议说自己对牌一无所知。他那质朴的外表印证了这一点。在降落前的最后一晚，三个赌徒让他喝醉了。当第一手牌发牌时，他脸上露出了欢欣鼓舞的表情。赌注变得激烈起来。所有礼仪都被抛诸脑后，一万美元很快摆在桌上。最后一次赌注时，其中一位赌徒出示了四张K牌。他的搭档本该给傻瓜发四张Q。此时，受害者，这个骗子，摘下了体面的表象，亮出了四张A！三名职业赌徒中有一人是“富有的养牛农”的秘密同伙。他们已经策划了好几个星期*这场欺骗。

reviewWords.word: in the lap of the gods

reviewOptions.text: unlimited in power or authority, almighty

days.story:
A great deal of controversy surrounds the efforts of environmentalists to protect rare species of animals and birds from becoming ______ . In order to save these creatures from destruction stemming from a loss of forests or water pollution, environmentalists try to ______ large numbers of people to pressure politicians into passing conservation legislation. Often, however, these proposed ______ laws are thought to be a burden placed upon business, resulting in a loss of employment.

In the 21st century, the energy and food requirements of an increasing population are at odds with those who would set aside land for birds or animals. There is a great temptation to ______ the motives of environmental advocates. It will take people of good will and ______ to resolve the many difficulties that lie ahead.
days.storyZh:
围绕环保人士保护稀有动物和鸟类物种免于______的努力存在诸多争议。为了避免因森林流失或水污染而导致的破坏，环保人士试图______动员大量民众施压政客通过保护立法。然而，这些拟议的______法律常被认为是对企业的负担，导致失业。

进入21世纪，人口不断增长的能源和食物需求与那些为鸟类或动物划拨土地的人们产生了冲突。人们很容易去探究环保倡导者的动机______。解决未来诸多困难需要善意和______的人。

days.title: CHOOSE SAGELY*

examples.en: It is not a heresy to our democratic beliefs to state that pressure groups play an important part in our lives.
examples.zh: 说压力团体在我们生活中扮演重要角色，这并不违背我们的民主信仰。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be heresy.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了什么是异端。

examples.en: She used the word "heresy" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“异端”一词。

examples.en: The prudent person will choose between cogent* and specious propaganda efforts.
examples.zh: 谨慎的人会在有说服力*和虚假宣传努力之间做出选择。

examples.en: His prudent career moves reliably brought him to the top
examples.zh: 他谨慎的职业选择始终稳健地将他带到了顶峰

examples.en: Only prudent expenditure may provide quality within a restrictive budget
examples.zh: 只有谨慎的支出才能在有限的预算内提供质量

examples.en: While propaganda has the ostensible purpose of informing the public, the most fervid propagandists use methods that must be examined by the thoughtful citizen.
examples.zh: 虽然宣传表面上是为了告知公众，但最激进的宣传者采用的方法必须由有思想的公民审视。

examples.en: The ostensible reason for his visit to New York was to see his mother, but the real reason was to get to the Yankees game the next day.
examples.zh: 他此行表面上是为了看望母亲，但真正的原因是第二天去看洋基队的比赛。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be ostensible.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“表面化”的含义。

examples.en: While propaganda has the ostensible purpose of informing the public, the most fervid propagandists use methods that must be examined by the thoughtful citizen.
examples.zh: 虽然宣传表面上是为了告知公众，但最激进的宣传者采用的方法必须由有思想的公民审视。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be fervid.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是热情。

examples.en: She used the word "fervid" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“热情”一词。

examples.en: The ability to distinguish the spurious from the true facts requires more than a perfunctory* examination of prevalent* propaganda efforts.
examples.zh: 区分伪造与真实事实的能力，需要的不仅仅是对盛行宣传的敷衍*审查。

examples.en: His argument was spurious and had no validity.
examples.zh: 他的论点是无根据且毫无根据的。

examples.en: I tried to concentrate on the matter in hand, but spurious thoughts kept intruding.
examples.zh: 我试图专注于手头的事情，但各种杂念不断侵入。

days.passage: Today, the paramount* influence in the forming of public opinion is propaganda. It is not a heresy to our democratic beliefs to state that pressure groups play an important part in our lives. Propaganda makes one vulnerable* to the influences of others. The prudent person will choose between cogent* and specious propaganda efforts. While propaganda has the ostensible purpose of informing the public, the most fervid propagandists use methods that must be examined by the thoughtful citizen. The ability to distinguish the spurious from the true facts requires more than a perfunctory* examination of prevalent* propaganda efforts.

exercises.text: His ________________ appeal for action threw his adherents* into a frenzy*.
He accused the leader of the opposition of political ________________ , and the mob was exhorted* to burn his effigy*.
In the bedlam* that followed it was not ________________ to appear too apathetic*.
While the ________________ enemy was the opposition leader, the main purpose of this rash* behavior was the eradication* of all opponents.
In the conflagration* that followed, no one questioned whether the original charge had been ________________ .

idiom.meaning: to disregard or ignore
idiom.example: She was so piqued* at his uncouth behavior, she gave him the cold shoulder for over a week.

days.passageZh: 如今，公众舆论形成中最重要的*影响力是宣传。说压力团体在我们生活中扮演重要角色，这并不违背我们的民主信仰。宣传使人变得脆弱*，容易受到他人的影响。谨慎的人会在有说服力*和虚假宣传努力之间做出选择。虽然宣传表面上是为了告知公众，但最激进的宣传者采用的方法必须由有思想的公民审视。区分伪造与真实事实的能力，需要的不仅仅是对盛行宣传的敷衍*审查。

days.title: A FREE SOCIETY

examples.en: In a free society it is intrinsic* that individuals and groups have the inherent* right to propagate ideas and try to win converts.
examples.zh: 在自由社会中，个人和团体固有的权利*传播思想并努力争取信徒。

examples.en: to propagate sound or light
examples.zh: 以传播声音或光

examples.en: It takes 24 hours for password changes to propagate throughout the system.
examples.zh: 密码更改在系统内传播需要24小时。

examples.en: We do not look upon an idea different from ours as an anomaly that should be precluded*.
examples.zh: 我们不会把与自己不同的观点视为应当被排除的异常现象*。

examples.en: He is an anomaly among his friends in that he's the only one who's unmarried.
examples.zh: 他在朋友中是个异类，因为他是唯一未婚的。

examples.en: She disregarded some of the anamolies in the experiment, putting them down to miscalculation.
examples.zh: 她忽略了实验中的一些异常现象，归咎于计算失误。

examples.en: Nor do we permit only innocuous or congenial* beliefs and forbid those that we believe are dubious* or spurious*.
examples.zh: 我们也不允许仅仅无害或友好的*信念，禁止我们认为可疑*或虚假*的信念。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be innocuous.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了无害的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "innocuous" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“无害”这个词。

examples.en: In a country of competing pressures we are accosted* by a surfeit of propaganda that tends to overwhelm us.
examples.zh: 在一个充满竞争压力的国家，我们被大量宣传淹没*，这些宣传往往让我们不堪重负。

examples.en: A surfeit of wheat is driving down the price.
examples.zh: 小麦过剩导致价格下降。

examples.en: King Henry I is said to have died of a surfeit of lampreys.
examples.zh: 据说亨利一世国王死于过量的七鳃鳗。

examples.en: Thus, we live in a milieu of ubiquitous* bombardment from countless, and often unrecognized, propagandists.
examples.zh: 因此，我们生活在无数且常常未被识别的宣传者无处不在的轰炸环境中。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be milieu.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“环境”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "milieu" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“环境”一词。

days.passage: In a free society it is intrinsic* that individuals and groups have the inherent* right to propagate ideas and try to win converts. We do not look upon an idea different from ours as an anomaly that should be precluded*. Nor do we permit only innocuous or congenial* beliefs and forbid those that we believe are dubious* or spurious*. In a country of competing pressures we are accosted* by a surfeit of propaganda that tends to overwhelm us. Thus, we live in a milieu of ubiquitous* bombardment from countless, and often unrecognized, propagandists.

exercises.text: I must inveigh* against your attempt to ________________ the belief that your political system will result in a panacea* for all problems.
It is incongruous* to find an abstemious* person in a ________________ of avarice* and affluence*.
Siamese twins are considered a birth ________________ .
There appears to be no such thing as an ________________ heresy*.
When can we expect a respite* from the ________________ of TV commercials?

idiom.meaning: making no sense
idiom.example: Without rhyme or reason the pennant-winning baseball team decided to jettison* its manager.

days.passageZh: 在自由社会中，个人和团体固有的权利*传播思想并努力争取信徒。我们不会把与自己不同的观点视为应当被排除的异常现象*。我们也不允许仅仅无害或友好的*信念，禁止我们认为可疑*或虚假*的信念。在一个充满竞争压力的国家，我们被大量宣传淹没*，这些宣传往往让我们不堪重负。因此，我们生活在无数且常常未被识别的宣传者无处不在的轰炸环境中。

days.title: WHO LISTENS?

examples.en: As the quantity of propaganda becomes greater, ideas are presented in more strident tones in order to overcome the increased competition.
examples.zh: 随着宣传数量的增加，观点以更激烈的语调呈现，以克服竞争的加剧。

examples.en: The trumpet sounded strident against the string orchestra.
examples.zh: 小号在弦乐团中发出刺耳的声音。

examples.en: The artist chose a strident mixture of colors.
examples.zh: 艺术家选择了鲜明的色彩混合。

examples.en: The concomitant situation that develops with the stepped-up propaganda is one in which the individual retreats into a state of lassitude.
examples.zh: 随着宣传升级而产生的局面，是个体退缩到一种无力状态。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be concomitant.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“共产”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "concomitant" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“concomitant”一词。

examples.en: The concomitant situation that develops with the stepped-up propaganda is one in which the individual retreats into a state of lassitude.
examples.zh: 随着宣传升级而产生的局面，是个体退缩到一种无力状态。

examples.en: Because they had no person on whom to place their attention, they soon took on the appearance of extreme lassitude; most students became lethargic* and rested their heads on their desks.
examples.zh: 由于没有人可以关注，他们很快表现出极度的倦怠;大多数学生变得无精打采*，把头靠在课桌上。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be lassitude.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是懒散。

examples.en: It has the deleterious result of reducing its efficacy upon the individuals or groups who were its objective.
examples.zh: 其有害结果是降低了其对个人或群体的效力。

examples.en: deleterious effects
examples.zh: 有害影响

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be deleterious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了有害的含义。

examples.en: It has the deleterious result of reducing its efficacy upon the individuals or groups who were its objective.
examples.zh: 其有害结果是降低了其对个人或群体的效力。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be efficacy.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了效力的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "efficacy" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“效能”一词。

days.passage: As the quantity of propaganda becomes greater, ideas are presented in more strident tones in order to overcome the increased competition. Those who are the targets of the propaganda find it more difficult to discern* between or analyze the new and expanded pressures. The concomitant situation that develops with the stepped-up propaganda is one in which the individual retreats into a state of lassitude. He has an aversion* to all attempts to influence him. So we can see the intrinsic* weakness inherent* in an increased level of propaganda. It has the deleterious result of reducing its efficacy upon the individuals or groups who were its objective.

exercises.text: There are many ________________ dangers to obesity.*
Her ________________ voice added to the bedlam.*
After the frenzy* that accompanied the burning of the effigy,* they were all acutely* aware of a feeling of ________________ .
The gist* of the report was that smoking will have a ________________ effect on health.
The ________________ of new drugs cannot be determined without a plethora* of evidence.

idiom.meaning: final or last (swans are said to sing before they die)
idiom.example: The ex-champion said that if he lost this fight it would be his swan song.

days.passageZh: 随着宣传数量的增加，观点以更激烈的语调呈现，以克服竞争的加剧。那些成为宣传目标的人发现，更难区分或分析这些新的、扩大的压力。随着宣传升级而产生的局面，是个体退缩到一种无力状态。他对所有影响他的尝试都抱有反感*。因此，我们可以看到宣传水平增加中固有的*弱点*。其有害结果是降低了其对个人或群体的效力。

days.title: THE PEOPLE DECIDE

examples.en: Those who dissent from the official line may do so only in a clandestine* manner.
examples.zh: 持不同意见者只能以秘密*方式表达。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be dissent.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了异议的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "dissent" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“异议”一词。

examples.en: Where there is no open ferment of ideas, the possibility of discerning* the true from the spurious* is attenuated.
examples.zh: 当思想没有公开激荡时，辨别真伪的可能性就会减弱。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be ferment.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是发酵。

examples.en: She used the word "ferment" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“发酵”一词。

examples.en: Where there is no open ferment of ideas, the possibility of discerning* the true from the spurious* is attenuated.
examples.zh: 当思想没有公开激荡时，辨别真伪的可能性就会减弱。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be attenuated.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了被削弱的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "attenuated" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“减弱”一词。

examples.en: In a democracy, the inevitable* arbiter of what propaganda is to be permitted is the people.
examples.zh: 在民主社会中，决定哪些宣传被允许的不可避免的*裁判是人民。

examples.en: Television and film, not Vogue and similar magazines, are the arbiters of fashion.
examples.zh: 时尚的裁决者是电视和电影，而非《Vogue》等杂志。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be arbiter.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了仲裁者的含义。

examples.en: It is incumbent upon each citizen to choose between competing propagandas while remaining cognizant* of the value for a democracy in the existence of all points of view.
examples.zh: 每个公民都有责任在竞争的宣传之间做出选择，同时保持意识*，意识到民主在所有观点存在中的重要性。

examples.en: Proper behavior is incumbent on all holders of positions of trust.
examples.zh: 所有信任职位持有者都必须保持正确的行为规范。

examples.en: the incumbent toe of a bird
examples.zh: 鸟儿的脚趾

days.passage: The place of propaganda in a milieu* that is not free differs from its place in an open society. In a dictatorship there is no competing propaganda. Those who dissent from the official line may do so only in a clandestine* manner. Where there is no open ferment of ideas, the possibility of discerning* the true from the spurious* is attenuated. In a democracy, the inevitable* arbiter of what propaganda is to be permitted is the people. It is incumbent upon each citizen to choose between competing propagandas while remaining cognizant* of the value for a democracy in the existence of all points of view.

exercises.text: It is ________________ on us to be zealous* in combatting the deleterious* effects of drugs.
With each generation it becomes the vogue* for the youth to be in a state of ________________ .
The gist* of his ominous* suggestion was that we ________________ from the majority opinion.
The strength of her appeal was ________________ by the flamboyant* embellishments* for which many had a strong aversion.*
The Supreme Court is our ultimate* ________________ of legality.

idiom.meaning: to be discharged or fired
idiom.example: Despite the fact that he was so obsequious* toward the boss, he got the sack because he was lethargic* about doing his job.

days.passageZh: 宣传在非自由环境中的位置*与在开放社会中的位置不同。在独裁政权中，没有竞争性的宣传。持不同意见者只能以秘密*方式表达。当思想没有公开激荡时，辨别真伪的可能性就会减弱。在民主社会中，决定哪些宣传被允许的不可避免的*裁判是人民。每个公民都有责任在竞争的宣传之间做出选择，同时保持意识*，意识到民主在所有观点存在中的重要性。

days.story:
People are fascinated by those who say they can predict the future. Fortune tellers continue to attract gullible customers, and horoscopes are examined daily to see if there is something ______ to worry about in the day ahead. One specialist who seems to have found a way to predict something of our future is the palm reader. It is her belief that a long “life line” in the hand means the customer will enjoy longevity.

While this appears to be a ______ way to predict long life, a study done in England measured “life lines” of 100 corpses and came up with ______ support for the claim: the length of life matched the length of line. The longer the line, the older the person lived to be.

However, there are scientists who ______ with believers in this apparent connection. The “life line” of older people is longer only because the hand becomes more wrinkled with age. Length of line is a ______ of length of life, not the reverse, say scientists.
days.storyZh:
人们对那些声称能预测未来的人感到着迷。算命师继续吸引轻信的顾客，每天还会查看星座运势，看看接下来一天是否有______值得担心的事情。一位似乎找到了预测未来某些事情的专家是手相师。她相信，手中长长的“生命线”意味着客户能享受长久的使用。

虽然这似乎是预测长寿的______方法，但英国一项研究测量了100具尸体的“生命线”，并______支持这一说法：寿命长度与生命线长度相匹配。排队越长，代表该人寿命越长。

然而，也有科学家______相信这种表面联系的人。老年人的“生命线”之所以变长，只是因为手随着年龄增长而皱纹更深。科学家们表示，线长是生命长度的______，而不是相反。

days.title: ANYONE FOR GOOGLE?

examples.en: As automation permeates* many new areas of life, its effect upon us becomes concomitantly* more profound.
examples.zh: 随着自动化渗透*生活的许多新领域，它对我们的影响也变得*更深远。

examples.en: a profound investigation
examples.zh: 一场深刻的调查

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be profound.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了深刻的含义。

examples.en: Here they alleviate the burden of storing and providing us with an accumulation of information that is becoming more prodigious in this era of specialization and threatening to inundate* our society.
examples.zh: 在这里，它们减轻了存储和提供信息积累的负担，而这些信息在专业化时代日益丰富，甚至威胁要淹没我们的社会。

examples.en: Alcohol is often a cheap tool to alleviate the stress of a hard day.
examples.zh: 酒精往往是一种廉价的工具，用来缓解艰难一天的压力。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be alleviate.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“缓解”的含义。

examples.en: Here they alleviate the burden of storing and providing us with an accumulation of information that is becoming more prodigious in this era of specialization and threatening to inundate* our society.
examples.zh: 在这里，它们减轻了存储和提供信息积累的负担，而这些信息在专业化时代日益丰富，甚至威胁要淹没我们的社会。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be prodigious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“天才”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "prodigious" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“天才”一词。

examples.en: He expedited the search by alphabetizing the papers.
examples.zh: 他通过按字母顺序排列文件加快了搜索速度。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be expedite.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“加速”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "expedite" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“加速”一词。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be celerity.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了什么是“celerity”。

examples.en: She used the word "celerity" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“celerity”一词。

examples.en: He described the situation as celerity.
examples.zh: 他形容当时的情况非常迅速。

days.passage: As automation permeates* many new areas of life, its effect upon us becomes concomitantly* more profound. Information processing, blogs, search engines of all types have found their ways into businesses, as well as our homes, schools, and libraries. Here they alleviate the burden of storing and providing us with an accumulation of information that is becoming more prodigious in this era of specialization and threatening to inundate* our society. Youngsters in the primary grades now know how to manipulate their computers to extract information that would have taken their grandparents an eternity to produce. Machines whose celerity can scan thousands of words in nanoseconds help expedite the selection of pertinent* information for those schoolchildren.

exercises.text: We hoped that the arbiter* would ________________ the solution to the fracas* that had been so elusive* for a long time.
He accepted the lucrative* position with ________________ .
It is easy to construe* a superficial* remark to be a ________________ one.
If we cannot ________________ the harmful effects entirely, at least we can attenuate* them.
The enemy made a ________________ effort to repress* the uprising.

idiom.meaning: isolated from life; not in touch with life’s problems
idiom.example: Many artists have been said to be living in an ivory tower.

days.passageZh: 随着自动化渗透*生活的许多新领域，它对我们的影响也变得*更深远。信息处理、博客、各种搜索引擎都进入了企业，也进入了我们的家庭、学校和图书馆。在这里，它们减轻了存储和提供信息积累的负担，而这些信息在专业化时代日益丰富，甚至威胁要淹没我们的社会。小学年级的孩子们现在知道如何操控电脑，提取那些祖辈需要花费永恒才能产生的信息。能够在纳秒内扫描数千字的机器，帮助加快了学童对相关*信息的选择。

days.title: EVERYONE IS TALKING

examples.en: With cell phone companies competing for customers, they eschew* offering a paltry number of minutes of talking time.
examples.zh: 由于手机公司在争夺客户，他们避免*提供极其有限的通话时间。

examples.en: She made some paltry excuse and left.
examples.zh: 她找了个蹩脚的借口离开了。

examples.en: Could someone hope to survive on such a paltry income?
examples.zh: 有人能靠如此微薄的收入生存吗？

examples.en: Often, these personal talks are held in the most public places, and those within hearing find it difficult to condone the inconvenience caused by the indiscriminate* use of this ubiquitous* instrument.
examples.zh: 这些私人谈话往往在最公开的场所举行，听众难以接受这种无差别*无处不在*工具的无差别*使用所带来的不便。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be condone.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了被宽容的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "condone" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“宽容”一词。

examples.en: The cell phone user can take advantage of a plethora* of special deals and carry on with significant or trivial conversations for seemingly endless time, and in almost any location.
examples.zh: 手机用户可以享受大量*的特别优惠，几乎在任何地点进行无尽的重大或琐碎的对话。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be trivial.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是琐碎。

examples.en: She used the word "trivial" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“琐碎”一词。

examples.en: In the course of their studies of other cultures, anthropologists have reported numerous customs and practices that seem bizarre to the average American.
examples.zh: 在研究其他文化的过程中，人类学家报告了许多对普通美国人来说看似奇怪的习俗和做法。

examples.en: What a bizarre concept this must seem for today’s youth.
examples.zh: 对当今的年轻人来说，这个概念一定很奇怪。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be bizarre.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是怪异。

days.passage: Can anyone under the age of 20 remember a time when the dial telephone was the only method of voice communication over long distances? What a bizarre concept this must seem for today’s youth. It has become an antiquated* cultural form of personal contact. The instrument for the modern communicator is the cell phone, which has usurped the wire-connected stationary model. With cell phone companies competing for customers, they eschew* offering a paltry number of minutes of talking time. The cell phone user can take advantage of a plethora* of special deals and carry on with significant or trivial conversations for seemingly endless time, and in almost any location. Often, these personal talks are held in the most public places, and those within hearing find it difficult to condone the inconvenience caused by the indiscriminate* use of this ubiquitous* instrument.

exercises.text: Most of us scoff* at and belittle* ________________ behavior.
They exacerbated* a ________________ difference of opinion into a prodigious* conflict.
It is during a period of ferment* that a dictator can ________________ power.
Do you expect me to ________________ that reprehensible* act with such celerity?*
The most ________________ defects may have a deleterious* effect upon the efficacy* of that new process.

idiom.meaning: to enrich oneself on the sly or at every opportunity
idiom.example: He played up to his senile* aunt in the hope of feathering his nest when she made out her will.

days.passageZh: 有没有20岁以下的人还记得，拨号电话是长距离语音通信的唯一方式？对当今的年轻人来说，这个概念一定很奇怪。它已成为一种过时的*文化个人接触形式。现代通讯工具是手机，它已经取代了有线固定模式。由于手机公司在争夺客户，他们避免*提供极其有限的通话时间。手机用户可以享受大量*的特别优惠，几乎在任何地点进行无尽的重大或琐碎的对话。这些私人谈话往往在最公开的场所举行，听众难以接受这种无差别*无处不在*工具的无差别*使用所带来的不便。

days.title: THE FUTURE IS HERE

examples.en: The dial-up phone is restricted to the menial task of mere conversation.
examples.zh: 拨号电话只能进行简单的对话。

examples.en: a menial wretch
examples.zh: 一个卑微的可怜人

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be menial.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是卑微的。

examples.en: We have ardently* taken to the cell phone as a replacement for the venerable dial-up model.
examples.zh: 我们热切地*将手机作为传统拨号上网模式的替代品。

examples.en: Extraneous substances were found on my cup of water.

examples.en: Should one come upon an ambiguous event that one wishes to keep, one may photograph it or make it into a film for further study.
examples.zh: 如果遇到一个模糊的事件想要保留，可以拍照或制作成影片以便进一步研究。

examples.en: The politician was criticized for his ambiguous statements and lack of precision.
examples.zh: 这位政治家因言辞含糊且缺乏精确性而受到批评。

examples.en: He gave an ambiguous answer.
examples.zh: 他给出了一个含糊的回答。

examples.en: Should one be in an area that requires silence, there is an ability to utilize the succinct text messaging feature.
examples.zh: 如果身处需要安静的地区，可以使用简洁短信功能。

examples.en: Unlike general lossless data compression algorithms, succinct data structures retain the ability to use them in-place, without decompressing them first.
examples.zh: 与通用的无损数据压缩算法不同，简洁数据结构保留了原位使用它们的能力，而无需先解压。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be succinct.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了简洁的含义。

days.passage: We have ardently* taken to the cell phone as a replacement for the venerable dial-up model. The most striking feature of the cell phone is the variety of uses to which it can be put. The dial-up phone is restricted to the menial task of mere conversation. For the garrulous* person who isn’t restricted to one place, the mobile cell phone has a myriad* of uses. Should one be in an area that requires silence, there is an ability to utilize the succinct text messaging feature. Should one come upon an ambiguous event that one wishes to keep, one may photograph it or make it into a film for further study. Do not preclude* from the list of uses the access to your computer, music, and weather reports. One can hardly imagine an extraneous technical marvel that will not embellish* the cell phone in the future.

exercises.text: The prodigy* revered* the ________________ master.
To those who could understand every nuance* of the cryptic* message, there was nothing ________________ about it.
He could say the most vitriolic* things in a ________________ way.
Although she did not find it congenial,* we cajoled* our daughter into doing some of the ________________ tasks around the house.
The astute* voter is not susceptible* to the many ________________ shibboleths that saturate* a politician’s speech.

idiom.meaning: an incident or event that shows what will happen in the future
idiom.example: In retrospect* he should have seen the writing on the wall when his girlfriend gave him only a cursory* greeting on his birthday.

days.passageZh: 我们热切地*将手机作为传统拨号上网模式的替代品。手机最显著的特点是它可以被广泛应用于各种用途。拨号电话只能进行简单的对话。对于那些话多且不局限于一个地方的人来说，手机有无数*的用途。如果身处需要安静的地区，可以使用简洁短信功能。如果遇到一个模糊的事件想要保留，可以拍照或制作成影片以便进一步研究。不要*排除*访问电脑、音乐和天气报告的使用权限。很难想象未来手机还会有无关紧要的技术奇迹。

days.title: IT’S HAPPENING NOW

examples.en: The flood of new technology makes each modern marvel appear archaic within the briefest time period.
examples.zh: 新技术的洪流让每一个现代奇迹在极短的时间内显得过时。

examples.en: She used the word "archaic" correctly in her essay.

examples.en: An assiduous* examination of today’s communication methods will make clear how quickly a rabid purchaser of the newest product will want to emulate friends and buy the next one.
examples.zh: 对当今沟通方式的细致*分析会清楚地发现，狂热购买新产品的玩家会多快地想要模仿朋友并购买下一个产品。

examples.en: It would not be facetious to claim that scientists will persevere* in devising ways for us to contact each other by voice, photographs, and print messages that give us access* to a more salubrious social network.

examples.en: a rabid socialist
examples.zh: 一个狂热的社会主义者

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be rabid.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了狂犬病的含义。

examples.en: It would not be facetious to claim that scientists will persevere* in devising ways for us to contact each other by voice, photographs, and print messages that give us access* to a more salubrious social network.
examples.zh: 说科学家们会坚持不懈地想办法让我们通过语音、照片和印刷信息相互联系，从而获得更健康的社交网络。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be salubrious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是有益的。

examples.en: She used the word "salubrious" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“有益”一词。

days.passage: The flood of new technology makes each modern marvel appear archaic within the briefest time period. An assiduous* examination of today’s communication methods will make clear how quickly a rabid purchaser of the newest product will want to emulate friends and buy the next one. The cell phone that can track down the location of a user, or the music-downloading pod that has a potential* to record almost countless songs are being replaced with more powerful and exciting products. It would not be facetious to claim that scientists will persevere* in devising ways for us to contact each other by voice, photographs, and print messages that give us access* to a more salubrious social network.

exercises.text: Some maintain that the ascetic* leads a ________________ life.
With all candor,* I cannot wish for a return to the ________________ times when a moribund* society provided an opulent* existence for some, but a loathsome* life for the majority.
There is something ________________ about an egotist* who has the temerity* to begin a speech with, “In all humility* . . . .”
It is not prudent* to malign* or castigate,* or be derogatory* in any way toward a ________________ political adherent.*
The wish to ________________ a great person is laudable.*

idiom.meaning: joining with the majority; going along with the trend
idiom.example: Most advertisements showing many people using a product hope to convince the viewer to get on the bandwagon and buy the item.

days.passageZh: 新技术的洪流让每一个现代奇迹在极短的时间内显得过时。对当今沟通方式的细致*分析会清楚地发现，狂热购买新产品的玩家会多快地想要模仿朋友并购买下一个产品。能够追踪用户位置的手机，或有潜力*录制无数歌曲的音乐下载舱，正被更强大、更令人兴奋的产品所取代。说科学家们会坚持不懈地想办法让我们通过语音、照片和印刷信息相互联系，从而获得更健康的社交网络。

days.story:
One of the most interesting tests of a computer’s ability to “think” occurred in 1992. The world’s chess champion, a man of ______ mental ability in this sport, was challenged to compete against the most powerful computer programmed to play chess. The question was, could a machine ______ a human’s place as the best chess player in the world?

The match took place before hundreds of chess enthusiasts and was recorded on film. While the computer lacked the champion’s experience and emotional capacity, it worked with such ______ that it could search ahead for many thousands of choices, well beyond what any human could envision. In fact, the computer had already defeated many ______ chess masters in preparation for the contest.

The result of this test match was ______ as far as human self-esteem was concerned. The champion won fairly easily. However, there is almost total agreement that it is only a matter of time before we have an electronic chess champion, one incapable of making a blunder. At that point it will be checkmate for all of us.
days.storyZh:
1992年，计算机“思考”能力最有趣的测试之一发生在那个阶段。这位世界国际象棋冠军，在这项运动中拥有______的智力，他被挑战与最强大的国际象棋编程电脑对决。问题是，机器能否______人类成为世界上最优秀的国际象棋手？

比赛在数百名国际象棋爱好者面前进行，并被拍摄成影片。虽然电脑缺乏冠军的经验和情感能力，但它的运作方式极______，能够提前搜索成千上万的选择，远超任何人类的想象。事实上，计算机已经击败了许多______国际象棋大师，为比赛做准备。

这场测试赛的结果在人类自尊心方面是______的。冠军轻松获胜。然而，几乎所有人都同意，我们会有一个电子国际象棋冠军，一个永远不会犯错的人。到那时，我们所有人都将死。

days.title: AT A LOSS

examples.en: With the trivial* sum of five dollars in his pockets, Robert Lacy was feeling far from complacent about the future.
examples.zh: 口袋里只有那点微不足道的五美元，罗伯特·莱西对未来一点也不自满。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be complacent.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是自满。

examples.en: She used the word "complacent" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“自满”这个词。

examples.en: In fact, it was his somber estimate that no matter how frugal* he was, his money would run out before the next day.
examples.zh: 事实上，他沉重地估计，无论他多么节俭*，钱都会在第二天之前耗尽。

examples.en: a sombre situation
examples.zh: 一个沉重的局面

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be somber.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展现了沉重的含义。

examples.en: Hunger would debilitate him to the point where he could not continue his fervid* search for Evelyn.
examples.zh: 饥饿会让他虚弱到无法继续热烈寻找伊芙琳。

examples.en: The American Dream suffered a debilitating effect after the subprime crisis.
examples.zh: 次贷危机后，美国梦遭受了严重打击。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be debilitate.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是残疾。

examples.en: There was no hope of an impetuous stranger suddenly thrusting money upon him.
examples.zh: 没有希望会有冲动的陌生人突然把钱塞给他。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be impetuous.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了冲动的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "impetuous" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“冲动”一词。

examples.en: There was still less solace* for him in the hope that, after all this time, he might develop the occult power that would give him a mental image of where Evelyn could be found.
examples.zh: 对他来说，安慰依然少*，希望在经历了这么多年后，他能获得那种能让他脑海中看到伊芙琳所在之处的神秘力量。

examples.en: The Earth occults the Moon during a lunar eclipse.
examples.zh: 地球在月食期间遮挡了月亮。

examples.en: occult blood loss;  occult cancer
examples.zh: 隐匿性失血; 隐匿性癌症

days.passage: With the trivial* sum of five dollars in his pockets, Robert Lacy was feeling far from complacent about the future. In fact, it was his somber estimate that no matter how frugal* he was, his money would run out before the next day. He owed $3.50 in debts to friends; with the remainder he would have to eat enough to maintain his strength. Hunger would debilitate him to the point where he could not continue his fervid* search for Evelyn. There was no hope of an impetuous stranger suddenly thrusting money upon him. There was still less solace* for him in the hope that, after all this time, he might develop the occult power that would give him a mental image of where Evelyn could be found.

exercises.text: The guard was so ________________ about the danger of escape that he gave the prisoner only a cursory* inspection.
We should be prudent* in our play or work during very hot weather, because the sun has the power to enervate* and ________________ those that scoff* at its effects.
He looked for a propitious* moment to exhibit his ________________ abilities.
The deleterious* results of his irate* outburst put the previously jocose* audience in a ________________ mood.
They were so moved by the idyllic* setting, they exchanged surreptitious,* ________________ kisses.

idiom.phrase: to hit the nail on the head
idiom.meaning: to state or guess something correctly
idiom.example: When Charlie said there were 3,627 beans in that jar, he hit the nail on the head.

days.passageZh: 口袋里只有那点微不足道的五美元，罗伯特·莱西对未来一点也不自满。事实上，他沉重地估计，无论他多么节俭*，钱都会在第二天之前耗尽。他欠朋友3.50美元的债务;剩下的他必须吃足够的东西来维持体力。饥饿会让他虚弱到无法继续热烈寻找伊芙琳。没有希望会有冲动的陌生人突然把钱塞给他。对他来说，安慰依然少*，希望在经历了这么多年后，他能获得那种能让他脑海中看到伊芙琳所在之处的神秘力量。

days.title: MAKING PLANS

examples.en: He had begun by asking discreet questions of Evelyn’s former landlord.
examples.zh: 他一开始是向伊芙琳的前房东低调地问了些问题。

examples.en: John just doesn't understand that laughing at Mary all day is not very discreet.
examples.zh: 约翰就是不明白整天嘲笑玛丽并不掩饰。

examples.en: The landlord was recondite,* and all Robert had been able to glean from the cryptic* replies was that Evelyn had moved to a residence that catered to single women.
examples.zh: 房东说得很神秘，罗伯特从那些神秘的回答中只能得知伊芙琳搬到了一处专为单身女性服务的住所。

examples.en: Robert was in a hapless* situation; in this immense city his quarry could be hiding in one of dozens of such places.

days.passage: Robert had arrived in New York a week earlier. He had begun by asking discreet questions of Evelyn’s former landlord. There was no need to foment opposition at the very beginning. The landlord was recondite,* and all Robert had been able to glean from the cryptic* replies was that Evelyn had moved to a residence that catered to single women. Robert was in a hapless* situation; in this immense city his quarry could be hiding in one of dozens of such places. This would obviate* the possibility of his dashing from one place to another in an impetuous* manner. His search, while it had to be concluded with celerity,* could not be carried out in such slovenly fashion. He required a succinct* and meticulous* plan.

exercises.text: In order to ________________ trouble, they fabricated* a deplorable* and blatant* untruth.
She loathed* doing menial* tasks, and she did them in a ________________ manner.
Although it seemed inane,* they sought their ________________ in the midst of rustic* surroundings that were not its natural habitat*.
Despite the plethora* of offers to write her life story, the recently divorced movie queen kept a ________________ silence.
The reporters could not ________________ anything from her servants.

idiom.meaning: exactly on time
idiom.example: Despite his having taken forty winks,* he got to his appointment on the dot.

days.passageZh: 罗伯特一周前抵达纽约。他一开始是向伊芙琳的前房东低调地问了些问题。一开始就没必要煽动反对。房东说得很神秘，罗伯特从那些神秘的回答中只能得知伊芙琳搬到了一处专为单身女性服务的住所。罗伯特处于一个无助的境地;在这座庞大的城市里，他的猎物可能藏身于数十个类似的地方之一。这样就避免了他冲动地从一个地方跑到另一个地方的可能性。他的搜寻虽然必须迅速完成，但不能以如此邋遢的方式进行。他需要一个简洁且细致的计划。

days.passage: On the premise* that Evelyn knew she was being sought, Robert’s first step was to abjure fruitless* searching and place an ad in the leading morning newspaper. He would importune* in a most careful way for her return. The ad read, “Evelyn. Come out of hiding. I do not reproach you for your actions. I expect no penitent confession. There is nothing ambiguous* about my offer. Please contact. Robert.” He added a box number for a reply. When Robert went to the paper the next morning, he felt sanguine* about the chances of locating her. His evanescent concerns disappeared; there was a letter for him, and with tremulous* fingers he tore it open. It contained one sentence, and it was tantamount to a challenge; “If you really care about me, you will find me by midnight, Friday, Evelyn.”

exercises.text: The inveterate* gambler became ________________ and contrite when faced with the results of his reprehensible* behavior.
The optimist knows that the vicissitudes* of life are ________________ , and she always looks on the sanguine* side of things.
You should not condone* his sordid* behavior; rather, ________________ him for his fractious* manner.
At the zenith* of his career, he was ________________ to a final arbiter* on matters of economic policy.
In vain, the entire family tried to importune* him to ________________ gambling.

idiom.meaning: to become responsible for

days.passageZh: 基于伊芙琳知道自己被寻找的前提*，罗伯特的第一步是放弃徒劳*的搜寻，在领先早报上登广告。他会以极其谨慎的方式恳求她归来。广告写着：“伊芙琳。出来吧。我不责怪你的行为。我不指望忏悔的忏悔。我的提议没有任何含糊*。请联系。罗伯特。”他补充了一个回复的邮箱号。第二天早上罗伯特去报社时，他对找到她的机会感到乐观*。他那些转瞬即逝的担忧消失了;有一封信给他，他颤抖着手指撕开信封。信里只有一句话，几乎等同于挑战;“如果你真的在乎我，星期五午夜前你会找到我，伊芙琳。”

days.title: AT THE BALLET

examples.en: Evelyn was an anomaly*: she had a propensity for folk music and rock and roll dancing, and, at the same time, she was an avid* fan of classical ballet.

days.passage: Evelyn was an anomaly*: she had a propensity for folk music and rock and roll dancing, and, at the same time, she was an avid* fan of classical ballet. At one time she had been a fledgling* ballet dancer. Robert headed for a theater where a venerable* ballet company was performing. He knew he had to be wary so that Evelyn might not see him first. It was Tuesday evening; two days gone with so little to show. Only three more remaining before the deadline set by Evelyn. He tried hard to allay the sudden fear that came over him that he might not locate her. Nothing would deter him from succeeding! And so, although he was far from a connoisseur of the dance, he was standing among the throng* in the lobby, hoping it would be a propitious* evening for him.

exercises.text: The ________________ scoffs* at the dilettante,* who has only a veneer* of knowledge.
It is difficult to ________________ the concern of parents about how susceptible* their children are and how easily they succumb* to drugs.
Some girls have a ________________ for swarthy men who wear gaudy* clothes.
Her father warned her to be ________________ of adding the encumbrance* of a steady boyfriend as this would attenuate* her chances of finishing college.
This did not ________________ her from getting into a deplorable* situation due to her rash* and perverse* actions.

idiom.meaning: in a situation that is too difficult to handle
idiom.example: We thought he knew the ropes,* but we found him behind the eight ball* because he was out of his depth.

days.passageZh: 伊芙琳是个异类：她喜欢民谣和摇滚舞，同时又是古典芭蕾的狂热粉丝。她曾是一名初出茅庐的芭蕾舞者。罗伯特前往一家剧院，那里有一个历史悠久的*芭蕾舞团正在演出。他知道自己必须小心，免得伊芙琳先发现他。那是星期二晚上;两天过去了，收获寥寥无几。距离伊芙琳设定的截止日期只剩下三场。他努力平息突然涌上心头的恐惧——可能找不到她。没有什么能阻止他成功！所以，尽管他远非舞蹈鉴赏家，他却站在大厅的人群中，希望今晚对他来说是个吉祥的夜晚。

to state or guess something correctly

days.story:
There seems to be universal agreement that exposure to the ultraviolet light from the sun is deleterious to one’s health. Also, except for tobacco industry spokesmen, there is no dispute about the damage done to us from cigarette smoke. What is shocking is the fact that almost everything we once regarded as either beneficial, or harmless, soon gets challenged by scientists. We are urged to ______ foods that have high fat content. There go butter and cheese. Even milk has now been added to the list of foods of which we must be ______ .

Whatever diet we are on, we cannot become ______ about its nutritional value. We are left, ultimately, with the ______ thought that, sooner or later, almost everything we eat or drink may be found to jeopardize our health.

Given that there are many obstacles to maintaining good health, would it be wise to embrace every new laboratory report in order to ______ information? Let’s not discard old, proven, sensible food habits. Also, there is always the possibility that ice cream sundaes will be found to cure baldness, and that chocolate chip cookies will eliminate our cholesterol problems.
days.storyZh:
似乎普遍认为，暴露在太阳紫外线下对健康有害。此外，除了烟草行业发言人外，关于香烟烟雾对我们造成的伤害，没有争议。令人震惊的是，几乎所有我们曾经认为有益或无害的观点，很快都被科学家挑战了。我们被敦促______高脂肪的食物。黄油和奶酪就没了。甚至牛奶现在也被列入我们必须______的食物清单。

无论我们采用什么饮食，都不能对其营养价值______。最终，我们只能______地认为，迟早我们吃喝的几乎所有东西都可能危及我们的健康。

鉴于维持健康存在诸多障碍，是否明智地接受每一份新的实验室报告以______信息？我们不要抛弃那些老掉牙、经过验证的合理饮食习惯。此外，冰淇淋圣代也有可能被发现能治愈秃头，巧克力曲奇则可能消除我们的胆固醇问题。

days.passage: Robert was far from tranquil* as he waited in the lobby for almost an hour after the performance had begun. Disgruntled,* he quit the site of his vigil. He had to face the fact that he was making no tangible* progress. Tomorrow he would telephone several women’s residences. It was a cumbersome way of going about the hunt, but it was all that he could think of at the moment. He would interrogate the desk clerks, and perhaps he might uncover a pertinent* clue to Evelyn’s whereabouts. If he could only get someone to divulge her hiding place! Perhaps tomorrow would culminate* in success.

exercises.text: With rancor he faced the ________________ job of transporting the voluminous* records to his new office.

The hunter maintained a discreet* and wary* ________________ as he waited for the propitious* moment to bag his quarry*.
Even under duress,* he was adamant* and would not ________________ the secret.
The newly married couple selected the ________________ for their new home with meticulous* care.

idiom.phrase: to take a leaf out of someone’s book

idiom.example: The chip off the old block* took a leaf from his father’s book and never sowed wild oats*.

days.passageZh: 演出开始后，罗伯特在大厅等待了将近一个小时，心情远非平静*。他心生不满，*离开了守夜地点。他不得不面对自己没有任何实质进展的事实。明天他会给几位女性的住所打电话。这是一种繁琐的狩猎方式，但此刻他脑海中唯一能想到的就是这个。他会审问前台职员，也许能找到关于伊芙琳下落的相关线索。要是有人能透露她的藏身之处就好了！也许明天会以成功告终。

days.passage: The next day, Wednesday, saw Robert become more frustrated.* He would fluctuate between high hopes of finding Evelyn and unmitigated despair when he was almost ready to desist* in his search. The phone calls had elicited* almost nothing. Robert had rushed to one women’s residence when the clerk described a girl who might just be Evelyn. The desk clerk phoned to her room on the pretext* that she had a special delivery letter. Robert waited in the commodious lobby, replete* with large, antiquated pieces of furniture. He watched from a discreet* distance as she came down the stairs. One look at her wan* face, slovenly* dress, and disheveled hair was enough to inform Robert that he needed no further scrutiny.* This could not be his impeccable* Evelyn.

The current trend* in the stock market is for stocks to ________________ in a sporadic* fashion.

days.passageZh: 第二天，也就是星期三，罗伯特变得更加沮丧。* 他在寻找伊芙琳的希望与几乎准备放弃寻找时却又陷入无尽的绝望之间摇摆。电话几乎没有带来任何反应。当店员描述一个可能就是伊芙琳的女孩时，罗伯特急忙赶往一位女性住所。前台工作人员以有特快信为由打电话到她房间。罗伯特在宽敞的大堂里等候，那里摆满了*大型陈旧家具*。他从一个隐秘的距离看着她下楼。只要看一眼她那张苍白的脸、邋遢的衣装和凌乱的头发，罗伯特就知道他不需要再多看一眼。* 这不可能是他那无可挑剔的* 伊芙琳。

days.passage: Thursday was his next-to-last day. He had been tenacious in following up every lead. Now he was behind the eight ball.* He could hardly galvanize* himself to do anything else. The façade of hope he had worn for almost a week was crumbling; there was nothing left to be sanguine* about. In desperation he turned to the police and placed his problem within their jurisdiction.* They asked many questions, and they requested that he not expurgate anything. Some of the questions seemed asinine. When they inquired about his relationship to the missing girl, he replied, with a grimace, “Fiancee.” When they suggested she might be hiding in that part of the city where the “punk” coterie* congregated, he was incredulous* and accused the police of calumny against her good name and reputation.

exercises.text: He held on to his antiquated* beliefs with a ________________ obsession.*
The woman was noted for her vituperative ________________ against her innocuous,* although senile,* neighbor.
She could not abjure* a ________________ when she saw the disheveled figure.
How ________________ of the boy to fabricate* that bizarre* story!
His face wore the most doleful* ________________ .

idiom.meaning: completely, all the way
idiom.example: The teacher fell for the practical joke hook, line, and sinker.

days.passageZh: 星期四是他倒数第二天。他一直坚持不懈地追查每一条线索。现在他已经陷入困境。* 他几乎无法鼓舞自己做别的事。他戴着近一周的希望假象正在崩塌;已经没有什么值得乐观的了。绝望之下，他向警方求助，将自己的问题归警方管辖。* 警方问了很多问题，并请求他不要删除任何内容。有些问题听起来很荒谬。当他们问起他和失踪女孩的关系时，他皱着眉回答：“未婚妻。”当他们暗示她可能藏身于“朋克”小团体聚集的那个城市区域时，他难以置信*，指责警方诽谤她的名声和名誉。

days.passage: Failure was imminent,* and Robert was bereft* of hope. It was now Friday. Despite his abstemious* and parsimonious* way of living, his money had been reduced to a mere pittance. A perverse* impulse brought him to the section where young people in strange clothing and with uncouth* manners made him recoil* in unmitigated* disgust. He had never been au courant with the “hippies” and “punks.” He was always fastidious about proper dress and behavior. A moment later he saw her! Evelyn! She was sitting at a table in a coffee shop, surrounded by a coterie* of the most noisome individuals he had ever seen. Evelyn was not incongruous,* for she herself was unkempt. So this was her new habitat*! At that instant Robert knew as an incontrovertible* fact that he had lost her. With a grimace,* he turned and walked, a doleful* and melancholy figure, toward the bus depot and home.

exercises.text: Styles are such transient* things that what is ________________ today, is archaic* tomorrow.
The tip he had been offered was a mere ________________ , and the taxi driver threw it on the ground in disdain.*
Children think mothers are asinine* to get upset about ________________ rooms.
It was inevitable* that they discover the hidden body by its ________________ aroma.
He was so ________________ about table manners that he lost his equanimity* when his son reached for the bread.

idiom.meaning: cowardly
idiom.example: The lily-livered gangster got cold feet* and spilled the beans.*

days.passageZh: 失败已迫在眉睫，*罗伯特已失去*希望。现在是星期五。尽管他过着节制且吝啬的生活，但他的钱却只剩微薄。一种的冲动让他来到那个让他厌恶、穿着奇怪、举止粗鲁的年轻人的区域。他从未与“嬉皮士”和“朋克”打交道。他总是对穿着和行为举止一丝不苟。片刻后他看见了她！伊芙琳！她坐在咖啡馆的一张桌子旁，周围是他见过的最吵闹的人。伊芙琳并不突兀，*因为她自己也邋遢。原来这就是她的新栖息地*！那一刻，罗伯特无可辩驳地知道，他已经失去了她。他皱着眉头转身，带着忧郁的神情走向公交车站和家。

days.story:
What sport requires the timing of tennis, the energy of aerobics, the stamina of cross-country running, and the physical contact of football? The answer is: boxing. And now that seemingly male spectacle is attracting women. What was once viewed as ______ brutality has been transformed in gymnasiums across the country into the latest form of workout, weight reduction, and energy stimulator.

To suggest that women should not expose themselves to the sharp jabs and powerful uppercuts of boxing because they are the “weaker” sex is ______ . Properly trained by experts, in good shape from punching bags and jumping rope, women can be as ______ in the ring as men.

With women jockeys, race car drivers, hockey goalies, and basketball players, it would require a man with ______ prejudice, if not sheer ignorance, to argue that boxing is solely a man’s sport. Anyone who is ______ with the status of liberated women need not be surprised by their entry into the ring.
days.storyZh:
什么运动需要网球的时机掌握、有氧运动的能量、越野跑的耐力，以及足球的身体接触？答案是：拳击。而现在，这看似男性化的表演吸引了女性。曾经被视为______暴力的行为，如今在全国各地的健身房中被转变为最新的锻炼、减重和能量刺激工具。

认为女性不应因女性是“弱势”性别而暴露在拳击中的尖锐刺拳和强力上勾拳之下，这很______。经过专家的专业训练，身体状况良好，无论是沙袋还是跳绳训练，女性在擂台上的表现可以和男性一样______。

对于女骑师、赛车手、冰球守门员和篮球运动员来说，要说拳击完全是男性运动，恐怕只有一个带有______偏见甚至纯粹无知的男人。任何被______为解放女性的人都不必对自己进入拳击场感到惊讶。

days.title: A MODERN AESOP

examples.en: The parable may be found teaching a moral lesson in the Bible.

examples.en: Catholic sermons normally draw on at least one Biblical lecture, often parables.

days.passage: The telling of a story in simple terms that has an inherently* important message is a venerable* art form. The parable may be found teaching a moral lesson in the Bible. Aesop is an incontrovertible* master of the fable. This story form is far from antiquated* as shown by the whimsical approach to life taken by the modern Aesop, James Thurber. His stories lampoon the strange behavior of his fellow men. Thurber seems unable to countenance the ideas that permeate* our society regarding the rules by which we should live. Least of all is he able to accept the sanctimonious notion that some people promulgate* that good always wins out against evil. Thurber’s stories often take an exactly opposite point of view.

exercises.text: Jonathan Swift was never reticent* to ________________ the egotist* in order to bring him down with alacrity.*
What one person finds ________________ , the other may find asinine.*
The expression, “Sour grapes,*” is the gist* of a famous ________________ about a fox who couldn’t get what he wanted.
We should eschew* our ________________ façade;* away with pretext!*
If we want to live in a salubrious* milieu,* we can not ________________ the noisome* fumes that are deleterious* to health.

idiom.meaning: to quit a place
idiom.example: He could no longer rule the roost* or get the lion’s share,* so he pulled up stakes and moved on.

days.passageZh: 用简单明了的方式讲述一个带有内在*重要信息的故事，是一种古老的艺术形式。这个比喻可以在圣经中传授道德课题。伊索是无可争议的寓言大师。这种故事形式远非过时*，现代伊索作家詹姆斯·瑟伯对生活的奇思妙想态度就是明证。他的故事讽刺了同胞们的怪异行为。瑟伯似乎无法接受渗透我们社会的关于我们应遵守规则的观念。最不重要的是，他无法接受一些人宣扬的“善必战胜恶”的伪善观念。瑟伯的故事往往持完全相反的观点。

days.title: MODERNIZING A PARABLE*

examples.en: One gradually discovers this equanimity of daily existence is not in accord with the intrinsic* nature of the Dutch.
examples.zh: 人们逐渐发现，这种日常生活的平静与荷兰人本质的本质并不一致。

examples.en: With equanimity he hunted for a hare and soon found one.
examples.zh: 他镇定地寻找一只野兔，很快找到了一只。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be equanimity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是平静。

examples.en: “Do you have the effrontery to challenge me?” asked the incredulous* hare.
examples.zh: “你竟敢挑战我？”那只难以置信的*兔子问。

examples.en: We even had the effrontery to suggest that he should leave the country.
examples.zh: 我们甚至厚颜无耻地建议他离开这个国家。

examples.en: Any refusal to salute the president shall be counted as an effrontery.
examples.zh: 任何拒绝向总统敬礼的行为都将被视为无礼。

examples.en: “You are a nonentity,” he scoffed* at the tortoise.
examples.zh: “你真是个无足轻重的存在，”他对嗤之以鼻。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be nonentity.

examples.en: She used the word "nonentity" correctly in her essay.

examples.en: When the hare crossed the finish line, the flabbergasted tortoise had gone approximately eight and three-quarter inches.
examples.zh: 当野兔冲过终点线时，这只惊愕的陆龟已经飞了大约八又四分之三英寸。

examples.en: He was flabbergasted to find that his work had been done for him before he began.
examples.zh: 他惊讶地发现，工作在他开始之前就已经有人帮他完成了。

examples.zh: 他惊讶于自己体重增加了多少。

examples.en: The moral Thurber draws from this debacle for the tortoise: A new broom may sweep clean, but never trust an old saw.
examples.zh: 瑟伯从这场的惨败中汲取了道德教训：新扫帚可以扫得干净利落，但永远不要信任一把旧锯子。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be debacle.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“灾难”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "debacle" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“灾难”一词。

days.passage: Thurber punctures in an incisive* way the platitudes* that come from stories handed down through the generations. These old saws are accepted by everyone. One such tale is about a tortoise who had read in an ancient book that a tortoise had beaten a hare in a race. The sage* old tortoise construed* this story to mean that he could outrun a hare. With equanimity he hunted for a hare and soon found one. “Do you have the effrontery to challenge me?” asked the incredulous* hare. “You are a nonentity,” he scoffed* at the tortoise. A course of fifty feet was set out. The other animals gathered around the site*. At the sound of the gun they were off. When the hare crossed the finish line, the flabbergasted tortoise had gone approximately eight and three-quarter inches. The moral Thurber draws from this debacle for the tortoise: A new broom may sweep clean, but never trust an old saw.

exercises.text: He was a precocious* youngster, but he soon reached the nadir* of his career, lost all of his prestige*, and became a ________________ .
Do you have the ________________ to take that supercilious and facetious* attitude toward something as sinister* as this?
These turbulent* times require a leader who does not go into a capricious* pique,* but rather one who faces acrimonious* criticism with ________________ .
When the judge exonerated* the charlatan,* we were all ________________ .
The fortuitous* appearance of a relief column permitted an adroit* escape from the imminent* ________________ .

idiom.meaning: to cause trouble, make a fuss
idiom.example: When he found he was left holding the bag,* he decided to raise Cain.

days.passageZh: 瑟伯以犀利的方式*戳破了代代相传的故事中的陈词滥调。这些老锯子被所有人接受。其中一个故事讲述了一只在古书中读到一只在赛跑中击败了一只野兔。这位智者*老解读*这个故事，认为他能跑赢兔子。他镇定地寻找一只野兔，很快找到了一只。“你竟敢挑战我？”那只难以置信的*兔子问。“你真是个无足轻重的存在，”他对嗤之以鼻。规划了一条五十英尺的航线。其他动物聚集在营地周围*。枪声一响，他们就出发了。当野兔冲过终点线时，这只惊愕的陆龟已经飞了大约八又四分之三英寸。瑟伯从这场的惨败中汲取了道德教训：新扫帚可以扫得干净利落，但永远不要信任一把旧锯子。

examples.en: While the figure was dressed like her grandmother, the little girl surmised* with only a perfunctory* glance that it didn’t have the old lady’s mien.
examples.zh: 虽然那身影穿着像她祖母的衣服，但小女孩只是敷衍地看了一眼，推断它没有老太太的气质。

examples.en: She approached and became cognizant* of the hirsute face of the wolf.

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be hirsute.

examples.en: Thurber arrives at a moral for this story that anyone would find difficult to refute: It is not so easy to fool little girls nowadays as it used to be.
examples.zh: 瑟伯为这个故事提出了一个难以反驳的寓意：如今要骗小女孩已经不像以前那么容易了。

days.passage: Thurber modernizes an old story that everyone has read or heard. It has to do with a nefarious* wolf who kept a vigil* in an ominous* forest until a little girl came along carrying a basket of food for her grandmother. With alacrity,* this vivacious youngster told the wolf the address to which she was going. Hungry and gaunt the wolf rushed to the house. When the girl arrived and entered, she saw someone in bed wearing a nightcap and a nightgown. While the figure was dressed like her grandmother, the little girl surmised* with only a perfunctory* glance that it didn’t have the old lady’s mien. She approached and became cognizant* of the hirsute face of the wolf. She drew a revolver from her purse and shot the interloper* dead. Thurber arrives at a moral for this story that anyone would find difficult to refute: It is not so easy to fool little girls nowadays as it used to be.

exercises.text: She had a ________________ of humility,* but it was only a façade.*

They were struck by the anomaly* of one twin who was phlegmatic* while the other was ________________ .
Women strive for the slender and au courant* ________________ look.

days.passageZh: 瑟伯对一个大家都读过或听过的老故事进行了现代化改造。这与一只邪恶的*狼有关，它在一片阴森的森林中守夜*，直到一个小女孩带着一篮食物来给她的祖母。这只活泼的小家伙迅速告诉了狼她要去的地址。狼饿得憔悴，冲向屋内。女孩到达并进入时，看到有人穿着睡帽和睡裙躺在床上。虽然那身影穿着像她祖母的衣服，但小女孩只是敷衍地看了一眼，推断它没有老太太的气质。她走近，意识到了那只狼的浓密脸庞。她从包里掏出左轮手枪，射杀了闯入者*。瑟伯为这个故事提出了一个难以反驳的寓意：如今要骗小女孩已经不像以前那么容易了。

days.passage: Thurber’s stories are written in a jocose* manner, but they contain enough serious matter to make one pensive. He tells of some builders who left a pane of glass standing upright in a field near a house they were constructing. A goldfinch flew across the field, struck the glass and was knocked inert.* He rushed back and divulged* to his friends that the air had crystallized. The other birds derided* him, said he had become irrational,* and gave a number of reasons for the accident. The only bird who believed the goldfinch was the swallow. The goldfinch challenged the large birds to follow the same path he had flown. This challenge served to whet their interest, and they agreed with gusto.* Only the swallow abjured.* The large birds flew together and struck the glass; they were knocked into a stupor. This caused the astute* swallow to wince with pain. Thurber drew a moral that is the antithesis* of the cliché we all accept: He who hesitates is sometimes saved.

days.passageZh: 瑟伯的故事以轻松的风格写成，但其中包含足够严肃的内容，让人沉思。他讲述了一些建筑工人在他们正在建造的房屋附近的田野里，留下一块直立的玻璃板。一只金翅雀飞过田野，撞上玻璃，失去了动力。* 他赶回去，向朋友们透露空气已经结晶。其他鸟儿嘲笑他，说他变得不理智，并给出了许多事故原因。唯一相信金翅雀的鸟是燕子。金翅雀挑战大鸟们跟随他飞过的路径。这一挑战激发了它们的兴趣，它们欣然同意。* 只有燕子拒绝了。* 大鸟们飞在一起撞击玻璃;他们被击昏了。这让那位敏锐的吞咽者痛苦地皱眉。瑟伯提出了一个与我们普遍接受的陈词滥调相反的道德教训：犹豫者有时会得救。

days.story:
In 1966 a television program appeared that quickly established itself as the most successful science fiction series, moved on to become a series of popular films, and continues in reruns to be seen somewhere in this country every night of the year. This original series, Star Trek, became so popular that there are huge fan clubs across the country and the stars of the original series are mobbed when they make personal appearances.

What makes this form of science fiction so popular? Some may say that each story of the future is a ______ showing us our own world through a presentation of other worlds. There are those who would ______ this analysis and argue that it is the odd characters, the ______ aliens, who attract us. We watch with ______ as worlds battle, knowing it will turn out well in the end.

After many years and many TV episodes and movies, “Star Trek” and its successors continue to ______ our appetite and bring excitement to our screens. As long as space remains an almost total mystery, the unexplained will capture our imaginations.
days.storyZh:
1966年，一档电视节目出现，迅速确立了自己作为最成功的科幻剧的地位，随后成为一系列受欢迎的电影，并在美国各地每晚重播。这部原创剧集《星际迷航》非常受欢迎，全国各地都有庞大的粉丝俱乐部，原剧的主角们在亲自亮相时会被人群包围。

是什么让这种科幻形式如此受欢迎？有人可能会说，每个未来的故事都是通过展示其他世界的展示，向我们展示自己的世界______。有人______这种分析，认为正是那些奇特的角色、______外星人吸引了我们。我们______地看着世界争斗，知道最终会有好结果。

经过多年和众多电视剧集和电影，《星际迷航》及其续集依然______我们的胃口，为我们的银幕带来兴奋。只要太空依然几乎是个谜，无法解释的事物就会激发我们的想象力。

days.passage: The nineteenth century saw the woman novelist attain the same prestige* as men. England was prolific* in producing women writers. One of the foremost in this genre was Charlotte Brontë. In Jane Eyre she presented a candid portrait of a woman caught up in a clandestine* affair with a married man. Miss Bronte’s readers were engrossed* in this story. She took this unsavory subject and presented it in a way that did not degrade the relationship. She showed that true passion can be healthy. Miss Brontë did not disparage* Jane’s feelings or besmirch* her character. The author was generous in her verdict. The affair was considered merely a venial sin because Jane was never false in her feelings or her actions.

It is reprehensible,* but it doesn’t require much gossip to give a person a(n) ________________ reputation.
In my ________________ opinion he is a sanctimonious* fool.

days.passageZh: 十九世纪，女性小说家获得了与男性同等的声望*。英国在培养女性作家方面非常多产*。该流派中最杰出的人物之一是夏洛蒂·勃朗特。在《简·爱》中，她坦率描绘了一位女性卷入与已婚男子秘密*恋情的画面。勃朗特小姐的读者被这个故事深深吸引*。她把这个不讨喜的话题呈现得不让关系变得不那么糟糕。她证明了真正的热情可以是健康的。勃朗特小姐没有贬低简的感情，也没有玷污她的品格。作者对此评价颇为宽容。这段婚外情被视为轻罪，因为简的感情和行为从未虚假。

days.passage: The epitome of French romantic writers in the nineteenth century was Victor Hugo. With the utmost dexterity he wrote poetry, novels, and drama. His highly popular novels, Notre Dame de Paris and Les Miserables, are replete* with melodramatic situations and grotesque characters. He had a profound* sense of social justice and a compassion for the poor, hapless,* and downtrodden. He could not work under the aegis* of Napoleon III and fled into exile. When the repugnant rule came to an end, the expatriate* returned. He was received with adulation* and acclaim as the idol of the Third Republic.

days.passage: The movement toward realism in the English novel of the nineteenth century reached its acme with the works of Charles Dickens and William Makepeace Thackeray. Charles Dickens was a prolific* writer. Among his copious works are Oliver Twist, a candid* exposure of the repugnant* poor laws; Nicholas Nickleby, in which the life of boys in a boarding school is vehemently attacked; Hard Times, in which the author wanted to depict the infamous* life in a factory during an early period of the industrial revolution; The Pickwick Papers, about a naive gentleman who has numerous misadventures. The novels, aimed at exposing the sordid* and pernicious* elements of English life, were said to have helped galvanize* people into action leading to improvement in these conditions.

exercises.text: At the ________________ of his power, the dictator was obsessed* with the belief that those who dissented* were trying to usurp* his position.
As a perspicacious* newspaper reporter, he felt it incumbent* upon him to ________________ the abortive* coup* as a reprehensible* act.
The urbane* gentleman was flabbergasted* by the fervid* interest in wrestling shown by the ________________ young girl.
She lost her decorum* and wept ________________ tears at the poignant* story.
He objected ________________ to a vote taking place in the absence of a quorum.*

idiom.meaning: solid, firm land
idiom.example: The rough ocean crossing took the wind out of his sails*, and he was happy to be on terra firma again.

days.passageZh: 十九世纪英国小说向现实主义的运动在查尔斯·狄更斯和威廉·梅克皮斯·萨克雷的作品中达到顶峰。查尔斯·狄更斯是一位多产的作家。他的大量作品包括《雾都孤儿》，坦率揭露令人厌恶的贫困法律;尼古拉斯·尼克尔比，书中对寄宿学校男孩生活遭到猛烈抨击;《艰难时代》，作者想描绘工业革命早期工厂中臭名昭著的生活;《匹克威克外传》，讲述一位天真的绅士经历了无数的冒险。这些小说旨在揭露英国生活中肮脏且有害的元素，据说帮助激励人们采取行动，改善这些状况。

days.passage: William Makepeace Thackeray was known for his moralistic study of upper and middle class English life. His best known work, Vanity Fair, has as its central character Becky Sharp. She is a perfidious woman who has an insatiable* desire to get ahead in the world. She covets the wealth of one man, but when marriage is not feasible* she succeeds in a plan to ingratiate herself into the heart of her employer’s son. Their marriage is not a salubrious* one and Becky, who lives ostentatiously,* forms a surreptitious* liaison with another man. The affair culminates* in a debacle.* She is exposed, her husband leaves her, and she must live in penury in Europe. This is the ignominious end for a clever, but misguided woman.

exercises.text: Under the aegis* of a zealous* campaign manager, the candidate was able to ________________ herself into the hearts of the public.
A favorite parable* has to do with teaching the lesson that one should not ________________ that which belongs to someone else.
His fortune fluctuated* between ________________ and wealth.
They made an effigy* of their ________________ enemy.
There was bedlam* as the favored team went down to ________________ defeat at the hands of the underdog.

days.passageZh: 威廉·梅克皮斯·萨克雷以其对英国上层和中产阶级生活的道德主义研究而闻名。他最著名的作品《名利场》以贝基·夏普为主角。她是个背信弃义的女人，有着永无止境的渴望，想在世界上出人高临下。她渴望拥有一个男人的财富，但当婚姻不可行*时，她成功地实现了一个计划，讨好雇主儿子的心。他们的婚姻并不美好*，而生活奢华的贝基*与另一名男子秘密交往。这段恋情以一场灾难告终。她被揭露，丈夫离开她，她不得不在欧洲贫困中生活。这是一个聪明却误入歧途的女性的耻辱结局。

days.story:
We are all aware that 1992 was the year during which there were ______ reminders that it marked the 500th anniversary of Columbus’ arrival in this part of the hemisphere. Along with the celebrations and historical reenactments, there was controversy regarding the lives of those who had been here for many centuries before that fateful event.

Historical research shows that it would be extremely ______ to believe that “civilization” began on this continent with Columbus’ arrival. The Native American tribes had formed nations and had come together in an organization known as the Five Nations. They had regulations for governance that were the ______ of self-rule and that became the models on which our Constitution was partly based.

It was to remove the ______ portrayal of the Native American as savage and wild that historians adopted 1992 as the year to ______ them in their true light as members of civilizations worthy of study and respect.
days.storyZh:
我们都知道，1992年是______提醒哥伦布抵达这片半球500周年的一年。除了庆祝和历史重演，关于那些在那场灾难发生前几百年都在这里生活的人们的生死也引发了争议。

历史研究表明，认为“文明”始于哥伦布抵达这片大陆是极其______的。美洲原住民部落组建了国家，并联合组成了一个被称为五国的组织。他们制定了自治的治理规章，这些法规______自治，这也成为我们宪法部分的范本。

正是为了消除对美洲原住民野蛮和野蛮的______描绘，历史学家才将1992年定为______他们作为值得研究和尊重文明成员的真实面貌。

days.passage: Henry Thoreau attempted to confront the problem and solve the enigma* of how one might earn a living and yet not become an ignominious* slave to the task. He viewed the industrial revolution with antipathy. Man in a servile role to extraneous* possessions was a main target of his writings. He believed that one could attain genuine wealth not by accumulating objects or money, but through enjoyment and perusal* of nature. By his own volition he gave up friends and comforts for a two year sojourn by himself at Walden Pond. What others might judge as penury,* was seen by Thoreau as the epitome* of wealth.

exercises.text: He found his ________________ position a degrading* one and could not accept it with equanimity.*
The expatriate* decided to make his ________________ in France a permanent one in order to give up his nomadic* way of life.
Why do we refuse to ________________ the unsavory* problems of our times in a candid* and incisive* way?
He was a tenacious* competitor, and at his own ________________ he placed his title in jeopardy* on many occasions.
Her ________________ towards men was based on rather nebulous* events that she construed* to prove that they were all perfidious.*

idiom.meaning: to get set for bad times or poverty
idiom.example: He knew he would have to draw in his horns* and tighten his belt or he would wind up on skid row*.

days.passageZh: 亨利·梭罗试图直面这个问题，解开一个谜团：如何谋生却又不沦为屈辱的奴隶。他对工业革命持反感态度。他作品的主要主题是对外在财产的奴役角色。他认为，真正的财富不是通过积累物品或金钱，而是通过享受和对自然的细致探索。他自愿放弃朋友和舒适，独自在沃尔登湖度过了两年。别人可能认为贫困的现象，*却被梭罗视为财富的缩影。

examples.zh: 梭罗关于沃尔登湖严酷而幸福生活的著作，使他的名声传遍全球。

days.passage: Thoreau’s book about the austere but happy life at Walden Pond propagated* his fame around the world. He built a small hut and began living an ascetic* existence. He found it to be a felicitous experience. In this idyllic* setting he was able to spend his time reading, studying nature, writing, and thinking. Far from being indolent,* he kept busy in many ways. At the end of the experiment he recalled the halcyon days with pleasure. He believed he had learned the secret of the truly happy life. The only tenable way of life is one in harmony with nature; material possessions are superfluous.

exercises.text: When he found his sinecure* was no longer ________________ , he felt it a propitious* time to resign.
Far from being ostentatious,* she was considered the acme* of fashion because of her ________________ manner of dress.
Because he was an itinerant* worker, he had to disdain* carrying ________________ equipment.
On that ________________ occasion the amount of money he spent was irrelevant.*
During the turbulent* days of the war, they wished for the ________________ days of earlier times.

idiom.meaning: not usual, out of the ordinary
idiom.example: Because his ideas were always off the beaten track, he lived under a sword of Damocles* on his job.

days.passageZh: 梭罗关于沃尔登湖严酷而幸福生活的著作，使他的名声传遍全球。他建造了一座小屋，开始过苦行*的生活。他觉得这是一次幸运的经历。在这个田园诗般的环境中，他得以花时间阅读、研究自然、写作和思考。他远非懒散，*在许多方面都保持忙碌。实验结束时，他愉快地回忆起那些宁静的日子。他相信自己学会了真正幸福生活的秘密。唯一可行的生活方式是与自然和谐共处;物质财产是多余的。

days.passage: The study of the human mind and behavior has had many prominent practitioners, but no one is more revered* than Sigmund Freud. An Austrian physician, he is said to be the father of psychoanalysis. He taught that man has a subconscious mind in which he keeps repugnant* memories that come to the surface surreptitiously* and motivate behavior. Man often tries to rationalize his actions, when, in reality, they are really the result of suppressed memories coming to the surface. Freud’s approach to the disturbed person was to attempt therapy by examining the dreams that make cognizant* what the cause of the illness might be. Only with the airing of deleterious*, buried emotions can the person move from the nascent stage to that of full health. Freud was considered an iconoclast in the field of psychology when his ideas first appeared at the beginning of the twentieth century.

idiom.example: It was a bitter pill to swallow* when they had to fire him because he was a square peg in a round hole.

days.passageZh: 人类心智和行为的研究有许多杰出的实践者，但没有人比西格蒙德·弗洛伊德更受尊敬*。他是一位奥地利医生，被誉为精神分析学之父。他教导人有一个潜意识，潜意识中储存着令人厌恶的*记忆，这些记忆悄悄浮现*，并激发行为。人类常常试图为自己的行为找理由，但实际上，这些行为只是被压抑记忆浮现的结果。弗洛伊德对精神失常者的方法是通过审视那些让人们意识到疾病可能原因的梦境来尝试治疗。只有在释放有害且埋藏的情绪时，这个人才能从初创阶段迈向完全健康的状态。弗洛伊德在20世纪初的思想首次出现时，被认为是心理学领域的反传统者。

days.title: AMATEUR PSYCHOLOGISTS

days.passage: The ideas of Freudian psychology have become part of our everyday life. Our language is replete* with clichés* that have their origin in Freud’s writings. There is a surfeit* of amateur psychologists who, with celerity,* analyze an individual’s problems from the slightest evidence. Despite their dubious* education and training in this field, they discuss symptoms and cures on a most erudite fashion. Should a person express a fear of heights, this phobia is examined; events from childhood are considered germane to the problem. Is it possible he or she was dropped as an infant? Perhaps something in a dream is pertinent* to explain the feelings of vertigo that accompany height. For some reason, non-trained people find the Freudian approach to the workings of the human mind most conducive to their practicing as amateur psychologists.

days.passageZh: 弗洛伊德心理学的思想已成为我们日常生活的一部分。我们的语言充满了*起源于弗洛伊德著作的陈词滥调*。业余心理学家过剩*，他们迅速地从最微小的证据分析个体的问题。尽管他们在该领域的教育和培训存疑，但他们以极为博学的方式讨论症状和治疗方法。如果有人表达了恐高症，会对这种恐惧症进行检查;童年时期的事件被认为是与问题密切相关。有可能他或她在婴儿时期就被抛弃了吗？也许梦中某些内容*对伴随身高的眩晕感有帮助。不知为何，未经训练的人发现弗洛伊德关于人类心智运作的方法最适合他们作为业余心理学家的实践。

days.storyTitle: Make My Ostrich Burger Well Done
days.story:
Just about 100 years ago, there arose an industry in the state of Arizona that seems very odd to us today. We know of cattle ranches and sheep ranches, but would you believe . . . ostrich ranches? This ______ business became popular as women found ostrich feathers a ______ addition to their wardrobes.

Ostriches are easy to raise. They eat and drink less than cattle, and their eggs are large enough to feed ten people! During the ______ days of ostrich ranching, feathers were sold for as much as $300 a pound, so it is easy to see why that business was so attractive.

However, women’s fashions changed after World War I, and the market for ostrich plumes fell. Growers had to ______ a shrinking market. The price tumbled to about $10 for a bird. As ostrich feathers became ______ in the fashion world, ostrich ranching came to an end.

Interestingly enough, ostrich ranchers may be coming back into vogue because nutritionists tell us that ostrich meat is low in cholesterol. We may not go wild over the feathers, but pass the lean meat, please. Hold the mayo, too.
days.storyZh:
大约一百年前，亚利桑那州出现了一个对我们今天来说非常奇怪的产业。我们知道有牛场和羊场，但你能相信吗......鸵鸟牧场？随着女性发现鸵鸟羽毛成为衣橱______补充，这一______业务变得流行起来。

鸵鸟很容易养。它们的饮食和饮水都比牛少，而且它们的蛋足够大，可以喂饱十个人！在鸵鸟牧场的______时期，羽毛的售价高达每磅300美元，因此这生意的吸引力不难理解。

然而，第一次世界大战后，女性的时尚发生了变化，鸵鸟羽毛的市场也随之下降。种植者不得不______市场萎缩。价格暴跌至一只鸟约10美元。随着鸵鸟羽毛在时尚界变得______，鸵鸟牧场也随之终结。

有趣的是，鸵鸟牧场主可能正在重新流行，因为营养学家告诉我们鸵鸟肉胆固醇低。我们不能对羽毛大肆争取，但请把瘦肉递过来。蛋黄酱也别加。

days.passage: Of all the pressures young people face, the most pernicious* is that of fashion. By this is meant the current vogue* in dress. The teenagers, who are so glib when they speak of “individuality,” are turned into a homogeneous mass by the latest craze in fashion. How can youngsters who vehemently* resist advice from the older generation become so malleable in the hands of those who “make” fashion? Perhaps the sudden shifts in fashion occur fortuitously*. Or is there some group who, through legerdemain, switches styles and customs on us right before our eyes? Today’s teenagers seem to be quite gullible* when it comes to embracing the latest trend in fashions. But then, they have their elders as sage* examples to follow.

They were engrossed* as an ill man was “cured” before their eyes; some of the more urbane* said it was ________________ .
He ingratiated* himself into their confidence, and the ________________ crowd was shaped into a subjugated* mass.
While they started out as individuals, they became a ________________ group whom he could motivate as he willed.

days.passage: In dress, the fashion appears to be “set” by a few foreign designers and a handful of affluent* individuals who purchase these designs. The fashion industry is cognizant* of the fact that fashions must change rapidly and often or their economy would become stagnant. For this industry it would prove fatal if it were not vigilant* and prepared well in advance for a new fashion trend.* As the old fashion becomes passé and a new fashion seems to be in the making, the garment manufacturers cannot afford to procrastinate. They rush large sums of money into production for a mass market. Having invested heavily, the manufacturers do everything possible to influence and motivate* the purchasers. Through every facet of publicity and advertising the industry exploits* the natural desire for people to be au courant* with the latest fashions.

days.passageZh: 在服装方面，这种时尚似乎由少数外国设计师和少数富裕人士“定型”，他们购买了这些设计。时尚行业深知时尚必须迅速且频繁地变化，否则经济将陷入停滞。对于这个行业来说，如果不保持警惕*并提前做好应对新时尚潮流的准备，这将是致命的。* 随着旧时尚逐渐过时，新时尚似乎正在酝酿，服装制造商不能拖延。他们急于投入大量资金生产，面向大众市场。制造商投入大量资金后，竭尽全力影响和激励客户。通过宣传和广告的各个环节，行业利用了人们与最新时尚紧密相连的自然需求。
