days.passage: Once the fashion industry has been able to foist a new style on the teenager, the older generation tends to stigmatize it as some form of rebellion. What is often ignored is that the young consumers capitulate to what is originated* by someone outside of their group. The feelings of individuality and audacity that the teenager gets from a new style of dress result from the propensity* of their elders to disparage* them. The actual situation is that the clothing fashions soon become accepted by all; there is nothing upsetting or revolutionary about them. While people are becoming complacent* about the “new,” the clothing industry is busy planning how to tantalize the teenager with next year’s “fashion.” This arbitrary* decision is guaranteed to foment* consternation* among adults once again in the following year.

days.passageZh: 一旦时尚行业能够将新风格强加给青少年，老一辈往往会将其视为某种叛逆。常被忽视的是，年轻消费者最终屈服于来自群体外的人*的发起。青少年从新穿着风格中获得的个性和大胆感，源于长辈倾向于贬低他们。实际情况是，服装时尚很快被所有人接受;这些都没有令人不安或革命性的东西。当人们对“新潮”变得自满*时，服装行业正忙着策划如何用明年的“时尚”来吸引青少年。这一武断的决定注定将在明年再次引发成年人的忧虑。

days.title: SOMETHING FOR EVERYONE

examples.en: To the derogatory* comments from the older generation the teenagers might retort that new fashions and styles are adopted by the elders with alacrity.* Though they complain, women emulate* their daughters by shortening or lengthening their hems.

days.passage: To the derogatory* comments from the older generation the teenagers might retort that new fashions and styles are adopted by the elders with alacrity.* Though they complain, women emulate* their daughters by shortening or lengthening their hems. They may appear reticent about the bother and expense of altering their wardrobe, but they give tacit approval to the change by rushing to the department stores where they jostle* each other to buy copies of the more expensive dresses. The conclusion one might reach after observing how women countenance* the arbitrary* changes year after year is that they are naïve* or victims of some chicanery practiced by the clothing industry. Women may appear hapless* before the intimidation* of “style,” but the real truth may lie in the fact that they are so docile because they secretly enjoy the yearly excitement around the latest fashions.

days.story:
Is there anyone you know who can remember a time when there was no television? Perhaps a grandparent, but no one much younger is able to do so. At the beginning, only a handful of stations existed. Early programs imitated each other and tended to be ______ . Some time later, there was the cable TV expansion and greater variety was available. The developing ______ was for ever-larger numbers of programs dealing with information as well as entertainment.

The TV industry, never ______ when it comes to expanding viewer interests, brought even more channels to the air, broadcasting 24 hours every day of the week. The objective was to ______ special groups with programs directed to special tastes and interests. Soon channels devoted to games, to how to fix or make things, to romance dramas, to cartoons, etc., sprang into existence. It appears that every ______ of a viewer’s interest is being addressed. As more and more channels come on the air, as the result of new technology, the variety is expanding beyond anything imagined by those who can recall the beginnings of this magical medium.
days.storyZh:
你认识谁还记得没有电视的时代吗？也许是祖父母，但比他年轻得多的人都做不到。起初，只有少数几个车站。早期的节目相互模仿，往往______。不久之后，有线电视扩展，节目种类更加丰富。发展______是越来越多涉及信息和娱乐的节目。

电视行业在扩大观众兴趣方面从不______，因此增加了更多频道，每周7天24小时播出。目标是______针对特定口味和兴趣的特别小组。很快，专门讨论游戏、如何修理或制作物品、爱情剧、动画片等的频道纷纷涌现。看起来观众的每一个______兴趣都得到了回应。随着新技术带来的频道数量不断增加，节目种类也远超那些能回忆起这种魔幻媒介起源的人所能想象的范围。

days.passage: An unforgettable saga of World War II has to do with the small French coastal town of Dunkirk. There, in 1940, thousands of British troops made a belated escape from the awesome* power of the German army and air force. They were removed by an array* of private boats, from huge yachts to decrepit fishing boats. At their own volition,* the skippers came close to the shore, while German planes bombed implacably.* They remained imperturbable under heavy fire. When their vessels were loaded, they dashed back to England. Once unloaded, they did not vacillate, but returned with equanimity* to their vigil* in the danger zone. The British proved once again that they are paragons* of comradeship in times of jeopardy.*

days.passageZh: 二战中一段令人难忘的故事与法国小海滨小镇敦刻尔克有关。1940年，成千上万的英国士兵在德国陆军和空军的强大力量下，迟来地逃脱。他们被各种私人船只运走，从巨大的游艇到破旧的渔船。船长们主动靠近岸边，德国飞机则无情轰炸。* 他们在猛烈火力下依然毫无波澜。当他们的船只装载完毕后，他们迅速返回英格兰。卸货后，他们没有犹豫，而是平静地返回危险区，继续守夜。英国人再次证明了他们是危急时刻的同志精神典范。*

days.passage: The international adventure stories prevalent* on television follow meticulously* a plot that is inexorable* in its development. Those on the side of law and justice face perfidious* men and organizations. These are anathema* to those values the staunch heroes would defend. These infamous* men have no capacity for compassion,* and they treat the lovely women with opprobrium. The intrepid* heroes are placed in deleterious* situations as a result of the Machiavellian maneuvers of their opponents. One unconscionable act of duplicity* follows another until the total destruction of the “good guys” seems at hand. At the last moment, usually amidst the pandemonium of a battle, the cause for which the heroes strive triumphs. However, evil is ubiquitous,* and next week another fracas* will erupt.

There was ________________ as the presidential nominee entered the convention site.*

days.passageZh: 电视上盛行的国际冒险故事细致地遵循*一个不可阻挡*的情节发展。支持法律和正义的人面对背信弃义的人和组织。这些与坚定英雄所捍卫的价值观背道而驰*。这些臭名昭著的男人毫无同情心，*他们对美丽的女性充满羞辱。勇敢的英雄们因对手的马基雅维利式手段而陷入了不利的境地。一件不可原谅的欺骗行为接着一件接着一件，直到“好人”的彻底毁灭似乎指日可待。在最后时刻，通常在战斗的混乱中，英雄们所为之奋斗的事业取得了胜利。然而，邪恶无处不在，*下周又会爆发一场风波*。

days.passage: One of the most repugnant* names in popular legend is that of Captain William Bligh. He was the captain of the H.M.S. Bounty in 1789, and the mutiny that erupted* aboard that ship was the basis for a film in which Charles Laughton portrayed Bligh as an awesome* bully and an unmitigated* villain. He would flay both the body and the spirit of anyone who crossed him. The crew developed such an aversion* to Bligh’s mortifying actions and demeanor that, led by Fletcher Christian, they set the captain and 17 shipmates off in a lifeboat in the South Pacific. The ship continued to the Pitcairn Islands where the crew remained to live with the islanders. Laughton’s delineation of Bligh remains as the image we have of him. Only recently has any attempt been made to vindicate Captain Bligh and to remove the heinous reputation that permeates* history.

days.passageZh: 在流行传说中，最令人厌恶的名字之一是威廉·布莱船长。1789年，他是英国皇家海军“赏金号”的舰长，而在该船上爆发的哗变成为电影的基础，查尔斯·劳顿将布莱塑造成一个令人敬畏的恶霸和彻头彻尾的反派。他会剥掉任何得罪他的人的身体和灵魂。船员们对布莱令人尴尬的行为和态度产生了极大的反感*，在弗莱彻·克里斯蒂安的带领下，他们将船长和17名船员送上南太平洋的救生艇。船只继续前往皮特凯恩群岛，船员们留在那里与岛民同住。劳顿对布莱的描绘依然是我们对他的印象。直到最近，才有人试图为布莱船长平反，并抹去那贯穿历史的恶名。

words.definition: Inherent baseness, depravity or wickedness; corruptness and evilness.

examples.en: We do not condone* or tolerate* an infraction of even the most trivial kind.
While an infraction aboard ship was quickly criticized, Bligh never carried out those callous actions the movie dramatized in order to depict* an evil man, say his defenders.
examples.zh: 虽然船上的违规行为很快受到批评，但布莱从未采取电影中戏剧化的那些冷酷行为，以描绘一个邪恶的人，他的辩护者如是说。

examples.en: It takes a callous person to watch with equanimity* as a gullible,* naive* girl falls for the line of a loathsome* boy.
While an infraction aboard ship was quickly criticized, Bligh never carried out those callous actions the movie dramatized in order to depict* an evil man, say his defenders.
examples.zh: 虽然船上的违规行为很快受到批评，但布莱从未采取电影中戏剧化的那些冷酷行为，以描绘一个邪恶的人，他的辩护者如是说。

examples.en: The advertisers must influence the malleable* consumer, and often they do it in the most callous ways.

examples.en: There seems to be no way to redress a grievance against an omnipotent* ruler.
This prodigious* feat alone, say those who would restore Bligh’s good name, should be enough to allow for a full redress of the wrongs that have been blamed on him for over 150 years.

days.passage: Recently, there has been an attempt to improve Captain Bligh’s tainted* image. Historians maintain that there was no turpitude in Bligh’s actions aboard the H.M.S. Bounty. Perhaps he was imprudent* in failing to keep his temper under control. While an infraction aboard ship was quickly criticized, Bligh never carried out those callous actions the movie dramatized in order to depict* an evil man, say his defenders. After the mutiny, Captain Bligh astutely* navigated the lifeboat with the other 17 men for over 3,000 miles to safety. This prodigious* feat alone, say those who would restore Bligh’s good name, should be enough to allow for a full redress of the wrongs that have been blamed on him for over 150 years. While the coterie* defending Captain Bligh do not ask the public to praise him, they do request a more benevolent* attitude toward this traditionally* reprehensible* figure, and an end to the vituperation* heaped upon him for these many years.

exercises.text: We do not condone* or tolerate* an ________________ of even the most trivial kind.

How easy it is to heap ________________ upon someone at the nadir* of his career.
There seems to be no way to ________________ a grievance against an omnipotent* ruler.
From any facet* of his life, the acme* of moral ________________ was reached by Adolf Hitler.

idiom.example: He did a lot of woolgathering* and was in fine fettle during the whole of the Indian summer.*

days.passageZh: 最近，有人试图改善布莱船长的污点形象。历史学家认为，布莱在“赏金号”上的行为并无恶意。也许他不小心*没能控制住脾气。虽然船上的违规行为很快受到批评，但布莱从未采取电影中戏剧化的那些冷酷行为，以描绘一个邪恶的人，他的辩护者如是说。哗变后，布莱船长机敏地*与其他17名船员一起驾驶救生艇航行超过3000英里，成功逃离安全。那些想恢复布莱名声的人说，仅这项惊人的壮举就足以彻底纠正那些被指责为他150多年的冤屈。虽然为布莱船长辩护的小圈子*并不要求公众赞扬他，但他们请求对这位传统上*令人厌恶*的人物采取更宽容*的态度，并结束多年来对他的谩骂*。

days.story:
Think about this for a moment. Is there anything wrong in buying something for one dollar and reselling it for two dollars? Naturally, you would be correct if you saw nothing amiss with this transaction; it’s the way a capitalist economy works. But, if you bought a ticket to a rock concert or baseball game for ten dollars and sold it for twenty, you would be committing an ______ of the law. You might ask, “What’s so ______ about this?” The answer is that you would be guilty of the practice known as “scalping.” Does an individual who offers a scarce ticket at a price above the original price deserve the ______ connected with the word “scalping”?

These hard-working and risk-taking individuals see themselves as go-betweens in a world where people are willing to spend additional money for a popular event. However, law enforcement officials remain ______ in the face of all reason as they arrest and fine these enterprising salesmen. Those ______ believers in punishing law-breakers find nothing wrong with trying to halt the scalping of tickets. For others, it is a way of doing business that they claim hurts no one and is in keeping with a profit-driven economy.
days.storyZh:
想一想。买一美元再转卖两美元有什么问题吗？当然，如果你觉得这笔交易没有异常，那你说得没错;这就是资本主义经济的运作方式。但是，如果你用十美元买了摇滚演唱会或棒球赛的门票，然后卖成二十美元，那你就是犯法______。你可能会问，“这有什么______的？”答案是你会犯下所谓的“剥头皮”行为。一个以高于原价的价格提供稀缺彩票的人，是否应得“黄牛”一词带来的______？

这些勤奋且敢于冒险的人，把自己视为一个愿意为热门活动多花钱的世界中的中间人。然而，执法人员在逮捕和罚款这些有进取心的推销员时，依然保持理智的态度______。那些______相信惩罚违法者的人认为试图阻止黄牛票没什么问题。对另一些人来说，这是一种他们声称不伤害任何人、符合利润驱动经济的商业方式。

days.passage: The 2012 elections offered another example of politics as show business. Most politicians have prepared speeches dealing with the prevalent* topics of the day. They can maintain a fervid* flow of rhetoric for hours at a time. In each locality where he is to appear, the advance work is prepared by a clique of trustworthy aides. In preparation for the show, they have dispersed* leaflets, put up posters, and sent out cars and trucks with loudspeakers to extol the erudite* qualities of their candidate. Soon, the crowd gathers. Loyal party workers come forward to shake the hand of their mentor. Now, with the facile solutions to complex problems carefully memorized, the show is ready to begin. One moment facetious,* the next moment profound,* the candidate works to convince the incredulous* among the voters.

days.passageZh: 2012年选举又展示了政治作为演艺事业的范例。大多数政治家都准备了关于当下热门话题的演讲稿。他们能保持热烈的言辞流畅好几个小时。在他将出庭的每个地方，前期工作由一群值得信赖的助手准备。为筹备展会，他们散发了*传单，张贴了海报，并派出装有扬声器的汽车和卡车，以赞扬候选人的博学*品质。很快，人群聚集起来。忠诚的党务工作者走上前来与导师握手。现在，随着对复杂问题的简单解决方案被仔细记住，节目准备开始。一会儿是讽刺，* 下一刻却深刻，* 候选人努力说服选民中难以置信的人。

days.passage: The television press interview is conducive* to close scrutiny* of a candidate. His public speeches may contain many cant phrases, but a sharp question by an astute* reporter can destroy a cliché* filled statement. The politician now will procrastinate* in his answer; a new facet* of his personality may be revealed by his demeanor.* Perhaps he will take umbrage at a suggestion that he favors the affluent.* His record is searched for evidence that he has been equally magnanimous to the indigent.* He accuses the reporter of attempting to vilify him. Is he being accused of turpitude* in office? It is time to discreetly* go on to another topic. The candidate wishes to extol* the virtues of his program and record. The press wants to allude* to things that keep him in the midst of controversy. They insist that he elucidate positions that the politician would rather leave in a nebulous* state.

The judge ordered the censor to ________________ his reasons for removing passages from the book in such a capricious* manner.

examples.en: Gestures are an important concomitant* to even the most vapid speech, enhancing it and giving the hearer something to look at while he listens.

examples.en: The concomitant situation that develops with the stepped-up propaganda is one in which the individual retreats into a state of lassitude.

examples.en: Because they had no person on whom to place their attention, they soon took on the appearance of extreme lassitude; most students became lethargic* and rested their heads on their desks.

examples.zh: 视觉与听觉交流的分离往往削弱了学习过程。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be vitiate.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是vitiate。

examples.en: She used the word "vitiate" correctly in her essay.

days.passage: While we are all cognizant* of the importance of words to create certain impressions, gesture is relegated* to a much lesser role. Gestures are an important concomitant* to even the most vapid speech, enhancing it and giving the hearer something to look at while he listens. The value of seeing at the same time as listening was shown when a class at a university, unwieldy because of its large size, was split up. One group was put into a room in proximity to good loudspeakers. Every nuance* of the lecturer’s voice could be heard clearly. Because they had no person on whom to place their attention, they soon took on the appearance of extreme lassitude; most students became lethargic* and rested their heads on their desks. The separation of visual and aural communication tended to vitiate the learning process. The listening group received grades lower than those received by those who could look at as well as hear the instructor.

exercises.text: As the scion* of an affluent* family, he was often in ________________ to opulence.*
After playing with his progeny* in the enervating sun, he staggered back to his room where he was overcome with ________________ .
As a concomitant* to his belligerent* and vituperative* antipathy* toward his government, he became an expatriate,* but he found it a ________________ life.
Kyra was so disgruntled* about having to move the ________________ piano, she procrastinated* for days.
The irrelevant* evidence seemed to ________________ the prosecutor’s case and precluded* a conviction.

idiom.meaning: to be on edge, jumpy
idiom.example: He was on pins and needles while he cooled his heels* in the principal’s office.

days.passageZh: 虽然我们都意识到*语言在创造某些印象中的重要性*，但手势的作用却被降级*到了一个很小的位置。手势是即使是最空洞的言语的重要配套*，增强了听众的视角，让听者在聆听时有东西可以看。一所大学的一门因规模庞大而难以管理的班级被拆开，展示了同时观看和听力的价值。一组被安排在靠近良好扬声器的房间里。讲师声音的每一个细微差别都清晰可闻。由于没有人可以关注，他们很快表现出极度的倦怠;大多数学生变得无精打采*，把头靠在课桌上。视觉与听觉交流的分离往往削弱了学习过程。听力组的成绩低于能同时观察和听到教师的学生。

examples.en: It was only much later that actors became skilled enough to communicate with the audience through discreet* gestures and almost imperceptible changes in facial expression that could transmit nuances* of emotion.
examples.zh: 直到很久以后，演员才变得足够熟练，能够通过细微的*手势和几乎察觉不到的面部表情变化与观众沟通，传递情感的细微差别*。

examples.en: We all missed the imperceptible shake of his head as he tried to warn us without being seen.
examples.zh: 我们都没注意到他试图在不被发现的情况下警告我们时，轻微摇头。

examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“无人察觉”的含义。

days.passage: Actors depend upon their ability to gesticulate* almost as much as upon speech to obtain their desired histrionic* effects. With them, gesture serves much more than merely to augment speech. When their communication is by gesture alone, it is called pantomime. In the early silent motion picture period, gestures were flamboyant.* To show that he was distraught* about the danger in which the heroine had been placed, the hero would go through the most fatuous actions. He would stagger, beat his breast, tear his hair, and contort his face into the most doleful* appearance. There weren’t many simple or restrained gestures in his repertoire. The heroine, to indicate her love, would fling her arms wide and ardently* jump into her sweetheart’s arms. It was only much later that actors became skilled enough to communicate with the audience through discreet* gestures and almost imperceptible changes in facial expression that could transmit nuances* of emotion.

days.story:
In this country we take for granted our right to speak out about our elected officials in any way we wish, without fear of arrest or imprisonment. The most disrespectful language is allowed. While some may take ______ at an insult against the president, our Constitution protects that right.

Now, consider the country of Thailand. That land in southeastern Asia is ruled by a king. What happens to an individual who fails to ______ this monarch? There is a case of a person who joked that if he were king he could sleep late every day and drink wine in the afternoon. For this somewhat ______ remark, he was sent to prison for seven years. Or take the story of the woman who was hanging up the king’s photograph. When the police asked her what she was doing, she replied, “I’m nailing it up there on my wall.” She said “it” instead of “the king’s photograph” and for this ______ alleged insult, she also was sent away for seven years.

While some U.S. citizens may ______ our leaders, in Thailand the less said the better.
days.storyZh:
在这个国家，我们理所当然地拥有以任何方式公开谈论当选官员的权利，无需担心被逮捕或监禁。允许使用最不尊重人的语言。虽然有人可能会把______视为对总统的侮辱，但我们的宪法保护了这一权利。

现在，看看泰国这个国家。东南亚的那片土地由一位国王统治。如果一个人未能在______这位君主，会发生什么？有一个人开玩笑说，如果他是国王，他可以每天睡懒觉，下午喝酒。因这句颇为过于严______的言论，他被判入狱七年。或者说那个挂国王照片的女人的故事。当警察问她在做什么时，她回答：“我在墙上钉钉。”她说的是“它”，而不是“国王的照片”，因此______被指控的侮辱，她也被判处七年监禁。

虽然一些美国公民可能______我们的领导人，但在泰国，越少说越好。

examples.en: In the larger supermarkets the housewife is faced with a satiety of merchandise, particularly in the copiously* stocked laundry detergent section.

examples.en: It is intrinsic to the operation of all mass media that they avoid being extremist in their news coverage or editorials.

examples.en: Man sets the price of gold; it has no intrinsic value.
examples.zh: 人决定黄金的价格;它没有内在价值。

examples.en: While there may be almost no intrinsic difference among the many brands, advertising and packaging serves to importune* her to buy one rather than another.
examples.zh: 虽然众多品牌之间几乎没有本质区别，但广告和包装却让她不断催促她买其中一个而不是另一个。

days.passage: The American housewife is queen of all she surveys in the supermarket. She decides what items shall be purchased. Grocery manufacturers are well aware of her power to make one product a success and another a failure. They spend huge sums developing new products with which to curry her favor. Fearful that a successful product will soon begin to pall, the manufacturers, without cessation,* come out with “new and improved” versions to whet* her appetite. Sometimes it is only a box or package that has been changed—perhaps a colorful photo of a succulent meal on a TV dinner box. In the larger supermarkets the housewife is faced with a satiety of merchandise, particularly in the copiously* stocked laundry detergent section. While there may be almost no intrinsic difference among the many brands, advertising and packaging serves to importune* her to buy one rather than another.

days.passageZh: 这位美国家庭主妇是她在超市里观察的一切的女王。她决定购买哪些物品。杂货制造商非常清楚她能让某款产品成功，另一款产品失败的能力。他们花费巨资开发新产品以讨好她。制造商担心成功的产品很快会让人失望，于是不断推出“全新改进版”来激发她的胃口。有时只是换了个盒子或包裹——比如电视餐盒上一张彩色多汁餐点的照片。在大型超市里，家庭主妇面对商品的满足感，尤其是在备货充足的洗衣粉区。虽然众多品牌之间几乎没有本质区别，但广告和包装却让她不断催促她买其中一个而不是另一个。

days.passage: Packaging of grocery items is a facet* of advertising that is too little appreciated by consumers. Walking up and down the aisles of a supermarket, one seldom stops to analyze the individual package in the potpourri of items on the shelves. The manufacturer had to glean* and test many different designs before he accepted the one you see in the array* before you. Before he will sanction the use of a particular can, box, or bottle, he must know many things about its efficacy.* He wants to know if the colors attract: a white box may denote cleanliness, a red one, strength. There may be a photo or a drawing that will allude to the product’s use or special qualities. A lackluster* package may be fatal.* Next, the size and shape are important elements. The housewife may want a small package for easy storing, but a larger package may suggest economy. A round bottle may look attractive, but a square one is easier to stack. These are some of the insidious aspects of packaging, the main purpose of which is to attract your attention as you peruse* the crowded supermarket shelves.

days.passageZh: 食品包装是广告中消费者很少重视的一个方面。在超市的过道间走动时，很少会停下来仔细分析货架上各种商品中的每一个包装。制造商必须收集*并测试许多不同的设计，才接受了你在阵列中看到的*设计。在批准使用某个罐子、盒子或瓶子之前，他必须了解许多关于其功效的信息。* 他想知道颜色是否吸引人：白色盒子可能代表干净，红色盒子代表力量。可能有照片或图画，暗示产品的用途或特殊性质。平庸的包装可能致命。* 其次，尺寸和形状是重要因素。家庭主妇可能想要一个小包装以便收纳，但大包装可能更经济。圆形瓶子看起来很吸引人，但方形瓶子更容易叠放。这些是包装中一些隐蔽的方面，其主要目的是在你浏览*拥挤的超市货架时吸引你的注意。

days.passage: Few question the propriety of the current haste on the part of manufacturers to bring out “new and improved” products at the prevalent* rate. At one time, in the dim, distant past before the advent of television, it was the vogue* for products to be advertised on the merits of their “tried and true” qualities. Few advertisers were impious enough to jettison* any part of a product that had been accepted by the public. Year after year, the local grocery store owner would proffer the same box of cereal, the same house cleaner. The acceptance was of the time-tested product, and it appeared almost unconscionable* for the manufacturer to change his merchandise. Today’s spate of transient* products would have been considered an anomaly* in those days.

days.title: WHAT’S IN A NAME?

examples.en: People often try to compensate* for their deplorable* lack of culture by repeating the shibboleth , “I know what I like.”
Many people believe the shibboleth,* “You get what you pay for,” and they purchase items on the premise* that quality varies as the price does.

examples.en: At night, raucous sounds come from the swamp.
examples.zh: 夜晚，沼泽中传来喧闹的声音。

examples.en: Acts of vandalism were committed by a raucous gang of drunkards.
examples.zh: 一群喧闹的醉汉犯下了破坏公物的行为。

days.passage: Supermarkets now carry their own products to compete with the national brands. These “house” brands are not in a felicitous* position because they cannot be advertised widely. Supermarkets overcome this encumbrance* by making these brands less expensive. Many people believe the shibboleth,* “You get what you pay for,” and they purchase items on the premise* that quality varies as the price does. Are the claims made by nationally advertised brands bogus? How can one bread company substantiate its nutritive superiority over another? As there is no incontrovertible* evidence, the more expensive bread (or coffee, etc.) must compensate* by increased advertising. They make inordinate* claims, using those raucous techniques proven so successful in convincing the frugal* consumer to switch to a more costly brand.

exercises.text: Mothers should be vigilant* that their children’s food has the proper ________________ value.
There were ________________ complaints about the inordinate* number of fatal* accidents caused by inebriated* drivers.
People often try to compensate* for their deplorable* lack of culture by repeating the ________________ , “I know what I like.”
He had the audacity* to try to foist* a ________________ dollar on me.
The reporter wanted to elicit* the pertinent* facts from the reticent* witness so he could ________________ the charge of moral turpitude* against the high city official.

idiom.meaning: to control completely
idiom.example: He winked at* the little girl’s bad behavior; she had him twisted around her finger.

days.passageZh: 超市现在也销售自有产品，以与全国品牌竞争。这些“自有”品牌并不处于有利位置*，因为它们无法广泛宣传。超市通过降低这些品牌的价格来克服这些负担*。许多人相信“一分钱一分货”的原则，他们购买商品的前提是质量会随着价格变化。全国性品牌所宣称的说法是假的吗？一家面包公司如何证明其营养优于另一家？由于没有确凿证据，更昂贵的面包（或咖啡等）必须通过增加广告来弥补。他们做出过于夸张的宣传，使用那些被证明极其成功地说服节俭消费者转向更昂贵品牌的喧闹手法。

days.story:
One of the most ______ forms of discrimination is that based upon age. We have become aware through publicity and education that bias and discrimination based upon race, color, creed, and sex are not to be accepted. Through laws passed by the Congress of the United States and by individual states, we agree that using these criteria for hiring, promoting, or firing in the workplace is a ______ and undemocratic excuse. Many lawsuits have supported this most basic right to “life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness” protected by our Constitution.

Why is it, then, that so few question the ______ of preventing those viewed as “too old” from getting positions, or, if already on the job, promotions? Advanced age also leads to the firing of such employees and their replacement with younger applicants. Is there something ______ in youth that suggests that older workers cannot do the job as well? Until age discrimination goes the way of all of the other forms of prejudice, we may continue to ______ the reasoning that “younger is better.”
days.storyZh:
最______的歧视形式之一是基于年龄的歧视。通过宣传和教育，我们意识到基于种族、肤色、信仰和性别的偏见和歧视是不可接受的。通过美国国会和各州通过的法律，我们一致认为，使用这些职场招聘、晋升或解雇的标准是一种______且不民主的借口。许多诉讼支持了宪法保护的最基本权利——“生命、自由和追求幸福”。

那么，为什么很少有人质疑阻止被视为“太老”的人获得职位，或者如果已经在职的人获得晋升______？年事已高还会导致这些员工被解雇，并由更年轻的申请者替代。青少年中是否有什么______表明年长工人无法胜任这项工作？除非年龄歧视像其他所有形式的偏见一样消失，否则我们可能会继续______“年轻者更好”的理由。

days.title: YOU CAN’T HELP BUT WATCH

examples.en: The consumer is in a quandary about making a felicitous* selection among the array* of products.
examples.zh: 消费者在众多产品中选择合适的顾虑。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be quandary.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“两难”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "quandary" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“困境”一词。

examples.en: While an infraction aboard ship was quickly criticized, Bligh never carried out those callous actions the movie dramatized in order to depict* an evil man, say his defenders.
examples.zh: 虽然船上的违规行为很快受到批评，但布莱从未采取电影中戏剧化的那些冷酷行为，以描绘一个邪恶的人，他的辩护者如是说。

examples.en: The advertisers must influence the malleable* consumer, and often they do it in the most callous ways.
examples.zh: 广告商必须影响那个易塑的消费者，而且往往以最冷酷的方式进行。

examples.en: She was so callous that she could criticise a cancer patient for wearing a wig.
examples.zh: 她冷酷到能批评癌症患者戴假发。

examples.en: The advertiser knows that a television commercial is the most expedient way to reach large numbers of people.
examples.zh: 广告商知道电视广告是触达大量人群的最快捷方式。

examples.en: Most people, faced with a decision, will choose the most expedient option.
examples.zh: 大多数人在面临决定时会选择最快捷的方案。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be expedient.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“权宜之计”的含义。

words.definition: A method or means for achieving a particular result, especially when direct or efficient; a resource.

examples.en: The cost for each commercial film is prodigious,* but because the audience is so large, the cost per viewer is negligible.
examples.zh: 每部商业电影的成本非常高昂，*但由于观众众多，每位观众的成本微乎其微。

examples.en: We found errors, but their effects were negligible.
examples.zh: 我们发现了错误，但其影响微乎其微。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be negligible.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“微不足道”的含义。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be blasé.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“冷漠”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "blasé" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“冷漠”这个词。

examples.en: He described the situation as blasé.
examples.zh: 他形容当时的情况是冷漠无所谓。

days.passage: The consumer is in a quandary about making a felicitous* selection among the array* of products. The advertisers must influence the malleable* consumer, and often they do it in the most callous ways. Television offers many tangible* advantages for reaching the consumer. As a result, the consumer is inundated* by commercials. The advertiser knows that a television commercial is the most expedient way to reach large numbers of people. The cost for each commercial film is prodigious,* but because the audience is so large, the cost per viewer is negligible. Each commercial is prepared in the most meticulous* way in order to catch the attention of even the most blasé viewer and hold it until the message is through.

exercises.text: It was fortuitous* that the accident occurred when there were ________________ numbers of children in the buses.
He was in a ________________ about which selection from his extensive repertoire* it would be feasible* to perform for the children.
Because she had committed only a venial* offense, he thought it ________________ to abjure* a severe punishment.
Who can be ________________ about the presence of many indigent* families in proximity* to affluence?*
People have become so ________________ about the once thrilling, now mundane* flights into space.

idiom.meaning: to make one feel happy or better
idiom.example: It did my heart good to see that inveterate* egotist* eat humble pie.*

days.passageZh: 消费者在众多产品中选择合适的顾虑。广告商必须影响那个易塑的消费者，而且往往以最冷酷的方式进行。电视为触达消费者提供了许多切实的*优势。因此，消费者被广告淹没*。广告商知道电视广告是触达大量人群的最快捷方式。每部商业电影的成本非常高昂，*但由于观众众多，每位观众的成本微乎其微。每则广告都以最细致*的方式准备，目的是吸引即使是最冷漠的观众，并持续关注直到信息完整。

days.title: TRICKS OF THE TRADE

examples.en: Some television commercials, trying to break through the ennui built up in the viewer by the plethora* of competition, employ humor.
examples.zh: 一些电视广告试图打破观众因竞争而产生的厌倦感，运用幽默元素。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be ennui.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是无聊。

examples.en: She used the word "ennui" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“无聊”一词。

examples.en: Others feature a comely girl as a pretext* for getting the viewer to stay tuned in.
examples.zh: 还有一些影片以一位漂亮女孩为借口*，吸引观众继续关注。

examples.en: a comely woman
examples.zh: 一位美丽的女子

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be comely.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“美丽”的含义。

examples.en: At times raucous* music, accompanied by some frenetic activities, is designed to preclude* the viewer’s loss of attention.
examples.zh: 有时喧闹的*音乐伴随着一些狂热的活动，旨在避免观众分心。

examples.en: After a week of working at a frenetic pace, she was ready for Saturday.
examples.zh: 经过一周的紧张工作，她终于准备好迎接周六的活动。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be frenetic.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是狂热。

examples.en: The advertiser will employ every bit of artifice at the film maker’s command to make a trenchant* commercial.
examples.zh: 广告商会根据电影制作者的指令，运用所有的人工手段来制作犀利的*广告。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be artifice.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了什么是人造。

examples.en: She used the word "artifice" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“人为”一词。

examples.en: The diversity of appeals made to the viewer is a concomitant* of the many ways people react to commercials.
examples.zh: 对观众的多样化呼吁是人们对广告反应方式的必然结果。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be diversity.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了多样性的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "diversity" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“多样性”一词。

days.passage: Some television commercials, trying to break through the ennui built up in the viewer by the plethora* of competition, employ humor. Others feature a comely girl as a pretext* for getting the viewer to stay tuned in. At times raucous* music, accompanied by some frenetic activities, is designed to preclude* the viewer’s loss of attention. The advertiser will employ every bit of artifice at the film maker’s command to make a trenchant* commercial. The diversity of appeals made to the viewer is a concomitant* of the many ways people react to commercials. A great deal of time and money has gone into placing the consumer’s psychological make-up under scrutiny.*

exercises.text: The omnipotent* dictator employed all of his rhetoric* to vilify* those who would be brash* enough to suggest that a ________________ of opinions should be expressed.
The fledgling* pianist knew that his mentor* would take umbrage* at his yawning during the lesson, but the feeling of ________________ was overwhelming.
He was reticent* about revealing his clandestine* meetings with a ________________ young girl counselor at this camp.
They furtively* employed every kind of ________________ to be able to meet.
They were vigilant* in order that their surreptitious* meetings would not be discovered, and it often required ________________ changes of plans to preclude* exposure.

idiom.meaning: extremely valuable, very useful
idiom.example: The coach said the new star center was worth his weight in gold.

days.passageZh: 一些电视广告试图打破观众因竞争而产生的厌倦感，运用幽默元素。还有一些影片以一位漂亮女孩为借口*，吸引观众继续关注。有时喧闹的*音乐伴随着一些狂热的活动，旨在避免观众分心。广告商会根据电影制作者的指令，运用所有的人工手段来制作犀利的*广告。对观众的多样化呼吁是人们对广告反应方式的必然结果。大量时间和金钱都投入到对消费者心理构成进行审视。*

days.title: GOING TO THE SOURCE

examples.en: They do not have the slightest qualm about questioning the consumer about personal things in her own domicile.* The consumer is requested not to expurgate* her answers.
examples.zh: 他们对质问消费者在自己住所的个人事务毫无顾忌。* 请消费者不要删除*她的回答。

examples.en: This lawyer has no qualms about saving people who are on the wrong side of the law.
examples.zh: 这位律师毫不犹豫地拯救那些触犯法律的人。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be qualm.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是“疑虑”。

examples.en: They do not have the slightest qualm about questioning the consumer about personal things in her own domicile.* The consumer is requested not to expurgate* her answers.
examples.zh: 他们对质问消费者在自己住所的个人事务毫无顾忌。* 请消费者不要删除*她的回答。

examples.en: The publisher decided to expurgate the love scene from the book, to make it more child-friendly.
examples.zh: 出版社决定删减爱情戏，使其更适合儿童阅读。

examples.en: The publisher decided to expurgate the book, which meant removing the love scene.
examples.zh: 出版社决定删减这本书，这意味着要删掉爱情戏。

examples.en: Generally, people are not reticent* and do not begrudge giving the time and effort.
examples.zh: 一般来说，人们不会吝啬*，也不介意付出时间和精力。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be begrudge.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了怨恨的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "begrudge" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“怨恨”一词。

examples.en: The questions delve rather deeply, and what the artless responses divulge* will help the advertiser decide what to put into his next commercial.
examples.zh: 这些问题相当深入，而那些无精明的回答所透露的内容*将帮助广告商决定下一个广告中该放什么内容。

examples.en: This pendant has artless charm.
examples.zh: 这条吊坠有着无艺术感的魅力。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be artless.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“无艺术”的含义。

examples.en: The interviewer usually offers no gratuity to the person who has helped, but often a sample of the product is proffered* as thanks.
examples.zh: 面试官通常不会给予帮助者小费，但通常会提供产品样品*以示感谢。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be gratuity.
examples.zh: 背景清楚地展示了“小费”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "gratuity" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“小费”这个词。

days.passage: The wide diversity* of reasons people have for buying one product rather than another are investigated by the advertising people in order to prepare efficacious* commercials. They do not have the slightest qualm about questioning the consumer about personal things in her own domicile.* The consumer is requested not to expurgate* her answers. Generally, people are not reticent* and do not begrudge giving the time and effort. The questions delve rather deeply, and what the artless responses divulge* will help the advertiser decide what to put into his next commercial. After a large number of interviews, the copious* results make it feasible* to prognosticate* how well the commercial will do. The interviewer usually offers no gratuity to the person who has helped, but often a sample of the product is proffered* as thanks.

exercises.text: A successful television program can be built around the ________________ comments of very young children.
At times, the producer must ________________ some of the things said by these children because they are too candid.*
He had a serious ________________ about hunting for the nearly extinct* quarry.*
He took umbrage* when I offered a ________________ to augment* his small salary.
She did not ________________ paying the pittance* extra for a better coat.

idiom.phrase: to make the best of a bad bargain
idiom.meaning: to change or go along with a poor situation
idiom.example: After he bought the white elephant,* he made the best of a bad bargain and let sleeping dogs lie.*

days.passageZh: 广告人员会调查人们购买某一产品而非另一种产品的各种原因，以便制作有效的广告。他们对质问消费者在自己住所的个人事务毫无顾忌。* 请消费者不要删除*她的回答。一般来说，人们不会吝啬*，也不介意付出时间和精力。这些问题相当深入，而那些无精明的回答所透露的内容*将帮助广告商决定下一个广告中该放什么内容。经过大量访谈，丰富的*结果使得预测*广告的表现变得可行。面试官通常不会给予帮助者小费，但通常会提供产品样品*以示感谢。

days.title: IT SEEMS TO WORK

examples.en: It was manifest that an arbiter* would be needed because neither side would capitulate* to a plan foisted* on them by the other side.

Despite the antipathy* toward commercials expressed by the viewers, the remarkable success of television commercials in selling products makes it manifest that the advertiser has gleaned* what the viewer wants to see and hear from his research interview.
examples.zh: 尽管观众对广告持反感*态度，但电视广告在产品销售上的显著成功表明，广告主已经从他的研究访谈中获得了观众想看到和听到的内容。

examples.en: His courage manifested itself through the look on his face.
examples.zh: 他的勇气从脸上的表情中显现出来。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be manifest.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是显化。

words.definition: A list or invoice of the passengers or goods being carried by a commercial vehicle or ship.

examples.en: If we delve below and behind the rhetoric* and invective,* we may discover the profound* reasons for the ferment* in our land.
This has helped the advertiser delve deeply into what motivates* people when they go into the supermarket to purchase products.
examples.zh: 这帮助广告主深入挖掘人们在超市购买产品的动机。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be delve.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“深渊”的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "delve" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“深入”一词。

words.definition: To dig the ground, especially with a shovel.

examples.en: When the acrimonious* discussion about his capricious actions had attenuated,* he was able to vindicate* his conduct.
The advertising agency is never capricious and can vindicate* spending large sums of money on research.
examples.zh: 广告公司从不任性，能够证明*他们投入大量资金进行调研是正确的。

examples.en: I almost died in a capricious winter storm.
examples.zh: 我差点死在一场任性无常的冬季风暴中。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be capricious.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了反复无常的含义。

words.definition: Impulsive and unpredictable; determined by chance, impulse, or whim

examples.en: One mortifying* requisite for the position was that he would have to work for one year under the aegis* of a fatuous* egotist.*
Having uncovered what the public wants, the advertiser expedites* putting the requisite words, music, and photographs of the product on film.
examples.zh: 在发现公众需求后，广告主会加快*将产品的必要文字、音乐和照片拍成胶片。

examples.en: Please submit the requisite papers before the end of the financial year.
examples.zh: 请在财政年度结束前提交所需文件。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be requisite.
examples.zh: 语境清楚地展示了“必需品”的含义。

examples.en: He was reticent* about emulating* those who, after eating almost to satiety,* rushed to replenish the food on their plates.
He will thus replenish the never-ending, ubiquitous* television commercial supply in the hope that the consumer will remember some facet* of the film and buy the product.
examples.zh: 他将补充源源不断、无处不在的电视广告供应，希望观众能记住电影的某些方面，从而购买产品。

examples.en: It's a popular product, and they have to replenish their stock of it frequently.
examples.zh: 这是个受欢迎的产品，他们必须频繁补充库存。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be replenish.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了“补充”的含义。

words.definition: To refill; to renew; to supply again or to add a fresh quantity to.

days.passage: Despite the antipathy* toward commercials expressed by the viewers, the remarkable success of television commercials in selling products makes it manifest that the advertiser has gleaned* what the viewer wants to see and hear from his research interview. This has helped the advertiser delve deeply into what motivates* people when they go into the supermarket to purchase products. The advertising agency is never capricious and can vindicate* spending large sums of money on research. Having uncovered what the public wants, the advertiser expedites* putting the requisite words, music, and photographs of the product on film. He will thus replenish the never-ending, ubiquitous* television commercial supply in the hope that the consumer will remember some facet* of the film and buy the product.

exercises.text: If we ________________ below and behind the rhetoric* and invective,* we may discover the profound* reasons for the ferment* in our land.
He was reticent* about emulating* those who, after eating almost to satiety,* rushed to ________________ the food on their plates.
It was ________________ that an arbiter* would be needed because neither side would capitulate* to a plan foisted* on them by the other side.
When the acrimonious* discussion about his ________________ actions had attenuated,* he was able to vindicate* his conduct.
One mortifying* ________________ for the position was that he would have to work for one year under the aegis* of a fatuous* egotist.*

idiom.meaning: to manage on a given income
idiom.example: He turned thumbs down* on a new car; he was having enough trouble making ends meet, as it was.

days.passageZh: 尽管观众对广告持反感*态度，但电视广告在产品销售上的显著成功表明，广告主已经从他的研究访谈中获得了观众想看到和听到的内容。这帮助广告主深入挖掘人们在超市购买产品的动机。广告公司从不任性，能够证明*他们投入大量资金进行调研是正确的。在发现公众需求后，广告主会加快*将产品的必要文字、音乐和照片拍成胶片。他将补充源源不断、无处不在的电视广告供应，希望观众能记住电影的某些方面，从而购买产品。

to make one feel happy or better

days.story:
One event that takes place so rarely that almost no one alive when it happens can remember the previous occurrence is the changing of the century number. The passing of the ______ number of years brings about the end of the 20th century and the advent of the 21st. Is there anyone ______ enough to reach this historic date without experiencing the excitement of this once-in-a-lifetime moment?

While we may feel that events in our lifetime happen in a ______ way, the stroke of midnight on December 31, 2000, ushered in a new century. It served as a time to reflect upon the ______ of events in our lives, both positive and negative, that the 20th century encompassed. It is obvious to all that the past 100 years have altered the world in ways no one could anticipate at the end of the 19th century. There are many who ______ into the past and make predictions for the new century. December 31, 2000, was a time for reflection and promise.
days.storyZh:
有一个极其罕见的事件，以至于几乎没有人能记得之前发生过的事，那就是世纪数字的变换。______年的流逝标志着20世纪的结束和21世纪的到来。有没有人足够______，能在没有体验过这千载难逢时刻的激动人心的情况下，达到这一历史性的时刻？

虽然我们可能觉得自己一生中的事件以______的方式发生，但2000年12月31日午夜的钟声响起，迎来了一个新世纪。这段时间让人们反思20世纪生活中各种事件的积极与负面______。众所周知，过去100年以19世纪末无人预料的方式改变了世界。许多人______回溯过去，对新世纪做出预测。2000年12月31日，是反思和充满希望的时刻。

days.title: IT TAKES MORE THAN MEDICINE

days.passage: If one were to look at the roster of physical handicaps, one would reach the somber* conclusion that the list is a long one. Included would be stunted development of an arm or leg due to a birth anomaly.* Others would be the result of a crippling disease that has caused muscles to atrophy. The list would go on with illnesses and injuries that maim and debilitate.* Modern medicine has done much to ameliorate the physical problems. However, there are an inordinate* number of problems of the handicapped that have still to be alleviated.* People are not naturally callous,* but in some perverse* way they have the propensity* to repress* any concern with the physically handicapped. The social problems seem to be inherent* in our own attitudes.

exercises.text: If you heap opprobrium* on an impious* child, it probably will not ________________ the conditions that led to the rebelliousness.
The coach knew he would have to add experienced players to the ________________ to compensate* for the spate* of freshmen on the team.
There seems to be voluminous* evidence that the mother’s smoking will ________________ the baby’s growth.
The prodigy* allowed his musical talent to ________________ as he redirected his career.
When it seemed that Reggie would ________________ his opponent, we broke up the fight.

days.passageZh: 如果看身体残疾名单，会得出一个沉重的结论：名单很长。包括因出生异常导致手臂或腿部发育迟缓。* 其他则是因严重疾病导致肌肉萎缩。还有许多疾病和伤害，导致残疾和虚弱。* 现代医学在改善身体问题方面做了很大努力。然而，残障人士仍有大量问题需要解决。人们天生冷漠，但以某种扭曲的方式，他们倾向于压抑对身体残障者的关切。社会问题似乎根植于我们自身的态度中。

days.title: DOING THE RIGHT THING

examples.en: The obstacles that frustrate* the physically handicapped person who is seeking employment may turn him into a cynic.
examples.zh: 让身体残障人士感到沮丧的障碍，可能会让他变得愤世嫉俗。

examples.en: Too often a prospective employer, with a rather unctuous manner, actually tends to degrade* the handicapped by proffering* employment that is really beneath them and their abilities.

examples.en: The employer appears to be acting in a benevolent manner, but this attitude shows no compassion,* for he really expects the person seeking the job to remain subservient.

examples.en: Chinese and Eastern mythologies describe dragons as benevolent.

examples.en: The employer appears to be acting in a benevolent manner, but this attitude shows no compassion,* for he really expects the person seeking the job to remain subservient.

days.passage: The obstacles that frustrate* the physically handicapped person who is seeking employment may turn him into a cynic. Too often a prospective employer, with a rather unctuous manner, actually tends to degrade* the handicapped by proffering* employment that is really beneath them and their abilities. The employer appears to be acting in a benevolent manner, but this attitude shows no compassion,* for he really expects the person seeking the job to remain subservient. This iniquity cannot but give the handicapped a feeling that they are being discriminated against. He does not expect a sinecure,* but he has an aversion* to the prevalent* belief that he should consider himself lucky to find any employment.

exercises.text: We had to wince* as we watched the newcomer try to wheedle* and ingratiate* himself into the teacher’s favor in the most ________________ manner.
It is easy to become a ________________ when the same adults who inveigh* most vehemently* against the uncouth actions that they say permeate* our youth drink to satiety* and behave fatuously.*
We all have moments when we vacillate* between selfish and ________________ desires.
While his demeanor* remained imperturbable,* there was latent* anger at the ignominious* and ________________ role he had to play.
Those who are complacent* about any ________________ in our society should be wary* of the unsavory* consequences for all.

idiom.phrase: to lay one’s cards on the table
idiom.meaning: to talk frankly
idiom.example: He knew he was out of his depth* so he laid his cards on the table and asked for assistance.

days.passageZh: 让身体残障人士感到沮丧的障碍，可能会让他变得愤世嫉俗。太多时候，潜在雇主以相当油滑的态度，实际上通过提供不符合残疾人和能力的工作来贬低残障人士。雇主表面上表现得仁慈，但这种态度毫无怜悯之心*，因为他实际上期望求职者保持顺从。这种不公无疑让残障人士感到自己被歧视。他并不指望获得闲职，*但他反感普遍认为自己应该幸运找到工作的想法。

days.title: A BETTER WAY

examples.zh: 而雇主则会觉得这有利于良好工作，也不会后悔尝试新方法以安抚良心。

examples.en: Even for the most mercenary employer, there should be no reticence* in eliciting* the best that is possible from the handicapped worker.

words.definition: One motivated by gain, especially monetary.

days.passage: Why is there any question about the propriety* of hiring the physically handicapped? No one who understands their needs can condone* this attitude. The offering of employment should not be considered a largess. There should be no need to vindicate* the hiring of a handicapped person. The only criterion should be what he is capable of doing. If this is the approach, the handicapped worker will not feel he is an encumbrance* to his boss. The employer, on the other hand, will find it conducive* to good work and will not repent his having tried something new just to mollify his conscience. Even for the most mercenary employer, there should be no reticence* in eliciting* the best that is possible from the handicapped worker.

exercises.text: He felt it would be ignominious* for him to accept any ________________ from the charlatan* whose Machiavellian* schemes had made him affluent.*
Behind the façade* of ostensible* benevolence* there was a ________________ streak.
The platitude, “I know what I like,” is often used to rationalize* our lack of a ________________ for things about which we are dubious.*
When Mother is in a pique* about some infraction* of a rule, it takes all of our dexterity* to ________________ her.
After every election we ________________ , in a belated* criticism, the apathy* and complacency* of so many people who failed to vote.

days.passageZh: 为什么雇佣身体残障人士的适当性*会受到质疑？任何理解他们需求的人都无法容忍*这种态度。提供就业不应被视为慷慨施行。没有必要为雇佣残障人士辩护。唯一的标准应该是他能做什么。如果采用这种方式，残障工人就不会觉得自己是老板的负担*。而雇主则会觉得这有利于良好工作，也不会后悔尝试新方法以安抚良心。即使是最为唯利是图的雇主，也不应有任何顾虑*去激发*残疾工人发挥*最大潜力。

days.title: JUST BE YOURSELF

examples.en: Socially, the handicapped person is often treated as a pariah.
examples.zh: 在社会层面，残障人士常被视为弃儿。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be pariah.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了被弃儿的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "pariah" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确使用了“弃儿”一词。

examples.en: Most people hold themselves aloof from normal contact with those who are “different.” This social separation propagates* additional feelings of antipathy*.
examples.zh: 大多数人都与那些“与众不同”的人保持距离。这种社会隔离会带来*更多的反感*。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be aloof.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是冷漠。

examples.en: She used the word "aloof" correctly in her essay.
examples.zh: 她在文章中正确地使用了“冷漠”这个词。

examples.en: If “normal” individuals would socialize with the handicapped individual, they would learn in a pragmatic way that these are people who happen to have a physical handicap; the handicap does not make them any less human.
examples.zh: 如果“正常”的人愿意与残障人士社交，他们会以务实的方式了解到，这些人恰好有身体残疾;残疾并不意味着他们变得不够人性。

examples.en: The sturdy furniture in the student lounge was pragmatic, but unattractive.
examples.zh: 学生休息室里结实的家具很实用，但并不美观。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be pragmatic.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了务实的含义。

examples.en: As long as there is a vestige of feeling that handicapped people are inferior, then we are all handicapped in one way or another.
examples.zh: 只要残留者仍被认为低人一等，我们每个人在某种程度上都是残障的。

examples.en: the vestiges of ancient magnificence in Palmyra
examples.zh: 帕尔米拉古老辉煌的遗迹

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be vestige.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了什么是残留物。

examples.en: Under the guise of physical superiority we demonstrate a moral turpitude* that is harmful to all.
examples.zh: 在身体优越的幌子下，我们展现出一种对所有人都有害的道德败坏*。

examples.en: Under the guise of patriotism
examples.zh: 以爱国主义的名义

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be guise.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了伪装的含义。

days.passage: Socially, the handicapped person is often treated as a pariah. Most people hold themselves aloof from normal contact with those who are “different.” This social separation propagates* additional feelings of antipathy*. If “normal” individuals would socialize with the handicapped individual, they would learn in a pragmatic way that these are people who happen to have a physical handicap; the handicap does not make them any less human. The iniquity* of assuming that physical superiority equals moral superiority prevents all of us from direct human relationships. As long as there is a vestige of feeling that handicapped people are inferior, then we are all handicapped in one way or another. Under the guise of physical superiority we demonstrate a moral turpitude* that is harmful to all.

exercises.text: After therapy*, there remained hardly a ________________ of his phobia*.
He was stigmatized* as a ________________ when he had the audacity* to boast of his nefarious* and sordid* career printing bogus* money.
Although many people say this is a propitious* time to invest in the stock market, there is a tenable* argument for remaining ________________ .
In the ________________ of maintaining national unity under military rule, there was a paucity* of even innocuous* dissent*.
“You can’t argue with success,” was his ________________ reply to derogatory* remarks about a movie star who had only superficial* talent as an actor.

idiom.phrase: to tell tales out of school
idiom.meaning: to reveal harmful secrets
idiom.example: The fat was in the fire* for the politician when his private secretary started telling tales out of school about his secret sources of income.

days.passageZh: 在社会层面，残障人士常被视为弃儿。大多数人都与那些“与众不同”的人保持距离。这种社会隔离会带来*更多的反感*。如果“正常”的人愿意与残障人士社交，他们会以务实的方式了解到，这些人恰好有身体残疾;残疾并不意味着他们变得不够人性。认为身体优越等同于道德优越的不公*，阻碍了我们所有人与人建立直接的人际关系。只要残留者仍被认为低人一等，我们每个人在某种程度上都是残障的。在身体优越的幌子下，我们展现出一种对所有人都有害的道德败坏*。

days.story:
There appears to be a question of how much loyalty employees owe to their employers—whether private or governmental. Many companies go out of their way to encourage employees to make suggestions that will improve the way they operate. A ______ employer will not criticize or reprimand an employee who points out problems having to do with the way other employees are harming the business. In fact, it should be in the bosses’ interest that the person who has become known as a “whistle blower” is encouraged to alert them to a problem.

However, many such whistle blowers face harsh punishment for calling attention to illegal or unethical actions. The whistle blower soon becomes a ______ in the workplace. Under the ______ of some minor error, or other excuse, the informer might be demoted, transferred, or fired. This ______ often goes unreported. As a result, the employees go back to “business as usual” without any change. They become used to whatever they may see around them and to the belief that they should not make waves. Thus, no attempt to ______ the situation actually takes place.
days.storyZh:
员工对雇主——无论是私人还是政府——应负多少忠诚度，似乎存在一个问题。许多公司都不遗余力地鼓励员工提出改进工作方式的建议。______雇主不会批评或训斥指出其他员工损害企业问题的员工。事实上，老板们应该有利益，鼓励被称为“举报人”的人及时提醒他们问题。

然而，许多此类举报人因揭露非法或不道德行为而受到严厉惩罚。举报人很快成为职场上的______。______因某些小错误或其他借口，举报者可能会被降职、调职或解雇。这种______常常未被报道。结果，员工们又回到了“照常营业”，没有任何变化。他们习惯了周围所见的一切，并相信自己不应该惹事生非。因此，实际上并没有试图______局面。

days.title: HAVE WE MASTERED OUR ENVIRONMENT?

examples.en: Natural disasters tend to nullify the best efforts of mankind.
examples.zh: 自然灾害往往使人类的努力失效。

examples.en: The contract has been nullified.
examples.zh: 合同已被取消。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be nullify.
examples.zh: 上下文清楚地展示了“无效化”的含义。

examples.en: Who has not read of or seen the waters that deluge our towns and cities, jeopardizing* lives and culminating* in the destruction of the results of endless work in the space of a few moments?
examples.zh: 谁没读过或见过淹没我们城镇的洪水，危及*生命*，最终*在短短几秒钟内毁灭了无尽劳动的成果？

examples.en: The deluge continued for hours, drenching the land and slowing traffic to a halt.
examples.zh: 暴雨持续了数小时，淋湿了土地，交通也因此停滞。

examples.en: The rock concert was a deluge of sound.
examples.zh: 摇滚音乐会是一场声音洪流。

examples.en: We are all vulnerable* to feelings of futility as we view the carnage caused to cattle from the sudden inundation.* Despite the laudable* advances made in technology,* it can be seen that we cannot yet say we have mastered our environment.
examples.zh: 当我们看到突如其来的洪水对牛群造成的屠杀时，我们都容易感到无力感。* 尽管技术取得了值得称赞的*进步，* 但可以看出，我们还不能说我们已经掌控了环境。

examples.en: His taking the bar exam for a third time was pure futility.
examples.zh: 他第三次参加律师资格考试纯属徒劳。

examples.en: We are all vulnerable* to feelings of futility as we view the carnage caused to cattle from the sudden inundation.* Despite the laudable* advances made in technology,* it can be seen that we cannot yet say we have mastered our environment.
examples.zh: 当我们看到突如其来的洪水对牛群造成的屠杀时，我们都容易感到无力感。* 尽管技术取得了值得称赞的*进步，* 但可以看出，我们还不能说我们已经掌控了环境。

examples.en: The context clearly shows what it means to be carnage.
examples.zh: 这个背景清楚地展示了屠杀的含义。

examples.en: She used the word "carnage" correctly in her essay.

examples.en: Yet modern technology progresses so rapidly that what may be today’s wild dream may be next year’s kitchen appliance.
examples.zh: 然而，现代科技进步如此之快，今天可能成为梦想中的厨房电器，明年才会成为厨房电器。

examples.en: Man may be freed from backbreaking labor by the products of scientific technology.
examples.zh: 人类可以通过科学技术的成果摆脱繁重劳动。

examples.en: With his expertise in technology, my uncle is able to earn a good salary.
examples.zh: 凭借他的技术专长，我叔叔能赚到不错的薪水。

days.passage: Natural disasters tend to nullify the best efforts of mankind. It is as though there are forces at work that are contemptuous* of our proud achievements. Who has not read of or seen the waters that deluge our towns and cities, jeopardizing* lives and culminating* in the destruction of the results of endless work in the space of a few moments? We are all vulnerable* to feelings of futility as we view the carnage caused to cattle from the sudden inundation.* Despite the laudable* advances made in technology,* it can be seen that we cannot yet say we have mastered our environment. Disasters of this type, leaving only pathetic* vestiges* of homes and shops, are accepted as inevitable,* and all we can do is to attempt to ameliorate* the conditions that result.

exercises.text: In spite of his efforts to cajole* the girl, she remained aloof,* and the ________________ of his efforts made him lugubrious.*
To our consternation,* modern ________________ has made feasible* a spate* of lethal* devices that could lead to the inadvertent* destruction of the world.
In order to ________________ the height advantage of his adversary,* he abjured* smoking and did an inordinate amount of exercise until he was the acme* of litheness* and dexterity.*
We found it impossible to mollify* the irate* owner of three prize cats as he viewed the ________________ caused by our large dog.
The office was ________________ with requests for his autograph as the girls became cognizant* of his identity.

idiom.meaning: to have a poor base, or not sufficient preparation
idiom.example: Because they were amateurs and without money, the political campaign was built upon sand and the candidate was a flash in the pan.*

days.passageZh: 自然灾害往往使人类的努力失效。仿佛有力量在作祟，轻视我们引以为傲的成就。谁没读过或见过淹没我们城镇的洪水，危及*生命*，最终*在短短几秒钟内毁灭了无尽劳动的成果？当我们看到突如其来的洪水对牛群造成的屠杀时，我们都容易感到无力感。* 尽管技术取得了值得称赞的*进步，* 但可以看出，我们还不能说我们已经掌控了环境。这类灾难只留下可怜的*残余*房屋和商店*，被视为不可避免*，我们所能做的就是努力改善*由此产生的条件。

days.passage: One of the latent* dangers indigenous* to our constitutional guarantee of freedom of the press has to do with the protection of the individual against the detriment* that might come from news reports involving him. There are libel laws that protect against false charges. If an individual believes his character or livelihood have been damaged by a defamatory article, he can sue. As the plaintiff he must refute* the story and show how the defendant caused him harm by printing a canard. The defendant attempts to substantiate* the truth of the article. The printing of news may besmirch* an individual’s character, but there is no way to alleviate* this problem without changes in the Constitution. This would be tantamount* to destroying the efficacy* of our coveted* right to learn the truth from the press. We all deprecate a situation in which someone suffers because of exposure in the newspapers. Only when the harm is caused by someone with a desire to malign* under the guise* of printing the news can the individual expect to win compensation* through the courts.

Publishers of newspapers and magazines augment* their staff with lawyers to represent them when they are sued for ________________ .
The cynic* will ________________ the motives of anyone who tries to ameliorate* the iniquities* in our society.

days.passageZh: 我们宪法保障新闻自由的一个潜在*本土*危险*，是关于保护个人免受涉及其的新闻报道可能带来的损害。有诽谤法保护免受虚假指控。如果个人认为自己的人格或生计因诽谤性文章而受损，他可以提起诉讼。作为原告，他必须驳斥*该说法，并展示被告通过印刷谣言对他造成了伤害。被告试图证明*文章的真实性。新闻的印刷可能会玷污个人的品格，但没有修改宪法，无法缓解*这一问题。这无异于摧毁我们珍视的*从媒体获取真相的权利的效力。我们都反感因为报纸曝光而受苦的情况。只有当伤害是由有人以印刷新闻为名进行诽谤的人造成时，个人才能期望通过法院获得赔偿。

days.passage: Soren Kierkegaard was a Danish philosopher who is reputed to be the forerunner of the current vogue* of existentialism. In appearance he was a frail and ungainly man. An extremely erudite* thinker and writer, he was a potent force in propagating* the new approach to life. His philosophy would excoriate those who believed that man could stand aside from life. In his philosophy it is a heresy* to take a detached point of view; it is incumbent* upon the individual to get involved. What is germane* is not that we exist, but that our existence is determined by our acts. He was a religiously devout man who fervidly* believed that the individual is always paramount.*

days.passage: The saga* of the introduction of that diminutive song bird, the canary, into the homes of the world as tame pets is an interesting one. In the sixteenth century a trading ship going to Italy stopped at an island named “Canis,” from the Latin word for wild dog, which could be found there in profuse numbers, off the coast of Africa. The dulcet song of the wild birds whetted* the interest of the captain. In impromptu cages hundreds were taken aboard to be traded. The sailors called these gray-green birds, spotted with yellow, “canaries.” As they approached the island of Elba, near Italy, a malevolent storm put the boat in jeopardy* of sinking. A member of the crew released the birds, and the intrepid* canaries instinctively flew towards land. The peasants on Elba took the wild canaries in as pets. Eventually, the birds found their way into homes throughout Europe where they were domesticated and bred for variety of song and shades of colors. The canaries prevalent* today differ greatly from the ones discovered over four hundred years ago.

days.passageZh: 关于那只小巧的鸣禽——金丝雀作为驯养宠物进入世界家庭的故事*，是一段有趣的故事。十六世纪时，一艘前往意大利的商船停靠在一个名为“Canis”的岛屿，该岛源自拉丁语中意为野狗的词，野生狗数量众多，位于非洲海岸附近。野鸟的悦耳歌声激起了船长的兴趣。数百人被临时囚笼带上船进行交易。水手们称这些灰绿色、带有黄色斑点的鸟为“金丝雀”。当他们接近意大利附近的厄尔巴岛时，一场恶意的风暴使船只陷入沉没危险。船员中的一名成员放飞了鸟类，勇敢的金丝雀本能地朝陆地飞去。厄尔巴岛的农民们把野生金丝雀当作宠物收养。最终，这些鸟类进入了欧洲各地的家园，被驯化和培育，以获得多样的歌声和色彩。如今盛行的金丝雀*与四百多年前发现的金丝雀有很大不同。

days.story:
One of the most difficult problems to resolve has to do with the conflicting interests of environmentalists and profit-making businesses. Examples of this dilemma appear frequently. While the dispute about cutting down a forest to preserve owls has been in the news, there appeared another conflict in the state of Alaska. Hoping to increase the number of tourists who seek to hunt deer and caribou, the State of Alaska ordered the killing of some of the ______ number of wolves that prey on those animals.

This resulted in a ______ of letters and articles condemning the ______ that would result from the anti-wolf policy. So, once again, the environmentalists, who maintain that the natural balance should not be interfered with, ran up against the Alaskan tourist industry, which wants to attract hunters who will increase the state’s revenue. After much publicity about the wolf hunt and articles that tended to ______ this policy, Alaska decided to ______ the proposed action.
days.storyZh:
最难解决的问题之一，是环保人士与盈利企业之间的利益冲突。这种困境的例子经常出现。关于砍伐森林以保护猫头鹰的争议成为新闻焦点，阿拉斯加州又出现了另一场冲突。为了增加游客数量，阿拉斯加州下令猎杀部分捕食这些动物的______狼。

这导致______信件和文章谴责反狼政策带来的______。因此，环保人士再次与阿拉斯加旅游业发生冲突，他们主张自然平衡不应涉，后者希望吸引猎人以增加州财政收入。在关于狼狩猎的广泛宣传和倾向于______支持这一政策的文章之后，阿拉斯加决定______该提议的行动。

words.definition: A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.

days.passage: Current novels are replete* with lurid* crimes, carnage* and death. Do you get wistful when you recall the romantic tales that begin with an innocent maiden travelling through the rustic* countryside? She is dressed in glittering raiment. The scene is idyllic.* Without warning, the group is set upon by a virile* brigand, who, in the most perfunctory* and callous* fashion, carries her off. Pandemonium* results! Her entourage* is in a state of bedlam.* Her corpulent escort is irate,* but unable to do anything to thwart* this debacle.* All he can do is rail against the catastrophe. What to do? What to do?

exercises.text: The potpourri* of au courant* fashionable ________________ includes the fatuous* and the discreet.*
While all disgruntled* men may ________________ against malevolent* or Machiavellian* leaders, democracy offers a way to ameliorate* iniquities* through the ballot.
Is there any veracity in the platitude* that ________________ men are jocose?*
To be candid,* there is little to be ________________ about in the “good old days.”
They captured the ________________ , and he was incarcerated* for a mandatory* period.

idiom.meaning: to continue to make an issue of something that is over

days.passageZh: 当前的小说充斥着*骇人听闻*的犯罪、屠杀和死亡。当你回想起那些以一位纯真少女穿越乡村为开头的浪漫故事时，你会感到一丝惆怅吗？她穿着闪亮的服装。场景如画。* 毫无预警，一行人遭到一名阳刚*强盗袭击，他以最敷衍且冷酷的方式将她带走。混乱* 结果！她的随从们*陷入了混乱。*她那肥胖的护卫愤怒不已，*但无能为力阻止*这场灾难。*他所能做的只有对这场灾难的愤怒。该怎么办？该怎么办？

days.passage: The raconteur of our story about idyllic* times gone by goes on to elucidate* how the comely* heroine is taken to the bandits’ hideout. There, a sullen crew of cutthroats is gathered. They don’t wish to procrastinate;* she must be taken immediately to a foreign land where much treasure will be paid for her. Their cupidity* knows no bounds. The leader wants to hold her for ransom from her wealthy parents. The gang demurs;* they are reticent.* There is a rift among the criminals. Their leader remains truculent, and they agree to wait for just two days for the ransom money. An emissary from the grief-stricken parents is expected at any moment. The wan* maiden, her spirits at their nadir,* has time to ruminate about her lugubrious* fate.

exercises.text: He alluded* to the ________________ caused in the school by the plethora* of hirsute* boys who ignored the criterion* for appearance.

Despite all attempts to mollify* her, she remained ________________ about the levity* caused by her slovenly* raiment.*
The obscure* country, an aspirant* for membership in the United Nations, sent a(n) ________________ .
An anomaly* of our modern technology* is that the more we need to know, the less time we have to ________________ .

days.passageZh: 讲述我们关于田园诗般美好时光的故事讲述者继续阐述了这位美丽的女主角如何被带到土匪的藏身处。在那里，一群阴郁的无情之徒聚集在一起。他们不想拖延;* 她必须立刻被带到异国他乡，那里将有大量宝藏为她付出。他们的贪婪*无边无际。头目想从她富有的父母那里扣押她勒索赎金。帮派推辞;*他们很沉默。* 罪犯之间出现了裂痕。他们的首领依然强硬，他们同意只等两天赎金。悲痛欲绝的父母随时会派来使者。苍白的少女，精神处于最低谷，*有时间沉思自己那忧郁*的命运。

days.passage: Back at the castle, the situation is taut with emotion. The fair maiden’s mother is livid with fear and anxiety; she has attacks of vertigo.* She talks about her daughter’s audacity* in riding out into the ominous* forests despite many similar kidnappings. The girl’s father, a martinet who rules his family with an iron hand, staunchly* refuses to pay the ransom. Iniquity* shall not be rewarded! At this moment of crisis a heroic knight volunteers to rescue our heroine; he has had a secret yen for the young beauty. Avoiding rhetoric,* he pledges his all to castigate* those responsible for this ignominious* deed. He holds his life as a mere bagatelle against the duty he owes his beloved mistress. At the propitious* moment, he rides off to do or die for her.

exercises.text: The rabid* baseball fan lost his equanimity* and became ________________ when the star pitcher became pugnacious* and was removed from the game.

When one enlists in the army, one expects to be under the aegis* of a _______________ .
His ________________ for imbibing* and romping* with girls worked to his detriment*.
The little boy tried to wheedle* a larger allowance from his father by the caustic* observations that it was a mere ________________ when compared to the allowances of his friends.

days.passage: Seeking his adversaries,* the knight rides to their hideout. Despite his callow appearance, he is reputed* to disdain* danger and to be a prodigious* horseman. The kidnappers lose their equanimity* at his approach. They are appalled at the prospect, and they are in a quandary* as to which one will meet him on the field of combat. The leader, under duress,* rides out. “Do you have a penchant to die?” derides* the knight. More vituperative* remarks follow. They spur their horses toward each other. It takes but one blow for our hero to decapitate the villain. The others flee to avoid their imminent* destruction. The knight takes the maiden on his horse, and they ride back to the castle. Their wedding soon follows. Little does the knight realize that the fair maiden is a garrulous* termagant who will make his life miserable with caustic* remarks. Still, the cliché,* “And they lived happily ever after,” must conclude our fabricated* tale.

exercises.text: We do not ________________ criminals because of our aversion* to such repugnant* punishments.

Why do you remain docile* while that ________________ besmirches,* maligns,* and belittles* you?
Each long holiday weekend we are ________________ at the carnage* on our highways.
It was deplorable* the way the capricious* girl led the ________________ youth on a merry chase.

idiom.meaning: the finishing stroke
idiom.example: When my girlfriend left me, it was a bitter pill to swallow,* but the coup de grâce was that she kept my engagement ring.

days.passageZh: 寻找对手，*骑士骑马前往他们的藏身处。尽管外表稚嫩，他据说*鄙视*危险，并且是一位天才*的骑手。绑匪们在他接近时失去了平静*。他们对这一前景感到震惊，陷入了两难*，不知道谁会在战场上与他相遇。领头人在压力下*骑马离开。“你有死的倾向吗？”骑士嘲讽道。接下来是更多恶毒的*言论。他们催促马匹相互靠近。我们的英雄只需一击，就能斩首反派。其他人逃离以避免即将到来的*毁灭。骑士将少女骑上马，一同骑回城堡。他们的婚礼很快举行。骑士并不知道，这位美丽的少女其实是个多话的“”，会用尖刻的言语让他的生活变得痛苦。不过，陈词滥调“他们从此幸福地生活着”必须为我们虚构的故事画上句号。

days.story:
Students in the United States should consider themselves lucky when it comes to studying the country’s history. The United States has been a nation for approximately 225 years. We would be ______ if we had to learn as much history as students in Great Britain, for their history goes back some 1,000 years! In that time England has had many interesting and unusual rulers. One who has fascinated us is Henry VIII. Ruling some 450 years ago, he became well known because of his many marriages and his ______ for doing away with some wives who displeased him.

In physical appearance he was unattractive—he was large and ______ . When his first wife could not bear him a son who would be heir to the throne, he divorced her. This caused a break with the Pope who refused to recognize the divorce. Henry VIII sent an ______ to the Pope and renounced Catholicism. He then married Anne Boleyn but decided to ______ her after quickly tiring of her. His third wife died in childbirth, and he divorced his fourth. His fifth, Katherine Howard, was also beheaded. Only his sixth wife was able to live on after Henry’s death in 1547. From this brief history of only one English ruler, it is easy to imagine how much an English history student must learn in order to prepare for an exam. In Henry VIII’s case, one would have to get a “head start.”
days.storyZh:
在美国的学生，在学习国家历史方面应当感到幸运。美国已经建国大约225年了。如果我们必须像英国的学生一样学习历史，那就太______好了，因为他们的历史可以追溯到大约一千年前！在这段时间里，英格兰经历了许多有趣且不寻常的统治者。其中一位令我们着迷的是亨利八世。他大约450年前统治，因多次婚姻和______除掉一些令他不满的妻子而闻名。

外貌上他不讨喜——身材高大且体型______。当他的第一任妻子无法为他生下继承王位的儿子时，他与她离婚。这导致教皇与教皇决裂，教皇拒绝承认离婚。亨利八世向教皇发出______，并放弃了天主教。随后他娶了安妮·博林，但很快厌倦了她，决定与她______。他的第三任妻子在分娩时去世，第四任妻子离婚。他的第五个女王凯瑟琳·霍华德也被斩首。亨利于1547年去世后，只有他的第六任妻子得以继续存活。从这段仅有一位英格兰统治者的简短历史中，很容易想象英国历史学生为了备考需要学习多少。在亨利八世的情况下，必须“抢先一步”。

days.title: A MIGHTY EMPIRE

examples.en: One of the anomalies* of our approach to history is the propensity* to study the venerable* empires of Europe, but we do not feel it incumbent* upon us to ascertain anything about the civilizations in our own hemisphere.
examples.zh: 我们历史观的一个异常*是倾向于研究欧洲那些古老的*帝国，但我们并不觉得有责任*去了解我们自己半球的文明。

days.passage: One of the anomalies* of our approach to history is the propensity* to study the venerable* empires of Europe, but we do not feel it incumbent* upon us to ascertain anything about the civilizations in our own hemisphere. We deprecate* the history of this part of the world as though progress lay dormant and that other peoples were irrelevant* until the settlers of North America arrived at Plymouth Rock. In South America, from 2000 B.C. until their empire reached its acme* at the beginning of the 16th century, lived the Incas. The site* of the capital city of the Inca empire, Cusco, lay at a height of 11,000 feet. This civilization is reputed* to have burgeoned until it covered more than 2,500 miles of the western part of the continent. Its population fluctuated* between 4 and 7 million. This empire had a highly efficacious* political and social system. Its potentate ruled with absolute power. As the empire conquered new lands, it would disseminate its language, religion, and social customs.

days.passageZh: 我们历史观的一个异常*是倾向于研究欧洲那些古老的*帝国，但我们并不觉得有责任*去了解我们自己半球的文明。我们贬低*这片地区的历史，仿佛进步沉睡，其他民族在北美定居者抵达普利茅斯岩之前都无关紧要*。在南美洲，从公元前2000年直到16世纪初帝国达到巅峰*，印加人居住着。印加帝国首都库斯科的遗址*位于海拔11,000英尺。据说该文明曾迅速发展，覆盖了大陆西部超过2500英里。其人口在400万到700万之间波动。这个帝国拥有极具效率的政治和社会体系。其权贵以绝对权力统治。随着帝国征服新土地，它传播了自己的语言、宗教和社会习俗。

days.passage: The Inca emperor derived his prodigious* power and authority from the gods. The paramount* god was the sun god. It was from him the ruler passed on his prerogative to rule to his most astute* son. This nepotism had worked with great efficacy* for centuries. The land holdings were immense;* there were rich farmlands and llamas and alpacas for wool. Precious metals were plentiful: silver, copper, bronze, and the most sacred of all, gold. This metal resembled the sun god whom they extolled.* There was no dearth of idols and ornaments hammered from this gleaming metal. There was always more gold coming from the mines to replenish* the supply. At the acme* of his power, the Inca ruler died without naming the requisite* successor. In 1528 two sons began an internecine struggle for control. For the next 4 years the empire sank into the lassitude* caused by civil war.

days.passage: The feuding between the rival sons reached its pinnacle* in 1532; at that moment Francisco Pizarro came onto the scene. A native of Spain, he was sojourning* in Panama when he heard of the riches to be found in that far off land. Overwhelmed with cupidity,* but still a tyro when it came to wresting* power and wealth from hapless* people, he joined with an inveterate* adventurer. They gathered a small band of mercenaries.* The first two attempts failed, and Pizarro returned to Spain to request authority and money in order to conquer the West Coast of South America. Whether by sophistry or cajolery,* he was given the requisite* aid. With a force of 180 men, the dregs* of society, he invaded Inca territory. He reached the city where the current ruler, Atahualpa, was holding court. The Incas welcomed Pizarro who, in a factitious display of friendship, heaped encomiums upon Atahualpa. Unknown to the Incas, Pizarro had brought guns that were still beyond the technology* of these people. The obloquy of his next act, ambushing the Incas and taking Atahualpa prisoner, will live in the history books that are replete* with tales of conquest.

days.passageZh: 竞争儿子之间的争斗在1532年达到顶峰*;就在这时，弗朗西斯科·皮萨罗出现了。他是西班牙人，在巴拿马停留*时，听说了那片遥远土地上的财富。他贪婪欲望强烈，*但在从无助者手中夺取*权力和财富*方面依然是个“铁腕”，于是他加入了一位根深蒂固的*冒险者。他们召集了一支小队雇佣兵。* 前两次尝试均告失败，皮萨罗返回西班牙请求权力和资金以征服南美西海岸。无论是通过诡辩还是劝说，*他都得到了必要的*援助。他率领180名士兵——社会的渣滓*入侵印加领土。他抵达了现任统治者阿塔瓦尔帕主持朝会的城市。印加人欢迎皮萨罗，皮萨罗以虚假的友谊表现，对阿塔瓦尔帕赞美不已。印加人并不知道，皮萨罗带来了那些仍然超出这些人技术水平的枪械。他接下来的行为——伏击印加人并俘虏阿塔瓦尔帕——的诽谤将载入史册，书中充满了征服的故事。

days.passage: The Machiavellian* Pizarro held the captured Atahualpa for ransom. He was adamant* about receiving a room filled with gold to the height of a man’s shoulder. This was taken as a hyperbole at first, but Pizarro knew the gullible* Incas would be munificent when it came to rescuing their sacred ruler. They did not procrastinate,* and a frenetic* collection of gold took place. Pizarro, to whom prevarication was natural in dealing with the Incas, had no qualms* about executing their ruler as soon as he had the gold. The Inca empire was moribund,* but the charisma that surrounded Atahualpa was such that, after his death, the Incas fought on tenaciously* in his name for several years. Eventually, superior weapons quelled* all opposition. A policy of genocide was adopted by the Spanish conquerors, and almost two million of these proud people died in the carnage* that followed. The saga* of an ancient civilization thus came to an end.

days.storyZh:
你在阅读这本书的过程中不断强化和建立基础词汇。测试、测验和练习帮助你______了进步。我们希望你已经带着每周______的词汇掌握度读完《你需要知道的1100个词》。你的关注和关注在很多方面都得到了回报。你会从阅读各种主题的文章中获得______乐趣和知识。你更有信心地阅读、学习、交流和写作。

让你开始建立词汇的目标现在不应该变成______。从这本书中，能够获得一种持久的学习新词的渴望。我们希望你因英语词汇掌握而获得的任何评______，都能激励你不断掌握所需词汇。

days.title: TITANIC MYSTERY
days.passage: On April 14, 1912, an incident took place that became a front page story in newspapers all over the world. It is a tale that has continued to capture the attention of movie and theatre goers, of opera and television audiences, of novelists and playwrights—it’s the story of the allegedly impregnable Titanic, the unsinkable majestic ocean liner that tumbled to the bottom of the icy Atlantic waters with 1600 passengers still aboard. How could such a toxic tragedy have occurred? Could it have been avoided? How could the naval patriarch, Captain Edward Smith, no neophyte he, have allowed the disaster to happen? What were the extenuating circumstances that led to the death of that glorious White Star queen? In September 1985, the hulk of the Titanic was found on the ocean’s floor, providing many answers to the questions that seamen and landlubbers had wrestled with over the years.

exercises.text: Coal miners are often subject to ________________ fumes.

idiom.meaning: trial, test, exercise

days.passageZh: 1912年4月14日，发生了一起事件，成为全球报纸头条。这是一个持续吸引电影和戏剧观众、歌剧和电视观众、小说家和剧作家关注的故事——它讲述了据称坚不可摧的泰坦尼克号，这艘不沉的雄伟远洋客轮沉入冰冷的大西洋海底，船上仍有1600名乘客。如此有毒的悲剧怎么会发生？这本可以避免吗？海军族长爱德华·史密斯船长，他可不是新手，怎么会允许这场灾难发生？导致那位光荣的白星女王去世的特殊情况是什么？1985年9月，泰坦尼克号的残骸在海底被发现，这为多年来海员和陆地居民一直苦苦挣扎的问题提供了许多答案。
